title: Design and Implementation of Cloud-Ground-End Collaborative Zero-Trust Security Architecture for Industrial Drones
authors: Chen Yuan, Haifeng Shan, Mei Chen, Zhanfeng Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Faced with the security challenges of industrial drones in the “cloud-ground-end” dynamic collaborative operation, traditional static boundary protection is ineffective, leading to out-of-control access and data leakage. This paper designs and implements a collaborative zero-trust security architecture. First, an overall architecture with dynamic trust assessment as the core and separation of policy decision-making and execution was built. Then, a real-time trust quantification model and dynamic access control strategy that integrated multi-dimensional contextual information were designed. Then, a lightweight security module suitable for resource-constrained terminals was developed. Finally, a policy synchronization and trust chain transfer mechanism that supports seamless cross-domain switching was implemented. Experiments show that this architecture can effectively trigger active blocking (e.g., 8 blocks/600 seconds), control performance overhead (e.g., CPU increase of approximately 52%), and ensure business continuity (cross-domain interruption of approximately 158ms). The conclusion confirms that this architecture provides a dynamic and endogenous safety solution for industrial drone operations in an open environment.

title: IoT-Integrated Smart Monitoring of Buck-Boost Converter Performance in Multi-Voltage Battery Systems
authors: G. Rajasekar, N V Kishore Kumar, K. Pradeepa, R. Priyadharshini, G. Janani, A. Naveena Sree
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The proliferation of multi-voltage battery systems in portable electronics, electric mobility and smart energy platforms has generated an increased demand for real-time monitoring and efficient voltage regulation capabilities inside power converters. Therefore, this work presents an Internet of Things (IoT) based smart monitoring architecture for a Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) driven buck-boost converter connected to a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 V} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{2 4 V} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{4 8 V}$</tex> battery system, with a focus on converter operation characterised by thermal deviation and mode-wise performance evaluation. The research gap being filled is the unique combination of converter operation analysis coupled with IoT-based performance observation in diverse application; a multi-level battery scenario wherein stable voltage conversion and current control are required while also maintaining thermal reliability under varying input conditions. Duty-cycle, switching-frequency, buck, boost and buck-boost conversion relations are used to develop the mathematical modelling. The results detailed indicate that for the selected battery inputs, buck-mode output voltages of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.2 ~\mathrm{V}, 14.4 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> and 28.8 V are achieved alongside boost-mode output voltages of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 ~\mathrm{V}, 60 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> and 120 V. The respective output currents in buck mode are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.72 ~\mathrm{A}, 1.44$</tex> A and 2.88 A. While boost-mode currents are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.5 ~\mathrm{A}, 3.0 ~\mathrm{A}$</tex> and 6.0 A, thermal profiling shows a variation from 34° C to 57° C across the range of operation, indicating the practical necessity for some spot thermal control in intelligent converter applications.

title: Design of a Compact Diamond-Shaped MIMO Antenna with Enhanced Isolation Characteristics Using Hybrid DGS and EBG Techniques
authors: Abdulbari M. Almaghribi, Moustafa M. Abdalla, Albatool A. Elmezwghi
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: This paper presents a compact dual-port diamondshaped planar wideband MIMO antenna for millimeter-wave $\mathbf{5 G}$ applications operating over the $20-48 \mathrm{GHz}$ frequency range. A new hybrid decoupling approach combining a Defected Ground Structure (DGS) and Electromagnetic Band-Gap (EBG) elements is employed to effectively mitigate mutual coupling between antenna ports. The proposed antenna demonstrates excellent MIMO performance, particularly at 30 GHz, with isolation better than -30 dB and ultra-low envelope correlation coefficient (ECC) well below 0.0001, a channel capacity loss (CCL) below 0.15 bits/s/Hz, and a total active reflection coefficient (TARC) of -8.36 dB. The synergistic integration of DGS and EBG structures significantly enhances port isolation and diversity characteristics while maintaining wide impedance bandwidth. To ensure numerical reliability, the antenna performance is cross-validated using two independent full-wave electromagnetic solvers: Ansys HFSS and CST Microwave Studio. The results confirm that the proposed antenna is a strong candidate for compact, highperformance wideband 5G millimeter-wave wireless communication systems.

title: Research on Pipeline Risk Event Recognition Algorithm Based on CNN-Swin Transformer
authors: Jinlu Wang, Yingying Wang, Liang Zhu, Jinhuan Xu, Zhenbo Sun, Xingyu Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The monitoring of pipelines is of great significance for reducing safety hazards and improving economic benefits. To address the issue of low accuracy in the identification of pipeline risk events by distributed acoustic sensing (DAS) systems, an event recognition algorithm combining the convolutional neural network (CNN) and the Swin Transformer architecture is proposed. Firstly, the Swin Transformer is used to process the spatiotemporal graph, modeling the global relationship through its attention mechanism. Subsequently, the one-dimensional data is transformed into a two-dimensional time-frequency graph via the generalized S transform (GST), and then the time-frequency graph is processed by the CNN to extract time-frequency features. Finally, the multi-level features are fused and the pipeline risk events are accurately identified through a classifier. Experimental results show that the average recognition accuracy of the proposed algorithm reaches 95.20 %, which is an improvement over other algorithms, providing an effective means for pipeline monitoring.

title: Indian Currency Recognition with Audio Feedback for Visually Impaired People Using YOLOv5
authors: M. Malathi, A. Philomina Jenifer, N. Ashok Kumar, M.I. Anju, M. Bharathi, P. Nagarajan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: One of the most important elements of financial independence for the blind and visually impaired is the ability to identify and differentiate between currency denominations rapidly. This relatively straightforward activity poses tremendous challenges, however, to the complete engagement of the blind and visually impaired in economic transactions. Equal access to money's acceptance is key to promoting inclusiveness and independence because money continues to be a basic aspect of everyday commerce. Since conventional means of determining currency often depend on touch characteristics or external aid, visually impaired individuals are less autonomous when dealing with money. This paper solves these issues by proposing a new solution that is particularly beneficial for visually impaired individuals: it employs the YOLOv5 algorithm to detect various denominations of currency and offer auditory feedback We propose a solution using computer vision and deep learning to develop real-time, accurate, and rapid answers to credit and banknote denomination detection problems using latest techniques. Our goal is to develop a robust system that is able to appropriately identify the different denominations of banknotes in various settings, to help address the challenge of banknote denomination detection under various climatic conditions This article describes how we set up, deployed and tested our YOLOv5 currency recognition system. The quality of this system has been determined through extensive testing with an accuracy of 96.4 %. As part of the effort to promote the independence of visually impaired persons, we aim to eliminate the barriers posed by cash transactions

title: AI Based Irrigation and Weather Forecasting System Using Lorawan
authors: G.Ravikumar Reddy, Bandari Manisha, Ramadevi Vemula
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: For Precision agriculture, the model developed in this research provides efficient method for field monitoring and automatic irrigation management, it uses LoRa technology, Arduino, Raspberry pi and cloud. By utilizing cutting-edge technology, a novel method for improving irrigation as developed. Plant parameters are measured using sensors, which are interfaced with an Arduino UNO to collect the data. Using LoRa technology the Sensor data is transferred to Raspberry Pi. integrating sensor data with meteorological information weather forecast model optimize irrigation. Sensors located near plants guarantee accurate data for watering plants individually. The proposed model focuses on precision forming and effective utilization of natural resources for harmony with nature.

title: Fault Diagnosis of TRD Machine Based on TimeGAN-LSTM-LightGBM
authors: Liang Zhao, Jingxi Cao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The construction of the TRD (Trench Cutting Remixing Deep Wall) machine faces many challenges, such as complex working conditions and numerous disturbance factors. Its fault conditions often manifest as a combination of multiple operational parameters. Due to the requirement that frequent faults are not allowed during construction, fault samples are typically difficult to obtain, which poses challenges for training fault diagnosis models. Additionally, if faults and their types cannot be diagnosed promptly and accurately, significant property losses and casualties may occur. To address the scarcity of fault samples for the TRD machine and leverage the temporal characteristics of faults, a fault diagnosis method based on the TimeGAN-LSTM-LightGBM model is proposed. First, the collected raw time-series dataset is cleaned and used to train the TimeGAN model with three types of fault samples for sample expansion. Next, a three-layer stacked LSTM network is employed to extract temporal features. Finally, the generated samples are used to train the LightGBM model for fault diagnosis and performance metric analysis. An experimental study was conducted using the operational data from a selected TRD machine over the past year, following the above steps. The results demonstrate that the proposed model exhibits good diagnostic performance and can detect a higher proportion of fault samples, thereby verifying the effectiveness of the diagnosis method.

title: An Improved DEIM-Based Algorithm for X-Ray Prohibited Item Detection
authors: Xuemei Ni, Chunling Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: X-ray security inspection faces challenges of blurred boundaries, occlusion, and small objects. This paper proposes an improved DEIM-D-FINE-N algorithm for prohibited item detection. The MANet-LWGA backbone enhances boundary features via multi-path aggregation. The SPAE module decouples features for occlusion reasoning. FMFFNet integrates shallow features to boost small object detection. Experiments on SIXray show 95.5% mAP50 with 3.17M parameters and 129.3 FPS, outperforming mainstream detectors while meeting real-time requirements.

title: Robust Trajectory Tracking Control for Quadrotor UAV Based on Adaptive Dynamic Programming and Finite-Time Observer
authors: Yixiang Guo
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address the trajectory tracking problem of quadrotor unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) under model parameter uncertainties (mass, moment of inertia, friction coefficients) and external time-varying disturbances, this paper proposes a robust control scheme that combines a Finite-Time Neural Network Observer with Adaptive Dynamic Programming. First, the nonlinear dynamic model of the quadrotor UAV is established and decoupled into position and attitude subsystems. Second, a finite-time neural network observer based on an integral sliding mode surface is designed for fast and accurate estimation of the lumped disturbances in the system. Subsequently, an approximate optimal controller is designed based on the ActorCritic architecture, utilizing neural networks to online approximate the solution of the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equation, achieving optimal tracking in unknown dynamic environments. Finally, MATLAB simulations verify the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed control strategy under complex disturbances.

title: CNC-MIND: An Explainable Multi-Task Digital Twin for CNC Machining
authors: Shakil Mohammad Nurus Sobha, Xiaobin Li, Islam Ariful, Fatemeh Bahmaninezhad
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Computer Numerical Control (CNC) machining generates rich multi-modal sensor data during operation, yet practical monitoring systems typically assess tool condition, process execution, and part quality in isolation. This separation limits diagnostic insight and reduces the effectiveness of digital twin-based decision support in production environments. This paper presents <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$C N C$</tex>-MIND, an explainable multi-task digital twin framework for CNC machining that jointly predicts tool condition, machining completion status, and post-process visual inspection outcomes from time-windowed sensor signals and process metadata. The framework employs a hybrid CNN-LSTM-DNN architecture to capture local signal patterns, longterm temporal dynamics, and operational context within a shared representation. To improve physical consistency, a physics-guided regularization term penalizes predictions that violate known relationships between vibration energy and tool degradation. Model transparency is achieved through integrated SHAP explanations, enabling both global feature attribution and instancelevel diagnostic reasoning. The proposed approach is evaluated on a real-world CNC milling dataset from the University of Michigan SMART Lab, demonstrating consistent performance gains over tuned DNN, CNN, and LSTM baselines across all tasks. CNC-MIND is further deployed within an interactive web-based digital twin platform, illustrating how multi-task predictions and explainability can be operationalized for human-in-the-loop CNC monitoring.

title: Hybrid RL-PID for Intelligent Lighting Control in Container-based Indoor Farming
authors: Albert, Good Fried Panggabean, Rahmad Romadhon, Endra Joelianto
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)
abstract: Container-based indoor farming presents a viable solution for increasing food production efficiency while reducing land use and mitigating environmental uncertainties. However, maintaining optimal lighting conditions remains a challenge due to the complete reliance on artificial light sources. This study proposes a Hybrid RL-PID (Reinforcement Learning - Proportional-Integral-Derivative) control approach for intelligent lighting regulation in an indoor controlled environment agriculture (CEA) system. The hybrid method integrates the fast response of PID controllers with the adaptive learning capabilities of reinforcement learning to optimize energy consumption while ensuring ideal light intensity for plant growth. Simulation results indicate that the proposed Hybrid RL-PID achieved 22% energy savings compared to conventional PID control, reduced oscillations by 50% through moving average filtering, and stabilized light intensity in 15 iterations without overshooting. The findings highlight the potential of Hybrid RL-PID as a superior lighting control solution for smart farming applications and energy-efficient CEA systems.

title: Pushing Bit-Width Limits in LLM Quantization with Saliency-Guided Mix-Precision Allocation and Learnable Affine Transformation
authors: Shuoyu Ma, Wenrui Dai, Maida Cao, Shaohui Li, Ziyang Zheng, Chenglin Li, Junni Zou, Hongkai Xiong
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: Despite strong generalization ability, large language models (LLMs) are resource-intensive to deploy. Post-training quantization (PTQ) effectively compresses pretrained LLMs without retraining. Although recent studies target extremely low-bit quantization, most methods rely on extra parameters and the effective bit-width usually exceeds 2 bits. We introduce a saliency-guided mixed-precision PTQ framework that achieves an average bit-width of 1.70 bits while maintaining competitive performance. The proposed method allocates bit-widths across weight groups under a strict total budget, guided by a cross-precision saliency metric. The key innovation is a learnable affine transformation consisting of rotation, scaling, and shifting to align quantization grids with local weight distributions to minimize error. Experiments show that the proposed method matches state-of-the-art low-bit PTQ performance, while reducing the average bit-width by approximately 0.55 bits in Table 1.

title: Sparse2Dense: A Keypoint-Driven Generative Framework for Human Video Compression and Vertex Prediction
authors: Bolin Chen, Ru-Ling Liao, Yan Ye, Jie Chen, Shanzhi Yin, Xinrui Ju, Shiqi Wang, Yibo Fan
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: For bandwidth-constrained multimedia applications, simultaneously achieving ultra-low bitrate human video compression and accurate vertex prediction remains a critical challenge, as it demands the harmonization of dynamic motion modeling, detailed appearance synthesis, and geometric consistency. To address this challenge, we propose Sparse2Dense, a keypoint-driven generative framework that leverages extremely sparse 3D keypoints as compact transmitted symbols to enable ultra-low bitrate human video compression and precise human vertex prediction. The key innovation is the multi-task learning-based and keypointaware deep generative model, which could encode complex human motion via compact 3D keypoints and leverage these sparse keypoints to estimate dense motion for video synthesis with temporal coherence and realistic textures. Additionally, a vertex predictor is integrated to learn human vertex geometry through joint optimization with video generation, ensuring alignment between visual content and geometric structure. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed Sparse2Dense framework achieves competitive compression performance for human video over traditional/generative video codecs, whilst enabling precise human vertex prediction for downstream geometry applications. As such, Sparse2Dense is expected to facilitate bandwidth-efficient human-centric media transmission, such as realtime motion analysis, virtual human animation, and immersive entertainment.

title: A 28nm 11.2-to-20-GHz PLL-based 16-Phase Clock Generator with High-Order-Mode Ring Oscillator Achieving 24.3-fs Jitter for 320-Gsps-Sample-Rate Data Converter
authors: Zonglin Ye, Longxiang Hou, Yuxuan Sun, Yuhan Ding, Yixuan Wen, Hongyang Zhang, Hao Luo, Yi Yang, Chenming Zhang, Xinlin Geng, Qian Xie, Cheng Wang, Jun Zhou, Zheng Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This work proposes a PLL-based multi-phase clock generator with a high-order-mode ring oscillator (HOMRO) that achieves high frequency and multi-phase capability simultaneously and an ultra-low-noise sampling loop. The proposed MPCG is measured to achieve 24.3-fs jitter and generate 20-GHz 16-phase clock with 440-fs skew. The tuning range of the MPCG is across 11.2 to 20 GHz, with jitter variation of ±6.3 fs and skew variation of ±60 fs.

title: Rapid Diagnosis of Foot-and-Mouth Disease in Livestock Using Supervised Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Ebtsam Mahmmod, Almabruk Sultan
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Foot and mouth disease (FMD) is considered a major cause of economic losses in many countries, and early diagnosis is crucial to minimizing these losses. This research proposes a novel approach for early FMD diagnosis using a supervised learning algorithm. The main objective is to develop an FMD diagnosis model using a preprocessing approach and selecting important features with an Extra Tree. The cattle symptom dataset, a freely available and publicly accessible resource, was utilized, and its application was examined using the Random Forest (RF) and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) algorithms. After comparing the two, the XGBoost algorithm achieved the highest accuracy, reaching 100% in FMD diagnosis. This demonstrates that machine learning (ML) can be relied upon to develop more effective methods for FMD diagnosis, assisting veterinarians in early disease diagnosis and preserving livestock.

title: Distance Enhancement and Misalignment Robustness in WPT System Employing Higher Order Compensation Topology and ZVS
authors: Praduman Amroliya, S. K. Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Wireless power transfer (WPT) related issues such as distance optimization, misalignment resilience and zero voltage switching (ZVS) can be handled by higher order compensation. The proposed work aims to improve the effectiveness of wireless power transfer (WPT) systems. The study explores the function of compensation design in maximizing mutual inductance and guaranteeing effective power transmission over long distances utilizing a MATLABbased modeling technique. To preserve steady ZVS functioning and lessen the negative consequences of coil misalignment— in real-world WPT applications—advanced compensation topologies are examined. The simulation findings show that high-order correction increases the resilience of WPT systems over varying operating circumstances in addition to increase efficiency and dependability. The quality factor (Q) and the coupling coefficient (k) of the system is calculated as 42.73 and 0.9 respectively, which ensure optimum power transfer efficiency of the system. The proposed MATLAB model design is a potential option for high-performance WPT applications including industrial automation and electric car charging as it achieves dependable long-distance operation and resilience against misalignment.

title: A 500kHz 600V Capacitive Isolated SiC Converter with Switching-On Transition Detection for Hard-Switching Fault Prognosis Achieving 58ns Short-Circuit Protection
authors: Qing Yuan, Yingping Chen, Ming Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: Focusing on silicon-carbide (SiC) reliability, a capacitive isolated SiC power system is designed for 500kHz 600V power processing. A switching-on transition detector is developed to achieve fast hard-switching fault (HSF) prognosis. In addition, a tri-state H-bridge gate driver is fully integrated to avoid false conduction of the low-side SiC power transistor, mitigating the crosstalk under high-speed and high-voltage switching operation. Fabricated in a 0.35μm BCD process, the design realizes an HSF detection within 45.5ns and short-circuit protection within 58ns, improved by at least 4 times. The Miller coupling effect successfully removed under a voltage slew rate of 125V/ns, while reducing the area of the fully integrated tri-state gate driver by 2.7 times.

title: TOKCIM: A 65nm All-Digital Compute-in-Memory Transformer Accelerator with Advanced Weight Sparsity Handling and SoftMax Acceleration
authors: Shaoxuan Li, Zikun Wei, Ruihao He, Cong Wang, Ye Gu, Tianchu Dong, Yucheng Cai, Hongwu Jiang
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper presents TOKCIM, a Transformer accelerator based on digital compute-in-memory (DCIM) structure. TOKCIM adopts all-zero initialization (AZI) characteristics in the DCIM macros, together with group pruning of weights to fully leverage the benefits of AZI, speeding up both weight loading process and in-memory computing process. The SoftMax unit (SMU) in TOKCIM employs an optimal value selection strategy. By selecting several optimal values among inputs and performing SoftMax operations only on the selected values, the proposed SMU can significantly reduce the overhead of activation calculations with negligible degradation on performance. Fabricated in 65-nm CMOS process and benchmarked on Q8BERT model and GLUE dataset, TOKCIM achieves a measured energy efficiency of 5.73 TOPS/W and a compute efficiency of 0.29 TOPS/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance.

title: A 10-bit 20-GS/s 4-Channel Time-Interleaved DAC Employing Hybrid-Segmentation in 28-nm CMOS
authors: Yunjie Chen, Duran Ckristian, Koji Asami, Sota Kano, Ritaro Takenaka, Zolboo Byambadorj, Masahiro Ishida, Akio Higo, Tetsuya Iizuka
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper proposes a four-channel (4-ch) time-interleaved (TI) high-speed current-steering Digital-to-Analog Converter (DAC) with hybrid segmentation that employs a single-stage analog multiplexer followed by a current summation. Compared with conventional 4-ch structures, the proposed architecture simplifies the clocking scheme by reducing the number of required clocks and eliminating the need for high-speed clock signals. In addition, the proposed design does not have the dead-zone in the middle of the bandwidth and reduces the sensitivity to duty-cycle mismatches. The DAC was fabricated in 28 nm CMOS, incorporating a simple differential clock timing calibration to ensure precise synchronization of sub-DACs. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed 10-bit, 20-GS/s DAC achieves >40 dBc spurious-free dynamic range (SFDR) for a signal frequency up to 6.3 GHz, representing an improvement over state-of-the-art prior 4-ch time-interleaved DACs

title: Detection and Classification of Abnormal Human Actions for Video Surveillance on Edge Devices
authors: Jonathan Flores Monroy, Gibran Benitez Garcia, Mariko Nakano Miyatake, Hiroki Takahashi
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: The increase in violence in urban areas has created an urgent need for systems capable of accurately identifying actions that endanger citizens. In this context, video surveillance systems supported by video anomaly detection approaches have served as a foundation for developing automatic solutions to identify unusual behaviors; however, most of these methods focus exclusively on detection and do not address the detailed classification of anomalies. This work proposes a modular framework for the joint detection and classification of Abnormal Human Actions, understood as anomalous actions caused by humans that pose a threat to public safety. The proposed approach, evaluated on an edge device, achieved an accuracy of $59.02 \%$ in Abnormal Human Actions classification, a detection rate of $80.0 \%$ on the UCF-Crime dataset, and a processing speed of 283.49 fps, demonstrating its potential for deployment in real-world, resource-constrained video surveillance environments.

title: ICST 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 18th International Conference on Sensing Technology (ICST)
