title: Research on Sample Imbalanced Fault Diagnosis of Ship Water Pump Bearing Dataset Based on HCAB-SMOTE
authors: Zhijia Yan, Renchao Cai, Lin Huang, Baozhu Jia, Xuewei Song, Zhiqiang Liao, Feipeng Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Ships and Electromechanical System (ICISES)
abstract: Aiming at the case of imbalanced dataset in ship water pump bearing fault diagnosis, which lead to low diagnostic efficiency, this paper proposes a hybrid clustering-adaptive borderline synthetic minority oversampling technique (HCAB-SMOTE) for fault diagnosis. First, the proposed method applies band-pass filtering to filter the original vibration signals noise and extract fault-related time-frequency features. Then, HCAB-SMOTE is utilized to intelligently oversample minority fault samples, mitigating the impact of imbalance dataset on classifier performance. The effectiveness and feasibility of this method have been demonstrated through validation using laboratory bearing datasets and analysis of comparative experiments. The results indicate that the proposed method outperforms other methods in all evaluation metrics and achieves a maximum diagnostic accuracy of 98 %. These findings indicate that the proposed method is effective for diagnosing faults in ship water pump bearings.

title: Analyzing Performance–Memory–Security Trade-Offs of Convolutions for DNN Inference on Homomorphically Encrypted Data
authors: Núria Moreno-Chamorro, Maribel Castillo, José I. Aliaga, Manuel F. Dolz
publish: 2025 24th International Symposium on Parallel and Distributed Computing (ISPDC)
abstract: Homomorphic Encryption (HE) enables privacy-preserving Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) on encrypted data but incurs substantial computational overhead, especially in convolution operations. In this work, we implement and evaluate three baseline convolution methods in TenSEAL to enhance the efficiency of encrypted DNN inference: (1) Toeplitz, which restructures weight tensors; (2) Lowering, based on the im2row transform; and (3) Direct, which performs convolution directly on encrypted data. Experimental evaluation of synthetic configurations and the CryptoNet architecture shows that Direct reduces computation time by up to $\mathbf{1 5} \times$ and minimizes memory overhead compared to Toeplitz and Lowering. However, in settings where linear layers can be fused, as in CryptoNet, Toeplitz achieves better performance. This suggests that a hybrid approach, combining the Toeplitz and Direct methods, is more effective for optimizing DNN inference under HE. Furthermore, we show that avoiding explicit padding and leveraging encrypted indexing in Direct not only accelerate convolution but also reduce the memory footprint with larger kernels. These advances strengthen the balance between performance, memory consumption, scalability, and security in privacy-preserving deep learning with HE.

title: EEG-Based Early Detection System for Alzheimer's and Parkinson's Disease
authors: Ratnalata Gupta, Sandip Bhattacharya, Shraddha Jain Sharma, Mamta Anand
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Parkinson's disease (PD) and Alzheimer's disease (AD) are two prevalent and progressive neurodegenerative diseases affecting cognitive and motor functions in the brain. Early diagnoses are crucial in planning the appropriate treatment for the diseases. We propose a lightweight EEGbased diagnostic framework for the early detection of Alzheimer's disease, Mild Cognitive Impairment, and healthy controls. Multitaper power spectral density estimation for the frequency range of 1 to 49 Hz is used to analyze the EEG signals sampled at 512 Hz from each channel of the EEG headset with 32 channels. GANs or autoencoders for data augmentation, followed by feature selections or PCA, are used for better generalization performance. Ensemble classifiers XGBoost, LogitBoost, and Gradient Boosting are used for the classification task. The features in the power spectral density are identified using Explainable AI techniques such as SHAP and LIME. This approach shows promising results in accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC values for validating the efficiency of the approach.

title: HRM Practices in Small-sized Institutes within the Education Industry: Evidence from Qualitative Case Studies
authors: Piseth Lonh, Duongchan Boremey Sim, Sovannarith Chheng, Vannak Seng, Kimseng Tieng
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: Human Resource Management (HRM) plays a critical role in organizational development; however, small-sized educational institutes tend to face challenges in HRM. This leads us to define HRM practices in small-sized educational institutions. A qualitative approach was conducted in December 2024 by interviewing the top management of three private educational institutes, i.e., STEAM Tuition Centre, Chhouk Meas Centre, and Millennium School, at their offices. Each interview takes approximately 90-180 min. During the interview, we were allowed to record voices to cross-check information for data summary. Results indicate that HRM practices such as Recruitment & Selection, Training & Development, and Retention & Compensation, are mainly adopted by small-sized institutes. However, the HRM practices tend to be limited across the three institutes. For Recruitment & Selection, firstly, the institutes mainly adopt teacher-to-teacher referrals as their primary method for finding new teaching staff. For Training & Development, secondly, the institutes offer on-the-job training as the key practice to upgrade staff capabilities on classroom management and teaching methods. For Retention & Compensation, lastly, the institutions recognized the importance of staff appreciation by organizing a year-end party, providing seniority payments, and offering benefits based on KPI. For practical implication, the top managements should (1) realize the common framework of HRM practices including Recruitment & Selection, Training & Development, and Retention & Compensation; (2) adopt at least the teacher-to-teacher referral for Recruitment & Selection, offer on-the-job-training for Training & Development, and organize year-end party with bonus sharing based on KPI for Retention & Compensation; and (3) design more innovative HRM practices to pool highly competitive candidates from diverse sources to work at the institutes.

title: Ensemble ConvNeXt-LSTM Framework for Smart Surface Defect Detection in Hot-Rolled Steel Strips
authors: Lakshmaiya N
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Industrial Automation is a key trend, as manual inspection leads to time-lagging processes. By the manufacturers, hot-rolled steel strips with surface defects need to be identified based on the manufacturer's demand. Visual inspection obtains only a certain level of clarity in the defects; hence, to obtain the microstructural defects, this work proposes the ConvNeXt model for classification using the temporal features from the LSTM deep learning technique. To enhance the image for feature extraction, a Gabor filter and a thresholdbased segmentation process are used, which help obtain defectbased features from the Region of Interest segmented images. The ensemble ConvNeXt model using the tiny, small and base are used to obtain the seven defects of classification using the Metal Surface Defect Dataset from Kaggle. The proposed work shown 94.28% accuracy and 94.24% F1 score based on the softmax prediction of the classified output using the Fully connected neural network. the proposed work tested for the automation system purposes by real time basis.

title: IoT-Enabled EV Battery Monitoring System With Machine Learning-Based Predictive Analysis
authors: Y. Rajesh Babu, Modepalli. kavya, Ravuri. Bramhani, Manimela. Eswar, Chintha.Sravanthi Pavani, Gunda. Varsha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Thermal behavior is a very important factor in determining the safety level, effectiveness, and lifespan of the battery used in the electric vehicle. If the temperature increases beyond the normal level, it can lead to the aging of the battery, reduction of effectiveness, and safety issues. This paper proposes a machine learning-based system for the prediction and monitoring of the battery's temperature in the electric vehicle. This system will utilize a predictive model based on the deep learning method to forecast the changes in the battery's temperature in the near future based on the real-time acquisition of the battery's parameters. An Adaptive Convolutional Neural Network will be utilized to identify the complex nonlinear relations existing between the voltage, current, state of charge, and temperature of the battery. Based on the experimental analysis, it has been proven that the suggested methodology has the advantages of increased prediction accuracy and decreased errors compared to the machine learning approach. This system will help to manage the battery's heat level in the best way and increase the safety level of the battery.

title: Bio-Inspired Hybrid Antenna Synthesis for Optimizing 5G Network Slicing in Tripoli’s mmWave HetNet
authors: Sara Fkini, Amer R. Zerek
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: The deployment of millimeter-wave (mmWave) $\mathbf{5 G}$ networks in dense urban environments faces significant challenges regarding signal penetration and inter-cell interference, particularly in cities with complex morphologies like Tripoli, Libya. While mmWave offers substantial bandwidth, high path loss and susceptibility to blockage critically limit indoor coverage and spectral efficiency. This paper addresses these limitations by integrating physical layer antenna optimization directly into system-level network planning. We propose a hardware-aware bio-inspired Hybrid Cat Swarm Optimization (CSO) algorithm to synthesize nonuniform linear antenna arrays incorporating realistic active element patterns (AEP) and amplitude tapering for deep side lobe suppression. By mitigating spatial energy leakage, the proposed method reduces the peak side lobe level (SLL) from the standard uniform baseline of $\mathbf{- 1 3 . 5 ~ d B}$ to $\mathbf{- 2 6 ~ d B}$, achieving a 12.5 dB reduction in potential interference. This physical improvement is mapped to a system-level Heterogeneous Network (HetNet) simulation anchored to Tripoli’s geography. Results demonstrate that the CSO-enhanced interference rejection facilitates a massive load rebalancing, increasing small cell utilization from $2.5 \%$ to $22.0 \%$. Furthermore, the suppressed interference floor allows approximately $80 \%$ of users (including indoor UEs) to achieve high-order modulation (256-QAM), driving a 3.5-fold increase in aggregate network slicing capacity and ensuring stable Quality of Service (QoS) for eMBB and URLLC slices.

title: Flow-Guided ConvLSTM with Quality-Aware Reconstruction for Learned Video Compression
authors: Xuan Deng, Xiandong Meng, Hengyu Man, Xiaopeng Fan, Debin Zhao
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: In Learned Video Compression (LVC), contextual information plays a crucial role in reducing temporal redundancy and improving rate-distortion performance. However, most existing approaches rely on a single reference frame or a fixed set of neighboring frames, which limits their ability to exploit long-range temporal dependencies. To overcome this limitation, we propose FGC-QAR (Flow-Guided ConvLSTM with Quality-Aware Reconstruction), a unified framework that combines a Flow-Guided ConvLSTM (FG-ConvLSTM) with a Quality-Aware Reconstruction (QAR) module. FG-ConvLSTM aggregates features across extended temporal spans by explicitly incorporating optical flow as motion guidance. Meanwhile, QAR is implemented using ConvGRU and dynamically modulates its forget and input gates according to the compression quality and content similarity of reference frames. This allows the network to emphasize more reliable temporal cues while suppressing noisy or low-quality information. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed framework consistently improves compression efficiency across multiple datasets and bitrates, highlighting its effectiveness and scalability.

title: P-MEE: A Prompt-Guided Multimodal Dynamic Early Exiting Method for Intent Recognition
authors: Siyu Zhong, Xiaolin Ma, Hailan Kuang, Xinhua Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The rapid development of Edge Intelligence imposes stringent requirements on low-latency human-computer interaction (HCI) for edge devices such as service robots. However, the compute-intensive nature and complex cross-modal fusion mechanisms of existing large multimodal models lead to unacceptable inference lag on resource-constrained devices. To address this core pain point, we propose P-MEE (Prompt-Guided Multimodal Early Exiting), an edge-friendly dynamic inference framework designed to eliminate computational redundancy and achieve real-time intelligent interaction. The core innovation lies in utilizing audio and visual modalities as lightweight “acceleration catalysts,” which are prepended into a text foundation model as continuous prompts and integrated with an entropy-based dynamic early exiting mechanism. This validates our proposed “Multimodal Acceleration Hypothesis,” demonstrating that nonlinguistic clues significantly reduce semantic uncertainty and mechanically drive the model to exit early. Experimental results show that P-MEE achieves a 15 % latency reduction on highperformance servers, and its speedup ratio surges to 44 % under simulated resource-constrained edge scenarios, aligning perfectly with Amdahl's Law. Furthermore, P-MEE exhibits a “robustness-first” exiting behavior in Ultra-Speed Mode, avoiding blind guessing at shallow layers and achieving precise exiting concentrated at the 9th layer (61.8 % exit rate). Ultimately, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P}$</tex> MEE provides a highly adaptable, green, and energy-efficient new paradigm for multimodal perception on the edge.

title: Trend-Aware Time Series Forecasting via Frequency-Enhanced Inverted Architecture
authors: Zeyu Zheng, Zhaolong Teng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Long-term Time Series Forecasting (LTSF) is critical in domains such as meteorology and ene-rgy management. While recent State-of-the-Art (SO-TA) models, particularly xLSTM and its adaptations(e.g., xLSTMTime), demonstrate impressive memory capabilities, they often suffer from “Trend Hallucin-ation” in non-stationary scenarios. Driven by point-wise error minimization (MSE), these models freque-ntly prioritize fitting high-frequency noise over mai-ntaining physical consistency. To address this, we p-ropose the Time-Frequency Enhanced Inverted xLS-TM (TF-iLSTM). Our approach adopts an Inverted Architecture to independently model the temporal d-ynamics of each variate, leveraging the extended memory of xLSTM. Crucially, we introduce a Dual-Do-main Differential Loss, imposing physics-informed c-onstraints: the time-domain derivative acts as a dir-ectional anchor to rectify trend bias, while frequency-domain amplitude preserves global periodicity. Exte-nsive experiments on real-world benchmarks demon-strate that TF-iLSTM maintains competitive MSE performance while significantly improving DirectionalAccuracy (DA) and alleviating trend reversal pheno-mena.

title: The Optimal Solution of the Economic Load Dispatch Problem Using an Improved Gradient-Based Optimization Algorithm
authors: Saleh Masoud Abdallah Altbawi, Mohsen Bakouri, Abdulsalam Abraheem Almahdi, Mohamed Hussin Abdullah Altaher, Anis Suliman Ali Bakouri, Mohammed Abdulhadi Almahdi
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: The improved Gradient-Based Optimization (IGBO) Algorithm is simulated to deal with various real-world problems, like the economic load dispatch (ELD) problem. In the ELD problem, the produced power from the resources has to meet the optimal working conditions while supporting the load demand in order to have reliable operation. In this work, the IGBO has been proposed to find the optimal cost of the ELD problem. With this advanced research scheme, the fuel consumed and the operational costs can be minimized while preserving all the restrictions of the system. Moreover, the results are compared with state-of-the-art optimization algorithms like Gradient-Based Optimization (GBO) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) algorithms. The results displayed that the IGBO algorithm is highly advanced in aspects of robustness, solution quality, and convergence rate. This study proved that the proposed technology can be able to find the optimum generated power of the units while ensuring lower fuel costs.

title: Personnel Intelligent Control in Pumped Storage Power Station Construction Based on Digital Twin Technology
authors: Fei Wang, Zhipeng Tian, Jian Chen, Jianfeng Zhu, Guangran Liu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of large construction scale, complex working environment and difficult personnel management in pumped storage power station projects, this paper takes Xinhua pumped storage power station as the research object, and studies the personnel intelligent management system based on digital twin technology. The system realizes the whole-process management of personnel from entry registration to exit by integrating intelligent hardware such as intelligent safety helmets, IoT positioning equipment and AI video monitoring with digital management platform. Through the application practice, the system has achieved remarkable results in reducing management personnel, improving safety management efficiency and ensuring construction progress. The research shows that the personnel intelligent management mode based on digital twin technology can effectively solve the pain points of traditional personnel management in construction projects, and provide a feasible reference for the digital transformation of personnel management in the field of water conservancy and hydropower engineering.

title: Litchi-PestDet: An Efficient Litchi Disease and Pest Detection Algorithm Based on Deep Learning
authors: Moru Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Lychee yield and quality are greatly affected by diseases and pests, making rapid and accurate detection crucial for effective orchard management. Traditional detection methods are inefficient and struggle with complex orchard environments. This paper proposes Litchi-PestDet, an efficient detection framework based on YOLOv12. It introduces a multi-scale disease feature fusion module (MSPF) to improve detection of diseases and pests at various scales, a dynamic disease area attention mechanism (DHAM) to reduce background interference, and a small-scale pest detection enhancement module (SPDE) to detect early-stage diseases and tiny pests. Experimental results show that LitchiPestDet achieves 94.6% detection accuracy on the lychee dataset, demonstrating its effectiveness in complex orchard settings.

title: Synergistic Copper Sequestration by SA/PEI/GO Hydrogels: Chemisorption-Driven Langmuir Kinetics
authors: Pengbo Chang, Mengran Zhao, Jing Miao, Zixuan Li, Jiating Jing, Zhenting Zhao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: In the present work, composite hydrogels consisting of sodium alginate (SA), polyethyleneimine (PEI) and graphene oxide (GO) were prepared for efficient Cu<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2+</sup> removal from wastewater. The composites were comprehensively characterized by SEM, FT-IR, XRD and EDS. Systematised batch adsorption experiments evaluated the effects of pH, adsorbent dosing, isotherms, kinetics, and thermodynamics. The adsorption mechanism adhered predominantly to the pseudo-second-order kinetic model and the Langmuir isotherm, signifying that monolayer chemisorption was primary. Thermodynamic analysis revealed a spontaneous process with physisorption as the primary interaction. This work advances the design of multifunctional hydrogels, offering a scalable and high capacity (394.706 mg/g) strategy for heavy metal remediation in industrial wastewater treatment.

title: A Framework for Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Assembly Sequence Planning of Aircraft Tube
authors: Chun Liu, Zhikang Zhang, Guanghui Zhou, Jinhui Song, Chao Zhang, Changle Tian, Chenhao Ye
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Assembly sequence planning is a well-known NP-hard problem that must satisfy complex precedence constraints while minimizing time, cost and error rates. To address these challenges, we propose a three-layer deep reinforcement learning framework for aircraft tube assembly sequence planning. Under this framework, first, a unified data standardization scheme processes both CAD-extracted and topology-generated tube assembly information into fixed-size assembly state, precedence and direction matrices; second, the decision layer employs a Double Deep Q-Network (Double DQN) agent enhanced by prioritized experience replay and the sigmoid learning rate scheduler to generate optimal sequences under multiple constraints; third, the application layer converts the learned sequences into step-by-step instructions and interactive visual simulations. Finally, a case study on a forty-tube assembly is conducted to validate the effectiveness of the entire framework.

title: A Highly Sensitive and Robust Electrochemical Biosensor with Anti-Adhesion Property for BK Virus Detection
authors: Luyang Zhang, Ziran Wang, Zaiyu Zhang, Yifan Xu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Reactivation of BK polyomavirus (BK virus lurks) in urine is significantly correlated with the success rate of organ transplantation. Maintaining the stability of biosensor detection in body fluids is very important. In this article, the electrochemical biosensor is developed with anti-adhesion properties for highly sensitive detection of BK Virus. The picosecond laser ablation combined with the fluorination method is used to fabricate porous three-dimensional micro-nano structures on the surface of biosensor electrodes. Micro-nano structures with a diameter of 50 μm pore enable a large number of aptamer probes and fluoride ions modification, achieving the high sensitivity of the biosensor with a limit of detection down to 2.34 nM. The biosensor maintains long-term stability due to the micro-nano structures that can prevent platelet adhesion and bacterial biofilm aggregation. Results show that the biosensor offers the BK virus detection in the range of 5-1000nM and maintains high stability for one month.

title: Objective No-Reference Error Estimation at the Macroblock Level for Compressed Videos
authors: Melan Vijayaratnam, Didier Nicholson
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: We revisit a signal-based strategy grounded in the statistical distribution of DCT coefficients. Such approaches remain crucial to provide objective and reproducible evidence of how the signal evolves under compression, enabling the analysis of degradation mechanisms at their origin that can serve forensic applications. Our method estimates error without requiring a reference video and allows fine-grained analysis down to the macroblock level in H. 264 content, making it possible to identify localized regions within a frame where distortions occur.

title: Aphrophoridae Foam Detection using YOLOv11 for Sustainable Management of Xylella Fastidiosa Vectors
authors: Angelo Cardellicchio, Michele Elia, Arianna Rana, Antonio Petitti, Francesco Paciolla, Simone Pascuzzi, Vito Renò, Annalisa Milella
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: The growing threat of vector-borne pathogens, along with the impacts of pesticides on environmental ecosystems and human health, has significantly increased the interest in sustainable pest control strategies. This paper presents a novel approach for the early detection of Aphrophoridae, generally known as spittlebugs, which are the primary vectors of Xylella fastidiosa (Xf) in Europe. Following the infection, Xf bacteria colonies occlude the wood vessels and prevent water from reaching the canopy, resulting in leaf burning and plant death. The proposed solution exploits recent advances in deep learning techniques to automatically identify the distinctive white foamy secretion ("spittle") produced by Aphrophoridae nymphs in images acquired under field conditions. Specifically, the base versions of YOLOv11 and YOLOv12 are first compared to establish a baseline to determine the most effective approach for the case study. Then, a variant of YOLOv11 is tested by integrating a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) into the neck of the network to allow for more focus both on relevant areas and channels of the fused feature maps before the classification head. The image processing system is intended to guide the action and assess the efficiency of an aeroaulic machine able to generate an airstream with shape and thrust appropriate to remove the target organisms for sustainable management of Xf vectors. Experimental tests were performed in an area covered by wild tall grass, showing that, on average, all the considered models provide a precision above 50%, while achieving recall scores ranging between 35 and 45%. However, the use of CBAM attention provides interesting enhancements to the YOLOv11 model, highlighting its potential to be effectively deployed under constrained settings, while retaining relatively high accuracy and F1 score.

title: Multi-Index Vegetation Monitoring and Stress Detection for Smallholder Rice Farms in Telangana Using Sentinel-2
authors: Raghu Chaliganti, Shreya Champakbhai Chauhan, Ozan Türkes, Karam Tomotaki-Dawoud, Balaji Naik Banoth, Sebastian Bosse
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: This study demonstrates the use of satellite-derived vegetation indices to monitor rice health and detect stress in smallholder farms in Telangana, India, during the Rabi season. Sentinel 2 time series were used to compute NDVI, NDMI, and NDRE, aligned to transplanting dates for field level comparisons. Results showed clear seasonal patterns and variation in peak values and area under the curve (AUC), reflecting differences in management and environmental stress. Cross index analysis confirmed strong correlations, enabling reliable detection of water stress and senescence. The study contributes to the ACRAT project by validating a remote sensing approach that integrates satellite data with local agronomic knowledge to deliver timely, site specific decision support for smallholders in drought prone areas.

title: Non-Invasive Thermal Monitoring of Dairy Cows’ Core Body Temperature with Multi-Modal Sensing
authors: Mireia Molins, Camilla H. K. Hughes, Enrico Casella
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Heat stress significantly impacts the dairy industry, causing substantial economic losses and carrying multi-generational consequences. Current on-farm monitoring relies on environmental metrics like the temperature-humidity index, which fails to account for the animals’ individual thermoregulation. Thus, targeted heat abatement strategies are crucial for animal’s productivity and well-being. Additionally, an animal’s thermoregulatory capacity seems to be linked to other individual traits, such as feed efficiency and methane emission, making individual thermal monitoring a valuable phenotype for genetic selection. This study investigates a novel, multi-modal approach for non-invasive vaginal temperature prediction based on a machine learning framework. The approach integrates thermal imaging of the rump with environmental information, lactation status, and milk production data. Results suggest that combining thermal imaging with heterogeneous data sources may help predict core body temperature. However, a significant challenge may be posed by individual physiological variability, suggesting that personalized models are essential for the future development of this technology.

title: Towards Efficient 3D Gaussian Human Avatar Compression: A Prior-Guided Framework
authors: Shanzhi Yin, Bolin Chen, Xinju Wu, Ru-Ling Liao, Jie Chen, Shiqi Wang, Yan Ye
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: This paper proposes an efficient 3D avatar coding framework that leverages compact human priors and canonical-to-target transformation to enable high-quality 3D human avatar video compression at ultra-low bit rates. The framework begins by training a canonical Gaussian avatar using articulated splatting in a network-free manner, which serves as the foundation for avatar appearance modeling. Simultaneously, a human-prior template is employed to capture temporal body movements through compact parametric representations. This decomposition of appearance and temporal evolution minimizes redundancy, enabling efficient compression: the canonical avatar is shared across the sequence, requiring compression only once, while the temporal parameters, consisting of just 94 parameters per frame, are transmitted with minimal bit-rate. For each frame, the target human avatar is generated by deforming canonical avatar via Linear Blend Skinning transformation, facilitating temporalcoherent video reconstruction and novel view synthesis. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms conventional 2D/3D codecs and existing learnable dynamic 3D Gaussian splatting compression method in terms of rate-distortion performance on mainstream multi-view human video datasets, paving the way for seamless immersive multimedia experiences in meta-verse applications.

title: TCROC–Markov: A Two-Stage Framework for Economic Regime Analysis
authors: Norman Sabillon, Fredy Vides
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: Modeling economic and financial time series presents a dilemma between interpretability and stochastic flexibility. This paper introduces TCROC-Markov, a two-stage hybrid framework that first discretizes a time series into interpretable economic regimes via a local rate of change operator and K-Means clustering, and then models the transitions using a robustly estimated first-order Markov chain. A central contribution of this work is a detailed investigation into the framework’s sensitivity to its key hyperparameters ($W, \lambda, k$). Applying the model to Honduran fuel price series (2017-2025), our initial search revealed a critical pitfall: unrestricted optimization leads to model degeneracy, where excessively large lookback windows (W) yield artificially high performance. By constraining the search to a theoretically defensible domain ($W \leq 52$), our analysis uncovers a key empirical finding: the predictability of fuel prices is overwhelmingly dominated by long-term (annual) trends. The optimally-tuned framework demonstrates robust, interpretable, and statistically significant predictive performance, crucially surpassing the standard MS-AR benchmark, which proved unstable in our validation scenarios.

title: Comparative Security Analysis of Cross-Chain Protocols: An Empirical and Metrics-Based Approach
authors: Carlos Alfaro, Guillermo Calderon, Cesar Espinoza
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: Cross-chain protocols have become critical to blockchain interoperability but remain highly vulnerable to attacks. This paper presents a comparative security analysis based on metrics and empirical evidence, evaluating leading crosschain bridges. The study highlights major attack vectors, trust assumptions, and performance trade-offs, offering insights for more secure and resilient designs

title: MI-STA 2026 Conference Proceedings
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)

title: THReaD: A 22.8 Mbps/node Two-dimensional Hierarchical Reconfigurable and Distributed Network for E-textiles with Fault Tolerance and Cooperative Power Management
authors: Zhenghong Chen, Anjali Agrawal, Akiyoshi Tanaka, Trenton Elliott, Thomas Farrell, Zane Lewis, Will Farrell, Jim Owens, Daniel S. Truesdell, Benton H. Calhoun
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: To meet growing demands for scalability, modularity, and fault tolerance in e-textiles, this paper proposes THReaD: a Two-dimensional Hierarchical Reconfigurable and Distributed network-on-textiles (kNOTs) architecture using a 2-wire bi-Directional SPI-based (2DSPI) protocol to form yarn-connected meshes of direct-die-attached chiplets distributed at strategic locations on a textile. A System-on-Chip (SoC) chiplet (for computation or sensor interfacing) and a distributed power management unit (PMU) chiplet each contain dedicated 2DSPI blocks, enabling modular arrangements of both chiplet types for flexible and scalable 2D networks. The architecture supports hierarchical dimension-ordered routing and integrated fault tolerance mechanisms, achieving an effective bit rate of up to 22.8 Mbps per node, and PMU chiplets cooperate to scale 4.6× load current and 4.2× power reduction.

title: Imbalanced Fault Diagnosis of Rolling Bearings Fusing WGAN-GP and Self-Attention Mechanism
authors: Ziqiang Yang, Yan Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address the challenge of fault diagnosis under extremely limited data, this paper proposes an integrated diagnostic framework that fuses Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Network with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP), selfattention mechanism, and One-Dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN). By leveraging WGAN-GP, the proposed method effectively resolves the issues of training instability and mode collapse inherent in traditional GANs. Simultaneously, the introduction of the self-attention mechanism enhances the network's capability to capture the global long-range correlations of fault impact features, thereby generating high-quality and diversified pseudo-fault signals. Subsequently, the generated pseudo-samples are mixed with real samples to construct a category-balanced dataset, which is then fed into an improved 1DCNN for feature extraction and classification. Experimental results based on the Case Western Reserve University (CWRU) bearing dataset demonstrate that the model achieves a diagnostic accuracy of nearly <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 \%}$</tex> across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex> different bearing health states. Further validation through confusion matrix and t-SNE visualization analysis confirms that the framework exhibits excellent separability of extracted high-dimensional features when processing multi-class and highly similar complex bearing fault signals, manifesting superior robustness and anti-interference capabilities.

title: Verification of the Mold Material Model Through Simulation Studies at the Product Level
authors: Akshay Vivek Panchwagh, Przemyslaw Jakub Gromala, Sven Rzepka
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: To ensure trustworthiness in simulation results, establishing the credibility of underlying models through systematic verification and validation is essential. Verification addresses the question, "Is the model built right?", and one approach is to assess the sensitivity of material models on simulation outcomes. In our previous work, single-element and bi-material strip finite element (FE) simulations were used to evaluate epoxy mold compound (EMC) formulations—temperature-independent and temperature-dependent linear elastic, linear viscoelastic, and nonlinear viscoelastic. That study revealed that the nonlinear viscoelastic model failed to capture temperature-dependent relaxation behavior, exhibiting only strain-based relaxation, contrary to expected material response. Building on this foundation, the present work applies the previously verified models to the mold compound of a complex power module to investigate warpage under a prescribed thermal profile. This approach demonstrates how material model sensitivity can be systematically assessed in realistic geometries, providing an illustrative example of model-level code verification. The methodology is largely product-independent and can be adapted to verify material model implementations across a wide range of designs and material systems, supporting the development of credible simulation methodologies.

title: Synergistic Component Engineering and Surface Modification to Regulate the Crystallization Kinetics and Photovoltaic Performance of Perovskite Materials
authors: Guihong Chen, Min Zhou, Dingze Lu, Qiuping Wang
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: During perovskite crystallization, the formation of grain boundaries and lead iodide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{P b I}_{2})$</tex> defects induce severe nonradiative recombination, which limits the photovoltaic performance of solar cells. To address this, we modulated the nucleation and growth kinetics by incorporating varying concentrations of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P b}(\mathbf{S C N})_{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> into the precursor. Subsequently, a chlorobenzene/dimethyl sulfoxide (CB20) solution was employed for surface chemical polishing to dissolve residual <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{PbI}_{2}$</tex> and small grains from the as formed films. Morphological and optical characterization confirmed that this synergistic optimization strategy not only effectively removed surface <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P b I}_{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> impurities and promoted local recrystallization but also significantly enhanced grain growth, increasing the average grain size from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.674 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.650 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>. Consequently, the optical absorption capacity of the films was substantially improved. Benefiting from these enhancements, the optimized device exhibited a marked increase in power conversion efficiency (PCE) from 17.32% (standard process) to 21.01%, and achieved a stabilized power output of 19.36% during maximum power point tracking. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of combining composition engineering with interface modification in improving film quality, reducing defects, and boosting both the efficiency and operational stability of perovskite solar cells.

title: Lab-on-Chip Acoustic Detection Platform: Design, Fabrication and Performance Evaluation
authors: Makrem Ben Abdelkader, Hatem Dahmani, Reda Aitayad, Samuel Dupont, Chérif Dridi, Julien Carlier
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: The growing demand for compact and high- sensitivity analytical platforms has accelerated the integration of micro-electro-mechanical systems (MEMS) with acoustic and microfluidic technologies. However, conventional sensors still face challenges in terms of miniaturization, fabrication cost, and sensitivity, particularly for biomedical and environmental applications. In this work, we present the design, simulation, fabrication, and experimental validation of a miniaturized MEMS based bulk acoustic wave (BAW) sensor integrated into a Polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) microfluidic network for real-time detection and concentration monitoring of chemical pollutants in liquid environments. The device was fabricated using thin-film deposition of Ti, Pt and ZnO layers followed by photolithography and lift-off processes. To improve robustness and dimensional accuracy, aluminum molds designed via SolidWorks and machined by CNC were employed for PDMS microchannel fabrication. The bonded microsystem was electrically characterized using a vector network analyzer to evaluate its resonance behavior and reflection coefficient (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>). Experimental validation was conducted by monitoring ethanol and electrodeionized water (EDI) within the microfluidic platform. The sensor showed a stable, reproducible, and concentration-dependent acoustic response, proving high sensitivity and reliability. The integration of microchannels maintains the analyte in the liquid phase during measurement, reducing evaporation losses commonly seen in droplet-based methods and thereby improving the accuracy of volatile compound analysis.

title: A Data-Driven Clustering Analysis of Multi-Energy Load Profiles for Typical Scenario Extraction in Renewable Energy Systems
authors: Yihan Wang, Mulin Qiao
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: To address operating pattern recognition in wind-solar-load multi-energy systems, this study proposes a data-driven daily typical scenario clustering method. Using year-round hourly multi-dimensional data (meteorology, electrical, cooling, heating loads), anomalous days are first removed via Isolation Forest. Subsequently, KMeans clustering partitions normal days into three typical modes. Results show optimal <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K=3$</tex>. With RobustScaler and PCA (95% variance, 5 dimensions), Euclidean KMeans achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{S C} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 8 4 3}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{D B I} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 6 9 9}$</tex>. Bootstrap stability yields an average consistency index of 0.883 (80.6% highly stable). A Random Forest model using historical labels achieves 92.2% nextday cluster prediction accuracy, confirming strong temporal regularity. ANOVA identifies cooling load, temperature, and radiation as key distinguishing features. Consequently, days are categorized as Summer Peak, Winter Valley, and Transitional Season types. This study provides an effective tool for multi-energy system operational analysis and short-term pattern prediction.

title: Indoor Temperature Prediction Model Based on SVMD-CNN- Informer
authors: Ziyang Han, Fengyu Yang
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: Accurate indoor temperature prediction can effectively optimize the control strategies of heating systems, enhance users' thermal comfort, and achieve the goals of energy conservation and carbon reduction. To further improve the accuracy of indoor temperature prediction, this paper proposes a hybrid time-series prediction model based on SVMD-CNN-Informer and applies it to indoor temperature prediction for central heating in northern China in winter. The model first uses SVMD to adaptively decompose the time-series data of indoor temperature and related influencing factors, effectively separating noise components and multi-scale fluctuation features. It then employs a convolutional neural network to accurately extract local abrupt features such as sudden changes in outdoor temperature and short-term strong winds. Combined with the ProbSparse self-attention mechanism of the Informer model, it captures long-period temperature variation patterns, and realizes the deep fusion of bimodal features through an adaptive weight assignment mechanism. To verify the model performance, relevant experiments were conducted. The results show that the MAE, MSE, RMSE, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of the proposed model reach 0.37°, 0.03°C, 0.59° C, and 0.962, respectively. Compared with the traditional LSTM, CNN-LSTM, and original Informer models, the prediction accuracy is improved by 10.3%-35.7%, This study provides a basis for the refined regulation of central heating systems, and can serve as an engineering application reference for reducing heating energy consumption, cutting carbon emissions, and ensuring winter livelihood heating.

title: Research on Control Method of Ship Integrated Power System Based on Load Current Feedforward
authors: Yihua Wang, Haoyu Li, Baoni Sun, Yu Sun, Shuailin Lv, Xinyi Li
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of DC bus voltage fluctuation caused by high-power load switching in ship integrated power system, this paper proposes a source-storage coordinated control strategy based on load current feedforward. Initially, the largesignal stability analysis of the microgrid is carried out, and the stability boundary of the system is obtained to design the system parameters. Then, based on the load current feedforward method, the load current is decomposed into different frequency components according to the characteristics of the generator and the energy storage, so that the power generation system and the energy storage system can cooperate to mitigate load disturbances and suppress the fluctuation of the bus voltage. Finally, a detailed simulation model of ship microgrid is built. Through simulation verification, this method can improve the dynamic response speed of the control loop, enhance the load-carrying capability of the system, and reduce the DC bus voltage fluctuation from 7.8 % to less than 0.5 % when the load power changes suddenly.

title: Rapid and Accurate Estimation of Acoustic Coupling in MUT Devices Using finite Element Modeling
authors: Houri Samer, Haouari Rachid, A. Weeker Bart P., Veronique Rochus
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: This work investigates the finite element simulation of mechanical and acoustic coupling between discrete Micromachined Ultrasonic Transducers using COMSOL MultiPhysics. The simulations produce the coupling parameters which can then be used in reduced order models of MUT arrays. The work provides useful parameters for PMUT and CMUT array modeling.

title: IEEE Annals of the History of Computing
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Security & Privacy

title: Research on Multi-Sensor Array Detection Methods for Verifying GC-IMS Analysis of Neuraminidase-Metabolized Gases
authors: Ziming Chen, Jinzhou Liu, Haiyang Lu, Yunbo Shi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: To address issues in influenza virus neuraminidase detection, such as lengthy testing cycles, high equipment costs, and reduced accuracy due to sensor cross-sensitivity, this paper proposes a non-invasive detection method based on a gas sensor array and the GoogLeNet convolutional neural network. First, GC-IMS analysis identifies signature gases released by influenza viruses. Subsequently, a variety of multiple gas sensors is constructed and tested using experimental gas samples with varying compositions, collecting corresponding data. Time-series data from the sensors is converted into two-dimensional Gramian angular summation field (GASF) images to build training and testing datasets. Finally, the GoogLeNet convolutional neural network is employed to achieve qualitative identification of mixed gases. Experimental results demonstrate that this model meets the required accuracy, recall, and precision for mixed gases, effectively fulfilling the identification needs for influenza viruses.

title: ASIM 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)

title: EWA-DA: An EWA-Learning-Based Double Auction Mechanism for Multi-Agent Task Allocation
authors: Shiguan Li, Yuhao Cai, Zilong Wang, Wenqi He, Jirong Lin
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) are increasingly deployed in complex scenarios like the Internet of Things and cloud computing, where efficient task allocation remains challenging. Centralized allocation methods face computational bottlenecks and vulnerability to single-point failures. This paper models multi-agent task allocation as a double auction game, with agents acting as buyers or sellers of task resources. We present the Experience-Weighted Attraction based Double Auction (EWA-DA) algorithm, which combines reinforcement learning with belief learning. Agents adjust their bidding strategies using both their historical rewards and predictions of other agents' behaviors. Our simulation experiments show that EWA-DA converges rapidly to Nash Equilibrium and achieves higher system utility than baseline methods across different conditions. The proposed mechanism offers a decentralized approach to resource allocation in MAS.

title: Investigation of Dynamic Testing Methods for Composite Thermoelectric Devices under Multi-Condition Loading
authors: Zhiling Li, Gao Wang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: To improve dynamic thermal measurement accuracy on aero-engine hot-section components, integrated heat flux/temperature thin-film sensors (TFHFs) were fabricated using micro-nano technology. This study simulated and compared the time constant calibration of the TFHFs’ temperature component under step laser heating and step furnace heating, demonstrating the necessity of power feedback control in laser-based dynamic calibration. Factors influencing the dynamic response of the heat flux component under both radiation and convection were also analyzed. A laser-based test platform with constant-temperature feedback control was developed to validate the simulations. Tests revealed that overshoot in the heat flux response originates from non-ideal longitudinal heat transfer, while the feedback control reduced the temperature time constant by at least half, overcoming the challenge of inaccurate calibration due to non-steady conditions. High-speed wind tunnel tests under convective conditions confirmed that the dynamic response of TFHFs is predominantly influenced by flow velocity. The study enables accurate dynamic measurement of composite thermal parameters under varied thermal boundaries.

title: Research on Multi-target Detection Method of Transmission Line Based on YOLOv5
authors: Yanan Wang, Shucheng Zeng, Hua Yu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Transmission line inspection is an important link to ensure the safe operation of the power grid, but the traditional manual inspection method has the problems of low efficiency and high risk. In this paper, a multi-target detection method for transmission lines based on YOLOv5 algorithm is proposed to automatically identify key targets such as insulators, insulator defects, bird's nest and grading rings. Through training and testing on a data set containing 6000 images, the experimental results show that the YOLOv5 algorithm performs well in the multi-target detection task of transmission lines, with an average accuracy of 89. 7% and a detection speed of 85 frames per second, which can effectively meet the requirements of intelligent inspection of transmission lines for detection accuracy and real-time. This method provides a feasible automation solution for UAV power transmission line inspection.

title: A Plug-And-Play Deep Unrolling Method for Brain Source Imaging
authors: Ruijian Zheng, Jun Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Electroencephalography source imaging (ESI) is a highly ill-posed inverse problem in neuroimaging, where conventional methods rely on predefined regularization priors and require careful manual parameter tuning, while recent datadriven neural networks, despite their ability to learn parameters automatically, often suffer from limited interpretability and poor generalization. To integrate the advantages of model-based optimization and data-driven learning, this paper proposes a plug-and-play deep unrolling method for EEG source imaging that incorporates structured spatial sparsity and a learned temporal denoising prior. Specifically, a graph-based spatial difference operator with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\ell_{2,1}$</tex> mixed norm is employed to preserve spatial continuity and boundaries of cortical activations, while complex temporal dynamics and noise characteristics are captured by introducing a plug-and-play temporal denoiser as an implicit prior within an alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) formulation. By unfolding the iterative ADMM process into a trainable network, the proposed method enables end-toend parameter learning while retaining physical interpretability. Experimental results on simulated EEG data demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Decentralized Control Techniques for Grid-Forming Inverters Using Finite Control Set Model Predictive Control
authors: Mohamed Islam Grairia, Riad Toufouti, Abderezak Lashab, Markala Karthik, Nabil Mohammed
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: The rapid adoption of grid-forming (GFM)-based inverters in standalone AC microgrids has led to increased demand for decentralized control strategies that operate without relying on inter-inverter communication. A key challenge in this context is to guarantee both voltage and frequency stability while simultaneously achieving fair and equitable power distribution among several parallel units. Traditional approaches—such as droop method, virtual synchronous generator (VSG), and virtual oscillator control (VOC)—have been widely applied, yet they often suffer from limitations including sluggish dynamic performance and intricate control design, especially when cascaded loops involving proportional–integral–derivative (PID) regulators or proportional–resonant (PR) regulators are employed. To address these issues, the present work conducts a detailed comparative analysis of existing methods and introduces finite control set model predictive control (FCS-MPC) as a viable alternative. Numerical simulations performed in the MATLAB/Simulink platform confirm that FCS-MPC delivers superior performance with respect to voltage and frequency regulation and also effective load sharing. In particular, the VOC-based FCS-MPC scheme exhibits faster transient behavior and higher control precision when compared to droop and VSG strategies.

title: Latency-Bound Online Consistency Verification for Always-on Financial Databases
authors: Raghu Gollapudi
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Existing consistency verification mechanisms in financial databases rely on offline reconciliation, blocking checks, or static invariants that fail to scale under continuous transactional workloads. Such approaches introduce latency, disrupt availability, or require full-database scans that are incompatible with always-on financial systems. This paper presents a latency-bound online consistency verification framework that integrates transaction-aware feature encoding, streaming anomaly detection ensembles, and costconstrained probabilistic probing to detect semantic and transactional inconsistencies without interrupting transaction processing. The proposed approach treats consistency verification as a live control problem, balancing detection accuracy, verification overhead, and end-to-end latency under dynamic workload conditions. Experimental evaluation using streaming financial transaction datasets demonstrates that the framework achieves higher detection accuracy with reduced false positives and bounded verification overhead compared to offline and rule-based baselines. The results indicate that operational consistency assurance can be achieved continuously in live financial databases while preserving throughput, auditability, and system availability.

title: Post-COVID Cardiovascular Risk Stratification Using Independent Machine Learning and Deep Learning Pipelines
authors: M. Babu, Sandhiya C, Varsha P
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Post-COVID clinical follow-up studies have reported a significant rise in long-term cardiovascular complications among recovered patients, including myocarditis, arrhythmias, and thromboembolic events. Early identification of individuals at elevated cardiovascular risk is therefore essential for effective clinical intervention. This paper presents a modular artificial intelligence framework for post-COVID cardiovascular risk stratification using independent machine learning and deep learning pipelines. A hybrid ensemble combining Decision Tree and XGBoost models is employed to analyze structured clinical features, while a convolutional neural network independently evaluates ECG signal images. Unlike tightly coupled multimodal systems, the proposed design enables risk prediction even when only one type of patient data is available. Experimental evaluation on public ECG datasets and clinically aligned features demonstrates that the hybrid machine learning model achieves superior predictive performance compared to individual classifiers, while the CNN effectively captures ECG-based risk patterns. The proposed framework improves flexibility, scalability, and real-world applicability in post-COVID cardiovascular risk assessment.

title: A Hybrid Multi-Objective Optimization Framework for Energy-Aware UAV Swarm Coordination in Dynamic Environments
authors: Gurwinder Singh, Gurpreet Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) swarms operating in dynamic environments must simultaneously balance the propulsion-energy, communication-robustness and the collision avoidance. The existing coordination-strategies optimize these objectives independently which resulting in the suboptimal trade offs under the real-time constraints. Thereby, this paper formulates UAV swarm-coordination as a constrained multi-objective optimization problem that jointly minimizes the energy expenditure, connectivity-degradation as well and the safety-violations. In this proposed-framework, the integrating of global-Pareto exploration via the NSGA-II with a perturbationbased local refinement have been explored. The experimental-evaluation is conducted on a real-world drone swarmcoordination dataset using a representative snapshot of 20 UAVs. Therein, while compared with a random-search baseline, the proposed-framework reduces the median connectivity-penalty from 63.37 to 49.27 while maintaining near-zero collision violations. The convergence analysis using the hypervolume-indicator further demonstrates the improved Pareto-dominance and stability. Thus, the obtained results confirm that the proposed hybrid framework effectively captures the energy-connectivity-safety trade-offs and provides a computationally-tractable solution for the dynamic UAV swarm coordination.

title: Performance Evaluation of BCH, LDPC, and Turbo Codes in LTE-Based MIMO-OFDM Systems Using 64-QAM
authors: Hasan M. Redha, Samir Jasim Mohammed
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: This paper evaluates the BER performance of an LTE-based MIMO-OFDM system using QAM modulation under different channel coding and antenna configurations. The performance of BCH, LDPC, and Turbo coding schemes was investigated in comparison with an uncoded baseline. The influence of spatial diversity on the reliability of the system is evaluated with two MIMO configurations, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex>. The system is developed using MATLAB/Simulink and orthogonal space-time block coding (OSTBC) for spatial processing. For the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> configuration, the Alamouti scheme is applied, and for the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex> configuration, the rate 1/2 OSTBC is used. Simulations are performed in a Rayleigh fading channel with additive white Gaussian noise for different SNR values. The results from the simulations show that the addition of channel coding improves the performance of the system with respect to the bit error rate (BER) and is better than the system in the uncoded state. Utilization of Turbo coding achieved the best results among the different channel codes analyzed, and LDPC and BCH coding methods followed. Furthermore, the configuration of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \times 4$</tex> MIMO in the spatial diversity is better than the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> configuration. Combining MIMO-OSTBC with channel coding improves the reliability of wireless communication systems based on LTE technology, confirming the results from the simulations.

title: Performance-Driven Hybrid Scheduling Mechanism Using Nature-Inspired Algorithms for Mobile Edge Cloud Resource Management
authors: Santosh Gupta, Deepika Saxena, Arif. M. Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) has emerged as a promising paradigm to support latency-sensitive and computation-intensive mobile applications by enabling task execution at the network edge. However, efficient resource management in MEC-cloud environments remains a challenging problem due to heterogeneous resources, dynamic workloads, and user mobility. This paper proposes a performance-driven hybrid scheduling mechanism based on nature-inspired metaheuristic algorithms for effective resource management in mobile edge cloud environments. The proposed approach integrates complementary exploration and exploitation capabilities of multiple nature-inspired algorithms to jointly address task offloading and resource allocation. A multi-objective fitness function is formulated to optimize key performance metrics, including execution latency, makespan, resource utilization, and load balancing. The hybrid scheduler dynamically adapts to changing workload conditions and avoids premature convergence commonly observed in single-heuristic approaches. Extensive simulation-based experiments are conducted under varying task arrival rates and heterogeneous edge-cloud configurations. The results demonstrate that the proposed hybrid mechanism significantly improves latency reduction, system utilization, and load distribution compared to conventional scheduling strategies and individual metaheuristic-based schedulers. The findings confirm the suitability of hybrid nature-inspired scheduling for scalable and QoS-aware mobile edge cloud resource management.

title: ECOTRACK: An AI-Based Carbon Emission Tracking and Prediction System
authors: Mahesh Ananda Kamthe, Smriti Hundekari, Guruprasad Yewandage, Ankita Sunil Borkar, Sakshi Rane
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: The study provides a data-based framework of campus-level energy and carbon analytics with reference to multi-source consumption data and machine learning-based advanced techniques. The data of hourly electricity and natural gas were examined to describe the operational trends and to predict the carbon emission based on gas. Ensemble learning models such as XGBoost and LightGBM were implemented in comparison with a deep learning Temporal Convolutional Network, and evaluated based on the MAE and RMSE metrics. In order to increase the reliability of the decisions made, the framework applies troubleshooting probability Monte Carlo-based quantification of uncertainty and anomaly in order to capture operational variability and anomalous consumption patterns. It is shown that the ensemble models perform better than the deep temporal models on the structured campuses data and uncertainty bounds estimates the risk-aware emission. The methodology proposed is a blend of precision, elucidation, and uncertainty consciousness, providing a useful decision support system in institutional energy tracking and carbon control.

title: Imbalanced Bearing Fault Diagnosis Using WGAN-GP With Cascaded SIMAM-GAM Attention
authors: Xudong Liu, Chuansong Zhou
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Bearing fault diagnosis plays a vital part in the reliability of rotary equipment; however, class imbalances significantly affect the performance of bearing fault diagnostic models. In this study, an imbalanced fault diagnosis approach that combines a Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Network with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP) and a cascaded SimAM-GAM attention mechanism is proposed. WGAN-GP generates synthetic data of good quality through adversarial training, addressing class imbalances, while the cascaded attention mechanism extracts discriminative information through a series of parameter-free spatial attention and joint channel-spatial attention blocks. Experimental validation of the proposed approach, implemented as a bearing fault diagnostic model, showed that the proposed approach achieved a validation accuracy of 90.4 % under a severe class imbalance ratio of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50: 1$</tex> when validated on the bearing dataset from the CWRU, significantly outperforming traditional oversampling approaches like SMOTE and ADASYN, as well as other bearing fault diagnostic models that did not use an attention mechanism.

title: Lung Cancer Diagnosis: Visualizing Deep Learning Decision Pathways Using Yolov8
authors: S. Berlin Shaheema, I. Edwin Albert, Megiba Jasmine R, Johnprincy J, Jasper J, G. Gifta Jerith
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Automated lung cancer diagnosis remains a major challenge, where accurate and timely identification of malignant nodules is crucial for effective treatment and improved patient outcomes. This work proposes a unified framework integrating feature extraction, feature selection, tumor detection, and classification from lung CT images. YOLOv8 is employed for rapid and precise nodule detection, followed by an attention-enhanced UNet to perform accurate tumor segmentation. A dualdomain attention mechanism captures both spatial and semantic contextual information, while residual learning improves feature refinement. In addition, a dense featureextraction module enhances multiscale representation learning and mitigates vanishing-gradient issues. Experiments conducted on the LUNA16 dataset using Dice score, accuracy, Hausdorff distance, and recall demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: A Hybrid DeBERTa-BiLSTM Framework with Attention for Context-Aware Fake News Detection
authors: Mallepogu Naveen Babu, S Hupesh Naga Ketan, Mohebba Nazz
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: As the number of online news websites has continued to grow exponentially, so too has the volume of deliberately false and misleading information, making the development of automatic fake news detection methods a pressing issue. Transformer-based architectures, such as BERT, RoBERTa, and DeBERTa, have been proven effective for contextualization, yet applying sequential modelling atop them remains an underexplored area. We use a hybrid model in which we combine DeBERTa for context modelling, a BiLSTM for sequential feature modelling, and an attention block to select informative parts of the text. To justify the use of DeBERTa, we use an ablation study that compares the transformer-only architecture for several models of increasing complexity on the Kaggle Fake News dataset, and we conduct cross-dataset validation on the LIAR benchmark to test for generalizability of the model. Experimental results show that the proposed hybrid architectures outperform strong transformer baselines and are stable across datasets. Analysis of attention-weighted intermediate representations shows that they can help the model to identify misleading linguistic patterns. These findings are consistent with the need for contextual and sequential modelling in text-based fake news detection.

title: Intelligent Cluster Maintenance and Hybrid Routing Optimization for High-Mobility MANET Environments
authors: Payal Bansal, Deepika Dubey, Dinesh Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Mobile Ad Hoc Networks (MANETs) are self configuring wireless networks composed of mobile nodes that communicate without fixed infrastructure. Nodes can join or leave the network at any time, making MANETs highly dynamic and adaptable. They are particularly useful in scenarios where conventional network infrastructure is unavailable or damaged, such as military operations, disaster management, and emergency services. However, frequent topology changes and limited node resources, such as battery power, memory, and processing capacity, make routing a significant challenge. This research focuses on improving routing efficiency in MANETs through two approaches. First, an enhanced clustering scheme called IMS-CBRP is proposed by modifying the traditional Cluster-Based Routing Protocol (CBRP). Both IMS-CBRP and CBRP are simulated and compared based on packet delivery ratio, channel utilization, normalized routing load, control overhead, and average end-to-end delay under varying mobility conditions. Results indicate that replacing the cluster maintenance mechanism of CBRP with the IMS approach significantly improves routing performance. Second, a hybrid routing algorithm named Adaptive GPSR with Dynamic Thresholds (AGDT) is introduced. AGDT integrates Greedy Perimeter Stateless Routing (GPSR) with a dynamic threshold mechanism based on distance and direction. The algorithm intelligently switches between greedy forwarding and perimeter routing to handle high node mobility and topology changes effectively. Performance evaluation demonstrates that AGDT outperforms existing routing protocols in terms of packet delivery ratio, throughput, and end-to-end delay.

title: Comparative Analysis of RF, SVM and XGBoost for High-Accuracy Crop Recommendation
authors: E Tharun, K Vinod Kumar Reddy, Mohebbanaaz
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: In the current study, a machine learning-based crop suggestion system is proposed using three powerful algorithms, which are Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM) in addition XGBoost. The system takes into analysis the main agricultural parameters which are Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium, Soil Type, and Soil Moisture to properly determine the best crop to grow between rice and wheat to ensure fairness, these models were trained in addition tested using the similar dataset. As can be seen in the experimental results, Random Forest, SVM and XGBoost had the highest accuracy of 99.30%, 99.16% and 99.50% respectively. The suggested model shows better results than the past machine learning models and proves the prospect of smart data-driven systems in enhancing agricultural decision- making and crop yields.

title: Digital Implementation of Cordic-Based Fitzhugh Nagumo Neuron Models on FPGAs
authors: Trinankur Deb Roy, Vamika Chaturvedi, Babita Jajodia
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a precision-aware digital implementation of the FitzHugh-Nagumo (FHN) neuron model using the COrdinate Rotation DIgital Computer (CORDIC) algorithm on a field-programmable gate array (FPGA) platform. Unlike prior multiplierless CORDIC-based realizations that primarily target logic reduction, the proposed architecture systematically evaluates numerical accuracy across different precision domains using metrics such as mean absolute error (MAE), root mean square error (RMSE), normalized RMSE (nRMSE), and correlation. The model is validated through phase-plane and time-domain analyses, demonstrating close agreement with the original FHN dynamics under varying stimulus conditions. The proposed design achieves controlled accuracy degradation with a negligible increase in hardware resources, enabling predictable numerical behavior and improved model fidelity. Experimental results show that the implementation operates at a maximum clock frequency of 400 MHz while attaining a normalized RMSE of 0.0043, making it a suitable candidate for scalable and reliable neuromorphic hardware acceleration.

title: Joint Service Caching and Resource Allocation in DT-Empowered Cloud-Edge Networks Via MARL With Hierarchical Knowledge Transfer_supp1-3691308.pdf
authors: Zheyi Chen
publish: N/A
abstract: Through bypassing the long-distance transmission of cloud services, Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) reduces response delay and ensures Quality-of-Service (QoS). In resource-constrained MEC, due to the strong coupling between service provisioning and task execution, reasonable service caching and resource allocation face many challenges on 1) coordinating the limited storage resources to improve cache hit rate, 2) allocating limited computing resources under delay constraints, and 3) sample collection and model training in dynamic environments. To address these important challenges, we propose CAMART, a novel service Caching and resource Allocation framework via Multi-Agent Reinforcement learning (MARL) with hierarchical knowledge Transfer in Digital Twin (DT) empowered Cloud-Edge Networks (DTCEN). Specifically, we first construct a new DTCEN to realize the mapping from physical to virtual networks. Next, we decouple the joint optimization of service caching and resource allocation into two sub-problems. For the service caching sub-problem, we design an improved MARL-based method to capture global information via a shared-feature extraction network and optimize caching and offloading decisions via a dual-head feature processing network. For the resource allocation sub-problem, we convert it into 0-1 integer programming and design an improved branch-and-bound-based method to reduce computational complexity while guaranteeing a high-quality solution set. Finally, we develop an original DT-driven hierarchical knowledge transfer mechanism to realize cross-scenario knowledge reuse and convergence acceleration. Using real-world datasets, extensive experiments are conducted to validate the superiority of the proposed CAMART. Compared to the state-of-the-art methods, CAMART achieves higher rewards, task completion rate, and cache hit rate in different scenarios.

title: From Rules to Reasoning: Bridging Rule-based and LLM-based Machine Translation
authors: Jinyi Zhang, Dashuai Deng, Haosen Lou, Yingfeng Luo, Ye Tian, Tong Xiao, Tadahiro Matsumoto
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: In the evolution of machine translation, rule-based machine translation (RBMT) played a foundational role because of its systematic structure and interpretability, yet its knowledge has rarely been reused directly in large language model (LLM)-based machine translation. This paper bridges rule-based and neural paradigms by converting symbolic translation rules from a real-world RBMT system into instruction data for LLM fine-tuning. We construct a pipeline covering rule extraction, component analysis, prompt conversion, and rule-guided training. Using Japanese<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\rightarrow$</tex-math></inline-formula>Chinese rules from the jaw/Chinese system developed by the Ikeda–Matsumoto Laboratory at Gifu University, we fine-tune four LLMs of different scales and evaluate rule-based instruction fine-tuning across Japanese<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\leftrightarrow$</tex-math></inline-formula>Chinese, Chinese<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\leftrightarrow$</tex-math></inline-formula>English, and German<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\rightarrow$</tex-math></inline-formula>English translation. To our knowledge, this is the first systematic attempt to transform an operational RBMT rule base into instruction data for LLM-based machine translation. Experiments show that rule-based fine-tuning substantially improves small and medium-sized models in Japanese<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\rightarrow$</tex-math></inline-formula>Chinese translation, with Llama-3.2-3B gaining about 17 BLEU points and 5 COMET points, while larger models such as Gemma-2-9B obtain smaller BLEU gains but improved output consistency. Additional experiments on reverse, cross-lingual, and two-stage fine-tuning settings confirm that structured rules can provide useful guidance beyond ordinary parallel-data adaptation.
