The analysis indicates that, although stronger LLMs depend less on external rules, rule-based inputs still serve as linguistic scaffolds that improve syntactic alignment, terminology control, and translation reliability. These findings highlight the value of reusing RBMT knowledge for interpretable and controllable LLM-based machine translation.

title: On Information Theoretic Fairness: From Perfect To Bounded Demographic Parity
authors: Amirreza Zamani, Abolfazl Changizi, Mikael Skoglund
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: In this paper, we study the fundamental limits in the design of fair representations under different demographic (statistical) parity constraints through the lens of information theory. We first consider the design problem achieving perfect demographic parity. More specifically, an agent uses some useful data X to solve a task T. Since both X and T are correlated with some sensitive attribute or secret S, the agent designs a representation Y that has no information about the sensitive attribute S, i.e., satisfying perfect demographic parity constraint, implying that I(Y ; S) = 0. We then relax the perfect demographic parity and consider a bounded-parity constraint, implying that I(Y ; S) ≤ ϵ. Under perfect demographic parity, we consider two scenarios. First, we consider a design problem where we want to maximize the information I(Y ; T) that the representation contains about the task, while constraining the level of compression (or encoding rate), that is, ensuring that I(Y ;X) ≤ r. Second, inspired by the Conditional Fairness Bottleneck problem, we consider a design problem where we want to maximize the information I(Y ; T|S) that the representation contains about the task which is not shared by the sensitive attribute, while constraining the amount of irrelevant information, that is, ensuring that I(Y ;X|T, S) ≤ r. Under bounded demographic parity, we design Y that maximizes the mutual information I(Y ; T) about the task while satisfying a bounded compression (or encoding rate) constraint, that is, ensuring that I(Y ;X) ≤ r. Simultaneously, Y satisfies the bounded demographic parity constraint I(Y ; S) ≤ ϵ. To design Y , we use extended versions of the Functional Representation Lemma and the Strong Functional Representation Lemma which are based on randomization techniques and study the tightness of the obtained bounds in special cases. Every design problem studied in this paper can also be interpreted as a code design problem with either perfect privacy or a bounded leakage constraint and a limited rate, considering the sensitive attribute as a secret.

title: Mixture Density Networks Improve Inter-Sensor Consistency of Optical Water Type Classification
authors: Davide Lomeo, Stefan G. H. Simis, Mark A. Warren, David Moffat, Anne D. Jungblut, Emma J. Tebbs
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: In aquatic remote sensing, Optical Water Type (OWT) pre-classification has been used to map suitable algorithms to optically diverse targets and over optical gradients. However, current methods can yield inconsistent type assignments across sensors with different spectral capabilities. Probabilistic neural networks are increasingly used for constituent retrieval in optically complex inland water bodies because they can deliver higher accuracy than empirical or semi-analytical algorithms, while also quantifying estimation uncertainty. However, their interpretability within bio-optical theory remains elusive. To address the limitations brought by these two methodological streams, we train Mixture Density Networks (MDN) to predict OWT membership distributions. OWTs serve as an optical-biogeochemical interpretable scaffold, alongside associated classification uncertainty, while the classification misalignment between Sentinel-3 Ocean and Land Colour Instrument (OLCI) and Sentinel-2 Multispectral Instrument (MSI) is reduced. Using > 29,000 co-located OLCI and MSI observations from 59 lakes, MDN ensembles trained to reproduce OLCI-derived OWT membership scores achieved 99% overall accuracy for OLCI and improved MSI cross-sensor OWT agreement from 58% to 73%, compared to using spectral angle as OWT distance metric. The MDNs enabled identification of aleatoric (upstream bias) and epistemic (lack of representative data) components of the uncertainty envelope, with post-hoc uncertainty recalibration used to adjust uncertainty magnitudes to reliable confidence intervals, reducing miscalibration by 93% for OLCI and 80% for MSI, for both 68% and 95% intervals. Application of trained MDN ensembles across optically diverse systems confirmed that they learned generalisable optical relationships, improving OWT classification agreement across sensors, and providing diagnostics for bias identification. This approach preserves the interpretability of OWTs while enabling uncertainty aware, cross-mission processing, identifying likely sources of classification uncertainty, particularly useful for less capable sensors, which can guide appropriate post-processing and suitable algorithm selection and blending.

title: RadioYNet: Efficient 3D Path Loss Map Prediction with Height-Aware Feature Fusion
authors: Chengkai Liu, Guozhen Xia, Zeyu Cui, Shiyang Jiang, Xinyu Liao, Yuqiang Zhang, Jia Shi, Chen Zhou, Zan Li
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: Path loss (PL) map prediction is an important task for wireless propagation modeling and network planning. Neural network (NN)-based approaches have been widely studied due to their higher efficiency than ray tracing (RT). However, most existing methods focus on two-dimensional (2D) PL maps. To address the three-dimensional (3D) PL map prediction problem, this letter proposes a multi-altitude PL map prediction framework based on an NN architecture. The proposed model adopts a dual-encoder design to extract heterogeneous features from the environment and the transmitter (Tx) location, and uses a height-aware feature fusion module to embed altitude conditioning into the fused features, enabling the network to differentiate PL patterns across altitude planes. Experimental results show that the proposed model improves prediction accuracy over representative baselines, and substantially reduces inference time compared with RT.

title: IMRA 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing, Robotics and Automation (IMRA)

title: Unified Energy Consumption Modeling for Heterogeneous UAVs: An Adaptative Incremental Learning-Based Framework
authors: Liangqi Wang, Zhiwei Wei, Bing Li, Yu Fu, Rongqing Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Accurate energy consumption modeling is a prerequisite for reliable mission scheduling and network coverage in the low-altitude economy. However, the proliferation of heterogeneous Uncrewed Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) introduces significant challenges: existing models are typically platform-specific, failing to generalize across diverse aerodynamic structures, while retraining for every new UAV type is computationally prohibitive. To address these issues, we propose UECM-IL, an incremental learning-based framework that establishes a unified energy consumption model. First, to explicitly capture both the sequential evolution of flight states and the intrinsic dependencies among features, we develop a Knowledge Graph guided Temporal-Graph Network (KG-TGN). This dual-channel architecture utilizes a Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) to capture sequential flight dynamics and a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) guided by a domain-knowledge graph to model inter-feature correlations. Second, to enable cross-domain generalization without catastrophic forgetting, we introduce an incremental learning strategy termed Overfitting-aWare learning rate scheduling and Experience Replay (OWER). This mechanism allows the model to sequentially adapt to new aerial platforms while retaining historical domain knowledge. Finally, we construct the Multi-scenario Heterogeneous UAV Energy Consumption Dataset (MH-UECD), collected via the AirFogSim platform across five distinct aerodynamic configurations. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the unified model achieves an average Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 2.0% across heterogeneous domains, offering a scalable solution for energy-aware low-altitude management.

title: A Deterministic Boolean-Logic Switching Control for Grid-Connected Multi-Level PV Microinverters
authors: Abhishek Awasthi, Majid Pahlevani, Praveen Jain
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Grid-connected flying capacitor (FC)-based multi-level converter is investigated as the DC/AC stage in single-phase PV microinverter applications. To achieve reliable performance in grid-connected applications, the control system must effectively achieve inverter current control and flying capacitor voltage balancing in higher-level topologies. Previous research has focused on complex and resource-intensive FC voltage balancing control methods that are not easily scalable to higher-level converter designs. Some of these methods typically employ cost function-based control algorithms, further complicating higher level implementation. This study introduces a deterministic Boolean-logic switching control strategy by employing structured logical decision rules to generate real-time switching vector sequences without requiring an online cost-function solution. The proposed system generates real-time switching vector fields that (a) restrict transitions to adjacent switching vectors to reduce switching losses, (b) avoid consecutive charging/discharging FC states to reduce stress, and (c) prioritize switching vector fields with a minimal number of FCs in series for all voltage levels, thereby minimizing equivalent series resistance (ESR) losses. The proposed switching control provides scalable and deterministic voltage balancing behaviour in DC-AC FC multilevel systems compared with conventional control strategies. Experimental results under steady-state and transient conditions validate simulation results, exhibiting a peak efficiency of 98.7% under nominal AC grid voltage operation.

title: Portable Electrochemical Platform for Point-of-Care Bilirubin Sensing for Liver Diagnostics_supp2-3693203.mp4
authors: Sanket Goel
publish: N/A
abstract: Objective: Elevated bilirubin serves as a subtle yet early indicator of liver dysfunction, highlighting the need for a rapid, accessible, and reliable point-of-care solution. To the best of our knowledge, this study presents the first portable electrochemical platform for point-of-care bilirubin sensing for liver diagnostics. The portable device features onboard power management and a customized analog front end with precise signal acquisition, conditioning, and filtering to support Cyclic Voltammetry (CV), Differential Potential Voltammetry (DPV), and amperometric techniques for the electrochemical detection of any analyte. Herein, the optimised gold nanoparticles/SPCE enables effective bilirubin adsorption and subsequent oxidation at ∼0.35 V. An analyte-selective nafion–chitosan membrane suppressed interferences, achieving a 0.3 μM detection limit at pH 7.4. The on-device detection was evaluated by utilising the standard electrochemical setup, followed by high selectivity, repeatability, and reproducibility. Real-sample testing revealed ∼9.5% deviation from clinical values, confirming strong agreement with conventional methods. The on-device bilirubin detection through the amperometry technique delivers results both on-device and via a wireless user interface. In the future, the developed device can potentially detect other bioanalytes with minor modifications in terms of suitable chemistry.

title: ARe you watching the road? Comparing Gaze Behaviour and Hazard Prediction between Heads-Up and Heads-Down NDRT Presentation in Augmented Reality
authors: Thomas Goodge, Frank Pollick, Stephen Brewster
publish: ACM Transactions on Applied Perception
abstract: None

title: Battery Health Prognosis Using Physics-Informed Neural Network with Quantum Feature Mapping
authors: Muhammad Imran Hossain, Md Fazley Rafy, Sarika Khushalni Solanki, Anurag K. Srivastava
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: Accurate battery health prognosis using State of Health (SOH) estimation is essential for the reliability of multiscale battery energy storage, yet existing methods are limited in generalizability across diverse battery chemistries and operating conditions. The inability of standard neural networks to capture the complex, high-dimensional physics of battery degradation is a major contributor to these limitations. To address this, a physics-informed neural network with the Quantum Feature Mapping(QFM) technique (QPINN) is proposed. QPINN projects raw battery sensor data into a high-dimensional Hilbert space, creating a highly expressive feature set that effectively captures subtle, non-linear degradation patterns using Nyström method. These quantum-enhanced features are then processed by a physics-informed network that enforces physical constraints. The proposed method achieves an average SOH estimation accuracy of 99.46% across different datasets, substantially outperforming state-of-the-art baselines, with reductions in MAPE and RMSE of up to 65% and 62%, respectively. This method was validated on a large-scale, multi-chemistry dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 1 0, 7 0 5}$</tex> samples from 387 cells, and further showed notable adaptability in cross-validation settings, successfully transferring from one chemistry to another without relying on target-domain SOH labels.

title: Resource Adequacy in United States Power Markets During the Digital Era: A Comparative Review of Principles and Current Practices
authors: Etornam Komla Ahorsu, Mahdi Mehrtash, Muhammad R. Hammad, Dillon Seward, Abdelwahab D. Bebars, Poria Fajri, Hanif Livani
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: This paper provides a comparative review of resource adequacy mechanisms across major U.S. electricity market operators as they address contemporary challenges including data center demand growth, increased renewable integration, and climatic variability. By examining standards, modeling tools, and reserve margin requirements, this work elucidates key differences among Electric Reliability Council of Texas (ERCOT), Midcontinent Independent System Operator (MISO), New York Independent System Operator (NYISO), ISO New England (ISO-NE), Pennsylvania-New Jersey-Maryland (PJM), Southwest Power Pool (SPP), and California Independent System Operator (CAISO). The review synthesizes recent market operator, regulatory, and reliability assessment documents to highlight the evolving criteria and practices adopted by market operators. The results offer a comprehensive basis for understanding how varying regional approaches to resource adequacy collectively shape the reliability landscape of the U.S. electric grid.

title: Analysis and Mitigation of Power System Harmonics in Industrial Vfd Applications Using Passive Filter Configurations
authors: Md Shahin Munsi, Agboola B. Alao, Bharath Kumar Dasari, Yared Brhane Tadese, Bibek Dhakal, Manohar Chamana, Olatunji M. Adeyanju, Stephen Bayne
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: Harmonic distortion caused by non-linear industrial loads such as variable frequency drives (VFDs) poses major challenges to power quality issue and equipment reliability. This paper investigates harmonic mitigation techniques for an industrial distribution network comprising two transformers operating in parallel and supplying VFD driven loads. A simulation model was developed in MATLAB-Simulink to evaluate the effectiveness of passive filter configurations under realistic load and transformer conditions. The study analyzes critical filter design parameters, tuning frequency, and quality factor, and examines optimal placement of filters. Comparative results between filtered and unfiltered cases demonstrate substantial reductions in total harmonic distortion (THD) of current and voltage waveforms, confirming compliance with IEEE 519-2022 limits. The findings highlight that properly designed passive filters remain a cost-effective and reliable solution for mitigating harmonics in industrial facilities with VFDs, while emphasizing practical challenges such as resonance, component losses, and optimal placement.

title: Short-Circuit Blackbox Modeling of Inverter-Based Resources: Development, Validation and Key Insights
authors: Dol Raj Kunwar, Mohammad Zadeh, Yuhao Zhou
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: The fault characteristics of Inverter-Based Resources (IBRs) are highly dependent on their internal control strategies, which can vary among manufacturers. As a result, the existing generic Short-Circuit (SC) models often struggle to accurately represent the fault responses of IBRs from different vendors. To address this limitation, efforts are underway between IBR and SC program vendors towards the development of a proprietary Blackbox DLL (Dynamic Linked Library) model for steady-state SC analysis. This paper represents the first attempt to implement the SC Blackbox DLL concept. In this paper, Blackbox SC models are developed for five different IBR-vendors by studying the fault responses of their Electromagnetic Transient (EMT) models. The developed models were implemented in a SC software, and the accuracy of the developed model's response in SC software tool was validated by comparing the SC simulation results against EMT benchmarks. Throughout this study, valuable insights into the key attributes of the fault characteristics of IBRs were gained and shared in this paper aiming to contribute to a deeper understanding of fault behavior of IBRs. Moreover, the paper also briefly introduces a new Norton's/Thevenin's equivalent circuit-based current source model of an IBR that demonstrates significantly better convergence tendency as compared to the conventional current source model.

title: An In-Loop Filter PLL with Fixed-Frequency Enhanced ROGI Prefilter for Three-Phase Grid-Connected Converters
authors: Sapram Giridhar, Chandrasekaran S, Ashwani Kumar, Ishan Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: Accurate and fast estimation of grid voltage attributes namely phase, amplitude, and frequency is very challenging particularly under non-ideal and dynamically varying grid conditions. This paper presents an improved fast estimation technique based on an fixed-frequency enhanced reduced-order generalized integrator (FF-EROGI) as pre-filter for harmonic rejection and orthogonal signal generation. Moving average filter with phase-lead compensation incorporated within the phaselocked loop (PLL) mitigates the effects of grid disturbances. However, fixed-frequency structures introduce estimation errors under off-nominal frequency conditions, necessitating an effective correction mechanism with a fast settling time. To address this limitation, the proposed FF-EROGI technique implements a linear regression curve-fitting method to correct the amplitude and phase errors. The proposed approach significantly improves the dynamic response and robustness of the estimator under adverse grid conditions. Experimental validation is carried out using a dSPACE DS1104 real-time controller board interfaced with MATLAB/Simulink demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed technique.

title: Dynamic Operating Envelope Calculation for Grid Integration of Large Controllable Digital Loads
authors: Masoud Barati
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: This paper introduces a dynamic operating envelope (DOE) framework for integrating large dynamic digital loads (LDDLs), such as hyperscale data centers, into transmission networks. We develop a tractable LDDL model capturing fast IT dynamics, thermal cooling inertia, on-site BESS, and inverter constraints. The DOE is computed via a 5 -minute-resolution, chance-constrained multi-period AC optimal power flow that maximizes envelope width while enforcing voltage-dependent Volt-Watt caps, network security, BESS energy limits, and explicit voltage margins under uncertainty. Case studies on the IEEE 68-bus system demonstrates trade-offs between voltage security and renewable utilization, with voltage standard deviation reduced by 29.1 %, outperforming baselines in reliability and flexibility.

title: Self-Healing of Microgrids or Distribution Systems Using Radio Pulse Injected on a Neutral Wire
authors: Nathaniel Wallace, Olga Lavrova, Michael Ropp
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: Self-healing power systems offer significant resilience benefits. Most existing self-healing concepts depend on high-speed communications for data sharing, which can be cost-prohibitive or unreliable during emergency events. Greater benefit could be achieved if the system could autonomously identify the relative locations of the line relays. Using lower-bandwidth and less expensive communication methods. This paper presents and analyzes an approach using a radio-frequency pulse transmitted over the neutral line of a microgrid to enable line relays to automatically determine their electrical relationships and enhance self-healing capability.

title: Impact of Geographically Dispersed Solar Farms on System Reliability
authors: Anh Nguyen, Thi Ha Nguyen
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: Despite the environmental and economic benefits, high penetrations of solar photovoltaic generation with intermittent output and low inertia introduce more fluctuations into modern power grids. Due to the nondispatchable characteristic, developing appropriate methods to control the integration of photovoltaic energy is important to ensure system reliability. This paper proposes an advanced method for aggregating solar power plants across diverse geographic locations and taking advantage of the difference in time zones to reduce net variability and ramping magnitudes, thereby increasing system reliability while smoothing out and advancing PV integration. Using irradiance data from the National Solar Radiation Database, the proposed method proves that spatial and temporal diversity among solar farms reduces short-term variability, suppresses extreme ramps, and increases the effective capacity of solar farms. The IEEERTS 79 system and Monte Carlo simulation are used to assess the reliability of the power grid to prove the efficacy of the method.

title: Secure Data Aggregation for Oilfield Transport and Inspection Vehicles Using Onion-Routed Federated Learning
authors: Ramya Ganesh, Shruti Rana, Prushti Patel, Chinmay Trivedi, Jemi Kavar, Robin Bavarva, Lakshin Pathak, Devamm Patel
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: The safety and communication abilities of vehicular ad-hoc networks (VANETs) are highly dynamic and thus vulnerable to distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks, which exploit the network's dynamic topology. This paper proposes an onion-routed federated learning framework for privacy-preserving, real-time DDoS detection in VANETs deployed in the oil and gas industry. In such industrial environments, fleets of vehicles transport hazardous materials and equipment across remote and high-risk sites, where secure and reliable communication is vital for operational safety. A TinyML-based model is trained locally on each vehicle's network traffic, and the model updates are securely encrypted and anonymized using onion routing before aggregation, ensuring that raw data never leaves the node. A 5G-enabled communication layer guarantees low-latency and dependable update transfer among vehicles, roadside units, and the central aggregator. Intensive testing on the LR-HR DDoS 2024 data indicates that the suggested framework can attain a global model precision of 0.9988 and a global loss of 0.043, which guarantees a high level of accuracy and efficiency in detecting the low-rate and high-rate DDoS attacks. The findings demonstrate that the framework can achieve the privacy and security, and real-time performance balance in highly dynamic oil and gas VANET environments.

title: An Emotion - Driven System Offering Personalized Activities and Lifestyle Recommendations
authors: K. Priya, K. Omkar, Shaik Sulthan, M. Kishore
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Emotional context plays a significant role in shaping individual lifestyle preferences, decision making, and daily well being. This presents a multimodal Emotion Based Lifestyle Recommendation System designed to provide adaptive suggestions through facial expressions, speech input, and text interaction. The proposed framework integrates real time facial emotion analysis using DeepFace with audio and text based emotion interpretation to capture user mood across multiple modalities. The system processes camera input, microphone recordings, uploaded audio files, and manual text entries to detect emotional states such as happiness, sadness, anger, fear, surprise, and neutrality, along with extended emotional cues including stress, anxiety, and boredom. Based on the detected emotion, the platform generates personalized recommendations including music playlists, food suggestions, activities, travel ideas, and mental wellness guidance. The application is developed using Streamlit to enable an interactive and responsive user interface. Results demonstrate that combining visual and linguistic emotion signals improves engagement and enables context aware recommendations in real time environments. The proposed architecture highlights the potential of emotion aware computing to support personalized lifestyle assistance and enhance user experience through intelligent adaptive systems.

title: Spatio-Temporal Siamese Ensemble with Multi-Level Feature Fusion for Video-Based Person Re-Identification
authors: Roshni Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Person re-identification (Re-ID) on video datasets plays a vibrant role in surveillance systems but remains challenging due to angle variation, background clutter, occlusion, noisy frames, and inadequate training data. Existing approaches either depend on high-temporal embeddings while neglecting spatial cues or on computationally expensive architectures. To solve these issues, we introduced a spatiotemporal siamese ensemble network that mutually integrates multi-level spatial features, lightweight temporal modeling, and statistical aggregation of all features. The proposed method leverages patch-based, fine-tuned CNN backbones to preserve fine-grained local cues, dual-branch temporal modeling that combines gated recurrent units with statistical mean-variance for robust sequence representation while the multi-model ensemble approach is followed by triplet and pairwise regression losses to enhance discrimination across each angle. The summation-based similarity fusion mechanism further integrates information from multiple temporal and spatial features for reliable identity matching. The extensive experiments conducted on standard benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed spatio-temporal model achieves superior accuracy and computational efficiency compared to state-of-the-art methods, contributing towards real-world video surveillance applications.

title: Construction of Occupational Interest Profiles Based on Deep Learning (CNN-LSTM): Precise Matching Algorithms Integrating Multi-Source Behavioral Data
authors: Mingqian Shi, Zhonglou Lang, Qingqing Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: The present-day techniques in creating career interest profiles are troubled with inadequate data sources, inadequate extraction of interest measures, and the low accuracy of matching, which leads to minimal talent recommendation and job matching. In a bid to solve these problems, this paper suggests a model of construction of career interest profile that is founded on Convolutional Neural Network-Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-LSTM) that incorporates the multi-source behavioral data to produce dynamic modeling and precise matching of personal interest. The special processes involve: (1) feature fusion module to encode and vectorize the text and the behavioral data to reflect the multi-dimensional association of features; (2) local interest pattern features are obtained by use of CNNs; (3) the temporal feature of behavioral patterns are obtained by use of LSTM; (4) the multi-dimensional similarity between individual and job profiles is obtained by use of the matching layer. The sample used to validate the experiment was a sample of 20,000 users of an online career learning platform. The results show that the model achieves an accuracy of 92.4 % in the interest classification task, with an overall MAP index of 0.861. The method presented in this paper effectively improves the personalization and dynamic adaptability of career interest profiles, providing new ideas for the refinement and intelligence of career recommendation systems.

title: Influence of Free-Stream Turbulence on the Aerodynamic Performance of Paired Vertical-Axis Wind Turbine
authors: Ayoub Talamalek, Ahmed Chafik Guermache, Akram Gouaidia
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: Vertical-axis wind turbines (VAWTs), particularly when arranged in pairs, present promising opportunities for specific applications, yet their theoretical foundation and technological maturity still lag behind those of horizontal-axis wind turbines (HAWTs). Advances in experimental techniques and computational modelling now offer pathways to close this gap. The present work investigates the influence of inflow turbulence on the power output of paired VAWTs using a hybrid numerical framework that couples the actuator line model (ALM) with unsteady Reynolds-averaged Navier–Stokes (uRANS) simulations. Two modelling strategies are examined: a conventional ALM using static polars combined with the Leishman–Beddoes dynamic stall model, and a modified ALM that incorporates experimentally derived dynamic polars without any dynamic stall modelling. In both cases, aerodynamic coefficients are obtained from pitching aerofoil measurements and Xfoil. Model predictions are benchmarked against wind tunnel data to assess accuracy. The findings highlight the capability of the ALM–uRANS method to reliably capture the aerodynamic behaviour of VAWTs operating under turbulent inflow conditions.

title: Rural E-Commerce and Digital Platforms for Entrepreneurship: A Case Study in Northeast China
authors: Jinyong Zheng, Panom Gunawong, Ping Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: Rural e-commerce is a key driver of poverty alleviation and entrepreneurship in China. This study examines how Wuchang Taobao Village (WCTV) uses digital platforms to enhance livelihoods, guided by the Sustainable Livelihoods Framework (SLF) expanded with digital capital. A qualitative case study combines documentary analysis and consumer interviews. Findings show that digital platforms help WCTV mobilize natural, social, and digital assets, strengthen branding, and improve market access, thereby raising incomes, fostering entrepreneurship, and expanding social networks. However, weak human capital, financial constraints, and limited replicability remain challenges. The study contributes by extending SLF to the digital era and offering policy lessons on localization, product differentiation, and talent retention for rural e-commerce in underdeveloped regions.

title: A Hardware-in-the-Loop Platform for Evaluating MQTT-based Intelligent Traffic Light Systems
authors: Maulana Fauzan Athalla Halinda, Endra Joelianto, Faqihza Mukhlish, Herman Sutarto
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)
abstract: The validation of adaptive traffic control systems (ATCS) is often confined to software simulations, which overlook performance degradation from real-world network latency and jitter. This research proposes to bridge that gap by developing a high-fidelity hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) platform to evaluate IoT-based ATCS. The platform integrates the SUMO microscopic simulator, a Raspberry Pi edge controller, and ESP32/NodeMCU actuators using an MQTT framework. Three controller architectures were evaluated: (1) Fixed Time as the baseline, (2) adaptive Slotted Max Pressure based on time slots, and (3) adaptive Cycle-based Max Pressure based on event-driven logic. The experimental results revealed a critical trade-off: the Fixed Time controller failed to manage congestion, marked by uncontrolled vehicle accumulation. The Slotted MP controller successfully managed congestion but at the cost of extremely high end-to-end (E2E) latency (~1440 ms) and massive jitter (~3153 ms std. dev.). The Cycle-based MP controller emerged as the optimal solution, successfully managing congestion while maintaining low and stable E2E latency (~414 ms), proving its viability for real-world implementation.

title: Evaluating Cycle and Non-Cycle Traffic Control Approaches in Intersection Networks Under Sensor Cyber Attacks
authors: Gabriel Francesco Hostka, I Putu Agus Ananda Giri Putra, Endra Joelianto, Faqihza Mukhlish, Herman Y. Sutarto
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)
abstract: This paper develops a traffic signal control method using reinforcement learning to improve efficiency and resilience in Surabaya's traffic system, which currently relies on fixed-time control due to sensor failures in its previous adaptive system. Two approaches are proposed: cycle-based and non-cycle-based control, implemented using conventional algorithms and a Deep Q-Learning Network (DQN). The method determines traffic phases with reward signals derived from traffic pressure and queue length, and states consisting of queue lengths and vehicle flow rates. Simulations were conducted using a replica of Surabaya's road network in PTV VISSIM and Simulation of Urban Mobility. To enhance robustness, the Robust Adversarial Loss DQN (RADIAL-DQN) is introduced, which was tested under both normal and adversarial conditions, including Projected Gradient Descent attacks. The results depicted using Macroscopic Fundamental Diagram show that cycle-based control is 10% more effective in managing signals, while non-cycle-based control offers up to 40% greater robustness against disruptions in terms of throughput.

title: Model-Based Multi-Modal Volumetric Sensing via Fusion of Stereo HFR, ToF, and Thermal Cameras
authors: Tegar Palyus Fiqar, Feiyue Wang, Kohei Shimasaki, Idaku Ishii
publish: 2025 18th International Conference on Sensing Technology (ICST)
abstract: Multi-modal, non-contact sensing systems have become increasingly critical in diverse fields such as biomedical diagnostics, structural health monitoring, and manufacturing. These applications often require synchronized measurement of temperature, vibration, displacement, and surface appearance. Conventional methods that rely on separate sensors for each modality face challenges in spatial alignment, temporal synchronization, and physical integration, particularly when applied to non-rigid or moving targets. In this paper, we present a modelbased volumetric capture system capable of simultaneous threedimensional (3D) visualization of thermal and vibrational characteristics. The system integrates stereo high-frame-rate cameras at hundreds of frames per second (fps), a time-of-flight depth camera, and a thermal infrared camera operating at several dozens of fps. The system employs a unified 3D model as a spatial reference to which multi-modal data are registered. A model-based 3D digital image correlation (3D-DIC) algorithm enables the estimation of small displacements and vibrations in the range of several tens to hundreds of hertz. To demonstrate the effectiveness of our system, thermal mapping is performed on the same 3D model, enabling visualization of the spatial distribution and temporal evolution of both vibration and temperature during the heating and boiling process of an electric kettle.

title: Fractional-N PLL–Based Micro-Oven Control for Ultra-Stable MEMS Frequency References
authors: Karim Elmeligy, Rakibul Islam, Jintark Kim, Mohamed Badr Younis, Jie Yan, Jiawei Yang, Gabrielle Vukasin, Hyun-Keun Kwon, Thomas W. Kenny, Gaurav Bahl, Pavan Kumar Hanumolu
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper presents a fully integrated micro-oven temperature compensation method for dual-mode MEMS oscillators using a fractional-N PLL. By locking two temperature-dependent modes and driving the micro-heater with an amplitude-modulated signal, the system achieves a 2 ppb/°C TCF and sub-ppb Allan deviation while remaining fully CMOS compatible. A 65-nm prototype shows a three-order-of-magnitude improvement over previous work and offers a general framework for oscillator stabilization using temperature information encoded in frequency.

title: A Planar and Low-Profile CPW-Fed UWB MIMO Antenna with High Isolation
authors: Rahul Kumar Garg, Mayank Jalan, Nihal Mittal, Rahul Porwal
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: The paper presents a novel coplanar waveguide (CPW)-fed two-port MIMO antenna designed for ultrawideband (UWB) applications (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.1-10.6 \text{GHz}$</tex> frequency range). The antenna achieves a wide impedance bandwidth of 7.2 GHz, spanning from 3.2 GHz to 10.4 GHz, and is fabricated on a lowcost FR-4 substrate with a compact footprint of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16 \times 23 \text{mm}^{2}$</tex>. A fractional bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 5. 8 8 {\%}}$</tex> is achieved, ensuring robust performance across the entire UWB spectrum. The MIMO configuration employs two identical monopole elements, strategically spaced to maximize isolation and minimize mutual coupling, and achieves isolation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>16 \text{dB}$</tex> across the band. The antenna exhibits a maximum of 4 dB of gain with constant radiation patterns. The performance metrics of MIMO, such as envelope correlation coefficient (ECC) and diversity gain (DG), achieve values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.01$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>9.98$</tex>, respectively. The proposed design is fabricated and experimentally tested, and measurements have close agreement with simulated values. The antenna is compact in size, has a wide bandwidth, good isolation, and can be used in modern UWB MIMO communication.

title: ASIM 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)

title: Deep Non-Negative Matrix Factorization for Recommender Systems
authors: Chahrazed Mediani, Abdelaziz Lakhfif, Ferial Senator, Hanane Boutouta, Karima Anani
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Cyber Physical Systems (AICPS)
abstract: A recommender system is a specific form of information filtering that helps users find items from enormous catalogs to present them based on their preferences and behavior. Items can be books, films, news, and music. Selecting the right approach has gotten difficult due to the abundance of available methods. When a recommender system performs better than other approaches, it can be compared and found to be a valuable tool. This work uses deep learning approach to implement collaborative recommendation algorithms for online resources (MFDL Model). This model is based on the matrix factorization. The Matrix Factorization with Deep Learning contributes to deep learning collaborative recommender systems by effectively bridging the gap between traditional matrix factorization (MF) and the expressive power of deep neural networks (DNNs). To compare the proposed model, we have implemented, evaluated, and compared two machine learning approaches: Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and Non-Negative Matrix Factorization (NMF). The metrics evaluation allowed us to deduce that the MFDL model present good results for the dataset used.

title: Distributed Smart Agriculture Monitoring over the IoT Computing Continuum
authors: Carlos Kamienski, Ivan Zyrianoff, Lei Xue, Marco Di Felice
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Smart agriculture applications utilizing IoT sensors and actuators operate across a geographical and computing continuum, encompassing diverse and resource-constrained devices at the edge and more powerful infrastructure closer to the cloud. However, these applications are typically not designed to be aware of the continuum’s heterogeneity, leading to challenges in efficiency and reliability. We introduce DATUM (Distributed Applications for the IoTinuum), a framework for developing and deploying continuum-aware smart agriculture applications. We qualitatively evaluate DATUM with a smart irrigation use case, demonstrating its potential to address common challenges and leverage the strengths of the IoTinuum. Also, we introduce a system model and a problem formulation to minimize the latency, which can be used to conduct a performance analysis study.

title: Traceability and Research in the Agricultural Chain of Medicinal and Aromatic Plants (MAPs): An IoT-based Approach
authors: Ivan Zyrianoff, Andrea Iannoli, Federico Montori, Luca Sciullo, Luciano Bononi, Alice Baldissara, Beatrice Brintazzoli, Enrico Dall’Olio, Mattia Alpi, Rocco Enrico Sferrazza, Giovanni Dinelli, Ilaria Marotti, Marco Di Felice
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: In precision agriculture, the Internet of Things (IoT) provides essential technologies for collecting and processing real-time data from the field. While numerous deployments have been reported, the unique conditions and requirements of agricultural environments often demand customized IoT solutions. In this paper, we present an innovative use case from the TRACE project, focused on the value chain of Medicinal and Aromatic Plants (MAPS) in Italy. We describe a comprehensive framework designed to enable precision agriculture in this domain, encompassing IoT data collection, farm data integration, and supply chain certification. The TRACE deployment includes a medium-scale LoRaWAN network with nearly 30 IoT devices, comprising Weather Stations (WS) and Soil Moisture Sensors (SMS). Our measurements demonstrate that a single LoRa link can achieve a range exceeding 6.5 km. However, our findings also indicate that RSSI values do not consistently correlate with Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR) performance. Additionally, weather conditions like low temperature appear to slightly influence RSSI values, particularly depending on the exposure of the IoT devices.

title: OmniPlantSeg: Species Agnostic 3D Point Cloud Organ Segmentation for High-Resolution Plant Phenotyping Across Modalities
authors: Andreas Gilson, Lukas Meyer, Oliver Scholz, Ute Schmid
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Accurate point cloud segmentation for plant organs is crucial for 3D plant phenotyping. Existing solutions are designed problem-specific with a focus on certain plant species or specified sensor-modalities for data acquisition. Furthermore, it is common to use extensive pre-processing and down-sample the plant point clouds to meet hardware or neural network input size requirements. We propose a simple, yet effective algorithm KD-SS for sub-sampling of biological point clouds that is agnostic to sensor data and plant species. The main benefit of this approach is that we do not need to down-sample our input data and thus, enable segmentation of the full-resolution point cloud. Combining KD-SS with current state-of-the-art segmentation models shows satisfying results evaluated on different modalities such as photogrammetry, laser triangulation and LiDAR for various plant species. We propose KD-SS as lightweight resolution-retaining alternative to intensive pre-processing and down-sampling methods for plant organ segmentation regardless of used species and sensor modality.

title: 2025 IEEE Latin American Conference on Nanotechnology (LANANO)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Grapevine Leaf Phenotyping Using Deep Learning Based on Three-Dimensional Point Cloud
authors: Dimitrios-Tilemachos Davarakis, Yuqiao Ren, Nikolaos Tsoulias, Marco Bignardi, Dimitrios S. Paraforos, Dimitrios Argyropoulos
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: High-quality phenotyping of grapevine leaves is critical for monitoring plant health, growth, and yield. Traditional manual measurement methods are time-consuming, destructive, and labor-intensive. This study investigates the application of PointNet, a 3D point-based deep learning model, for predicting grapevine leaf traits—area, width, and length—directly from raw point cloud data. A dataset of 930 colorized 3D point clouds was generated from RGB-D images under controlled laboratory conditions. The data were preprocessed with filtering, normalization, imputation, down sampling, and augmentation before training. PointNet was adapted for regression by replacing its classification layers with a multilayer regression architecture. Comparative experiments with traditional machine learning models—KNN, DTR, RFR, and SVR—trained on geometric and statistical features showed that the PointNet regressor achieved superior overall performance, with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values up to 0.99 and consistently low RMSE and MAPE, particularly for leaf width (0.25 and 0.03 respectively) and length (0.5 and 0.05 respectively). While RFR outperformed PointNet in leaf area RMSE, the deep learning approach demonstrated stronger generalization and scalability. Results highlight the potential of 3D point-based deep learning for automated, non-destructive grapevine phenotyping. Future work will expand field datasets and explore alternative 3D models for improved trait prediction and automated organ segmentation.

title: A Timing Synchronization Algorithm for Single-Carrier Burst Signals Based on Segmented Smoothing Processing
authors: Yuefu Li, Shigang Liu, Yang Wang, Qinglong Wei
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address the problem of synchronization error caused by the time drift of synchronization parameters in singlecarrier burst signal, a “segment estimation and smooth connection” forward timing synchronization algorithm is proposed. This algorithm segments the signal and based on the quasi-static assumption, achieves precise parameter estimation within each segment. Through the smoothing factor, it balances the historical state with the current measurement value to suppress phase jumps between segments. Through MATLAB simulation and formula verification, the smoothing threshold formulas for four modulation signals (BPSK, QPSK, 8PSK, 16QAM) and the applicable signal frequency offset range were derived. The algorithm can improve the time offset of QPSK signals by 72%, the time offset of 16QAM signals by 54.2% and the MSE performance of the improved algorithm was superior to that of the original algorithm. Moreover, the inter-segment phase jump range of both algorithms is within the constraint range.

title: Robust Hevc Bitstream Stitching for Live Multiview Video Streaming
authors: Zaixi Shang, Yongjun Wu
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: Multiview streaming is increasingly used in sports and entertainment, but current solutions struggle to support adaptive bitrate (ABR) streaming and robustness on consumer devices that expose only a single hardware HEVC decoder. Existing HEVC bitstream stitching and cloud-mixing methods [1], [2] can merge multiple streams into one, but they are fragile when one input stream is lost. They also typically assume a fixed set of view resolutions and a static layout, so if ABR logic tries to change the resolution of one view independently, the resulting arrangement may no longer form a valid rectangular HEVC picture, making per-view ABR difficult to support.

title: Comparison of Deep Learning Architectures for Fault Detection in Power Grids: A Study on CNN, Vision Transformer, and Swin Transformer
authors: Himanshu Yadav, Deepanshu Yadav, Raghav Joshi, Nishant Srivastava, Sanjeev Kumar Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Accurate and rapid fault detection in power systems is increasingly challenged by harmonic distortions and noise introduced by renewable energy integration. While deep learning has shown promise for fault classification, no systematic comparison of CNN, Vision Transformer (ViT), and Swin Transformer architectures exists under realistic noisy conditions. This paper presents such a comparison using a 31-class dataset generated from an IEEE 5-bus MATLAB/Simulink simulation, augmented with white Gaussian noise (SNR 20-40 dB), 3rd/5th/7th harmonic distortions, and fault resistances up to 100Ω. All models were trained identically on an NVIDIA T4 GPU. The CNN achieved 99.24% fault-type and 99.49% fault-location accuracy using only 7,237 parameters at 14 s/epoch—outperforming ViT (43,461 parameters, 21 s/epoch) and Swin (514,693 parameters, 64 s/epoch) on fault-type accuracy and training efficiency. Results confirm that CNNs remain the most practical architecture for real-time power system protection where computational efficiency is critical.

title: A 26.4-to-56-Gb/s 0.38-pJ/bit PAM-4 Reference-less CDR Using Frequency-Offset Quantization Scheme Achieving 65-ns Constant-Band Acquisition Time in 28nm CMOS
authors: Runchen Wang, Yu Lv, Lingao Huang, Jiaxin Sun, Ya Zhao, Guohe Zhang, Li Geng, Chao Fan
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper reports a single-loop reference-less clock and data recovery (CDR) featuring a pattern-proportion frequency-offset quantization (PP-FQ) scheme to reduce the locking time. Specifically, the proposed PP-FQ scheme quantize the frequency offset using the sampling pattern proportion, which aids in a fast frequency band acquisition. Besides, the simple implementation of PP-FQ logic eliminates the additional frequency detector while upholding a wideband data rate range. Prototyped in 28-nm CMOS, the proposed reference-less CDR automatically could acquire 26.4-to-56 Gb/s PAM-4 data with a constant 65-ns full-band acquisition time and < 617-ns overall locking time, achieving an energy efficiency of 0.38 pJ/bit.

title: Attractor Matching: A New Paradigm for Structural String Comparison
authors: Simone Faro, Dominik Köppl, Francesco Pio Marino
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: We introduce Attractor Matching, a new framework for structural string comparison built upon the theory of string attractors. Given a pattern <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex> of length <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m$</tex> and one of its attractors <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Gamma_{x}$</tex>, the problem asks for all substrings <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y[i. . i+m-1]$</tex> of a text <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y$</tex> such that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Gamma_{x}$</tex> is also an attractor of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y[i. . i+m-1]$</tex>. Unlike classical notions of string matching, which rely on character equality or distance measures, attractor matching focuses on the structural properties that govern repetitiveness and compressibility. Our contribution is fourfold. First, we adapt the IsAttractor algorithm of Béal et al. by combining the DAWG with the slidingwindow technique of Blumer, enabling online attractor verification as the window advances over the text. Second, we reformulate the verification procedure on the Compressed DAWG (CDAWG), obtaining a more compact representation that preserves correctness. Third, we employ the sliding-window CDAWG method of Inenaga et al., which allows efficient attractor matching on sliding-window maintained CDAWGs with incremental updates. Finally, we introduce a relaxed variant, Attractor Matching with Mismatches, where the pattern attractor may be extended by at most <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\rho$</tex> additional positions, enabling structurally tolerant matching. This paradigm bridges compression and similarity, opening new directions for structure-aware pattern matching.

title: Design and Modelling of a Bidirectional ThreePhase Solid-State Transformer for Advanced Power System Applications
authors: Khaled Ghambirlou, Adel Ali Abosnina, Gerry Moschopoulos
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive design and dynamic modelling framework for a bidirectional three-phase Solid-State Transformer (SST) tailored for future powersystem architectures. The proposed SST unifies an actively controlled AC/DC rectifier, an isolated Dual Active Bridge (DAB) DC-DC converter, and a grid-forming DC/AC inverter within a coordinated control hierarchy that enables seamless bidirectional power transfer, adaptive voltage regulation, and intelligent grid-support functionality. A detailed dq-domain representation is developed in MATLAB/Simulink to capture the coupled electrical and control dynamics across all conversion stages under both steady-state and transient operating conditions. The simulated results confirm robust DC-link regulation, high-efficiency bidirectional energy exchange, and precise voltage synthesis during dynamic load and grid disturbances. The modelling framework establishes a validated foundation for the optimal design, coordination, and deployment of SSTs in advanced distribution and transmission networks.

title: IoT-Based Health Monitoring System for Textile Workers
authors: R. Ramya, Sowndar Raj R, Vijay L
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: For workers in physically demanding industries like the textile industry, unintentional falls and unexpected medical emergencies are major safety concerns. The risk of falls, which can result in serious injuries and delayed medical aid, is increased by fatigue, slick flooring, extended standing, and constant machine operation. Slow emergency reaction is caused by traditional safety systems' lack of real-time fall detection and fast alert methods. In addition to the physical risks, textile workers are subjected to dangerous environmental factors including loud noises and dust in the air, which have an adverse effect on their health and productivity. This project suggests a wearable safety and health monitoring system for textile industry workers that is integrated with the Internet of Things. Through a two-stage verification procedure that includes vibration alerts and manual cancelation to reduce false alarms, the wearable device employs motion sensors to identify fall- like actions. Supervisors or family members automatically receive emergency messages with location and health information if no response is received. Noise and dust sensors operate in tandem with fall detection to continuously monitor workplace conditions and send out alarms when safety criteria are exceeded. The suggested system promotes a safer and healthier textile manufacturing environment, guarantees prompt intervention, and enhances worker safety.

title: Real-Time AI-Driven Multi-Store Sales Forecasting and Automated Inventory Optimization System with Role-based Retail Intelligence
authors: K.V.N.S. Mahendra, Nukala Rohith, Rajaram V, Balakiruthiga B
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Retail companies have difficulties in keeping the optimal level of inventory because of the dynamic nature of demand, which is affected by seasonality, pricing, and promotions. This research work presents a Real-Time AI-Based Sales Forecasting and Inventory Optimization System that improves retail decision-making through the use of predictive analytics and automation. The system combines real-time sales and inventory data from cloud-based platforms such as Point-of-Sale (POS) systems and Google Sheets APIs, removing the need for manual uploading of the dataset for analysis. A hybrid forecasting approach that combines Random Forest, AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) models is used to make accurate predictions of demand. The system uses automation techniques like Economic Order Quantity (EOQ), safety stock, and reorder point models to make inventory decisions, automatically generating purchase orders when certain levels of inventory are reached. The system is capable of managing multiple stores through centralized analytics and uses Role-Based Access Control dashboards for Admin, Manager, and Supplier users. Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) tests show that the system has high predictive accuracy.

title: The Neurological Code: Deciphering Parkinson's with Deep Generative Models and Attention Mechanisms
authors: Pakanati Chandana Reddy, I.V. Shashikala, T. Aditya Sai Srinivas, K. Dora Babu, M. Ramu, B. Sahadeva Reddy
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: This paper examines how the scarcity of medical imaging datasets hinders the development of deep learning models for the diagnosis of Parkinson's Disease (PD). Although Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have been used for data augmentation in the past, they have been found to produce synthetic images of low quality while suffering from a phenomenon called mode collapse. Our research presents a new methodology in which the first step employs a Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DDPM) to produce images of PD. This is followed by the use of a Vision Transformer (ViT) image classifier to facilitate PD detection. Our diffusion-based generator has created images of healthy individuals and individuals with Parkinson's Disease. Compared to GAN-based models, our images have a Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$22.4 \pm 0.3$</tex> and are therefore of a much higher quality. After being trained on synthetic data supplemented with real-world images, the ViT classifier achieved an accuracy of 98.5% and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> score of 0.984, which demonstrates efficient generalization to new, clinically unencountered patient data. We further use Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) for model interpretability which generates visual explanations for Grad-CAM aligned with the clinical indicators. Our research demonstrates that diffusion models are a better option for medical image synthesis compared to GANs, and presents a new, scalable framework for the early diagnosis of Parkinson's Disease that is interpretable and scalable.

title: Research on Curing Degree Detection of Fiber-Wound Plastic-Lined Gas Cylinders Using RFID and Physical Information Neural Networks
authors: Zudong Cao, Kun Mao, Xuelin Zhang, Xiangwei Zuo
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The cure degree of plastic-lined fiber-wound cylinders is a critical indicator affecting their mechanical properties and service life. Traditional testing methods are often time-consuming, inefficient, or destructive. To address this issue, this study proposes a cure degree detection system based on a Radio Frequency Identification resistive resonant circuit array and a Physical Information Neural Network. This system utilizes an RFID resistive resonant circuit array to continuously capture the electromagnetic response of polymer materials during curing. Data normalization enhances sensitivity to curing degree variations. Building upon this foundation, a PINN model was constructed and optimized, deeply integrating the physical principles of curing kinetics into the neural network architecture. Comparative evaluations against pure LSTM and standard PINN models demonstrate that the proposed optimized model exhibits outstanding predictive performance: Mean Absolute Error of 0.067, Root Mean Square Error of 0.084, and a high Coefficient of Determination of 0.960. This significantly outperforms both the pure LSTM model and the standard PINN model, demonstrating the critical role of integrating physical information and optimizing network architecture in enhancing curing degree prediction accuracy and model interpretability. This study provides an efficient and feasible solution for non-destructive, precise, and intelligent monitoring of the curing degree in fiber-wound cylinders, holding significant implications for quality control in composite manufacturing processes.

title: A Σ-Structure Ripple-Cancellation Switching Regulator with Automatic Ripple Calibration Achieving Sub-mV Output Voltage Ripple
authors: Haoran Xu, Desheng Zhang, Jingbin Wu, Chang Liu, Jianming Lei, Han Peng, Xuecheng Zou, Run Min, Qiaoling Tong
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This work presents a Σ-structure ripple-cancellation switching regulator, achieving sub-mV output ripple with low-profile passive components in a wide operation range. Through feedback-based automatic ripple calibration, a low-voltage auxiliary branch provides a complementary current for dominant ripple cancellation. Furthermore, the residual ripple caused by parasitic effects is eliminated by a feedforward-based active nonlinear ripple decoupler. Fabricated in 180-nm BCD process, the regulator achieves an average output ripple of 700μV in the full operation range.

title: Multi-Spectral Filter-Less Computational Imager in Standard CMOS for Beyond-Visible Ubiquitous Machine Vision and Flexible Adaptation to Application
authors: Udara Samurdhi Harshanga Kalingage, Karim Ali, Ruiyuan Yang, Massimo Alioto
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: A 256×256 computational multispectral imager capable of extracting visual features from UV to visible and NIR is presented. A novel 14T pixel enables spectral selectivity without physical filters, while sliding-row biasing achieves 30 fps at sub-mW power. This architecture extends machine vision to beyond-visible conditions (e.g., fog, smoke, inspection) and supports seamless integration into standard CMOS SoCs. The computational approach enables die reuse and unprecedented adaptation to the application (e.g., setting wavelengths of interest).

title: Genetic Algorithm–Based Optimization of RoBERTa for Sexism Detection
authors: Luis Miguel Huerta-Venegas, Rodrigo Israel Hernandez-Marariegos, Jaime Cerda-Jacobo, José Ortiz-Bejar
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: This paper presents the results achieved with a RoBERTa model optimized using genetic algorithms (GAs). The proposal is evaluated over the dataset provided for the task CLEF 2025 EXIST: Sexism detection in social media content. Our strategy combines the power of RoBERTa with evolutionary optimization techniques to fine-tune hyperparameters in a multi-task learning framework. The genetic algorithm is used to adjust the learning rate, dropout, batch size, and loss weight across three interconnected tasks: binary sexism detection, source intention classification, and sexism categorization. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements over a direct fine-tuning approach, achieving accuracy improvements of $3.39 \%, 15.09 \%$, and $6.73 \%$ for the respective tasks.

title: Development of IoT stations for measuring air and water quality by using optical spectroscopy
authors: Marvin Ramos, Samuel De la O, Nelson Cisneros, Jorge Cuadra, Oscar Deodanes, Carlos Rudamas
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: This study presents the development and implementation of a low-cost IoT station for air quality monitoring using Differential Optical Absorption Spectroscopy (DOAS). The proposed technology enables real-time detection of key parameters such as atmospheric pollutants. In addition, this IoT devices can also be used to measure water quality through computer simulations, retrieving parameters of phytoplankton concentrations, particulate matter and total suspended matter.

title: GPU-Accelerated Chirplet Transform: Scalable Runtime Profiling and Analysis
authors: Nishant Kumar, Steve Mann
publish: 2025 18th International Conference on Sensing Technology (ICST)
abstract: The chirplet transform provides a powerful and flexible approach to analyzing non-stationary signals in many types of sensing modalities. Its broad application has been hindered, however, by the computational expense of its parameter search process, especially in cases where high-resolution parameter sweeps are needed. This paper presents a hardware-accelerated framework for chirplet decomposition based on GPU parallelism with unified memory that achieves scalable performance across a variety of signal types. The system was tested on seven simulated sensing signals with different temporal and spectral dynamics, including EEG (awake and sleep), EMG, radar, and vibration signals. Results demonstrate up to 18x acceleration in the chirplet search process, with considerable reduction of CPU usage and system memory load. Remarkably, the GPU version is increasingly faster for longer signals, e.g., sleep EEG, even with overhead from unified memory. The results prove the potential of GPU-accelerated chirplet transforms for large-scale or near real-time sensing applications, while also highlighting dictionary generation as an important area for further optimization.

title: Light Detector System for Analyzing Plant Cultivation Utilization of Reflected Light from Ground Surfaces
authors: Takashige Kaihatsu, Jo Kitada, Toshihiko Kinugasa, Daiju Taketoshi, Yusuke Miyazaki, Sang-Seok Lee, Tadao Matsunaga
publish: 2025 18th International Conference on Sensing Technology (ICST)
abstract: This study aims to elucidate how plants utilize reflected light and to develop a light detection system for the associated measurements and analysis. The light environment was measured using different types of ground surfaces. In addition, the differences between the intensities of upward and downward incident light, as well as changes in reflected light according to the ground surface type, were effectively compared.

title: Thermal-mechanical coupling simulation of Chiplet 2.5D packaging based on chiplet distribution and Full Copper Interconnection
authors: Shizhen Li, Chenshan Gao, Xu Liu, Huaiyu Ye, Guoqi Zhang
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: The semiconductor industry is facing significant challenges, including physical limitations such as heat dissipation, rising manufacturing costs, and the difficulty of further shrinking transistor sizes while maintaining performance. To address these issues, chiplet technology has emerged, integrating smaller, specialized chips into a single package. This approach improves modularity, manufacturing yields, and allows mixing different technology nodes for optimal performance. However, challenges like ensuring high-speed, low-latency interconnects and effective thermal management persist.This study explores several key aspects of chiplet technology using a simplified dual-chip model, focusing on the integration of nanocopper materials. By comparing nanocopper with traditional solder, we analyze differences in mechanical behavior and thermal management. Additionally, we investigate variations in chiplet layout, examining the impact of spacing and the number of chiplets on performance under identical power conditions. Our findings indicate that nanocopper interconnects provide lower stress and better thermal conductivity than traditional solder. Adjusting chiplet spacing and increasing the number of chiplets both enhance heat dissipation. Future work will involve larger-scale simulations and fatigue analyses to optimize chiplet packaging for improved performance and reliability.

title: Research on Vulnerable Regions of MEMS Sensors Based on Multi-Physics Coupling Simulation
authors: Gaoyu Sun, Wenchao You, Jie Cheng, Jiaoying Huang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Capacitive MEMS sensors with silicon microstructures exhibit high sensitivity, low noise, and low power consumption, making them widely applicable in various markets. To further enhance their reliability and identify potential failure mechanisms, this study focuses on the MAX53X accelerometer as a research subject. A device physical model was established to perform multi-physics coupling simulations, aiming to analyze vulnerable regions. Simulation results reveal that under electro-thermo-mechanical coupled stress, stress concentrations occur at solder joints, bonding necks, the roots of spring structures in micromechanical mechanisms, structure-chip interfaces, and geometric corners. These stress concentrations may exceed the yield limit, leading to potential failures. This research provides valuable insights for the structural design and optimization of MEMS sensors.

title: Deep Learning for Friction Velocity Estimation in Urban Roughness Sublayer
authors: Tao Chen, Renming Yuan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Urban friction velocity is a key parameter governing turbulent momentum transport. Traditional Monin-Obukhov similarity theory(MOST) fails over heterogeneous urban surfaces due to its assumptions of horizontal homogeneity and steady flux. To overcome this limitation, we propose multiscale TSMixer(MS-TSMixer), a specialized multi-scale extension of the TSMixer architecture that embeds parallel convolution modules across four physical scales to enable end-to-end friction velocity estimation without prior stability functions. Using 362,000 quality-controlled one-minute samples from an 18-meter urban tower in Hefei from January 2022 to June 2023, we conduct a systematic benchmark of MOST, RF, ANN, and MS-TSMixer. Results show MS-TSMixer achieves an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R$</tex> squared value of 0.8168 and a root mean square error of 0.0702 meters per second, reducing error by 42 percent relative to MOST. Although overall <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}$</tex> squared improves only modestly from 0.8018 to 0.8168, wind-speed stratification reveals significant gains: strong-wind performance exceeding three meters per second increases from 0.4745 to 0.5442, confirming that the multi-scale module effectively captures enhanced coupling under high winds. This study demonstrates that MS-TSMixer robustly characterizes urban turbulence without prior assumptions, providing a transferable framework for intelligent urban meteorological parameterization.

title: RGB-T Multi-modal Visual Question Answering in Nighttime and Adverse Environment
authors: Songyuan Yang, Fan Yang, Biwen Yang, Jing Zhao, Yongqiang Sun, Ruiheng Zhang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Visual Question Answering (VQA) models that rely only on RGB inputs often fail in nighttime and adverse environments due to poor illumination and semantic loss. To address this, we propose an RGB-T VQA framework that integrates visible and thermal infrared (TIR) modalities. The framework contains two key modules: a Cross-Modal Guided Attention (CGA) that uses thermal cues to refine RGB features, and a Thermal-Semantic Prior (TSP) that compensates for the limited semantics of TIR data. In addition, we construct a large-scale RGB-T VQA dataset covering diverse nighttime, low-light, and adverse weather scenes. Experiments show that our mode

title: OAFA-Net: A Lightweight Orientation-Aware Network for Remote Sensing Scene Classification
authors: Shuo Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Remote sensing scene classification (RSSC) is a significant research direction within the field of remote sensing. Traditional deep learning networks are hindered in practical deployment for RSSC due to their substantial parameter counts and high computational overhead. Furthermore, standard convolutional operations struggle to adapt to the arbitrary object orientations and multi-scale variations inherent in remote sensing imagery, which constrains further improvements in classification accuracy. To address the above challenges, we propose a lightweight orientation-aware adaptive feature aggregation network, OAFA-Net. The network enhances spatial awareness by incorporating CoordConv to resolve the position insensitivity of traditional Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). We also designed an Orientation-Aware Attention (OAA) module and an Adaptive Feature Aggregation (AFA) module. The OAA module improves feature capturing for arbitrarily oriented objects, while the AFA module intelligently fuses multi-scale features to generate a highly discriminative feature map for classification. Experiments on the publicly available AID and NWPU datasets show that OAFA-Net achieves excellent classification results while reducing computational costs and parameter counts, providing a new paradigm for constructing efficient and accurate RSSC models.

title: BWPF-XGB: A Gradient-Reshaped and Weighted Fusion Framework for Extreme Imbalanced Fault Detection in Semiconductor Manufacturing
authors: Tengkai Huang, Yaozong Wang, Jianming Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Semiconductor fault detection faces challenges like gradient swamping and boundary bias under extreme class imbalance (1.598% rate) and high-dimensional data. This paper proposes BWPF-XGB, a framework integrating gradientreshaping asymmetric loss (M1), weighted probability fusion (M2), and dynamic threshold calibration (M3) to enhance rare fault capture. Validation on the industrial P4-CV07 dataset demonstrates significant performance gains: Average Precision (AP) rose from 0.1042 to 0.5865, and the Matthews Correlation Coefficient (MCC) reached 0.5745. BWPF-XGB provides robust algorithmic support for monitoring high-dimensional sparse targets in advanced manufacturing environments.

title: Modeling and Robust Control of SEIG-Based Wind Energy Systems Using Linear RST and Nonlinear Sliding Mode VOC Techniques
authors: Zabouri Abdelhamid, Abdallah Belabbes, Della Krachai Mohamed, Riyadh Bouddou, Adel Oubelaid, Abdussalam Ali Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: In this work, we address the challenge of ensuring robust control of a self-excited induction generator (SEIG) operating in isolated wind energy systems, where parameter variations and operating conditions significantly affect performance. As a first contribution, the magnetization curve of the generator is experimentally extracted in order to ensure an accurate modeling of the SEIG under different operating points. Based on this model, two voltage-oriented control (VOC) strategies are implemented and compared: a linear cascade controller (RST for current control and PI for DC-bus regulation) and a nonlinear second-order sliding mode controller (SMC). The proposed study evaluates the robustness of both controllers against parameter variations, wind-speed fluctuations, and load-torque disturbances. Simulation results obtained under MATLAB/Simulink demonstrate that the SMC provides superior disturbance rejection and reduced current harmonic distortion, while the RST-PI structure offers fast dynamics with simpler implementation.

title: Multimodal Large Model-Driven AI Parking Recognition Technology
authors: Yu Luo, Yuhao Cao, Kun Cheng, Liang Yi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This paper proposes an AI parking recognition technology based on the multimodal visual model GLM-4.6V for parking detection tasks. First, prompt engineering is employed to optimize model outputs, enhancing their accuracy and practicality. Through formulaic prompt design, the model achieves low-code implementation and visualization, thereby lowering the barrier to entry. By training and fine-tuning the model, deeper integration with multimodal interaction is realized. For precise localization of parking space corners, GLM-4.6V utilizes special markers ˋ<\vert begin_of_box\vert >ˋ and ‘<\vert end_of_box\vert >’ to embed target region coordinates within linguistic descriptions, enabling accurate alignment between verbal instructions and visual elements. Finally, by integrating prompt engineering, the multimodal interaction capabilities of GLM-4.6 V are leveraged to perform parking space detection and parking maneuver detection tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that GLM-4.6V outperforms GLM-4.5V, YOLOv7, and YOLOv3 in overall detection speed, while offering more comprehensive and stable performance.

title: Improved ASD Detection from Brain fMRI Using Fine-Tuned Transfer Learning on Pretrained Deep Convolutional Models
authors: Fedia Bahloul, Raouia Mokni, Monji Kherallah
