title: Efficient EV Charging Station Management Using Battery SoC and BMS
authors: M. Venkatachalam, A. Faridul Arshad, S. Kovardhana, R. Anbu Thamizh, T. Logeswaran, P. Tamilarasu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The Electric Vehicle (EV) market is growing exponentially, the Electric Vehicle charging stations that already exist in the market are experiencing an inefficiency in the First Come First Served (FCFS) queuing logic. The queuing logic that is being followed is static and does not consider the State of Charge (SoC) of the Electric Vehicles. The Electric Vehicles that are completely drained of power have to wait longer for charging compared to Electric Vehicles that have enough power. This is creating a major roadblock for the efficient Electric Vehicle charging stations. A new Electric Vehicle distribution system is proposed in the research that completely changes the chronological queuing logic to the queuing logic that is based on priority. The new queuing logic that is proposed is based on the dual module system that consists of the client interface that provides the Electric Vehicles with the facility of real-time telemetry. The new queuing logic that is proposed is different from the traditional queuing logic as it treats the Electric Vehicle charging slots as a fluid system that is based on the concept of necessity.

title: Hybrid Graph Neural Network Model for Energy-efficient Route Planning in Autonomous EV Mobility Networks
authors: Sunith Babu L, K Rakesh, K Srinivasan, Shubhangi Nishikant Gurav, R. Manoj Kumar, S. Sathish
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: A major challenge in autonomous electric vehicle mobility networks is the optimisation of a renewable energy route, which is affected by varying traffic conditions, complex road layouts and constraints on battery size. The traditional shortest-path and rule-based routing techniques are not tailored enough to solve these related problems for energy efficient but short EV routes. In this paper, we propose a hybrid model for representing road networks by energy-aware graphs and inferring optimal routing decisions with spatial representation learning and physics-guided energy inductive modelling. The proposed system integrates the junction, edge and global attributes such as traffic congestion and route geometry with vehicle energy dynamics. A coherent optimization methodology enables to compose routes on-the-fly reacting to the mobility variations. Experimental results show that the proposed approach achieves superior performance, with 98.7 % accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 7 \%}$</tex> F1-score and a low RMSE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 0 4 1}$</tex>, outperforming traditional routing, reinforcement learning and independent graph-based learning models. The outcomes confirm that the incorporation of graph neural networks to ecacy-aware decision making significantly helps in perfecting optimal routes and reducing overall energy consumption. This work presents a scalable and adaptable solution for autonomous electric vehicle (EV) tracking, and supports greener intelligent transportation systems.

title: A Fast-Transient Dual-Domain Learning Digital LDO with Droop Timing Prediction and Proactive Timing-Aware Control for Adaptive Droop Mitigation
authors: Minkyu Song, Yunbeom Hwang, Taeho Lee, Jisu Kim, Jun-Eun Park
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper presents a digital low-dropout regulator (DLDO) that employs a dual-domain learning scheme for enhanced droop mitigation and fast transient response. The proposed architecture incorporates a dual-domain learning self-adaptive compensation (DL- SAC) scheme, which performs real-time learning in both the voltage-current (V-I) and time domains. The V-I domain learning path identifies the optimal compensation current for a given droop magnitude, enabling rapid one-shot recovery, while the time-domain learning path predicts recurring droop intervals and proactively strengthens the droop detector for faster detection. A prototype was fabricated in a 28-nm CMOS process with a fully integrated 900 pF on-chip output capacitor. The prototype supports an input voltage V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">IN</inf> of 0.8 to 1.2 V. Measurement results demonstrated a voltage droop of 120 mV and a droop settling time TS of 8.6 ns for an 82 mA load step with an on-chip test load, and a 59 mV droop with an 11.1 ns droop TS under a clock distribution emulation board integrating a clock buffer array at V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">IN</inf> range of 1.1 V and V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OUT</inf> of 1.05 V. The proposed DLDO achieved droop reductions of 64 mV and 21.9 mV and TS improvements of 3.6 ns and 4.6 ns for the on-chip and emulation board condition, respectively.

title: A Low-Power 1.2 TFLOPS/W Merge-Split RVV Processor for Mixed Scalar-Vector Workloads
authors: Mattia Sinigaglia, Marco Bertuletti, Gianmarco Ottavi, Amirhossein Kiamarzi, Matteo Perotti, Giuseppe Tagliavini, Luca Benini, Davide Rossi
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: Near-sensor workloads mix scalar and vector tasks under strict power and area constraints, requiring flexibility that neither multi-core nor wide-vector designs guarantee. We present a runtime-reconfigurable dual-core RISC-V vector SoC (GF 22FDX) switching in 5 cycles between Split-Mode (two vectors) and Merge-Mode (wide-vector + scalar). Consuming <155mW, it achieves SoA-leading FP8 1.2TFLOPS/W (@85.64GFLOPS) on highly data-parallel tasks. Split-Mode is 1.9× faster than Merge-Mode on modest parallelism, while Merge-Mode reaches 1.46× speed-up on mixed scalar-vector workloads.

title: E2ETMPN: An End-to-End Template Matching Prediction Network for Intra Coding
authors: Qijun Wang, Shiyu Xie, Feng Gao
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: Intra coding plays a critical role in video compression, yet conventional directional intra prediction mainly relies on boundary pixels and fails to capture complex textures and long-range spatial correlations. Template Matching Prediction (TMP) alleviates this issue by searching similar templates in reconstructed regions without motion-vector signaling; however, its MSE/MAE-based matching and simple averaging fusion are sensitive to noise and often unreliable for complex content. This paper proposes E2ETMPN, an end-to-end template matching prediction network for intra coding. E2ETMPN jointly models template matching and multi-candidate fusion under a unified loss function. It consists of two components: (1) a CNN-based multiscale matching sub-network that searches candidate predictions from reconstructed regions at different spatial scales; and (2) a Transformer-based fusion sub-network that adaptively fuses multiple candidates with template information using attention mechanisms to generate the final prediction. Experimental results demonstrate that integrating E2ETMPN into the reference encoder achieves consistent BD-rate reductions over conventional TMP and standard intra prediction methods on standard test sequences. Notably, E2ETMPN yields larger gains on screen content and scenes with rich non-local repetitive structures, validating the effectiveness of end-to-end learning for template matching prediction in intra coding.

title: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Audio-Driven Emotional Talking-Head Generation
authors: Yewei Xiao, Wei Zeng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Current audio-driven talking-head generation methods face significant challenges in achieving emotional control, often requiring fine-tuning of the entire model, relying on emotional reference videos, or suffering from insufficient generation quality. To address this, we propose a parameterefficient fine-tuning method for emotional talking-head generation, named Emo-PEFT. Emo-PEFT introduces three innovative lightweight modules: an Emotional Portrait Adapter (EPA) for decoder feature refinement, a Hierarchical Feature Refinement Network (HFR-Net) to enhance robust emotional mapping across different identities, and a Gated Emotional Adaptation Module (GEAM) to balance emotional intensity. By incorporating these novel designs, Emo-PEFT requires training only approximately 7.54% of additional parameters to efficiently adapt a pre-trained emotion-agnostic model to emotional generation tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that, using only emotion labels, Emo-PEFT can generate talking-head videos with superior visual quality, accurate audio-visual synchronization, and high emotional fidelity, outperforming baseline methods.

title: Multi-Source Decision Support System for Cold Stress Management in Dairy Buffalo Farming Using Heterogeneous Data, IoT Sensors, and Milking Robot Information: Development and Validation
authors: Maria Teresa Verde, Francesco Bonavolontà, Annalisa Liccardo, Matteo Santinello, Roberta Matera, Gianluca Neglia
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Cold stress is an often underestimated factor affecting buffalo welfare and productivity, with direct consequences on milk yield and farm sustainability. This paper presents the development and validation of a multi-source decision support system (DSS) designed to detect and mitigate cold stress in Italian Mediterranean buffalo farming. The system integrates data from an automated milking robot and environmental sensors through an edge–cloud architecture, applying specific indices (THI<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">COLD</inf>, WHI, and daily thermal variation) to quantify risk conditions. Results from field validation showed that cold stress events significantly reduced milk production, with yield drops exceeding 40% in a substantial proportion of animals. The DSS proved effective in identifying early risk conditions and enabling timely interventions, demonstrating the potential of IoT-driven monitoring to improve both welfare and economic performance. Future developments will involve large-scale validation across different farms, integration with herd management platforms, and coupling with automated barn infrastructures to create a digital twin for resilient and sustainable livestock management.

title: An Energy-Efficient 197-to-210GHz 4×4 Beam-Steerable Radiator Array with Integrated Microbump Radiation and 1.35% DC-to-RF Efficiency in 65-nm CMOS
authors: Huibo Wu, Xuan Liu, Xingcun Li, Yuqun Liu, Liang Gao, Yue Li, Wenhua Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper presents a 197-to-210GHz 4×4 beam-steerable radiator array in TSMC 65-nm CMOS. In this array, a scalable standing-wave loop is proposed for minor locking errors and robust coupling. Meanwhile, a low-cost compact integrated microbump array is proposed to achieve high-efficiency radiation and beam steering. The array achieves 28.3dBm lensless EIRP, 11.1dBm radiated power, 1.35% DC-to-RF efficiency, and 56° beam steering. Ultimately, millimeter-level terahertz SAR imaging is achieved based on this radiator array.

title: Ship power grid reconstruction optimization algorithm integrating GBNN-CS-ROA strategy
authors: Qirun Hao, Baoming Yang, Jiazheng Sun
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Ships and Electromechanical System (ICISES)
abstract: Aiming at the high-dimensional nonlinear optimization problem in the reconstruction of ship power grids, an improved algorithm (ROGCS) integrating discrete bio-inspired neural Network (GBNN), Cuckoos Algorithm (CS), and Rider Optimization Algorithm (ROA) is proposed. The convergence speed and accuracy are improved through dynamic Levy flight adjustment and multi-population cooperative strategies. Experiments show that ROGCS outperforms the comparison algorithms on four types of benchmark functions and the CEC2014 test set.

title: Real-Time IIoT Cloud-Based Monitoring and Analytics for Extrusion and Cooling Systems
authors: Manuel Cardona, Bryan Vásquez-Pineda, Rubén Torres, Jonatan Barahona
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: This paper presents a cost-effective, real-time Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) monitoring and analytics system designed for cable extrusion machines and cooling systems in a manufacturing environment at Enerwire, a leading Salvadoran cable manufacturing company. The system utilizes existing machine encoders to capture extrusion speed pulses and thermocouples which are processed by a Siemens S7-1200 PLC. Data from five extrusion machines, including temperature measurements, are aggregated and transmitted to an industrial IoT gateway (IOT2050). The gateway forwards the data to a MySQL cloud database hosted on Microsoft Azure, enabling live visualization through Grafana dashboards. The system also incorporates alert mechanisms for production stoppages and abnormal extrusion speeds, providing timely notifications to operators. Future work will focus on applying machine learning techniques to the collected data for predictive analytics and process optimization. This approach offers a scalable and affordable solution for digital transformation in industrial manufacturing.

title: Navigating Cruise Tourism via AI-Based Social Media Analysis: A Study of Taiwanese Consumers’ Travel Decisions
authors: Wen-Yu Chen, Kuo-Chuan Wu, Yu-Chien Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: With the rise of AI-driven analytics and social media influence, understanding consumer behavior is vital for the cruise tourism industry. This study investigates how Taiwanese consumers make travel decisions by analyzing 226 Facebook reviews and 535 Google Maps posts from 2022 to 2024. Using web crawling, text mining, and expert interviews, the research identifies platform-specific focuses: Facebook content centers on cruise brands, itineraries, and onboard services, while Google Maps emphasizes infrastructure, transportation, and port facilities. Six key decision-making factors emerge—itinerary, price, service, facilities, time, and brand awareness. The findings demonstrate how AI-based social media analysis can reveal consumer preferences and support data-informed strategies in cruise tourism development.

title: IEEE Xplore Digital Library
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Network

title: Tomato plants instance segmentation via numerical uniform colour space for drought stress resilience estimation
authors: Massimiliano Nitti, Angelo Cardellicchio, Francesco Cellini, Angelo Petrozza, Stephan Summerer, Filomena Carriero, Vito Renò
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Ensuring crop yields in a world with a changing climate and increasing population is of utmost importance. To achieve this goal, genotypical and phenotypical techniques can be exploited to evaluate whether specific mutations prove to be more resilient to drought stress or extreme climate phenomena. This process can be further improved by providing domain experts with automated analytics tools to assist them in the analysis of a large amount of data, therefore providing more statistical reliability and robustness. To this end, this work proposes an image processing pipeline for helping practitioners in the automatic evaluation of the response of tomato plant mutations to drought stress. The pipeline is based on an efficient implementation of a colour space conversion, aiming at reducing the dependency on illumination of the a and b components of the CIELab colour space and providing statistical uniformity, and anisotropic Gaussian mixture model clustering for improved feature extraction and analysis. The results were evaluated over a total of 388 mutations in three time instants, highlighting the effectiveness of the workflow in supporting domain experts in assessing the most resilient tomato variants.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Actuator Disk and Actuator Line Models for Wind Turbine Aeroacoustics Prediction
authors: Abdessabour Amoura, Mohammed Nadjib Hamlaoui, Abdelhamid Bouhelal, Arezki Smaili, Sofiane. Khelladi
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: Accurate prediction of wind turbine noise is essential for mitigating environmental impacts, improving public acceptance, and guiding the design of quieter turbine systems. This study aims to assess the impact of aerodynamic modeling fidelity on wind turbine noise prediction by comparing the Actuator Disk and Actuator Line approaches. The analysis is conducted on the New MEXICO rotor under uniform inflow conditions using a hybrid aeroacoustic framework. Aerodynamic simulations are performed using Large Eddy Simulation (LES) with the Smagorinsky subgrid scale model, while acoustic predictions are carried out using the Farassa 1A formulation based on the permeable Ffowcs Williams– Hawkings acoustic analogy. Results show that both actuator models reliably predict the overall noise levels. However, the Actuator Line model demonstrates superior accuracy in capturing tonal noise features, particularly the blade passing frequency.

title: An 83-F2 2T PUF Featuring Isolated Hot-Carrier Injection and Lightweight Majority-Voting-Based Strong PUF Mode
authors: Zhenzhe Chen, Kunyang Liu, Weirong Dong, Quan Cheng, Jiamin Li, Longyang Lin, Takashi Sato
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This work presents a high-density two-transistor (2T) physical unclonable function (PUF) featuring isolated hot-carrier injection (HCI) stabilization and a lightweight strong PUF mode. A StrongARM-like sense amplifier is implemented for energy-efficient readout near the subthreshold region. By employing a single-bit-controlled, isolated HCI burn-in, device mismatch is selectively enhanced while avoiding stress on non-targeted transistors, thereby improving stability without cell loss. Furthermore, a compact strong PUF mode resistant to machine-learning attacks is designed through analog majority voting with simultaneous triple-wordline activation, effectively expanding the challenge–response space. Fabricated in 180-nm CMOS, the weak PUF mode achieves an 83 F2/bit density, 17.3 fJ/bit energy at 0.6 V, and 113 Mbps throughput at 0.8 V. A 15-minute HCI burn-in reduces the bit error rate to nearly zero across −20 °C to 100 °C. Both weak and strong modes pass the NIST SP 800-22 and other statistical tests, while the strong mode remains resilient against machine-learning attacks trained on up to 30M CRPs. These results demonstrate the proposed 2T-PUF’s suitability as a hardware root of trust for energy- and area-constrained IoT systems.

title: A Format-Preserving Encryption Method for Securing Geospatial Vector Map Data
authors: Ahmed Abubahia, Adel Rahuma, Hisham AbouGrad, Salem Alsaid, Mohamed Alrayes
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: In recent years, advances in Geographic Information System (GIS) tools and applications have significantly enhanced the usability of spatial data. GIS has become a vital resource for mapping and visualising spatial information. Additionally, increased research in the GIS field has resulted in a wide availability of digital spatial data, which influences various reallife applications. The high value of spatial vector map data entails risks of unauthorised access, modification, or distribution. Therefore, it is crucial to maintain the confidentiality of this data and protect it from unauthorised access. To address this challenge, numerous research efforts have explored cryptographic methods as a promising solution. Encryption methods can be broadly classified into two main categories: frequency domain implementation and spatial domain implementation. Spatial domain implementation is faster and less computationally intensive compared to frequency domain encryption. However, many proposed methods fail to preserve the data format and maintain the accuracy of spatial coordinates. Additionally, most existing methods do not consider computational time performance, which extends their applicability to many real-time applications, such as drone surveillance systems. In this paper, we propose a spatial domain polygon-based encryption approach that encrypts spatial vector data while preserving the data format and improving computational time performance efficiently. The experimental results demonstrate the efficiency and performance of the proposed encryption approach compared to existing methods.

title: CyberShield V2.0: An Enhanced Hybrid Detection Framework with Autoencoder-Based Anomaly Detection and Temporal Intelligence
authors: Vigneshwaran J, Tharun C, Swaminathan K
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Based on the continuously increasing complexity of cyber threats, traditional signature-based intrusion detection systems and static machine learning-based models become ineffective and obsolete in current network settings. Despite the improvements brought by hybrid machine learning models that enhance the detection capability for known attack samples, they struggle with zero-day attack detection, temporal attack patterns, and explainability. In this article, we introduce the enhanced version of the hybrid attack detection system, namely CyberShield v2.0, an improved version of the existing system that introduces additional components such as anomaly detection based on the use of an autoencoder, temporal knowledge, the use of a meta-learner for combining models, and SHAP-based explainability. Indeed, the system utilizes an autoencoder for learning normal traffic in the network for detecting unknown attacks, while temporal aggregation of traffic parameters helps in identifying effective characterization of burst attack, port scan, and anomalous peaks. Moreover, this system utilizes an online meta-learner that combines the outputs of several supervised and unsupervised models, thus enhancing robustness and detection capability. Additionally, this system applies SHAP-based explainability for providing transparency into the decision made by the system based on specific attributes.

title: Feature Extraction and Refined Modeling of Complex Roadway Environments Based on Visual Images
authors: Jiuhong Wang, Yaomeng Xiao, Canguang Zheng, Yang Jiang, Mingchao Du, Kun Zhang, Tongzhu Yu, Dainxiang Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The spatial environment of coal mine roadways is complex. To restore the true appearance of underground roadways, this paper proposes a method for extracting the features of complex roadway environments and conducting refined modeling based on visual images. This paper pairs a working face intelligent lighting control system based on wireless Mesh networks, uses a tracked transfer robot equipped with an Intel D455 depth camera to collect three-dimensional data, and synchronously captures depth images and color images of the simulated roadway through infrared structured light and RGB cameras to record the geometric information and texture features of the roadway surface. It then performs denoising on the original depth images to eliminate random noise and outliers. Subsequently, the SIFT feature detection algorithm is used to extract image feature points and generate the initial point cloud of the roadway. Finally, three-dimensional contour reconstruction experiments are conducted on simulated rectangular and arched roadways, which can fully reflect the surface features of complex roadways and achieve refined modeling.

title: Design and Implementation of an Automatic Pressing Test System for Remote Controllers Based on Machine Vision and Mqtt
authors: Haoran Guo, Shangji Nong, Changlian Chen, Ge Zhu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This paper presents a movable intelligent automatic pressing test system for remote controls, based on a three-layer architecture of Raspberry Pi-based image recognition, cloud data interaction, and ESP32-controlled actuation. The system achieves high-precision key positioning via machine vision and MQTTbased communication, enabling remote and unmanned testing. Experimental results show that the system significantly improves testing efficiency and accuracy compared to manual and traditional methods.

title: Large-Scale MEMS Micro-Hotplates: A Fabrication and Characterization Approach Based on Eight-Cantilever Beam Structure
authors: Feixiang Cheng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive study on the fabrication and performance characterization of large-scale MEMS micro-hotplates featuring an eight-cantilever beam structure, designed for enhanced mechanical stability and uniform thermal distribution in gas sensing applications. The proposed design addresses the limitations of traditional micro-hotplates, such as small working areas and mechanical instability during extended operation, by incorporating a suspended platform with dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$300 \mu \mathrm{m} \times 300 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>, spiral heating electrodes, and circular interdigital electrodes. Fabrication processes include LPCVD and PECVD for layer deposition, photolithography for patterning, and wet etching with KOH and TMAH for structure release. Performance evaluation encompasses voltagetemperature characteristics, power consumption, temperature uniformity, stress, and displacement analyses across multiple devices and configurations. Results demonstrate consistent temperatures around 301° C at 3.5 V with power consumption of approximately 56 mW, high uniformity exceeding 98%, robust mechanical properties, and long-term reliability. Comparative analyses with state-of-the-art configurations highlight improvements in efficiency and stability, achieving up to 15% better performance relative to baselines. This work provides a reliable technical pathway for scalable production of high-performance MEMS gas sensors.

title: Fault Identification and Prediction in a Wind Turbine using Digital Twins
authors: S. Anbumalar, Parthiban B, M. Thogesh, S. Hemachandasanam, R. Iyyappan, S. Madhan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The paper describes an end-to-end system which integrates physical simulation and machine learning to predict inevitabilities within wind turbines before they happen. A Python based simulation platform represents the entire turbine system aerodynamics, mechanical subsystems and electrical elements creating synthetic operational data that encompasses normal operating conditions and several fault scenarios. This is the direct solution to one of the major wind turbine fault prediction challenges: the lack of actual fault data. By generating high-volume, balanced, and labelled training data, the framework facilitates building of quality machine learning models that would otherwise prove extremely hard to develop on actual turbine operations. A framework classifier trained on this synthetic dataset achieves high accuracy in the classification of various types of faults. The framework demonstrates a total end-to-end pipeline physics driven data generation verified through successful machine learning deployment compared to actual operational data of an NREL 5-MW reference wind turbine. The Random Forest model offers interpretable fault predictions and extensive feature-importance extraction, enabling operators to discover which sensor variables are most indicative of certain fault types, such as bearing deterioration, gearbox failures, generator failures, and structural blade damage.

title: Real-Time on-Site Grid Fault Detection and Automated Load Management Using Arduino with GSM Alert for Voltage and Frequency Thershold Monitoring
authors: V. Muneeswaran, S. Venkata Jeevan, G. Yashwanth Goud, U. Gowtham
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a system that spots grid failures based on frequency and voltage thresholds. It uses an Arduino microcontroller, for use in buildings and renewable energy setups. The regular SCADA or EMS monitoring systems depend on central control. They take forever to catch faults and cost a ton for all that infrastructure. But this new way does real-time checking right on site. It handles loads automatically and shoots off instant alerts via GSM. Sensors for current and voltage keep tabs on power levels all the time. The Arduino runs the show, using whatever thresholds set for things like too much voltage, not enough, or weird frequency shifts. When something goes wrong, it flips relays to cut loads, sets off buzzers and lights for alarms, and texts operators through SMS. We tested it out in experiments. It caught faults super quick, cut down on downtime, and made everything run more reliably than those old central setups. Overall, this cheap, easy-to-scale, spread-out method boosts how tough the grid is against problems. It fits great for smart buildings and mixed renewable systems with wind or solar hooked in.

title: A 12–25GHz Ring-PLL Clock Generator Achieving 98fs Jitter and <–52dBc Spurs Using a 22fs Quadrupler and Sub-Sampling Error Extraction
authors: Mohamed Badr Younis, Mahmoud A. Khalil, Mohamed O. Selim, Mohamed S. Aly, Ruhao Xia, Ahmed E. Abdelrahman, Yongxin Li, Ahmed E. Hegazi, Pavan Kumar Hanumolu
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This work presents a wideband, low-jitter ring-oscillator-based clock generator that combines a PLL with an edge-combining quadrupler. A novel sub-sampling error extraction method with a three-path LMS adaptation allows low-frequency, high-resolution correction of edge-combining errors. Fabricated in 16 nm FinFET, the prototype outputs 12–25 GHz clocks with worst-case spurs below –50 dBc across the entire band. At 25 GHz, it achieves 97.9 fs of integrated jitter with only 22 fs of quadrupler additive jitter while consuming 39.8 mW.

title: Mixed Reality Visualization of Abdominal Anatomy from CT Images Based on Bidirectional Optical Flow Interpolation
authors: Wei Zhang, Bo Yang, Youlong Lyu, Xing Zhou
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Mixed reality (MR)-based 3D visualization of abdominal anatomy is widely used in preoperative planning to provide intuitive spatial perception. High-quality and geometrically consistent 3D reconstruction from abdominal CT data is essential for achieving accurate spatial representation in MR environments. However, abdominal CT data often contain large inter-slice spacing, which leads to stair-step artifacts in 3D reconstruction. Existing interpolation methods are susceptible to errors with large inter-slice spacing, often resulting in blurred boundaries and structural discontinuities. To address this issue, an MR visualization method for abdominal anatomy based on bidirectional optical flow interpolation is proposed. The method first generates intermediate slices using optical flow estimation to compensate for sparse sampling, and then reconstructs the surface using an MC algorithm to obtain a high-quality triangular mesh. Experimental results show that the quality of the inter-layer slices generated by this method can meet the requirements for three-dimensional reconstruction of abdominal tissues. The reconstructed model can be visualized in a mixed reality environment, providing intuitive spatial perception of abdominal anatomy.

title: Design and Application of an Automatic Generation System for Primary Network Loss Reports
authors: Shi Liu, Jia Sun, Haitao Cui, Zehui Wang, Liang Zhao, Li Tian
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This paper introduces a system that uses Robotic Process Automation (RPA) programs to automatically generate and verify primary network loss reports for provincial power grid dispatching, along with the design ideas, system architecture, and application effects of the relevant RPA programs. RPA tools are used to write scripts that automatically capture and format data from the electric energy acquisition system, and obtain supplementary data through automatic We-chat conversations with power plant data managers. The formatted collected data is used to instantiate a copy of the primary network loss report template to realize the automatic generation of the initial report draft. The node and branch balance rates are used to automatically identify missing data in the initial draft, and the data obtained via We-chat is used to supplement the missing data in the initial draft, thereby achieving the automatic generation and verification of the primary network loss report.

title: Inductive Sensor for Real-Time, Autonomous and Non-Destructive Fruit Size and Growth Monitoring
authors: Lorenzo Mistral Peppi, Alessandro Gallo, Luigi Manfrini, Luca De Marchi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: This work presents preliminary studies of an innovative and low-cost sensing solution for real-time fruit growth measurement directly on the tree, overcoming traditional mechanical and electrical issues. Implementability of high resolution inductive angular sensing technique on a fruit caliper is evaluated while suitability of the same FR4 substrate as both PCB and structural material for the pliers is analysed. A 71 μm resolution over a 120 mm is achieved and a maximum diameter absolute error estimation lower than 247 μm is obtained exploiting a calibration procedure. Finally, a thermal compensation procedure which takes into account material expansion is proposed.

title: Low-Cost Plant Height Estimation: a Comparison of Image Processing and Deep Learning Methods
authors: Cristian Bua, Lorenzo Bianchi, Stefano Giordano
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: This work presents and compares several methods for automatic plant Height Estimation in a controlled agricultural environment. The proposed approaches involve the detection and bounding of the plant using either image processing based on color segmentation or deep learning method based on the YOLO family architectures. Once the plant is framed, the pixel-to-centimeter conversion is performed using either a geometric model or a corrected formulation that accounts for camera distortions and tilt angle. Experiments were conducted with both horizontal and inclined camera setups, varying the distance and height of the device relative to the plant. The best results were achieved using the neural network combined with the direct conversion method, particularly when the camera was positioned at 3.5 times the plant height and inclined at approximately 25 degrees. This configuration yielded a Mean Absolute Percentage Error as low as 1.61% and a Mean Absolute Error of 1.01 cm. The proposed system shows potential for replacing manual measurements in precision agriculture, enabling real-time monitoring of plant development and optimizing resource management.

title: Intra-Leaf Hyperspectral Analysis Method for Reflectance Variability Identification
authors: Elisa Cioccolo, Pierluigi Rossi, Giuseppe Colla, Mariateresa Cardarelli
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Characterizations of leaf properties through hyperspectral imaging relies on signatures on specific bands that indicate heterogeneities in certain spots of parts of the leaves. These characteristics can also be affected by presence of pathogens, fertilizations practices and plant stress, that all present traits that can be invisible to the eyes but that can be detected in spectral data analyses. In order to detect such anomalies in specific leaf areas, authors developed a region-of-interest (ROI)–aware pipeline that processes hyperspectral information in partitions that belong to the border of the leaf, core or whole leaf area. A SPECIM IQ hyperspectral sensor was used to gather data in 204 bands over the range of 400-1000 nm on 8 samples of zucchini leaves (Cucurbita pepo L., Tevere hybrid, La Semiorto Sementi srl, Sarno, Italy), extracting a set of indices that are sensitive to pigments or leaf structure. The resulting 24 ROIs of at least 1.600 pixels each displayed different spectral behaviors: results showed that, compared with whole-leaf data, leaf marginal and core ROIs reduced band-wise standard deviation by up to 35%; secondly, spectral analyses revealed wavelength-dependent shifts in green, red-edge and near infra-red (NIR) domains as ROI changed; lastly, a correlation analysis showed that several hyperspectral indices differed significantly between leaf blade and leaf midrib (p < 0.05), where spectral indices of leaf marginal ROIs reported a strong negative correlations with chlorophyll a (r = –0.79) and carotenoid (r = –0.74), plus a positive correlation between nitrogen and VIS-integral or Absolute Derivative Sum indices (r = 0.77). These findings demonstrate that intra-leaf spectral analyses can improve the interpretability of hyperspectral information, also providing a form of in-sample data augmentation that can increase trait prediction under variable plant growing conditions.

title: Reinforcement Learning-based Optimization for Cold Chain Electric Vehicle Routing
authors: Haoran Yang, Wanli Yi, Renqian Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address the range limitations and hard time window constraints in cold chain electric vehicle delivery, this article proposes EGT-TA, an end-to-end deep reinforcement learning framework designed to minimize comprehensive energy consumption costs while satisfying complex constraints. The model utilizes an Edge-augmented Graph Transformer (EGT) encoder to deeply integrate node and topological features. Furthermore, a dynamic urgency-biased mechanism is incorporated into the decoder to dynamically adjust node selection probabilities by perceiving remaining time windows, significantly enhancing the model's control over timeliness. Additionally, a constrained POMO training algorithm based on Lagrangian relaxation is introduced to guide the agent in adaptively balancing solution feasibility and optimality during exploration. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms Genetic Algorithms and classic Attention Models in solution quality across instances of varying scales.

title: Automatic Detection and Localization of Decapped Chips in Single-Event Effects Testing
authors: Shuyun Wen, Gang Guo, Jiancheng Liu, Yanwen Zhang, Yongle Zhao, Peng Su
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of electronic devices across numerous fields, the importance of radiation testing has become increasingly prominent. However, traditional methods for locating decapsulated chips primarily rely on manual operation, which suffer from significant issues such as low speed and limited accuracy. To address this, this paper proposes an automatic identification and localization algorithm for decapsulated chips based on multi-scale enhancement. The system consists of three core modules: image preprocessing, decapsulated chip detection, and coordinate transformation, enabling efficient automatic identification and precise localization of decapsulated chips. During training, a hybrid dataset comprising 3,786 images-formed by combining public and self-compiled datasets-was utilized. Compared to the baseline algorithm, the proposed method achieves a 2.4% improvement in Precision and a 2.1% improvement in Recall, with a particularly notable 43% increase in the recall rate for small objects. It also demonstrates certain performance advantages over other algorithms and traditional manual methods. This approach can be widely applied in various radiation testing scenarios, significantly reducing the preparation time required for localization before irradiation, thereby improving equipment utilization efficiency and providing efficient and accurate technical support for the radiation testing of electronic devices.

title: A Flexible Defect Demodulation Method Based on FPGA for High Speed Mixing-Frequency Electromagnetic Railway Inspection
authors: Jiwei Huo
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Mixing-frequency electromagnetic rail inspection has the characteristics of non-contact, fast, and no need for coupling agents, and is suitable for high-speed online rail inspection scenarios. But, this method need demodulate amplitude information of each elected frequency component from detection signal and requires a large amount of computation. To reduce computational complexity, a FPGA based flexible algorithm for rail defect demodulation is presented in this paper. The feasibility of apply this method to mixing-frequency electromagnetic railway inspection is proved through theoretical analysis and the implementation method based on FPGA is given. An experimental system is built and the effectiveness of apply the algorithm for rail defect inspection under high-speed condition is verified through experiment.

title: ACF-MATD3 Formation Control for Multi-Agent Wheeled Mobile Robot Systems
authors: Zhanfeng Hu, Junnian Gou
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Multi-agent wheeled mobile robot systems have been widely applied in tasks such as cooperative operations, environmental monitoring, and formation transportation. As such, formation control becomes an important topic to study for the area of unmanned systems. However, conventional formation control is based upon graph theory and consensus theory, requiring accurate modeling and complex mathematical analysis that restricts its flexibility in handling dynamic and high-dimensional settings. In this paper, the formation control problem is formulated as a multiagent deep reinforcement learning (MADRL) problem, and an Action Consistency-Formation Multi-Agent Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (ACF-MATD3) algorithm is developed to address formation control in an obstacle-free environment. Based on a leader-follower scheme, the algorithm introduces the action consistency loss and the formation maintenance loss to improve the coordination between agents as well as the robustness of their formations. In the square formation task, compared to the original MATD3 algorithm, the proposed approach reduces the mean squared error (MSE) by approximately 28.9 % and the standard deviation by 81.6 %. In the formation transformation task, a multi-branch input fusion architecture achieves an overall 47.4 % reduction in collision rate with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 2. 4 \%}$</tex> drop in global MSE. Additionally, when combined with Adaptive B-Spline Network (ABSN), the collision rate drops to zero while the global MSE decreases by another 20.7 %. Simulation and real-world experimental results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm outperforms existing deep reinforcement learning methods in terms of accuracy, stability, and robustness, indicating strong potential for practical engineering applications.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)

title: Electric Vehicle Cluster Charging Load Forecasting Based on Improved Q-Learning Prediction Algorithm
authors: Gubo Zhang, Jianyi Yang, Dingchao Qiu, Ziying Wang, Kuan Fu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The rapid increase in the number of electric vehicles has introduced new challenges for power grids in managing load fluctuations. To address this, this paper proposes a machine learning-based method for predicting the charging load of electric vehicle clusters. First, a comprehensive charging model covering disordered, scheduled, and intelligent charging scenarios is established. Second, an improved Q-learning algorithm is designed based on recurrent neural networks (RNN) and artificial neural networks (ANN), achieving precise charging load prediction through the construction of a targeted reward function. Finally, comparative experiments are conducted under three charging scenarios, demonstrating that the proposed improved Q-learning algorithm significantly outperforms both standalone ANN and RNN methods in prediction accuracy, with performance improvements reaching up to 36%, while also exhibiting strong adaptability to diverse charging scenarios.

title: 2025 18th International Conference on Sensing Technology (ICST)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Two-Stage Progressive Anomaly Segmentation Framework for Complete Industrial Surface Defect Detection
authors: Shaojiang Yuan, Chenglizhao Chen, Shiyu Hu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: In industrial visual inspection, existing anomaly segmentation frameworks often suffer from incomplete defect detection, where large or spatially separated defect regions cannot be fully segmented as a whole. This limitation mainly stems from the widespread use of a single-stage anomaly segmentation pipeline, which directly converts anomaly scores into segmentation masks based on a predefined threshold. As a result, some anomaly regions may be partially missed or fragmented, hindering downstream processes such as automated defect cutting or manual removal that rely on complete and precise segmentation. To address this issue, we propose a two-stage progressive anomaly segmentation framework that refines coarse predictions through superpixel-guided anomaly enhancement and progressive anomaly fusion. In the first stage, a coarse anomaly heatmap is generated using an existing anomaly segmentation model. This heatmap, along with the original image, is subjected to a superpixel extraction process to obtain localized and semantically consistent regions. In the second stage, we apply smoothing filters to the non-anomalous regions to suppress normal texture patterns and thereby highlight potential anomalies. The amplified anomaly image is then fused with the coarse segmentation result to produce a progressively enhanced and more complete segmentation result. Extensive experiments on industrial anomaly benchmarks demonstrate that our framework significantly improves segmentation completeness, especially in cases with large, fragmented, or low-contrast defect regions.

title: Proposed Smartwatch for Early Detection of Reptile Bites During Sleep: VenomAlert
authors: Khalid Aznag, Khayya El Kharrachi, Ahmed El Oirrak
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: In rural and desert areas, scorpion and snake bites are a real threat, particularly while you are asleep. For example, in Morocco, epidemiological data from the Moroccan Center for Poison Control and Pharmacovigilance suggests that the number of cases has increased from 1999 and 2024, an average of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 5, 0 0 0}$</tex> scorpion stings and more than 250 snakebites were recorded annually, with mortality rates particularly affecting children under the age of fifteen. In this innovation, we propose a smart device worn on the wrist before bedtime. It is equipped with biological and physical sensors that can detect sudden changes in skin temperature, increased heart rate or venom secretion. It then triggers an audible and vibrating alarm to immediately wake the sleeper. The device relies on an integrated algorithm to distinguish between normal changes and serious conditions, aiming to reduce mortality rates and delayed treatment.

title: Optimizing Crop Selection Based on Soil and Climatic Compatibility
authors: Ahmad Alshobaki, Belal Zaqaibeh
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Identifying crop types that are optimally suited to specific soil and climatic conditions remains a critical challenge in contemporary agricultural science. Recent advancements in Artificial Intelligence (AI) have significantly enhanced agricultural practices by promoting greater efficiency, sustainability, and productivity. A novel hybrid AI framework which is Identifying Crop Types compatible with Soil and Climate (ICTSC) is proposed. The ICTSC model employs a quantitative applied research methodology, integrating two robust machine learning algorithms: Random Forest, utilized for crop classification based on soil composition and nutrient profiles, and XGBoost, applied for forecasting climatic trends using historical weather data. The proposed model demonstrated a high predictive accuracy of 97.13%, underscoring its potential to deliver precise agronomic recommendations and improve the reliability of agricultural forecasting systems.
