title: Remote Sensing and Graph Neural Network Reveal Past and Future Glacier Changes in Buha River Basin (1966–2100)
authors: Xinde Chu, Shoukat Ali Shah, Hanxiao Yuan, Yaxuan Jin, Changhong Hu, Songtao Ai
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The Buha River is the primary tributary to Qinghai Lake, the largest inland lake in China, and its basin glaciers are critical to regional hydrological stability. However, their dynamic evolution and future trends have not been adequately quantified. This study quantifies glacier changes in Buha River Basin from 1966-2022 using remote sensing data and introduces Cryosphere Graph Temporal Model (CryoGTM), a novel hybrid model integrating Graph Neural Networks and Long Short-Term Memory, to project future evolution under four CMIP6 scenarios. Results show that CryoGTM achieved high accuracy in historical reconstruction, with an absolute error of 1.05 km² and a relative error of 9.30%. Between 1966 and 2022, glacier fragmentation increased the total count from 22 to 26, while the total glacier area shrank from 15.39 ± 0.94 km² to 7.34 ± 0.56 km². Similarly, ice volume declined at a rate of -0.0082 ± 0.0019 km³ a⁻¹, with 0.27 ± 0.05 km³ remaining by 2022. Projections under the four CMIP6 scenarios suggest that by 2100, the glacier area will decrease by an average of 60.01% compared to 2022. These findings provide valuable insights for water resource management and glacier change forecasting in the Buha River Basin.

title: Adaptive Graph Learning for Joint Optimization of Speed and Quality in Medical Supply Chains
authors: Zhu Wei, Zhao Jing, Chen Guanghui
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Balancing rapid delivery with strict quality compliance is a critical challenge in healthcare logistics. To address this, we propose an Adaptive Graph Learning framework centered on the dynamic joint optimization of speed and quality. Unlike traditional static models, our approach models the medical supply chain as an evolving, multi-relational graph. By ingesting heterogeneous operational data—such as delivery velocity and temperature volatility—into a unified representation, we introduce a Dynamic Multi-Modal Weighting mechanism. This mechanism uses reinforcement learning to automatically recalibrate the trade-off between temporal urgency and condition stability in response to real-time network disruptions. Furthermore, we employ graph attention networks to identify functionally robust supply communities, enabling targeted, facility-level optimizations. Extensive empirical evaluations on large-scale healthcare datasets validate our framework's efficacy. Notably, a real-world case study demonstrates a 37.8% reduction in emergency response time and a 68.3% reduction in quality defect rates (as detailed in Table 5). These results confirm that our adaptive approach significantly enhances supply chain resilience, providing a scalable, intelligent foundation for modern medical logistics.

title: Iterative Joint Target and Covert Data Detection Algorithm for FD-ISAC
authors: Cuong Huy Huynh, Nam Quoc Le, Hung Nguyen Manh, Lap Luat Nguyen, Le-Nam Tran, Hien Q. Ta
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: We investigate a full-duplex integrated sensing and communication system (FD-ISAC) where a base station (BS) simultaneously detects moving targets and receives covert uplink data. In such a system, self-interference (SI) from co-located antennas and reflections from static and slow-moving objects can severely degrade both sensing and communication performance. To address this critical issue, we propose an iterative frequency-domain algorithm for joint target and data detection. In each iteration, the estimated target echoes and covert communication signals are removed to obtain an improved estimate of the Doppler deviation, which is used to suppress SI via a smoothing technique. With SI significantly reduced, a simple radar processing method, such as the two-dimensional (2D) periodogram, can be effectively applied to the residual signal for target detection. In the final step, data detection is performed after the effects of SI and target echoes have been largely mitigated. Numerical results show that the proposed approach outperforms the best-known solution, particularly in the presence of slow-moving interference. Notably, it achieves near-optimal sensing and communication performance with only two iterations and a small number of pilot symbols, requiring only a 0.5 dB margin, compared to the ideal communication-only case. Under the low-power regime of covert transmissions, a single pilot symbol is sufficient to achieve near-optimal sensing and communication performance while maintaining the best covertness performance. These results highlight the practical potential of the proposed algorithm for FD-ISAC implementations with and without covertness considerations.

title: Energy Analysis of an AQFP Logic SHA-256 Hash
authors: Jaron Weyermüller, Johannes A. Delport, Kyle Jackman, Coenrad J. Fourie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: The calculation of cryptographic hash functions is of significant importance for data integrity, cryptography and applications built on blockchain technology. Bitcoin, as the first and most prominent cryptocurrency, makes use of the cryptographic SHA-256 hash algorithm. The calculation of the SHA-256 hash at scale is energy intensive, and Bitcoin mining currently accounts for a significant fraction of global energy use. In this paper, we present the first known implementation of a full SHA-256 accelerator with ultra low-power Adiabatic Quantum Flux Parametron (AQFP) superconducting digital logic circuits, complete with a comprehensive simulated energy analysis. The analysis leverages the latest superconductor circuit simulation tools, and the results project significant power efficiency gains over traditional CMOS accelerators. We show an estimated efficiency of 548.35 THash/J, 173.08 THash/J and 56.32 THash/J for 2 GHz, 5 GHz and 10 GHz system clocks respectively at the chip level, before any architectural optimization. We conclude that even after accounting for cooling power, an AQFP SHA-256 accelerator will outperform the best semiconductor Bitcoin miner.

title: Lightweight Forensic Triage Framework for Clandestine Operational Instructions: A Two-Case Leave-One-Case-Out Evaluation
authors: Sanghyeob Ko, Gibum Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Digital forensic casework rarely proceeds under laboratory conditions. Under time and resource constraints, analysts often triage thousands of seized text files, with critical documents embedded in large volumes of irrelevant material. This study presents a lightweight triage approach for prioritizing clandestine operational instruction (COI) documents in CPU-only deployment environments. The method combines TF-IDF features, a LinearSVC classifier, a tunable decision threshold, and SHAP-based analyst-facing rationale. Cross-case transfer was evaluated with a leave-one-case-out (LOCO) protocol using 133 court-adjudicated COI documents from two independent investigations and 500 synthetic negatives constructed as a structured stress-test contrast set. Across the two folds, the model achieved a mean accuracy of 99.29% and a mean F1 of 98.28%, with 5 false negatives in total (fold-wise FNR: 2.27% and 4.49%; pooled FNR: 3.76%). End-to-end screening required 0.32 ms per document, with a 1.34 MB model footprint on a single-threaded CPU. Comparisons with transformer-based and lightweight baselines showed that this configuration provided a favorable balance of accuracy, latency, model size, and feature-level auditability under CPU-constrained conditions. A proxy false-positive evaluation on 9,606 Korean Wikipedia paragraphs from KorQuAD v1.0 yielded a false-positive rate of 0.31% (95% CI: 0.21%–0.45%). Work Saved over Sampling reached 79%. The system is intended as a screening aid within a tiered forensic workflow. SHAP outputs are designed to support analyst review rather than serve as standalone evidentiary instruments. The findings are limited to the evaluated Korean-language counterintelligence cases and have not been validated across other languages, crime types, or jurisdictions.

title: Dynamic Modeling, Selection, and Placement of Mitigation Devices for HEMP/GMD Events
authors: Timothy J. Donnelly, Connor A. Lehman, David G. Wilson
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: A high-altitude electromagnetic pulse (HEMP) or solar-geomagnetic disturbance (GMD) can drive low-frequency geomagnetically induced currents (GICs) that force large power transformers into saturation, distorting AC waveforms, increasing losses, and risking thermal damage. This paper develops dynamic models of large synthetic grids that capture the transient response to simulated HEMP insults, then applies multi-objective optimization to quantify the trade-off between mitigation-device type, placement, and resulting transformer saturation. The study shows that flux-blocking devices provide the steepest reduction in relative saturation for a given device count, and that incorporating cost weights enables analysis of trade-offs between competing mitigation technologies and deployment strategies.

title: Techno-Economic Optimization of a Grid-Tied PV System with Virtual Storage for a 1 GW Tier IV Data Center in NEOM, KSA
authors: Ali Awad Nasser, Mehrdad Ehsani
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a techno-economic optimization of a grid-tied photovoltaic (PV) system for a 1 GW Tier IV data center in NEOM, Saudi Arabia, targeting 50% renewable energy for Vision 2030. Using actual NEOM meteorological data and an NSGA-II optimization, the optimal <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2,050 \text{MW}$</tex> PV system achieves a 50.0 % renewable fraction, a 14.8 % LCOE reduction, and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$35.0 \% \text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions decrease compared to grid-only operation. The system maintains Tier IV reliability via dual grid feeds without battery storage, achieving a PUE of 1.269. Under SEC tariffs and a net-billing framework, annual exports of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,071 \text{GWh}$</tex> yield $38.6 million in revenue. Results confirm that grid-tied PV with net billing is a cost-effective, reliable, and sustainable pathway for hyperscale data centers in solar-rich regions, even when accounting for significant grid interconnection infrastructure costs.

title: Learning-to-Warmstart: When AI Meets Linear Optimization to Exactly Solve Security-Constrained Economic Dispatch in Real-Time
authors: Syreen Banabilah, Malek Alduhaymi, Kamal Basulaiman, Fahad Aljoedi, Faisal Albeladi, Masoud Barati
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: Security-Constrained Economic Dispatch (SCED) is solved every few minutes in modern power systems and is routinely formulated as a large linear program (LP). Even with state-of-the-art interior-point methods (IPMs), repeatedly solving thousands of SCED instances per day remains a computational bottleneck, and performance is still sensitive to the choice of starting point. This paper investigates whether graph neural networks (GNNs) can learn effective warmstarts for SCED without changing its mathematical formulation or compromising solution quality. We encode each SCED instance as a weighted bipartite graph of variables and constraints and train a Bipartite GNN to predict primal warmstart points for a commercial LP solver. Using IEEE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 7}$</tex>-bus, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 1 8}$</tex>-bus, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 0}$</tex>-bus systems with synthetic but realistic load and reserve profiles, our IPM-warmstart yields consistent runtime reductions, with average time ratios between 0.90 and 0.92 and barrier-iteration ratios between 0.79 and 0.94 relative to the default initialization. The learned warmstarts are close to optimal (average cost gap below 6% and small normalized constraint violations), while the IPM always recovers the exact optimal SCED solution. These results demonstrate that AI-guided warmstarting is a practical and generalizable tool for accelerating real-time economic dispatch.

title: Assessing Impact of PV Forecasting Accuracy and Data Redundancy on Kalman Filter-Enhanced Economic Dispatch: A Comparative Study
authors: Ahmed M. Abouassy, Mahdi Mehrtash, Sami Fadali, Hanif Livani
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: Renewable energy resources (RESs) are essential components of modern power systems, offering substantial benefits in reducing carbon emissions and improving system reliability if their generation is forecasted accurately. However, their performance is highly dependent on the accuracy of ambient condition measurements and forecasts, which are often corrupted by noise. Such inaccuracies degrade system performance and hinder the optimal energy allocation within economic dispatch (ED) frameworks. To address this challenge, this work investigates the use of the Kalman Filter (KF) as an effective state estimation technique for improving the quality of irradiance and temperature measurements in the presence of process and measurement noise. A comparative analysis is conducted among three forecasting approaches-KF using individual sensor data, KF using averaged multi-sensor data, and direct averaging without KF -and benchmarked against the persistent forecast (PSS) model. Using noise-free measurements as the reference, the results demonstrate that the KF significantly enhances measurement accuracy and short-term prediction quality. Furthermore, integrating the improved forecasts into a 15 -minute-ahead ED formulation shows notable operation cost reduction, reduced energy deviations, and lower reserve requirements. These findings highlight the importance of robust estimation techniques in enabling reliable and economical operation of RES-integrated power systems.

title: High-Performance MPPT for Photovoltaic Systems Using Bald Eagle Search-Based Solar Cell Parameter Estimation
authors: Hassan Shokouhandeh, Hossein Gholipour Godarzi, Reza Hassankhani, Mahan Jafarian, Kamran Yousefpour, Mahmood Hosseini Imani
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: In this paper, a novel method based on an estimated solar cell model is proposed for maximum power point tracking (MPPT) of a photovoltaic (PV) system. In the proposed approach, the double diodes solar cell model is first estimated using experimental data, with the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) criterion serving as the objective function, by an improved version of the Bald Eagle Search (BES) algorithm. Then, the estimated model is employed to track the maximum power point (MPP) of the PV system under various operating conditions. Simulation results in MATLAB demonstrate that the proposed MPPT achieves faster tracking and higher power extraction from the PV system but also exhibits lower ripple compared to Perturb and Observe (P&O) method. Moreover, the optimal PV cell parameter estimation results indicate that the proposed BES algorithm achieves better performance in estimating the PV cell parameters compared to other algorithms.

title: Global Optimization of AC Transmission Expansion Planning with Series and Shunt Reactive Power Compensation
authors: Etornam Komla Ahorsu, Mahdi Mehrtash
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a method to find the globally optimal solution for AC transmission expansion planning (ACTEP) that jointly optimizes transmission line investments, controllable series (e.g., thyristor-controlled series capacitor), and shunt reactive power compensators. A convex reformulation of the nonconvex power flow equations induced by series compensators is presented. McCormick envelopes are employed to convexify the bilinear interactions between series compensation and voltage-dependent variables in the relaxed lower-bound model. This approach eliminates linearization approximations and metaheuristic dependencies, enabling convex optimization. A hierarchical solution algorithm combines a lower-bound problem, upper-bound problems, and a no-good cut technique to obtain solutions with a guaranteed optimality gap. Validation on a modified 46-bus Southern Brazilian test system demonstrates that joint series and shunt compensation can significantly reduce investment costs, generation costs, and power losses relative to line-only expansion, while achieving global optimality with tractable computation.

title: ECA-ResNet: An Enhanced Channel-Spatial Attention Residual Network for Facial Emotion Recognition in Mental Health Screening
authors: Piyush Gupta, Surendra Kumar Shukla, Bhavya Yadav, Rohit Bhatia, Hitesh Reddy Dereddy, Rakesh Chandra Joshi
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Due to their rising prevalence, early detection of mental health disorders has become a global challenge in modern healthcare. In order to address this problem, this study builds a facial emotion recognition system that can be used as a supplementary tool for evaluating mental health conditions. The proposed framework employs the ECA-ResNet, Enhanced Channel-Spatial Attention Residual Network with a Cosine Annealing Learning Rate Scheduler and Mixed Precision training for efficient optimization and stable convergence. In order to address this problem, this study builds a facial emotion recognition system that can be used as a supplementary tool for evaluating mental health conditions. The effectiveness of the proposed model was assessed on OAHEGA Emotion Recognition Dataset, which consists of facial images depicting six emotional states-Angry, Surprise, Sad, Ahegao, Happy and Neutral. The results of the experiment demonstrate strong recognition in all emotional classes with an overall accuracy of 81%. The attention mechanism in ECA-ResNet enhances the focus of the model on emotionally salient facial regions such as eyes and mouth while the residual learning blocks facilitate efficient feature reuse and mitigate vanishing gradients. Mixed Precision training reduces computational cost without affecting accuracy and the Cosine Annealing Scheduler helps to improve generalization and facilitate smoother convergence. These findings indicate that the proposed architecture provides a reliable foundation for mental health detection support systems through facial emotion analysis. The method represents a promising step toward multi-modal AI-driven chatbots capable of early emotional screening and digital mental health support. It can contribute to more accessible, efficient and technologyassisted psychological care.

title: ZRAM Cell Design and Analysis Based on SALtran for Ultra-Low Power Applications
authors: Prabhat Kumar, Ayush Gedam, Abdulbari Ansari, Dheeraj Kumar Sinha
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: This work involves the design of capacitor-less dynamic random access memory (DRAM) cell using a 2-D TCAD device simulator and the Surface Accumulation Layer Transistor (SALTran) idea with Double Gate Field Effect Transistor (DGFET). Under steady state conditions, we have explained the capacitor-less DRAM cell's design window and operational mechanism. The impulsive (steep) shift in the Sub-threshold slope (SS) in DGFETs and the features of current-voltage (I-V) hysteresis have been noted. We have demonstrated its properties of capacitor-less DRAM cells related to one another when they are operating. In addition, it is observed that the constructed SALTran-based DGFET transistor has a very high gain value, which may be utilized to sense margin (i.e. retention time) in dynamically accessible memory with enhancements. The outcomes of this experiment may present a chance for more effective nanoscale technology in the future with simpler capacitor-less DRAM cell designs.

title: AI-Driven Segmentation of UAV GNDVI Imagery in Shade-Grown Coffee: Challenges for Detecting Herbicides in Agroforestry Systems
authors: Carlos Roberto Martínez Martínez, Maurice Stuempel
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: Traces of herbicides pose a risk to human health, and recent drone-derived multispectral imagery was tested to assess its suitability for detecting their presence in coffee crops. This research was conducted in the volcanic mountains of Santa Ana, El Salvador, across six shade-coffee plantations, three of them managed with a mixture of glyphosate, glufosinateammonium, and metsulfuron-methyl herbicides and the other three with strictly mechanical weed control. The complex canopy structure of these agroforestry systems caused by the overlap of shade trees and variable weed cover, posed a significant challenge for accurate spectral analysis due to interference from non-coffee vegetation. To determine whether herbicide application can be detected through chlorophyll reflectance measurements, a drone equipped with a 20 MP RGB sensor and a four - band multispectral camera was used to capture high-resolution imagery. Orthophotos and GNDVI rasters were processed with WebODM. To isolate coffee canopies from weeds and shadows, a color-normalized segmentation process was applied, leveraging green channel ranges and several AI models: binary thresholding, Random Forest, K-Means clustering, Deep Neural Networks (DNN), and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) based on MobileNet. Foliar testing found no detectable glyphosate residues in any of the chemically managed fields. Similarly, GNDVI-based analysis revealed no significant difference in vegetation health between herbicides-treated and untreated plots. This spectral undetectability suggested that chemical traces may persist without visible stress, therefore highlighting the need to supplement remote sensing with other chemical residue analyses.

title: Conversion and Deployment Pipeline for Feedforward Neural Networks as Embedded Libraries on the ATmega328P
authors: Rodrigo Centeno, César Morales, Ernesto Morales
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: This paper presents a pipeline that converts feedforward neural networks trained in TensorFlow/Keras into a C/C++ library optimized for the ATmega328P. The process automates model loading, architecture extraction, weights and biases, and the generation of a header and implementation file that place the parameters in Flash memory, minimizing SRAM usage. Integration into Arduino IDE is reduced to including the header, instantiating the network, and calling a prediction function. Validation includes cases of 5 -input digital combinations with binary classification using the Wisconsin Breast Cancer dataset. The research demonstrated the correct implementation of neural networks, the simultaneous execution of networks, and explored growing topologies to delimit practical memory limits, confirming the viability of the approach.

title: Modeling Overhead of Elastic Applications
authors: Andrew P. Worley, Sheikh K. Ghafoor, Thomas Hines
publish: 2025 24th International Symposium on Parallel and Distributed Computing (ISPDC)
abstract: High-performance computing (HPC) systems are growing in capacity, complexity, and heterogeneity. At the same time, HPC workloads are shifting from traditional parallel simulations to more dynamic, data- and event-driven applications as well as AI workloads. These emerging applications are often malleable or evolving, adapting their behavior at runtime to meet changing conditions. However, current HPC software stacks and application models are not suitable to support or model such elastic workloads. This paper introduces a lightweight, general-purpose applications model that uses simple mathematical functions as proxies for complex performance behaviors. The model is flexible enough to capture a wide range of application dynamics while requiring minimal application-specific information. We explore how its parameters influence both job behavior and systemlevel outcomes. Additionally, we propose Effective Utilization and General Slowdown as novel performance metrics designed to capture important system-application interactions that are overlooked by traditional metrics.

title: Smart Door Access Control and Intrusion Alert System Using YOLOv10 and ArcFace
authors: Deepa S, Arthi C S, Ashika Aman A, Bhuvaneshwari S
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: In today's world, the evolution of security systems toward AI-powered face recognition is a rapidly growing trend. Machine learning and deep learning algorithms serve as the backbone for these modern systems. However, they also encounter significant challenges such as false recognition rates due to occlusion, appearance variations, and spoofing attacks. Another notable challenge is that these systems operate continuously, leading to redundant computations. In this paper, we propose an event-driven real-time face recognition for safe access control to reduce unnecessary processing. The proposed system automatically grants access to authorized residents and generates real-time alerts when unknown individuals are detected at the entrance. YOLOv10 is employed for real-time face detection due to its end-to-end architecture and low inference latency, making it suitable for embedded deployment. Detected faces are then processed using an ArcFace-based deep face recognition model to extract discriminative embeddings for user authentication. If the detected individual is recognized as an authorized user, the door is unlocked automatically; otherwise, intrusion alerts are triggered locally and remotely. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed system achieves a face recognition accuracy of 98.1% with a low false acceptance rate of 2.5% and a false rejection rate of 4.5%. The end-to-end inference latency remains below 45 ms, confirming real-time feasibility. These results highlight the effectiveness of the proposed hybrid framework for secure, efficient, and intelligent smart home access control.

title: Pruned MODNN for Efficient RSSI Prediction in IoT Communication Deployment Planning
authors: Salsabila Julianti Virna Joffana, Aji Gautama Putrada, Novian Anggis Suwastika
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)
abstract: Received signal strength indicator (RSSI) prediction is crucial for Internet of Things (IoT) communication deployment planning, as the value cannot be obtained at that stage due to the lack of wireless communication devices. However, the prediction model needs to be multi-output and efficient, as it has several outcome variables and will be applied on mobile devices. The purpose of this study is to evaluate a multi-output deep neural network (MODNN) for RSSI and energy prediction in IoT deployment planning. The first step is to download a secondary dataset from Kaggle about peer-to-peer (P2P) IoT energy and link quality. We use MODNN to predict three items: RSSI, energy, and efficiency. The last metric is a novel variable derived by dividing the absolute value of RSSI by energy. We successfully prove the novel theorem by showing that RSSI can increase without an increase in energy. We also apply pruning to remove insignificant weights. The test results show that our MODNN achieves a higher R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score than a single-output DNN for both RSSI and energy. Then the model size is also smaller, namely 8,914 kB compared to 26,686 kB, with CR 2.99×. Pruning not only yields the best CR and inference time (4.85× and 0.1 ms, respectively), but also improves RSSI and energy prediction by removing small weights, with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> scores of 0.963 and 0.994, respectively.

title: Explainable Deep Learning Models for Type 2 Diabetes Risk Prediction in Web-Integrated IoT Healthcare Environments
authors: Alireza Khadem Daghigh, Ali Zali, Karlo Abnoosian
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Web Research (ICWR)
abstract: Type 2diabetes (T2D) remains a pressing global health crisis, projected to affect 853million adults by 2050. Deep learning models offer strong predictive performance for early risk assessment, yet their opacity severely restricts clinical trust in webintegrated Internet of Things (IoT) healthcare systems. This paper proposes an explainable deep learning framework based on a multi-layer perceptron (MLP) enhanced with SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) for transparent feature attribution. The pipeline is evaluated on two publicly available datasets with distinct ethnic populations: the PIMA Indians Dataset (768 instances) and the Pabna Bangladesh Dataset (465 instances). Both are augmented with 1500IoT-simulated sensor streams using a multivariate normal distribution with 10% Gaussian noise. Preprocessing includes KNN imputation, Z-score standardization, and SMOTE oversampling restricted to training folds to prevent data leakage. Five-fold cross-validation yields mean accuracies of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 6. 4 3} \% \pm \mathbf{1. 8 2} \%$</tex> on PIMA and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4. 1 9} \% \pm \mathbf{4. 8 4} \%$</tex> on Pabna, with F1-scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 4. 1 1} \% \pm \mathbf{2. 4 6} \%$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 6. 3 5} \% \pm \mathbf{3. 0 9} \%$</tex>, respectively. SHAP consistently identifies Glucose as the dominant predictor (highest mean absolute impact: 0.1956 on PIMA, 0.2228 on Pabna). However, the analysis reveals critical population-specific differences: DiabetesPedigreeFunction shows a stronger influence in PIMA (0.0581), whereas BMI significance is drastically reduced in Pabna (0.0064) compared to PIMA (0.0726). A conceptual web-based framework integrates realtime IoT predictions with SHAP visualizations, promoting clinician confidence. Results underscore the necessity of localized explainability in revealing ethnic heterogeneity and enhancing trust in digital health applications without sacrificing predictive power.

title: Determining The Optimal Specification Of Different Cooling Systems To Optimize The Indoor Environment In Highly Glazed Buildings Using Taguchi Method And CFD Simulation
authors: Shengqiang Shi, Abdelatif Merabtine, Rachid Bennacer
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: This study addresses the challenge of optimizing cooling systems in glass buildings under intense solar radiation and high summer temperatures. While existing research primarily compares cooling system performance, it often fails to comprehensively evaluate the impact of varying system specifications on indoor environmental quality due to experimental limitations. To bridge this gap, we developed a computational fluid dynamics (CFD) based approach combined with the Taguchi Method, enabling efficient analysis of 16 representative scenarios from 96 possible combinations of 11 design parameters at two levels. Our findings demonstrate that for the air handling unit, air volume flow rate and inlet air temperature do not significantly affect the response variables. For the ceiling cooling system, reducing tube spacing and increasing inlet water temperature led to higher operative Temperature and thermal discomfort, while increasing tube inner diameter results in higher energy consumption. For the floor cooling system, inlet water temperature increases, operative Temperature rises, while thermal comfort improves, and energy consumption decreases. The results demonstrate that for different cooling systems, the proposed method adds value by enhancing thermal comfort and reducing energy consumption.

title: Towards Mobility Applications of Solid-State Hydrogen Storage Tanks: A Review
authors: Isra Teldjia, Abdelhamid Bouhelal, Arezki Smaili
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: As the global transition toward clean energy accelerates, hydrogen has emerged as a key enabler of sustainable mobility. Among the various hydrogen storage methods, solid-state storage using metal hydrides offers unique advantages in terms of safety, volumetric energy density, and system integration. This review explores the potential of solid-state hydrogen storage tanks for mobility applications, focusing on material selection, system technologies, and design considerations. Key hydride classes— including intermetallic compounds, magnesium-based alloys, and complex hydrides are assessed based on their thermodynamic properties, absorption/desorption kinetics, and operational conditions. The integration of these materials into practical tank systems requires addressing thermal management challenges, size and weight constraints, and rapid refueling targets. Special attention is given to Department of Energy (DOE) performance targets for 2025, and how current technologies approach or fall short of these benchmarks. The review concludes with future perspectives on advanced materials, and hybrid thermal strategies that can enhance the viability of solid-state hydrogen storage for vehicles, drones, and other mobile applications.

title: Large Tunneling Magnetoresistance in a Ferromagnetic Junction Controlled by Exchange Field and Band Gap Engineering in Monolayer Phosphorene
authors: Daniel Brian Gutiérrez Reyna, Mohammed Miniya, Mario Limon Mendoza, Mostafa Sadoqi, Eric Guzmán Ortiz, Outmane Oubram
publish: 2025 IEEE Latin American Conference on Nanotechnology (LANANO)
abstract: The transport properties of a single-layer phosphorene junction, including tunneling magnetoresistance (TMR) and conductance, are theoretically investigated under the influence of an exchange field and band gap energy using the transfer matrix method. Our results reveal that the conductance in parallel and anti-parallel ferromagnetic configurations exhibits distinct behavior in both the valence and conduction bands, leading to the emergence of large TMR values. Moreover, the magnitude of TMR can be effectively tuned by adjusting the strength of the exchange field and the band gap energy. These findings suggest that phosphorene-based structures hold significant potential for applications in nanoscale spintronic circuits.

title: Defect Detection in Solar Photovoltaic Modules Using IR Imaging and YOLOv11
authors: Sumit Verma, Shiv Lal, Aastha Joshi, Husain Ul Hasan Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Photovoltaic modules are susceptible to thermal defects such as hotspots, overheating cells, and short circuits, which reduce energy yield and increase safety risks. This paper presents an automated defect-detection framework based on the advanced object detection technique YOLOv11 applied to infrared images of PV modules. By leveraging YOLOv11's architectural advancements, namely C3k2 blocks, Spatial Pyramid Pooling Fast, C2PSA attention, and a decoupled detection head, the proposed method achieves high accuracy with real-time inference, making it suitable for UAVbased and on-site inspections. An experimental evaluation was conducted using a thermal infrared dataset comprising 500 images spanning seven defect categories. The proposed model achieved a mean Average Precision of 0.92 at an Intersection-over-Union threshold of 0.5 (mAP@0.5), while sustaining inference rates above 45 frames per second on mid-tier computational hardware. Compared with a YOLOv8 baseline trained under identical conditions, the proposed approach yields approximately 15% higher MAP while maintaining computational efficiency. This study provides a scalable, noninvasive approach for predictive maintenance for a large solar PV power plant.

title: Exploring Web Search Behavior of Elementary School Children
authors: Fatemeh Ejei, Amir Ghaebi, Roya Baradar
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Web Research (ICWR)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of emerging technologies, the World Wide Web has become a primary resource for quick and easy access to information. Elementary school children, who acquire basic reading and writing skills, are increasingly using the web to meet their information needs. Designing effective information retrieval systems for this user group requires a clear understanding of how children interact with web-based search engines. This study investigates the web search behavior of elementary school children through the analysis of search transaction logs. The results identify four distinct web search interaction patterns among children—Superficial Searching, Carousel Searching, Optimal Searching, and Deep Exploration—ordered by the degree of children's engagement in the search process and their effort to achieve their intended content. These findings provide insights into children's interaction with web search systems and can inform the design of intelligent agents and interfaces to improve the effectiveness of information retrieval in web-based environments.

title: Dynamic Obstacle Avoidance Algorithm for Flexible Robotic Arms Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Li Li
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: With the rapid development of industrial automation and service robotics, flexible robotic arms have been widely used in scenarios such as human-machine collaboration and precision operation due to their good flexibility and safety. However, its dynamic obstacle avoidance problem has always been a key bottleneck restricting its efficient application. Traditional obstacle avoidance algorithms are difficult to cope with the challenges brought by the random movement of obstacles in complex dynamic environments and the elastic deformation of the flexible arms themselves. This paper first constructs a dynamic model of the flexible manipulator, considering the impact of its elastic deformation on the motion trajectory, and then introduces a deep reinforcement learning framework to design a state space that integrates the state of the manipulator and the dynamic information of obstacles, as well as a reward function that takes into account both obstacle avoidance efficiency and motion smoothness. Then, the improved deep deterministic policy gradient (DDPG) algorithm is used for training to achieve real-time obstacle avoidance decisions in dynamic environments, and finally simulation experiments are conducted in a variety of dynamic obstacle scenarios. The results show that in terms of obstacle avoidance path length, the total path length of the proposed algorithm is 3.10 m, the PPO algorithm is 3.86 m, and the DQN algorithm reaches 6.11m. The shorter path length not only reduces the obstacle avoidance time, but also reduces the cumulative error caused by long-term movement of the flexible manipulator.

title: Comparative Analysis of ResNet50-V1 and Fine-Tuned ResNet50-V2 Models for Multi-Class Classification Using Ocular Surface Images
authors: Rajasekaran Thangaraj, Preethishri D, Sivaraj R
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Early and proper detection of ocular surface disorders such as Pterygium, Corneal Ulcer, and Conjunctivitis is crucial for timely diagnosis and prevention of vision impairment. In this paper, an automated deep-learning-based classification system is proposed based on two transfer learning architectures: a basic ResNet50-V1 model and an improved ResNet50-V2 model with partial fine-tuning and an extended classification head. A balanced dataset of 3000 ocular surface images was created (1000 from each class) and divided into training, validation, and testing. The ResNet50-V1 model achieved a validation accuracy of 85.49% by being trained tgftwith frozen convolutional layers. For better extraction and representation of the features, the ResNet50-V2 model unfreezes the last convolutional layers, integrates batch normalization regularization, and incorporates deeper fully connected layers. This optimized model results in a significantly higher validation accuracy of 97.00% and therefore demonstrated substantial improvements in classification performance. Experimental results have outlined the importance of fine-tuning pre-trained CNNs for medical image diagnosis. The proposed ResNet50-V2 model offers a robust, efficient, and scalable solution for automatic ocular disease classification and thus has strong potential for real-time clinical screening.

title: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Consensus control of MRS under integrity attacks using AI based event trigger controller
authors: Zaheer Abbas, Arslan Ameer, Muhammad Zafar
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: This paper proposes an AI-based event-triggered controller for secure consensus control of multi-robot systems (MRS) under integrity attacks. First, a time-series Transformer is employed to detect malicious data within the MRS. Second, the event-triggered controller selectively activates communication events to minimize the impact of such attacks. Third, the controller maintains smooth consensus in the presence of adversarial conditions and achieves rapid recovery from reachable-node, root-node, and worst-case divergence scenarios despite corrupted measurements. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves robust performance, with 99.75% detection accuracy, perfect precision, and near-perfect recall, ensuring reliable identification of attacks. Compared with conventional consensus strategies, the proposed approach reduces communication overhead while enhancing resilience, making it particularly suitable for bandwidth-constrained and adversarial multi-robot environments.

title: A Dual Merge Mode for Video Coding
authors: Chen Li, Ru-Ling Liao, Jie Chen, Xinwei Li, Yan Ye, Xi Li
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: In the latest international video coding standard, Versatile Video Coding, merge mode utilizes spatial or temporal adjacent motion information as motion vector predictors to reduce the signaling overhead. However, only one merge candidate's motion information is selected from previously coded blocks for motion compensation and prediction. To improve the prediction efficiency, a dual merge mode which inherits two candidates' motion information from previously coded blocks is proposed. In the proposed method, motion compensation is executed utilizing the motion information from both merge candidates to generate two predictions, and the two predictions are ultimately combined with equal weight to derive the final predicted results. Both of the merge candidates can be uni-prediction or bi-prediction, requiring up to four motion compensations for the proposed dual merge mode. Experimental results show that the proposed dual merge mode achieves an average coding gain of 0.03 % over ECM-14.0 with negligible complexity increase, and the extended ablation studies verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Practical Image Compression with Energy-Guided Asymmetric Entropy Modeling
authors: Qian He, Yuqi Li, Yiheng Jiang, Haotian Zhang, Li Li, Dong Liu
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: Recent learned image compression (LIC) methods have demonstrated superior ratedistortion performance compared to classical image compression standards. However, their substantially higher computational complexity and inefficient entropy modeling designs raise challenges to practical deployment. To address this issue, we propose Energy-Guided Asymmetric Entropy Modeling for ultra-low-complexity learned image compression. Building on the observation that low-energy channels can be effectively modeled with less complexity, we propose a novel Asymmetric Hyperprior Transform (AHT). AHT evenly split the latent features into channel groups. Low-energy groups are processed by lightweight subnetworks, achieving reduced complexity. To further ensure proper energy allocation on channel groups, we design an Energy Allocation (EA) Loss that constrains latent features with respect to their estimated mean, thus enabling flexible control over channel energy distribution. Overall, our entropy modeling design is extremely lightweight and can be efficiently deployed on CPUs, making it well-suited for practical deployment. Experiments demonstrate that our proposed model achieves a 5% reduction in BD-rate over BPG with decoding complexity below <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \text{kMACs} /$</tex> pixel, and achieves a BD-rate gain per decoding MACs/pixel of -645.89, striking a favorable balance between rate-distortion performance and computational cost.

title: Enhancing Cross-Document Entity Coreference Resolution with Argument Role Semantics
authors: Xiangyun Kong, Chengxiang Tan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Cross-Document Entity Coreference Resolution (CDECR) aims to identify and link entity mentions across multiple documents that refer to the same real-world entity. Existing approaches typically rely on direct similarity computations between mentions or utilize local contextual information. However, these methods often struggle to maintain stable alignment when faced with cross-document stylistic variations and noise. While Large Language Models have recently provided new avenues for cross-document semantic understanding, their inherent hallucinations and the noise generated by divergent descriptions can lead to false positives or false negatives. To address these challenges, we propose a Cross-Document Entity Coreference Resolution method enhanced by Argument Role Semantics. We utilize a lightweight set of event argument role labels as robust semantic constraints to guide the LLM in generating accurate entity explanations integrated with full-document context. Through a direct joint representation learning technique, we inject entity explanations into the representation space to bolster the model's coreference resolution capabilities in cross-document scenarios. Our method's CoNLL F1 score surpasses the baseline by 0.9% on the ECB+ dataset, demonstrating the effectiveness of our approach.

title: Prediction of Bearing Life Using the BIGRU-WIENER Hybrid Model Based on Physical Constraints
authors: Zhaojun Xie, Junjie Han
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Traditional data-driven deep learning methods demonstrate significant potential in bearing life prediction, yet often lack physical interpretability and uncertainty quantification capabilities. This paper proposes a novel physically constrained BiGRU-Wiener hybrid model, composed of a BiGRU module and a Wiener process module. First, the BiGRU serves as a feature extractor for input data, extracting multi-level temporal features from vibration data. Subsequently, a parameter estimation network maps these features to Wiener process parameters: trend features estimate drift parameters, while volatility features estimate diffusion coefficients. Finally, the bearing expected lifetime is calculated using estimated parameters λ and σ based on Wiener process theory. Experiments on the PHM2012 bearing dataset demonstrate that this model achieves approximately 60% higher predictive performance compared to existing purely data-driven methods.

title: Real-Time Protection System for Three-Phase Induction Motors
authors: Swathi K, T Shagun Shetty, Swapnil Naik, Shashank Shashidhar, Mohammed Rahil
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Several electrical faults such as over currents, over voltages, and inappropriate phase sequence faults can occur on industrial three-phase induction motors. These faults can lead to diminished function with some faults resulting in severe mechanical deterioration. The system proposed here addresses these issues by actively tracking the motor's electrical parameters to determine when there are rapid abnormalities in the level of current drawn or level of voltage. Once a fault occurs, the motors are disconnected from power to prevent additional fault damage. This promotes increased motor longevity and reduced costs in repairs or potential downtime. The system utilizes real time analysis and automated fault detection to provide increased motor safety and longevity and promotes increased usability of the machines by allowing for a prolongation of time between maintenance cycles.

title: Dual-Phase Entangled Capsule Network for Multi-Class Imbalanced Fault Diagnosis of Rolling Bearings
authors: Wenyu Ma, Funa Zhou
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: The probability of occurrence for different fault categories in rolling bearings is inherently imbalanced. Engineering monitoring often prioritizes faults incurring high maintenance costs, leading to fewer samples of low cost faults, while samples of the normal class and high frequency, high cost faults are more abundant. These two factors collectively result in a multi-class imbalance problem. As a typical data augmentation method, Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) are widely employed to address imbalanced fault diagnosis. However, these methods can’t guarantee the generation of reliable data, and existing solutions struggle to uncover the potential correlations among fault features under few-shot conditions. The dynamic routing mechanism of Capsule Networks enables hierarchical feature aggregation and dynamic relationship enhancement. Therefore, this paper proposes an imbalanced fault diagnosis method based on a Dual-Phase Entangled Capsule Network (DP-ECN) that combines coarse-grained and fine-grained routing. Finally, an entanglement operation maps the features into the complex domain to explore global correlations between features, enhancing the relevance of sparse features. Experimental results demonstrate the superior accuracy of the proposed method when handling imbalanced data.

title: EDG-UNet: Fusing Edge Perception and Attention Mechanism for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Dongyang Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Retinal vessel segmentation is a crucial component of medical image analysis, which holds significant value for the early diagnosis of diseases such as diabetic retinopathy and glaucoma. Due to the complex vascular structure, low contrast, as well as the frequent interference of noise, traditional methods struggle to balance precision and robustness simultaneously. This paper proposes EDG-UNet, an efficient segmentation framework that integrates edge prior information, and attention mechanism. Specifically, the model enhances boundary perception via an edge encoding branch. Enhance feature focus on tiny vessels and improve detail restoration capability via attention mechanism. Reduce computational costs by adopting depthwise separable convolution. Experiments conducted on datasets including DRIVE and STARE demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms mainstream methods in segmentation accuracy, boundary clarity, and noise resistance.

title: Optimization of Grid Connected Control Strategy for Offshore Wind Farms Considering Wind Power Uncertainty
authors: Huaide Lin
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: In response to the power output randomness and strong intermittency characteristics of offshore wind power grid connected systems in complex marine meteorological environments, traditional control strategies have limitations in dealing with frequency deviation, voltage fluctuations, and insufficient inertia support, which can easily lead to system instability. This article constructs a short-term control model based on multi-stage dynamic optimization, with the goal of maximizing the comprehensive control capability of wind power grid connected power. It integrates power prediction and hybrid energy storage coordination mechanism, and introduces particle swarm optimization algorithm for solution. Test data shows that under constant output mode, the average charge and discharge power of the battery is 7.69 MW (smooth output 6.06 MW), and the supercapacitor is 2.17 MW (smooth output 2.03 MW). The objective function value is 46.3% higher than the average smooth output value; In terms of static voltage adjustment accuracy, the fluctuation range of this method is within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 1 {\%}$</tex>.

title: Analysis and Correction Recommendations for Key Parameters in Planning of Distribution Networks
authors: Yu Song, Xuyang Wang, Xin Yang, Yalin Chong, Kai Cui
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: Against the backdrop of the “dual carbon” goals and the development of new power systems, the widespread integration of distributed renewable energy sources has driven the transformation of distribution grids toward active operation. This shift has fundamentally altered their structural characteristics and power flow patterns, rendering traditional distribution grid planning approaches inadequate for meeting the dual demands of safety, reliability, efficiency, and economic benefits. This paper focuses on key parameters for primary-side planning of active distribution grids, conducting systematic research. First, an optimized selection method for main transformer capacity is established. Through load forecasting, net load calculation, and capacity-to-load ratio determination-combined with supply type identification and reverse overload verification, the optimal main transformer capacity is determined via comprehensive technicaleconomic comparison. Second, principles for selecting key line planning parameters are clarified, including conductor crosssection selection for feeder lines, optimization of new line counts based on source-load power balance, supply area division using the rotating centerline method, and deployment schemes for sectionalizing switches, tie switches, and tie lines. Finally, Select a region in China as the empirical case study for selecting and refining key planning parameters for substations and lines. Empirical results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively addresses underloading and severe overload issues while reducing the capacity-to-load ratio, lowering main transformer capacity planning values, and saving construction costs. It enhances renewable energy absorption rates and power supply reliability, providing technical support for high-quality construction of active distribution grids in regions with varying resource endowments.

title: Modeling Electrode and Microchannel Geometry for Detection of Cadmium (Cd), Lead (Pb), and Copper (Cu) in Drinking Water through a Capillary-Driven Microfluidic Electrochemical Sensor
authors: Victore Anand D. Abao, Hannah Isabel A. Basbas, Jericho Kent M. Co, Ralph Maru Grande, Rafael Pangilinan, Maria Theresa De Leon
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: Trace levels of heavy metals such as lead (Pb), cadmium (Cd), and copper (Cu) pose serious health risks, necessitating a rapid and low-cost point-of-care (POC) detection system. This study presents a microfluidic electrochemical sensor model integrating a three-electrode differential pulse anodic stripping voltammetry system for simultaneous detection of Pb, Cd, and Cu. While many studies focus on material modifications, this work investigates how geometric parameters influence sensor sensitivity. In particular, the working electrode area, the distance between electrodes, and microfluidic structure geometry were explored. Parametric studies reveal that increasing the working electrode (WE) area increases the peak currents corresponding to all three target heavy metals, with the highest sensitivity observed at a WE area of 6 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Electrode spacing shows optimal sensitivity at 1 mm among the simulated dimensions, with Cu and Pb peaks increasing up to 1.25 mm, while Cd sensitivity shows a decreasing trend with increasing spacing. This shows that there is a trade-off that must be considered when selecting electrode spacing for sensors used for the detection of multiple heavy metals. Microchannel length slightly affects flow rate but has a minimal effect on current response. However, increasing capillary microstructure spacing to 0.36 mm enhances flow and marginally improves sensitivity within the dimensions explored. These findings highlight the role of geometric design in optimizing sensor performance beyond material selection.

title: A Multi-Feature Integration Method for Enhancer Identification and Strength Prediction
authors: Zihao Hu, Xiaoqiang Xu, Ye Li, Chunquan Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Enhancers are short DNA sequences located in non-coding regions that play a crucial role in gene transcription regulation by recruiting transcription factors to remotely control target gene expression. Consequently, identifying enhancers has remained a significant challenge. We propose iEnhancer-MAF, a multimodal feature method integrating DNA shape features and transcription factor motif features, for enhancer identification and strength prediction. We first convert raw DNA sequence features into numerical representations using a model, incorporating multi-scale feature extraction and attention mechanisms to optimize feature extraction. Simultaneously, we leverage both DNA shape features and transcription factor motif features to enhance the model's predictive performance. On benchmark datasets for enhancer identification, our method demonstrates superior performance. Compared to methods that also incorporate DNA shape features, our approach achieves 6 and 13% improvements in enhancer identification and strength prediction accuracy, respectively, validating its effectiveness and robustness.

title: Lightweight Convolutional Transformer Network Fusion of High and Low Frequency Attention Applied to Froth Image Analysis for On-line Recognition of Raw Ore Conditions in Gold Roughing Processes
authors: Hong Shi, Zhixun Li, Yaoyi Cai, Dan Huang, Zekai Yao, Yawen Xiao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Gold-antimony mixed flotation is the primary method for recovering gold-containing compounds from gold-antimony associated ores, but fluctuating ore feed conditions, subjective manual control, and difficulties in parameter regulation often cause instability in recovery and concentrate grade. To reduce subjectivity and improve accuracy, this paper proposes a lightweight convolutional Transformer network with high–low frequency attention (Hilo-L-Conv-ViT) for real-time gold roughing froth image analysis. The lightweight convolutional module reduces computational cost, while the high–low frequency attention replaces standard multi-head attention to enhance both detail and global features in froth images. Experiments show that the proposed method achieves 97.17% accuracy, 98.82% recall, and 98.00% F1-score, outperforming VGG19, ResNet50, and ViT. Industrial deployment further confirms its effectiveness, increasing gold recovery from 83.79% to 86.27%.

title: Edge AI Architecture for Real-Time Arrhythmia Detection Using Wearable ECG Devices
authors: R. Sundar, Avinash Deyyam, Anusha N, Kalisetti Purushotham Prasad, Shoyimkulov Asror, Jami Venkata Suman
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: As the world's population continues to age rapidly, more sophisticated IoT (Internet of Things) frameworks and smart home health assistance technology have emerged to help people remain independent while living at home. While strides have been made in the development of smart homes and robotic assistants that make it easier to monitor individuals in their homes, there are still limitations when it comes to constantly tracking people's body functions through traditional means of monitoring their cardiovascular system using new wireless medical technologies. The use of a centralized cloud computing architecture to process ECG data continuously creates a number of problems for monitoring cardiovascular conditions using on-device ECG devices. Therefore, this research presents an Edge AI architecture specifically for real-time arrhythmia identification using wearable ECG devices. Thus, this architecture reduces the latency associated with dying of a heart rhythm disorder to only milliseconds from the time it takes for the ECG device to send the ECG data to the centralized cloud server for processing, yet provides real-time detection of abnormal heart events while dramatically reducing power consumption and protecting user privacy. In addition to providing a complete health monitoring solution for those aging in place, the proposed Edge AI architecture can be easily incorporated into existing multi-agent smart home and Care Bot architectures.

title: Research on the response of underwater explosion shock wave and cavitation effect to stiffened plate structure
authors: Jinlei Wang, Qilong He, Mingmin Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Ships and Electromechanical System (ICISES)
abstract: Taking the stiffened plate as the research object, its dynamic response under the secondary loading of underwater explosion shock wave and cavitation effect is discussed. By using the coupled acoustic-solid method in ABAQUS software, the displacement response, acceleration response and velocity response of well-shaped, cross and double-cross stiffened plates under different blasting depths and different explosive amounts are studied and analyzed, and the variation law of each response is summarized. The results show that the secondary loading under the cavitation effect will have a greater impact on the stiffened plate, and the structural vibration will be stronger, especially on the acceleration response of the stiffened plate. The performance of the stiffened plate is better than the other two. The research results provide a theoretical basis for scholars to analyze the influence of the cavitation effect on the response of the ship under the action of the shock wave load, and the influence of the cavitation effect cannot be ignored.

title: Optimizing Crop Selection and Fertilizer Usage with a Hybrid SVM-Genetic Algorithm Framework in Precision Agriculture
authors: Venkata Kiran Kumar Ravi, Rachel Nallathamby, Shanmuga Priya S, Tamilarasi Rajamani, M. Abinaya, Nimisha Amrutkar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The concept of precision agriculture emerged in the 1980s as a means to tailor fertilizer rates and mixtures to individual growing sites, with positive results for a wide range of crops and regions. Inadequate baseline data, limited sample methods, lack of site-specific fertilizer recommendations, and a paucity of qualified specialists are agronomic issues; high costs and limited experience are socioeconomic constraints. As a result, adoption remains uneven despite global interest. In order to address these challenges, datasets for crop selection and fertilizer optimisation were cleaned and prepared using data preprocessing. The optimal feature selection was achieved via RFE. A structurally reduced hybrid model that combined a GA and an SVM was used to select the crop and usage of fertilizers, an important measure of crop health and nutrient status. When it came to complicated nonlinear connections, the GA-SVM model achieved an accuracy of 94.69 %, which was higher than rival models. They need to overcome socioeconomic and technical obstacles to disseminate advanced machine learning and precision agriculture, which can increase fertilizer efficiency and crop selection.

title: A Progressive Two-Stage Wiener Process Model for Remaining Useful Life Prediction
authors: Jin Xie, Zhizhong Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Modeling equipment degradation and predicting the remaining useful life (RUL) are critical yet challenging, primarily due to the commonly observed multi-stage characteristics of degradation processes. These characteristics arise from variations in internal mechanisms or external environments, making it difficult to accurately predict RUL before a change point occurs. Existing methods mostly assume an abrupt change in the degradation rate at the change point. In practice, degradation patterns often exhibit gradual transitions. To address this, this paper proposes a generalized progressive two-stage stochastic degradation model that unifies the description of both abrupt and gradual changes between degradation stages. Based on this model, analytical solutions for the probability distributions of equipment lifetime and RUL are derived. The simulation results demonstrate that under two-stage degradation scenarios, the proposed method can effectively and accurately predict the RUL, thereby verifying both the effectiveness of the method and the accuracy of its theoretical model.

title: TIME-E 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)

title: A 64-Gb/s QPSK Carrier-Phase-Recovery Loop with Wide IF Range Using Dual-Oscillator Frequency Translation
authors: Mohamed Osama Selim, Mohamed Saad Aly, Mahmoud A. Khalil, Mohamed Badr Younis, Mostafa G. Ahmed, Ahmed E. Abdelrahman, Sujay Patel, Karim Elmeligy, John Burns, Pavan Kumar Hanumolu
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper presents an analog carrier-phase-recovery (CPR) architecture that overcomes the fundamental trade-off between IF-offset range and loop bandwidth. A dual-VCO frequency translation loop simultaneously reduces phase noise and corrects ±2 GHz frequency offsets, allowing operation across wide IF variations. Measurements show 64-Gb/s operation with reopened eyes under severe offset and frequency modulation stress, achieving an LO phase deviation of 2.27° rms and a 5.5 pJ/bit efficiency, confirming the feasibility of analog high-speed CPR receivers.

title: Strategic Framework for Human Resource Management in Logistics Companies: Case Studies and Best Practices
authors: Sovannpanha Kong, Sreyneath Chhun, Vannak Seng, Kimseng Tieng
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: This paper create a comprehensive framework for Human Resources Management (HRM) in the logistics industry. The qualitative research is conducted by interviewing three logistics firms in 2023 including a medium-sized firm, a large-sized firm, and a conglomerate firm. Results indicate that the medium-sized firm tends to have less systematic recruitment and selection process. It mainly recruits new employees by advertising jobs on social media and shortlisting them for interviews. For training and development, the firm allows employees to join workshops. For retention and compensation, the firm uses KPIs to measure staff performance and promote existing employees with good KPIs to a higher position. The large firm also implements the above-mentioned practices and including promoting internal staff and building a partnership with educational centers to get highly capable students for recruitment and selection. For training and development, the firm provides extra training on the course needed, provides learning opportunities to attend local and international events, and offer job rotation to build employee capabilities. The firm provides job rotation and maternity leave to employees as a part of retention and compensation. The conglomerate firm it organizes job fairs to acquire talented resources. After recruitment and selection, they offer various training, including on-the-job training, classroom training, E-Learning, Language training, and compliance & safety training. To retain and compensate talented employees, the conglomerate firm offers career development opportunities by offering regular feedback, creating a positive work culture, promoting work-life balance, offering competitive salaries and bonuses, providing health benefits, offering retirement leave and paid leave benefits, and reviewing benefits regularly. Hence, top management of the firm should (1) have a systematic HRM process, (2) implement HRM practices based on the size of their organization, so that their company can effectively and efficiently use their resources, and (3) set up a clear structure of meetings and systematic HRM practices for effectively managing employees in the firm. The study is limited to the logistics industry, where other sectors may adopt different frameworks for HRM practice.

title: LOIC-DDoS: A Machine Learning–Ready Dataset for DDoS Detection Across Diverse Networks Environments
authors: Danny E. Acosta-Tejada, Javier E. Sanchez-Galan
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: Cybersecurity has evolved as a crucial discipline, focusing on safeguarding information and systems from digital threats and attacks. Foundational research, including studies on TCP/IP security issues and the vulnerabilities of servers facing the internet, has underscored the complexity and importance of robust cybersecurity measures. With the advancement of cloud computing and serverless systems, the introduction of the Internet of Things (IoT) has further complicated the digital landscape, integrating a myriad of devices that demand constant connectivity and security. Among various cyber threats, Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks are particularly noteworthy for their capacity to incapacitate systems by overwhelming them with traffic from multiple sources. The evolution of DDoS methodologies necessitates current and realistic datasets for defense strategy development. This paper presents LOIC-DDoS-Dataset, a novel dataset that surpasses existing benchmarks in simulating modern network environments and DDoS attack patterns. To validate the dataset’s effectiveness, we implemented an analytical framework combining statistical anomaly detection, DBSCAN clustering, and advanced dimensionality reduction techniques including PacMAP. Experimental evaluation using Random Forest, XGBoost, Isolation Forest, and One-Class SVM demonstrated the dataset’s discriminative power, with supervised learning models achieving perfect classification performance (0.999 accuracy and F1-score) on the sample of $1,000,000$-record. The substantial performance gap between supervised and unsupervised approaches validates the quality of engineered features and anomaly labels, facilitating the development of advanced DDoS mitigation strategies and contributing to refined Intrusion Detection Systems capable of addressing sophisticated cyber threats.

title: An Energy-Aware Real-Time Task Scheduling Framework for Low-Power Wireless IoT Networks
authors: Amarana Prabhakara Rao, Ramesh Babu Yathirajula, Varun Mishra
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has created unprecedented opportunities for ubiquitous sensing and actuation across diverse applications including smart cities, industrial automation, and healthcare monitoring. However, the battery-constrained nature of IoT devices poses significant challenges for sustainable network operation. This paper proposes a novel intelligent embedded systems framework for energy-aware task scheduling in low-power wireless IoT networks. We formulate a comprehensive optimization problem that jointly considers energy consumption, task deadlines, Quality of Service (QoS) requirements, and network lifetime maximization. The proposed framework integrates real-time operating system (RTOS) principles with machine learning-based workload prediction to enable dynamic task scheduling decisions. We develop a hybrid scheduling algorithm combining Earliest Deadline First (EDF) with Least Laxity First (LLF) policies, augmented by deep reinforcement learning for adaptive parameter tuning. Furthermore, we incorporate dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) techniques coordinated with wireless duty cycling to minimize energy consumption while meeting real-time constraints. Extensive experimental evaluations using ARM Cortex-M4 based IoT nodes demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves 43% energy savings, 58% improvement in task schedulability, 35% reduction in deadline misses, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.7 \times$</tex> enhancement in network lifetime compared to conventional scheduling approaches. The framework provides a practical solution for sustainable IoT deployments in resourceconstrained environments.

title: Development of a Reference Electrode Integrated Ir/IrOx Thin film pH Sensor for microRNA Detection
authors: Kazuya Suzuki, Miyuki Tabata, Yuji Miyahara, Takahiro Arakawa
publish: 2025 18th International Conference on Sensing Technology (ICST)
abstract: Liquid biopsy is a minimally invasive diagnostic method that detects disease markers in body fluids, offering advantages over traditional biopsy such as faster testing and reduced patient burden. Among potential biomarkers, microRNA (miRNA) is promising due to its stability in blood and disease-specific expression. In this study, we developed a reference-electrode-integrated $\mathrm{Ir} / \mathrm{IrOx}$ thin-film pH sensor to detect miRNA using hydrogen ions ($\mathrm{H}^{+}$) released during nucleic acid amplification. $\mathrm{Ir} / \mathrm{IrOx}$ films were fabricated via reactive sputtering, and the sensor formed under $\mathrm{Ar}: \mathrm{O}_{2}=0: 100$ showed a pH sensitivity of -58.5 $\mathrm{mV} / \mathrm{pH}$, close to the theoretical value. For reference integration, AgCl was deposited on Ag thin films using 3-diaminopropane-tetraacetic acid ferric ammonium salt monohydrate (PDTA·Fe(III)). The AgCl electrode formed with 10 mM PDTA and 5-minute immersion showed the best $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$sensitivity of $-51.9 \mathrm{mV} / \log \left[\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right]$and stable performance in the $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$range typical of isothermal amplification. The integrated sensor exhibited a pH sensitivity of $59.5 \mathrm{mV} / \mathrm{pH}$, indicating its potential for real-time miRNA detection in point-of-care testing.

title: A Simultaneous 16-Channel Adiabatic Charge-Tracking Stimulator with 87.4% Stimulator Efficiency and Low Load Sensitivity
authors: Eojin Kim, Janghwan Kim, Jaeseong Park, Seongjae Moon, Geunchang Seong, Su Yeon Jeon, Seonae Jang, Young Sang Cho, Sohmyung Ha, Chul Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper presents a simultaneous 16-channel adiabatic charge-tracking stimulator (SACT) for implantable systems. Time-multiplexed stimulation enables parallel operation using a single current-generation block. An on-chip capacitor ensures accurate per-channel charge balance, while an inductor adiabatically recovers energy each cycle. A time-based sensor predicts phase transitions. A stimulator efficiency of 87.4% with <3.7% variation and <0.54% charge mismatch across 500 <inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Ω</inf>–10 k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Ω</inf> and 60–250 nC stimulation charge was achieved. In-vivo multichannel stimulation was successfully demonstrated.

title: Multi-Model Machine Learning Framework for Evaluating Surface Roughness Characteristics in Abrasive Water Jet Cutting of Ramie Fibre Composites
authors: Lavanya. M
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Abrasive Water Jet (AWJ) cutting is an emerging non-traditional machining technique for fibre-reinforced polymer composites because it can cut complex shapes with less thermal damage. However, maintaining consistent surface quality remains a challenge due to the intricate relationship between machining variables and fibre-reinforced polymer composite properties. In this research, a multi-model machine learning approach is proposed to assess and predict the surface roughness properties of ramie fibre-reinforced polymer composites during AWJ cutting. Three machine learning models, namely Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), were created and compared for regression and classification tasks. Among the models compared, XGBoost was found to have the best predictive model with an outstanding <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> value of 0.999 and close to zero MSE and MAE, which showed excellent agreement with experimental values. Furthermore, XGBoost had the best recall and F1-score for surface roughness classification. The results demonstrate the strength of ensemble learning methods, especially the XGBoost algorithm, in modelling AWJM processes with complex nonlinear relationships. The proposed framework, which allows for the accurate prediction of surface quality and process optimisation, thus contributes to the achievement of SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation, and Infrastructure) through the use of intelligent manufacturing technologies.

title: Comparative Evaluation of End-to-End Automated Testing Tools for Web Applications: Cypress vs. Playwright
authors: Mabroukah Amarif, Hisham Imhmed, Fatma Awidat, Mansour Ahwidy, Khamiss Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
