title: Temporal Facial Dynamics-Driven Audio-Visual Emotion Recognition Using Vision Transformers
authors: Padma E, Mohana Prasath G, Chanthuru S R, Hariprasad M
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Emotion recognition plays a crucial role in systems that understand feelings. It enables smart interactions between people and machines in fields such as healthcare, education, and human-computer interaction. Recent progress in self-supervised learning has greatly improved our ability to gather emotional cues from speech and facial data. However, many current facial emotion recognition methods rely on static facial images taken from a single frame. This approach fails to capture how facial expressions change over time. In this paper, we introduce a new framework for audio-visual emotion recognition based on the dynamics of facial movements. This framework explicitly tracks the changing facial expressions throughout video sequences. We sample multiple facial frames from each video and use a vision transformer to create framelevel visual embeddings. These embeddings are combined over time to capture the dynamics of facial emotions and are fused with speech embeddings obtained from a selfsupervised audio model. A transformer-based network then merges the audio and visual data for emotion classification. We conducted extensive experiments on various datasets. The results show that including temporal facial modeling leads to better recognition accuracy and stability compared to static facial images. Additional evaluations on different datasets and ablation studies back up the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Rumor Detection in Social Networks Using Pre-Trained Transformer and LSTM Neural Networks
authors: Maedeh Atighinezhad, Sogol Mortazi, Reza Ravanmehr
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Web Research (ICWR)
abstract: Social networks have become an essential medium for sharing individuals' thoughts and opinions. This has led social media to act as a catalyst for the spread of misinformation and for influencing public opinion and decision-making. Rumors are among the most prominent forms of misleading information on social media and must be identified as early as possible to prevent their harmful effects. In this paper, both user-generated content and user profile-related features are simultaneously utilized for rumor detection. Rumor-related textual features are extracted using the GloVe word embedding method and are then fed into a deep neural network based on LSTM. Finally, the extracted textual features, along with the features obtained from user profiles, are provided as inputs to an MLP network for rumor classification. The evaluation of the proposed approach using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score demonstrates superior performance compared to existing methods.

title: Why Text-Based Emotion Recognition on Social Media Remains Limited: a Data- and Contextcentric Analysis
authors: Kimia Sadat Hashemi Arvanaghi, Anahita Alhouei, Niloofar Mirzaei Chahardeh
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Web Research (ICWR)
abstract: This paper presents a unified data- and context-centric analysis of text-based emotion recognition on social media, focusing on research published between 2020 and 2025. Although recent advances in neural language models have substantially improved benchmark performance, real-world robustness and cross-domain generalization remain limited. We examine the field along three structural dimensions: (1) dataset construction and annotation practices, including subjectivity and annotator disagreement; (2) platform bias and cross-domain generalization across heterogeneous social media environments; and (3) context-related failure modes and evaluation limitations that obscure systematic errors. Across these axes, we identify a shared bottleneck rooted in dataset fragmentation, context omission, and reliance on aggregate metrics. Evidence from cross-dataset evaluations reveals significant performance degradation under domain shift, indicating that models frequently capture platformspecific cues rather than transferable emotional representations. Based on this analysis, we outline concrete research directions, including disagreement-aware learning frameworks, minimal context-rich annotation protocols, and standardized multi-faceted evaluation procedures incorporating calibration and cross-dataset testing. Our findings suggest that advancing emotion recognition requires principled dataset design and evaluation reform to enable robust emotion understanding in heterogeneous web environments.

title: Personalized Learning Path Recommendation System Based on Artificial Intelligence
authors: Lijun Li, Ying Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: With the increasing popularity of online education and the rapid growth of learning data, existing personalized learning recommendation methods commonly suffer from problems such as single-path generation, inadequate learning state modeling, and low recommendation accuracy. To address this issue, this paper introduces an AI-based recommendation method based on knowledge graphs. By integrating multi-source learning resources such as courses, textbooks, and test questions, a knowledge graph with prerequisite relationships and semantic associations is constructed. This approach, combined with a graph neural network, achieves vectorized representation of knowledge points. A dynamic student modeling mechanism is designed, incorporating a forgetting factor and a time series update function to precisely characterize changes in learners' knowledge mastery. Furthermore, this paper proposes a multi-objective optimization path recommendation algorithm that simultaneously considers minimizing path cost, maximizing learning value, and balancing content diversity, achieving fine-grained control over the individual learning process. Experimental results show that over a 12-week experiment, both the recommendation system group and the control group show an upward trend in academic performance and knowledge mastery, with the improvement in the recommendation system group significantly exceeding that in the control group. Specifically, in week 1, the two groups show relatively little difference, with average scores and knowledge mastery rates of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$64.5 / 42.3 \%$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$63.9 / 41.7 \%$</tex>, respectively, indicating similar initial foundations. By week 4, the recommendation system group's average score has risen to 71.2, and its knowledge mastery rate reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${5 6. 4 \%}, {2. 5}$</tex> points and 3.6 percentage points higher than the control group, respectively.

title: MMWave Blockage-Aware Handover Strategy Using Mobility and Blockage Models
authors: C. Siva Kumar, Deeya Julka, Md Ankushavali
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) links enable multigigabit data rates but are highly sensitive to line-of-sight (LoS) blockages, which can trigger frequent handovers and service interruptions in dense small-cell deployments. This paper proposes a low-complexity blockage-aware handover strategy that leverages user mobility and an obstacle model to proactively select the serving base station (BS). At each decision epoch, the user scores candidate BSs over a short prediction horizon using the expected SNR in future time steps and hands over only when the predicted best BS changes. The approach is evaluated in a discrete-time simulator with a single mobile user moving along a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1-\text{km}$</tex> road, static building blockages, and random short-duration dynamic obstacles. Compared with a conventional instantaneous SNR-based baseline (with a hysteresis), the proposed strategy improves average throughput by up to 5.4 %, reduces the outage probability by up to 32 % in moderate BS densities, and reduces the handover event by up to 75 % by avoiding transient blockages. The results highlight that simple prediction using mobility and blockage models is able to improve the mmWave connectivity without the training overhead of learning-based methods.

title: Deep Learning–Based Anomaly Detection for Cancer Analysis Using Autoencoder Models
authors: Anju Mehra, Irfan Khan, Damodar Tiwari, Kailash Patidar
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Cancer diagnosis is still a challenging task because of the high dimensionality of the data and the large class imbalance that exists between various types of cancer. Conventional methods of diagnosis, as well as most of the existing supervised machine learning algorithms, often face difficulties in handling such data characteristics, thereby resulting in decreased analytical efficiency. These difficulties have led to the need for an alternative approach to cancer diagnosis. The proposed approach uses a deep learning framework that involves an autoencoder to effectively identify typical patterns present in normal cancer data and deviations that represent abnormal data. Prior to training the model, the cancer data was preprocessed with utmost care to improve the quality of the data. After that, the autoencoder model was trained, and its performance was assessed. Based on the performance parameters, the model showed excellent predictive power for the normal cancer class with a precision of 0.7568, recall of 0.9495, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of 0.8423. The results validate the effectiveness of reconstruction-based deep learning models for anomaly detection in cancer datasets, especially when there is a large class imbalance between the majority and minority classes. It is worth highlighting that the proposed framework is meant to assist medical professionals rather than replace them. In general, the use of deep learning methods for anomaly detection has a lot of potential as a reliable and efficient method for enhancing outcomes of cancer data analysis.

title: Contemporary Advancements in Nanotechnology Based Epidermal Wearable Device Materials
authors: Koushick Venkatesh, Janani. P.
publish: 2025 IEEE Latin American Conference on Nanotechnology (LANANO)
abstract: Over the past decade, the significance of real-time health monitoring has grown, emphasizing the need for accurate clinical diagnoses and swift access to treatment options. The demand for wearable biosensing modules has surged, becoming a popular method for monitoring and as a preventive healthcare. However, to ensure the widespread adoption of these wearables as mainstream electronics, user acceptability and comfort must be prioritized. Despite the current focus on developing skin-friendly wearable technology, there remains a lack of comprehensive evaluation concerning the integration of smart materials to enhance user adherence. This overview briefs into the principles and practical applications of sensors crafted from environmentally friendly smart materials. Medical equipment's now incorporates soft electronics to improve patient tolerance and compliance, leveraging Nano-materials and Nanotechnology to enable the construction of smart wearable devices.

title: Solar Integrated Contactless Charging Technique for Electric Transportation
authors: D. Rajesh Babu, Vasupalli Manoj, Navya Kella, Vardhan Gunta, BalaChandra Kumar Bandlamudi, Sai Charan Gadagamma
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Traditional conductive charging limits the growth of electric transportation due to mechanical fatigue and cable management problems in addition to concerns about safety when charging in poor weather conditions. A new sustainable and fully automatic alternative to conductive charging is offered through solar-integrated contactless charging (SICC) and consists of: 1) The collection of Photovoltaic Energy (PV) via Photovoltaic Arrays; and 2) The Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) of collected energy. The primary contribution of this paper is the design and experimental validation of a solar-integrated wireless charging system using an LCC-LCC compensation network. Unlike traditional grid-fed systems, this work focuses on optimizing the MPPT-to-Resonant-Inverter interface to maintain high efficiency (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>90 \%$</tex>) under variable solar irradiance and coil misalignment conditions. Resonant Inductive Coupling enables the transfer of energy to and from EVs via the groundembedded transmitter pads and the receiver coils on the vehicle. A comparative analysis of various WPT topologies demonstrates that the use of LCC-LCC compensation networks delivers higher efficiency and greater tolerance for misalignment of the coils than the use of other compensation networks. Furthermore, experimental results on a 1 kW prototype operating at 85 kHz demonstrate that the integration of Solar Power reduces grid dependence. The system achieves a peak DC-DC efficiency of 91.2% at a 15 cm air gap, maintaining <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>85 \%$</tex> efficiency under lateral misalignments of up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 c m}$</tex>. This research provides the basis for the development of self-sufficient charging infrastructure, making it easier for people to embrace sustainable transportation options.

title: An Edge-Native Machine Learning Framework for Proactive IoT Network Security
authors: Babul P. Tewari, Umair Lari, Poulomi Mukherjee
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: The deployment of heterogeneous IoT devices and the traffic generated from them creates a larger landscape in transmitting the data through the network. Often, the data in transit are sensitive and voluminous. In this context, appropriate security analysis in identifying the generated traffic alongside computationally efficient processing is required whcih is the main focus of this manuscript. So, an edge aware IoT security system is addressed using machine learning (ML), where the ML model is integrated with edge server for a reduced cloud overhead and computationally efficient processing. The use of machine learning is backed by extensive initial research demonstrating the limitations of traditional rule and signature-based security mechanisms in IoT networks. Due to the dynamic and heterogeneity of IoT traffic, static rules fail to generalize various devices and applications. Machine learning literature highlights the efficiency of data-driven models in learning baseline behavior, identifying deviations, and adapting to evolving attack patterns. Towards this, a detailed analysis between the ML model and DL model is presented.

title: Intelligent Navigation Control Through Visionbased Hand Gesture Recognition Using YOLOv8n
authors: Sachikanta Dash, Sakshi Kumari Sinha, Kenguva Vineesha, Priyanshu Sekhar Nanda, Naween Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Advances in human-computer interaction (HCI) have enabled the development of intuitive, contactless navigation systems that enhance usability and accessibility across a wide range of applications. This work presents an intelligent navigation control system based on vision-driven hand gesture recognition using the YOLOv8n deep learning model. The proposed system performs real-time detection and classification of hand gestures through a camera interface, enabling users to issue navigation commands without physical contact. YOLOv8n, a lightweight variant of the You Only Look Once (YOLO) family, is selected for its favorable balance between detection accuracy, inference speed, and computational efficiency, making it well suited for real-time and embedded environments. The model is trained on a custom dataset comprising hand gestures corresponding to directional and control commands such as forward, backward, left, right, and stop. To improve robustness under varying environmental conditions, the framework incorporates preprocessing and data augmentation techniques, including background suppression, image normalization, and illumination variation handling. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed system achieves a gesture detection accuracy of 97.8 %, outperforming conventional CNN-based approaches in both precision and inference latency. The system operates with minimal delay, supporting seamless gesture-controlled navigation for applications in robotics, automotive systems, and assistive technologies. Overall, the proposed vision-based navigation framework is reliable, scalable, and user-friendly, highlighting the potential of YOLOv8n in the design of safe and accessible intelligent control systems that reduce dependence on traditional input interfaces.

title: Performance Evaluation of REBAR, VBF, and HydroCast Routing Protocols for Underwater Sensor Networks Using ARGO-Aided Simulation
authors: Shubham Kumar Singh, Balakiruthiga B, Akshita Ganesh Putta, Rajaram V
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: The need to have underwater acoustic sensor networks (UASNs) was highlighted by recent developments in underwater surveying and the management of natural disasters. Acoustic communication is the most feasible way of transmitting data considering the extreme attenuation of the electromagnetic signal in the marine setting. However, UASNs are faced with several limitations, such as low bandwidth, high latency, signal attenuation, and node mobility to give rise to time-varying network topologies. Although a number of routing protocols have been suggested to address these problems, they have typically been tested only on idealistic or even fixed network models, and these do not adequately reflect reality. In a bid to overcome this weakness, the current simulation uses empirical measurements of an autonomous drifting sensor, which simulates real environmental and mobility conditions. The simulation compares the three routing protocols mostly mentioned, such as the Reliable and Energy Balanced Algorithm of Routing (REBAR), Vector-Based Forwarding (VBF), and the Hydro-Cast routing, and identifies that performance features and constraints that are not apparent in the assessment of statistical models are realized under real operating conditions.

title: Equilibrium Optimizer-Based Optimal Scheduling of Multi-Source Power Systems with Renewable Energy, Electric Vehicles, and Hydrogen Energy Storage
authors: Hardik Modha, Jatin Soni, Deep Patel
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: This study describes a method based on the Equilibrium Optimizer (EO) to address the multi, energy economic and emission dispatch problem in an IEEE 57, bus power system that includes not only the conventional power sources but also renewable energy sources, electric vehicles (EVs), and hydrogen energy storage systems (HES). The developed model is capable of optimizing the scheduling of thermal power generators, wind and solar power plants, EVs charging/discharging, and HES operation simultaneously while satisfying multiple technical constraints. The paper considers three different scenarios: cost minimization, emission minimization, and combined economic dispatch. To assess the strong point of the EO algorithm, simulation studies and statistical analysis are carried out based on 50 independent runs. Experiments comparing the EO algorithm with other metaheuristics recently published such as Marine Predators Algorithm (MPA), Sine Cosine Algorithm (SCA), Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO), and Biogeography, Based Optimization (BBO) showed that EO leads to lower operating costs, less emission, faster convergence, and more robust solutions consistently. The study of the convergence plots also showed that EO maintains a better balance between exploration and exploitation, which facilitates it in finding the optimal solutions more quickly and with fewer iterations. Consequently, the study results demonstrated that the EO, based approach that has been proposed is perfectly suitable for solving the large, scale, nonlinear, and multi, energy power system optimization problems.

title: Advanced Predictive Maintenance for Aircraft Engines Using Multi-Modal Deep Learning
authors: Ayan Ahmed Khan, Gagandeep Kushwah, Ayush Gupta, Prateek Bhadauria, Laxmi Shrivastava
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive aircraft engine predictive maintenance solution based on deep learning models with optimal early failure detection. This solution addresses the critical problem of catastrophic engine failure focusing on early warning rather than accurate remaining useful life (RUL) predictions. We train and test four neural networks based on different architectures (transformer, LSTM with asymmetric loss function, CNN-LSTM, and ensemble) across four NASA C-MAPSS datasets, with varying operational complexities. Several novel aspects constitute our solution: (1) the asymmetric loss function that NASA encourages for penalizing late predictions, (2) engine-based split validation dataset to avoid leakage, (3) complexityaware feature engineering for per-dataset engine complexity, and (4) multitasking learning for RUL prediction and early warning binary classification. We show that the LSTM with the asymmetric loss function beats all architectures for three out of four datasets, with NASA scores of 5.66 (FD003) and 5.75 (FD001). Our solution achieves an early prediction ratio of 78.0% across all datasets, avoiding “surprise” failures. The transformer model yielded the highest performance on the highest complexity (FD004) dataset, demonstrating that it is well-suited for multi-fault and multi-operational complexity scenarios. We present an industrially applicable solution and theoretical understanding of model architecture selection for time-series-based prognostics.

title: Design of U-Shape and T-Shape Slot Loaded Multiband Square Microstrip Patch Antenna for GSM/Wi-Fi/5G and WLAN Applications
authors: Raj Kumar, Hitendra Singh, Ramesh Kumar Verma
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: This article presents a compact multiband square microstrip patch antenna using U-shape and T-shape slots. The overall size of proposed antenna is 42×42×1.6 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>. The Ushape slot is responsible for generation of lower and upper bands while the combined slot configuration enhances impedance matching across multiple frequencies. The presented antenna operates in quad band between frequency range 1.74-1.84,2.17-2.55,3.56-3.99 and 5.05-5.39 GHz with impedance bandwidth of 100, 380, 430 and 340 MHz respectively. The quad band antenna resonates at 1.77, 2.40, 3.79 and 5.22 GHz to provide return losses of -16.7, -21.96, 21.31 and -15.27 dB respectively. The presented quad band antenna is suitable for GSM-1800 for cellular mobile communication, Wi-Fi (IEEE 802.11 b/g/n) and IoT devices, 5G NR mid-band (n77/n78)/WiMAX, and 5 GHz WLAN applications. The quad band antenna also exhibits peak gains of 2.57, 3.74, 4.72 and 3.22 dB at resonating frequencies 1.77, 2.40, 3.79 and 5.22 GHz.

title: Exploring Ethical and Legal Challenges of Artificial Intelligence in Research Practices
authors: Mohammad Abdallah, Qasem Abdallah, Laila Barqawi, Daniel Staegemann
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: This paper explores the ethical and legal dimensions associated with the application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in research practices. As AI tools become increasingly integrated across disciplines, concerns have risen regarding research integrity, accountability, bias, transparency, and legal frameworks. The paper discusses key ethical considerations such as autonomy, consent, fairness, and fabrication, alongside legal challenges involving intellectual property, data protection, and liability. Recommendations are offered for the responsible and ethical deployment of AI within research settings.

title: Speech Emotion Recognition Using Deep Learning Models
authors: Rania Agoune, Douaa Hanane Kadi, Nada Eryhane Heltali, Said Gadri
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: This paper concerns the SER system using deep learning to classify emotions from vocalizations. We designed and implemented a CNN trained by a hybrid dataset combining RAVDESS, SAVEE, IEMOCAP and CASIA. In essence, MFCCs were extracted to capture salient audio features, and the data were further augmented with noise injections, pitch shifts, and time-stretching. The model could classify seven emotions, namely anger, disgust, fear, happiness, sadness, surprise, and neutral, with a classification accuracy of over 96 %. The results thus prove CNN-based models to be effective for addressing the complexity and variation of human emotional expressions in speech.

title: Adaptive System for Constructing a Mobile Robot Trajectory Using Neural Network and Stochastic Optimizer
authors: Amer Abu-Jassar, Hani Attar, Mohamed Hafez, Mohammad Hamdan, Vladyslav Yevsieiev, Vyacheslav Lyashenko
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: This work addresses the challenge of building and modeling an adaptive system for trajectory construction of a mobile robot inside a dynamic environment, considering impediments, fluctuating variables, and the necessity for certain goal attainment. A method is proposed that integrates a stochastic particle swarm optimizer (PSO) with a multilayer perceptron (MLP) artificial neural network, whereby the training facilitates the approximation of the trajectory evaluation function. Utilizing the Python environment with the NumPy, Matplotlib, and Scikit-learn libraries, motion trajectories under random obstacle settings and graphs depicting the neural network's learning dynamics were generated and visualized. The experimental findings illustrate the system's capacity to adjust ambient variables, circumvent obstacles, and approach the goal point efficiently while minimizing costs. The program effectively simulates the behavior of an autonomous agent exhibiting cognitive adaptation, hence justifying its further application in intelligent control systems for mobile robotics. The proposed methodology may be advantageous in autonomous logistics, search and rescue robotics, health system access, and adaptable manufacturing systems, considering the tenets of Industry 5.0.

title: Computing Pure Consecutive Maximal Periodic Patterns with $k \Delta$-Errors in Raw and Compressed Data
authors: Samuel Bismuth, Avivit Levy, Dana Shapira
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: Identifying periodic patterns in time series data is crucial for uncovering hidden structures and predicting future events. Recognizing meaningful periodic patterns in real data requires handling approximation criteria since periodic phenomena are usually inexact. This paper introduces a suitable criterion and focuses on detecting Consecutive Periodic Patterns (CPPs) with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k \Delta$</tex>-errors, where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex> bounds the number of errors and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta$</tex> limits the size of the error. We develop efficient algorithms to detect the Longest Pure Consecutive Maximal Periodic Pattern with bounded errors in both raw and compressed data, the latter by means of the Arithmetic Progressions Tree (APT) data structure.

title: MiFuse: Continual Fusion of Multiple Industrial Networks under Domain Shift
authors: Tian-Yu Zuo, Pan Li, Hao Sun, Kai Di
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Progressive integration of industrial graphs creates a fusion-first continual-learning problem that is not well captured by standard task streams. A learner must repeatedly absorb new graph domains while retaining certified behavior from previously integrated systems. This paper presents a 4-page conference condensation of MiFuse, a fusion-aware continual graph-learning framework for this setting. MiFuse constructs a temporary graph that reconnects current-domain nodes with bounded historical memory, applies reliability-aware weighting to cross-domain edges, and optimizes a multi-term objective including replay, distillation, prototype alignment, and relational preservation. Compared with replay and regularization baselines, MiFuse delivers a stronger stability-plasticity tradeoff on controlled industrial graph streams. The largest benefits appear under topology and compound shift, where preserving structural context is as important as preserving labels. The results highlight fused replay as a practical approximation of ideal joint training under bounded-memory constraints.

title: AI Applications for Power Quality Challenges in Distribution Systems: Technical Barriers and Emerging Trends
authors: Mohamed Khaleel, Ziyodulla Yusupov, Ali Hesri, Pirimov Odil, Rashidov Jahongir, Ibrahim Imbayah
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: The integration of renewable energy sources and power electronic devices into modern distribution systems has intensified power quality (PQ) challenges, including voltage sags, harmonic distortions, and transient disturbances. This review presents a comprehensive analysis of artificial intelligence (AI) applications for PQ monitoring and control, addressing both technical limitations and emerging trends in distribution systems (DSS). Unlike previous studies that focus on isolated AI methods or specific PQ issues, this work provides a comprehensive synthesis encompassing detection, classification, and mitigation across multiple disturbance types. Using over 70 recent publications, the study classifies key PQ disturbances, evaluates AI techniques-including machine learning, deep learning, and hybrid models-and highlights their performance in terms of accuracy, real-time processing, and adaptability. The article illustrate the advantages of AI in diagnosing PQ issues more quickly and accurately than traditional methods. One significant finding is the limited application of AI-driven control in power converters (e.g., voltage source inverters, multilevel inverters) despite their central role in grid interfacing and stability. The findings emphasize that AI enhances PQ management by enabling predictive diagnostics and adaptive control in renewable-rich, bidirectional grids. However, real-time deployment is constrained by challenges such as data scarcity, model interpretability, and integration with existing SCADA systems. The review recommends advancing hybrid and federated learning models, as well as edge AI deployment and explainable AI frameworks, to overcome these barriers. Furthermore, this work provides practical insights for researchers and engineers seeking intelligent, scalable, and future-ready PQ solutions in decentralized power systems.

title: Data Mining-Based Intrusion Detection for IoT Environments
authors: Belkacem Athamena, Mohamed Raid Athamena, Zina Houhamdi
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) integrates vast numbers of resource-constrained devices, creating new security challenges. Traditional Intrusion Detection System (IDS) methods often fail under IoT constraints (limited memory, heterogeneous protocols, and massive data volume). This paper presents a data mining–based IDS framework tailored for IoT. The framework employs supervised classifiers (Decision Tree and SVM) and an unsupervised K-means clustering approach to detect intrusions. We evaluate the framework on two public IoT datasets, UNSWNB15 and Bot-IoT, using standard metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC). Results indicate that decision trees and SVM achieve high accuracy (¿ 95%) on labeled IoT traffic, while K-means can identify anomalous clusters. We analyze detection rates, compare algorithms, and discuss implications for IoT security.

title: Research on the Construction and Application of a Digital Twin System for an Automotive Lighting Plant
authors: Tao Zhu, Huafang Wang, Jueyuan Duan, Jin Cao
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: This paper takes the digital twin system project for the automotive lamp injection molding and assembly workshop as the research object. It expounds the overall architecture, construction scheme, key technologies, functions, and application practices of the system. By building a digital twin platform, the system realizes visual management of workshop production, cross-system data interaction, and virtual-real synchronization, meets the requirements of Intelligent Manufacturing Capability Maturity Level 4, provides strong support for enterprises to improve production efficiency and reduce costs, and has reference value for the intelligent transformation of the auto parts industry.

title: Community Energy Management Based on Improved Multi-Objective Snowmelt Algorithm
authors: Yulong Zhang, Yuhong Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: With the widespread application of user-side energy storage devices in real life, it has become urgent to reasonably schedule localized energy storage devices to address microgrid voltage fluctuations and cost issues. This paper proposes a community energy management optimization scheme based on an improved multi-objective snow melt algorithm. First, the initialization is optimized using a chaotic mapping strategy, and a periodic oscillation mutation strategy significantly improves the model's convergence speed without increasing computational burden. Second, the non-dominated sorting strategy is introduced into the algorithm to improve its accuracy and convergence speed. At the same time, to maintain the diversity and uniform distribution of population individuals, a crowding distance calculation is introduced to measure the density of each layer of solutions after fast non-dominated sorting and to reorder the population, obtaining well-distributed Pareto solutions. This addresses the problems of the snow melt optimization algorithm being prone to local optima, lacking global search capability, and having low convergence accuracy. It reduces computational complexity while ensuring population uniformity and diversity. Finally, an optimization scheduling model is established with voltage fluctuation and operational cost as objectives, which is solved in the hourly stage using the multi-objective snow melt algorithm. Based on actual community data, simulations were conducted in MATLAB, and the results show that the proposed method can achieve good convergence and real-time performance while considering various optimization objectives.

title: Deep Learning Approach for Pneumonia Detection in Chest X-Rays with Visual Explanation Technique
authors: Abdelkader Alrabai
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Pneumonia remained a major public health challenge worldwide, particularly in low resource regions where access to timely and accurate diagnostic services was limited. Chest X-Ray (CXR) imaging has long served as a primary tool for pneumonia assessment; however, reliable interpretation often depended on experienced radiologists, whose availability was not guaranteed in many clinical settings. This study examined the application of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for automated pneumonia detection using CXR images, with emphasis on both predictive performance and interpretability of model decisions. Two CNN architectures, ResNet50 and ShuffleNet_V2, were evaluated using a publicly available dataset containing normal and pneumonia cases. The experimental results indicated that ResNet50 achieved superior performance, with an accuracy of 91.35%, precision of 91.72%, recall of 89.74%, and an $\mathbf{F 1}$-score of 90.56%. ShuffleNet_V2 also demonstrated competitive outcomes, recording an accuracy of 85.10%, precision of 84.68%, recall of 83.12%, and an F 1 -score of 83.76%. Beyond classification metrics, explainable AI techniques were integrated to enhance model transparency. In particular, integrated gradients were employed to highlight the image regions that contributed most significantly to each prediction, allowing visual assessment of clinically relevant lung features. The findings confirmed the effectiveness of deep learning approaches for pneumonia identification while underscoring the importance of explainability in medical imaging applications. The combination of reliable diagnostic performance and interpretable visual outputs supported the potential integration of such systems into clinical workflows, especially in healthcare environments constrained by limited expertise and resources.

title: Dynamics of Artificial Intelligence in Teaching and Learning
authors: Khawlah M. AL-Tkhayneh, Abdulrasheed Olowoselu, Hadil Hussein Farag
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) in education continues to evolve and offers many exciting possibilities for improving teaching and learning outcomes in schools. This study provides rational discussions on the concepts of AI, teaching, and learning. The chapter discusses processes and usage of artificial intelligence in education in the realm of language learning, assistive learning, early childhood learning, adaptive learning, lesson planning, text preparation, gamification, and learning management systems. This study further provides an overview of innovative roles of artificial intelligence in educational institutions, identified as professional development, cybersecurity, school security, students' safety, academic research, connected campuses, and universal access to learning resources. This study also explored the potential of artificial intelligence in educational institutions, such as collaborations, automation of information, and personalization of learning. This study further outlined challenges of artificial intelligence in education, such as limited ease of accessibility, ethical issues, difficulty integrating AI tools and high-cost resources, and implementation constraints. Fundamentally, the study concludes that artificial intelligence is an essential path for educational excellence.

title: Microwave-based Quality Control for Bread Production
authors: Marco Murgia, Luigi Di Michele, Matteo Bruno Lodi, Giacomo Muntoni, Andrea Melis, Giuseppe Mazzarella, Alessandro Fanti
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: This paper explores how microwave techniques can be leveraged to develop fast, non-destructive monitoring to the bread industry. We discuss a set of approaches that combine dielectric spectroscopy and microwave sensor design, and examine their relevance to modern bakery industry, reporting case studies related to traditional and gluten-free food products. Broadband spectroscopy from 0.2 to 20GHz has been used to characterize both Carasau and gluten-free doughs, with permittivity variations linked to water, salt and yeast content through three-pole Cole-Cole models showing errors below one percent. On the sensor side, we review solutions such as a hybrid Particle Swarm Optimization–Nonlinear Regression corrected coaxial fixture for in situ permittivity measurements (0.5 to 3GHz), and a 5.8GHz wireless patch antenna for non contact moisture tracking, with sensitivity of 1.25dB per percent water. The paper illustrates how combining advanced dielectric analysis with suitable sensor design enables real-time quality control and supports Industry 4.0 transformation in the bread sector.

title: A Comprehensive Evaluation of Photovoltaic Grid Integration Schemes Based on Entropy Weight and TOPSIS Methods
authors: Bin Liu, Lina Wu, Bo Yang, Dalong Luo, Xiulong Chen
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: The integration of photovoltaic (PV) systems significantly impacts distribution network voltage, frequency, harmonics, and network losses. To comprehensively evaluate these interactions, this paper proposes specific evaluation indices and a unified assessment framework. By combining the entropy weight method to eliminate subjective weighting bias and the Technique for Order Preference by Similarity to Ideal Solution (TOPSIS) method to rank alternative schemes, the framework provides an objective evaluation. A PSCAD simulation of three candidate PV integration schemes demonstrates that connecting a single large-capacity unit to the substation's primary side yields the optimal comprehensive performance. This method offers a scientific basis for optimizing PV grid-connection modes.

title: Operational Performance Comparison of Signalized Intersection, Multi-lane Roundabout, and Turbo Roundabout Using Traffic Microsimulation
authors: Abubakr A. B. Mohammed, Aeshah Alzayani
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Traffic congestion at signalized intersections within urban environments is one of the major issues throughout many cities. Therefore, it is extremely important to select the appropriate intersection control method for improving mobility, minimizing traffic delays, and enhancing traffic flow operations. This study presents the use of simulation-based comparative analysis to conduct a performance evaluation of a congested signalized intersection versus two alternative roundabout types: a multi-lane roundabout and a turbo roundabout. The comparisons were conducted via microsimulation using PTV VISSIM 2024 software under peak-hour traffic demand conditions, which provided a consistent basis for comparison. The simulation model was calibrated and validated. The key performance indicators, including average vehicle delay, queue length, and level of service (LOS), were extracted from the simulation outputs and analyzed for alternative intersection control techniques. The results indicated that the turbo roundabout demonstrates superior operational performance with an average delay reduction of 63 to 73% and a queue length reduction of 40% compared to the existing signalized intersection, along with an overall improvement in level of service. The findings suggest that turbo roundabouts represent an effective alternative to conventional roundabouts and signalized intersections in urban areas experiencing high and unbalanced traffic demand.

title: LiteMedNet: A Lightweight Direction–Frequency Enhanced Network for Cortical Bone Segmentation in X-ray Images
authors: Dongsheng Ji, Hao Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Accurate cortical bone segmentation from X-ray images is essential for bone structure analysis and osteoporosis assessment. However, conventional U-Net-based models often involve large parameter sizes and high computational costs when processing high-resolution medical images. To address this issue, this study proposes a lightweight segmentation network, LiteMedNet, which employs MobileNet as the encoder backbone while retaining the skip-connection mechanism of U-Net. To enhance the segmentation of elongated cortical structures, two modules are introduced: a Spatial-Frequency Hybrid Feature Enhancement (SFHFE) module and a Vertical Strip Convolution (VSC) module. The proposed modules improve global structural representation and directional feature extraction. Experiments on a femoral X-ray dataset demonstrate that LiteMedNet achieves competitive segmentation performance with significantly reduced computational complexity. Furthermore, the Cortical Thickness Index (CTI) derived from the segmentation results shows a significant correlation with Bone Mineral Density (BMD) (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r \approx 0.46$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p<0.001$</tex>), indicating its potential for automated bone health assessment.

title: Transformer Core Looseness Fault Diagnosis Based on Acoustic Spectrograms
authors: Chen Li, Fengyi Liu, Yanan Wang
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: With the ongoing expansion of power grids, monitoring the operational status and diagnosing faults in distribution transformers have become crucial for maintaining the safety and stability of power systems. Conventional approaches relying on electrical measurements often suffer from delayed response and limited robustness to noise. This paper introduces a novel non-intrusive fault detection method based on acoustic signals. The proposed technique transforms raw audio clips into Mel-spectrograms to generate discriminative acoustic fingerprints, which are subsequently classified using a deep convolutional neural network (CNN). Experimental evaluations demonstrate that our method effectively detects core looseness faults, achieving a validation accuracy of 98.26% and an independent test accuracy of 91.7%.

title: Research on Optimization Algorithm of Back Projection Reconstruction Based on Material Specificity
authors: Ying Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This study aims to optimize the CT back projection reconstruction algorithm for industrial components with different attenuation characteristics. Traditional general reconstruction algorithms such as filtered back projection have difficulty distinguishing the physical responses of different materials, leading to problems such as beam hardening and scattering artifacts, which affect the quality of reconstruction. To address this, this paper proposes a back projection reconstruction algorithm based on material specificity. By establishing an adaptive multi-material prior model, it precisely compensates and separates the contributions of different materials during the reconstruction process, thereby suppressing artifacts from a physical mechanism. Simulation results show that this algorithm can clearly distinguish the distribution of different materials in a fan-beam scanning system, with clear image edges and complete details, while balancing data acquisition and reconstruction efficiency. This research provides a feasible method for precise and efficient CT inspection of multi-material industrial components and has good engineering application potential.

title: Robust Multi-Modal Fault Diagnosis for Synchronous Motors Using DRSN and Attention Fusion
authors: Jiyu Wang, Linge Li, Pengfei He, Chao Mu, Xiaoqin Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: In this paper, a new electrical fault diagnosis framework of synchronous motor is proposed, which combines transient-aware data alignment, multi-modal anti-noise fusion and deep learning. The existing data-driven methods often lead to feature leakage due to improper preprocessing, and it is difficult to extract weak fault features under strong noise. In order to solve these problems, we first propose a transient-aware alignment technology based on gradient detection, which can accurately locate the fault start time and reconstruct samples to maximize the effective information density. Then, we developed a hybrid model combining CNN, BiLSTM and spatial-temporal attention to extract and weight the critical fault features from the spatial and temporal dimensions. In order to improve the robustness under strong noise, we add Deep Residual Shrinkage Network (DRSN) with soft-thresholding, which can adaptively eliminate noise components. In addition, we also design a multi-modal fusion strategy, which dynamically weights the electrical and mechanical signals through the confidence-gated network to suppress the lowreliability modes under noise conditions. Experiments on a public synchronous motor fault dataset containing six operating states demonstrate that the proposed method achieves over 99 % diagnostic accuracy, significantly outperforming baseline models, especially in high-noise environments. The framework effectively improves feature decoupling and noise immunity, offering a reliable solution for real-time motor health monitoring.

title: Research on Deep Learning-Based Temperature Control Methods for Heating Furnaces
authors: Jiaqi Shi, Pengfei Qu, Yangkexin An, Feng Pan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This study explores a temperature control method for industrial furnaces based on rolling optimization of temperature increment prediction and model predictive control. Industrial furnaces are widely used equipment in modern manufacturing, holding significant importance particularly in metallurgy, chemical processing, and glass manufacturing. As complex thermal systems characterized by large hysteresis, strong nonlinearity, and strong coupling, industrial furnaces exhibit temperature characteristics influenced by multiple factors, including nonlinearity, multiple time delays, and disturbance uncertainties. Traditional control methods often struggle to achieve high-precision temperature regulation in dynamic environments and lack broadly applicable temperature control solutions. This study proposes a temperature control method for industrial furnaces based on rolling optimization of temperature increment prediction and model predictive control. It employs a deep learning-based temperature prediction model for forecasting and model predictive control for rolling optimization. The temperature prediction model utilizes a two-layer neural network with residual connections. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method enables real-time temperature control for industrial furnaces. This approach significantly enhances both the precision and versatility of industrial furnace temperature regulation. This work contributes to advancements in the manufacturing sector and offers new insights into the application of deep learning for industrial furnace temperature control.

title: Software-Agnostic Thermo-Mechanical Model Exchange via FMUs for Microelectronics Reliability Simulation
authors: M. van Soestbergen
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: As microelectronic systems become increasingly complex, the need for accurate, efficient, and software-agnostic simulation models across the value chain has grown significantly. This work presents such a workflow by embedding thermo-mechanical Reduced Order Models (ROMs) of microelectronic packages in Functional Mock-up Units (FMUs) for efficient, IP-safe, model exchange. In this workflow, the full microelectronic system is partitioned into a microelectronic package (linear elastic, but temperature dependent), solder interconnects (highly nonlinear), and PCB (either reduced or full fidelity), allowing model complexity to be tailored to the application. The ROM of the package is obtained via static condensation (Guyan-Irons), retaining only interface degrees of freedom (DOFs). Rigid surfaces at the interfaces lower the number of retained DOFs even further while preserving dominant shear and rocking modes. To control the size and numerical cost of the fully populated condensed matrices, a nullspace preserving sparsification method is introduced. Here, singular value decomposition is used to extract the nullspace of the condensed stiffness matrix. Minimum norm correction removes negligible couplings from the matrix, while maintaining rigid body invariance using the nullspace, thus avoiding artificial internal forces. The FMU stores temperature indexed stiffness matrices and thermal force arrays in binary form and exposes them through a shared library. Compatibility is demonstrated in Marc/Mentat, confirming that the ROM FMU can be consumed without additional license dependencies and is portable across host platforms.

title: Non-Stationary Multivariate Time Series Prediction Model Based on MLP
authors: Luoqian Guo, Haifeng Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Non-stationary multivariate time series forecasting holds significant practical value for real-world applications. To construct a model with linear complexity and high prediction accuracy, the following challenges must be addressed: (1) Balancing non-stationarity handling to avoid over-normalization that eliminates sequence diversity and restricts the model's capacity to learn cross-temporal dependencies; (2) Optimizing long-term trend feature extraction to mitigate the over-simplification problem prevalent in MLP-based models while maintaining computational efficiency and feature representation capability; (3) Seeking breakthroughs in model design philosophy by appropriately increasing complexity upon simple architectures to resolve the contradiction between the low efficiency of complex deep models and the insufficient feature capture capability of minimalist models. Based on these considerations, we propose Sim-PolyNet. Experimental comparisons with both MLP-based and Transformer-based models demonstrate significantly improved accuracy.

title: Optimizing Kidney CT Image Classification: A Stacking Ensemble Approach with Preprocessing and Fine-Tuned Deep Models
authors: Fatima Ahmed ALjaafari, Amna Mohamed Elhawil
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Kidney diseases have seen a notable increase in recent years, with timely diagnosis being crucial for successful treatment. This study presents a comprehensive Deep Learning (DL) system for automatic classification of kidney diseases from Computed Tomography (CT) images. Four pre-trained models-EfficientNetV2B1, ResNet101, VGG16, and MobileNetV3 Large-were fine-tuned for multi-class classification of kidney CT images into normal, tumour, cyst, and stone categories. The predictions from these base models were combined using a stacking ensemble approach. The ensemble achieved a high accuracy of 99.80% with Gaussian filtering, outperforming individual models. Evaluation metrics were near perfect across all categories (AUC $=1.0$). These results demonstrate that proper preprocessing and ensemble learning significantly improve classification accuracy and reliability in diagnosing kidney diseases.

title: Robust Model Predictive Control for the Decoking Process of Ethylene Cracking Furnace Based on LSTM-ARX Model
authors: Baolin Chen, Zhong Zhao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To ensure the safety constraints of coke removal operations during ethylene cracking furnace decoking process and achieve optimal coil outlet temperature (COT) tracking performance, a robust model predictive control (RMPC) method is proposed based on a Long Short-term Memory-Auto Regressive with Exogenous Inputs (LSTM-ARX) model. With multi-stage historical data from the decoking process a time-varying parameter LSTM-ARX model is identified. Based on the identified model, a RMPC method is proposed by introducing a zeroing control barrier function (ZCBF) to a funnel model predictive control (FMPC) method. Industrial application results have verified the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: AI-Driven IoT-Blockchain Framework for Smart Construction and Safety Automation
authors: R. Ruthra, Aswathi K, Kaviyapriya A, Ahamed Sithik, Jeno C G
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: A paradigm changes in the construction industry, Smart Construction lays the groundwork for future building practices by incorporating technologies that make machines, gadgets, and materials more autonomous, aware of their surroundings, and able to interact with one another. Decisions may be made with more knowledge and efficiency thanks to this intelligence, which boosts productivity and safety in the construction industry. On the other hand, our knowledge of SC is still developing. Using ideas from smart items and ubiquitous computing, this study delves into what makes Smart Construction different from conventional methods and how it might be used to Safety Automation. The TF-IDF method was used for feature extraction after data preprocessing techniques were applied to transform, validate, and clean construction-related data. To improve the precision of analyses, a deep learning hybrid model was suggested, which would merge LSTM and GRU networks. Intelligent safety management and automated construction decisionmaking were both enhanced by the LSTM-GRU model's high accuracy rate of 95.29 percent. Adaptive, data-driven, and efficient construction systems that improve performance and safety in future infrastructure development are possible thanks to Smart Construction and Safety Automation, according to these studies.

title: A Gearbox Anomaly Detection Method for Rotating Pump Equipment Based on TadGAN Algorithm
authors: Zhibin Wei, Zelin Nie, Yuxin Guan, Kangning Zhou, Hao Liu, Zhaoguo Wang, Wei Cheng
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Rotating pump equipment may experience wear and corrosion of critical components during long-term operation, which can subsequently lead to various pump failures, resulting in significant economic losses and potential operational hazards. Traditional anomaly detection methods for pump equipment primarily rely on threshold judgments based on industry experience, which are often characterized by vagueness and latency. When confronted with massive, imbalanced data samples generated during actual operation, methods that rely solely on big data analysis without algorithmic support tend to yield suboptimal results. Therefore, this study adopts the TadGAN algorithm structure, based on generative adversarial networks (GAN), and designs an anomaly detection method grounded in reconstruction techniques. This approach provides an effective unsupervised learning framework for early fault detection in pump systems, enhancing operational reliability and reducing maintenance costs.

title: Visual Semantic-Enhanced Dense Reconstruction and Object Detection for Indoor Environments
authors: Shuo Kai, Wenli Li, Gang Bai, Yongfei Xiao, Yali Wang, Zhengguang Ma
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address challenges in indoor inventory scenarios-specifically the protocol conflicts between heterogeneous data streams (UVC for RGB and OpenNI for Depth) in low-cost cameras, spatial misalignment, and the lack of semantic information-this paper proposes a dense reconstruction scheme enhanced by visual semantics. First, a unified data acquisition interface is established, employing a hardware-software collaborative registration algorithm to achieve high-precision spatiotemporal synchronization of color and depth data, thereby resolving acquisition conflicts on Linux platforms. Second, the YOLOv8 object detection algorithm is utilized to identify inventory categories. Through a pixel-level semantic re-coloring strategy, semantic information is embedded into RGB textures. Combined with the GraphSLAM algorithm, this approach generates dense point clouds that integrate both geometric structures and inventory attributes, enabling a semantically enriched representation of the environment.

title: Sex-Specific Speech Features and their Association with Major Depressive Disorder
authors: Omar Zahy
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) is a mental health condition characterized by persistent feelings of distress. Women are more likely to develop MDD than men. Speech-based biomarkers offer a promising method for objective assessment. However, sex differences in speech features can lead to skewed performance and cause misdiagnosis. Our objective is to explore sex-specific speech biomarkers of MDD individuals and relate gender-specific speech features with MDD cases. We analysed 212 participants from the Extended Distress Analysis Interview Corpus - Wizard of Oz (E-DAIC-WOZ) dataset, containing 67 MDD participants (30 male, 37 female) and 145 health controls. Depression severity was assessed using PHQ-8 selfreport (cutoff <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 10$</tex> for MDD), which was pre-filled before the session by participants. We extracted 91 speech features across seven domains: energy, frequency, temporal, lexical richness, syntactic complexity, word types, and sentiment analysis. Statistical analysis employed Mann-Whitney U tests for gender comparisons, gender-stratified Spearman rank correlations with False Discovery Rate (FDR) correction for assessing the relations between speech features and MDD, and a linear regression model for examining gender and feature interaction effect on MDD status. After FDR correction, 25 out of 91 speech features exhibited significant differences between males and females of MDD individuals, primarily in the frequency domain. Male MDD patients had seven significant correlations with PHQ-8 scores (mainly in word types and energy domains), whereas female MDD patients showed 13 significant correlations (mainly in the frequency domain). This research suggests that gender-specific modelling may reduce diagnostic bias in speech-based MDD detection.

title: Multi-Objective Evolutionary Optimization for Fair Facility Location on Real-World Urban Road Networks
authors: Ranjan Veerabhadraswamy, P Dhanavanthini, Ajith Jubilson Emerson
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Infrastructure location fundamentally impacts community well-being, yet traditional k-center facility location algorithms inherently favor majority populations by optimizing for maximum global distance, often neglecting demographic equity. Incorporating fairness into these models is typically assumed to incur an efficiency cost. We measure this trade-off against standard baselines as the Relative Efficiency Change (REC). To address this efficiency-equity trade-off, we propose a multi-objective evolutionary optimization framework utilizing NSGA-II to simultaneously optimize for global efficiency and inter-group outcome fairness. We evaluate our approach across 240 experiments using real-world urban road network topologies extracted from OpenStreetMap (comprising 6,446 nodes across four neighborhoods in Bangalore, India), overlaid with synthetic demographic labels to model spatial segregation patterns. Our framework dramatically outperforms standard greedy and fair-local-search baselines, achieving an 84.72% improvement in the fairness gap. Crucially, we demonstrate a consistent negative Relative Efficiency Change (REC of -8.52%), suggesting that our evolutionary approach can achieve demographic equity while simultaneously discovering globally competitive, efficient solutions compared to traditional greedy heuristics. These findings challenge the conventional assumption that algorithmic fairness requires an efficiency sacrifice, highlighting multi-objective evolutionary algorithms as highly effective tools for equitable urban infrastructure planning.

title: DA2-LiDAR: A Generic Density-Adaptive Framework for Unsupervised Domain Adaptation in LiDAR Segmentation
authors: Yujia Chen, Rui Sun, Wangkai Li, Naisong Luo, Yuan Wang, Tianzhu Zhang, Feng Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: This paper addresses the critical challenge of domain adaptation for LiDAR-based semantic segmentation, particularly the significant density disparities that emerge when transferring models from synthetic to real-world environments. We present DA2-LiDAR, a novel density-adaptive domain adaptation framework that bridges domain gaps through the construction of intermediate domains with density-varying point distributions. Our approach employs a simple yet effective masking strategy that systematically reduces density discrepancies between domains while extracting more effective supervisory signals, as well as preserving critical semantic information. The framework consists of three key components: (1) a Density Adaptation Module that establishes a continuous spectrum of intermediate domains through dataset-agnostic masking operations; (2) a Contextual Consistency Module that enforces relational coherence across differently masked variants of the same scan at varying degrees, providing additional supervision signals, enhancing the model’s ability to extract features; and (3) a Semantic Preservation Module that mitigates information loss in heavily masked scans by reconstructing domain-specific data distributions. Extensive experiments on synthetic-to-real and other benchmarks demonstrate that DA2-LiDAR consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving significant improvements in cross-domain generalization without requiring dataset-specific prior knowledge or introducing computational overhead.

title: Fractional-Order Distributed Current Sharing Control for Power-Coupled Multi-Bus DC Microgrids: A Hierarchical Consensus Approach
authors: Rui Wang, Zhenghao Peng, Qiuye Sun, Dazhong Ma, Peng Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: Although the primary fractional-order PI controller, which can improve dynamic response features, has been widely applied in DC microgrids, the relative distributed secondary control has not been proposed to achieve accurate current sharing for multi-bus DC microgrids with power coupling items and composite load generalized disturbances (including constant power/current loads). Thus, this paper introduces a fractionalorder distributed secondary control (FODSC) strategy specifically designed for such multi-bus DC microgrids. Firstly, the standard fractional-order multi-agent state-space function model is built, including a primary fractional-order PI controller, a power coupling item, and so on. This standard model is an important foundation for hierarchical control design. Secondly, the state compensator is designed to solve the heterogeneous problem, which is caused by different energy resource rated capacity, RLC filter, and so on. Furthermore, the fractional-order distributed secondary control strategy is proposed to achieve accurate current sharing for multi-bus DC microgrids with power coupling items, effectively overcoming the negative damping effect induced by constant power loads. Meanwhile, the stability proof of the proposed control strategy is provided. Furthermore, a cyber-physical resilient mechanism is integrated to enable plug-and-play operations and system fault tolerance. Finally, simulation and Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) experimental results are presented to demonstrate the effectiveness and practical implementability of the proposed strategy in achieving accurate current sharing.

title: A PMIC-Based Component-Level Power Profiling Framework for On-Device AI Optimization
authors: Won-Seok Chang, Seung-Ryeol Ohk, Young-Jin Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: While model compression is crucial for on-device AI, conventional proxy metrics such as FLOPs and latency often fail to reflect actual energy consumption owing to complex system-on-chip (SoC) interactions involving memory hierarchies, interconnects, and dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS). Moreover, existing measurement techniques lack the temporal resolution and component-level decomposition required to analyze layer- and stage-wise energy variations. This paper proposes a noninvasive profiling framework that decomposes the power consumption across the CPU, GPU, TPU, and DRAM without external equipment. By combining power-rail-to-subsystem mapping, PMIC-based power acquisition, and statistical alignment with time-weighted attribution, the framework constructs stage- or layer-level energy profiles, even under low sampling rate conditions, adapting to delegate-specific execution characteristics. The evaluation of quantized and pruned ResNet50 variants revealed that the energy impact of identical compression techniques varied substantially by delegation and precision. Under CPU FP32, early stage pruning yielded the largest savings, whereas stem pruning incurred a penalty; under CPU INT8, only late-stage pruning offered marginal benefits. GPU results showed mixed outcomes in FP32 but overall reductions in INT8, whereas TPU efficiency diverged sharply by pruning location, with late-stage and global pruning proving beneficial, but stem- and middle-stage pruning increased consumption. These findings underscore the fact that no single proxy metric reliably generalizes energy efficiency, necessitating component-level measurements using real hardware. This study provides a quantitative analysis and data-driven guidelines for effective on-device energy optimization.

title: Diagnosis and Prognosis of Faults in Electric Vehicle Batteries Based on Real Data and Industry 4.0 Technologies: A Systematic Review
authors: Guilherme Matiolli, Vitor Furlan De Oliveira, Patrícia Teixeira Leite Asano, Romulo Gonçalves Lins
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: This systematic review synthesizes diagnostic and prognostic methods for electric-vehicle (EV) batteries, prioritizing real-world fleet telemetry and using laboratory/hybrid evidence as supporting context. Five databases (IEEE Xplore, Web of Science, Scopus, Engineering Village, and ScienceDirect) were searched following PRISMA principles, restricting the corpus to publications from 2019 to 2024. Eighteen eligible studies were retained (all published between 2021 and 2024); supplementary searches confirmed corpus saturation. The literature reports progress in data-driven health-state estimation, anomaly detection, and remaining-useful-life prediction, with selected studies achieving SOC estimation RMSEs of approximately 1% and fault classification accuracies above 90%. However, evidence for continuous real-time validation remains scarce, and cross-fleet generalization is rarely evaluated, with most real-world data originating from Chinese fleet deployments. A retroactive application of the proposed validation ladder reveals that 16 of 18 primary studies remain at L0 (offline retrospective), with none reaching L2 or L3. A complementary RAMI 4.0 layer mapping shows that the Communication, Information, and Business layers are systematically unaddressed. A study-level quality appraisal further identifies deployability evidence and missingness handling as the most severely under-reported items. To support translation into practice, we propose a deployability-oriented validation ladder (L0–L3) with a minimum reporting checklist and identify three priority directions: (i) harmonized information models (RAMI 4.0/AAS) for interoperability, (ii) lightweight and explainable algorithms for edge execution, and (iii) privacy-preserving multi-fleet benchmarking for robust generalization.

title: A Stabilized Map-Drift Algorithm for Airborne Video SAR Imaging
authors: Yanping Wang, Shuo Wang, Guanyong Wang, Zhirui Wang, Meng Zhao
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: For video synthetic aperture radar (ViSAR) imaging, motion errors often result in interframe drift, which degrades the stability of multi-frame imagery. The conventional map-drift (MD) algorithm compensates only for intraframe motion errors and neglects the influence of the interframe motion error phase. To overcome this limitation, this letter proposes a stabilized map-drift (SMD) algorithm to correct interframe drift errors that are not addressed by existing MD methods. Furthermore, a multi-initial-point parallel Broyden-Fletcher-Goldfarb-Shanno (BFGS) optimization strategy is introduced to precisely estimate the motion error. By constructing motion compensation functions for each frame and jointly performing defocus phase correction and interframe drift compensation during the imaging process, a well-focused and stable video SAR image sequence can be obtained. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated by both simulated and real data experiments.

title: Energy-Aware Routing to Large Reasoning Models_supp1-3688592.pdf
authors: Austin R. Ellis-Mohr
publish: N/A

title: Fast Simulation of Resonant and Conformal Metasurfaces Using DGTD-GSTC Method
authors: Kaiming Wu, Qiang Ren
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A discontinuous Galerkin time-domain (DGTD) method incorporating generalized sheet transition conditions (GSTCs) is improved for fast simulation of resonant and conformal metasurfaces. Existing DGTD-GSTC method replaces the metasurfaces’ subwavelength physical structure with surface susceptibilities, thereby avoiding the dense meshes and enabling fast time-domain simulations of curved metasurface. However, this method is limited to only non-resonant structure. To accurately simulate the resonant behavior of metasurfaces, we employ the complex-conjugate pole-residue (CCPR) dispersion model to fit the frequency-dependent surface susceptibilities and incorporate it into the existing DGTD-GSTC framework using the auxiliary differential equation (ADE) method. Furthermore, this paper addresses two key issues in GSTC-based simulation. First, the impact of normal components of surface susceptibility on stability is rigorously analyzed. These components are essential for accuracy but omitted in previous time-domain method due to a lack of stability analysis. This paper identifies the underlying mechanism of instability and proposes a corresponding suppression method. Second, the surface susceptibilities retrieval method for metasurface at media interfaces is provided, adopting the DGTD-GSTC method to conformal scenarios. Three representative examples are presented, along with comparisons to commercial software results in terms of accuracy and computational resources, highlighting the accuracy and efficiency of the developed DGTD-GSTC method for simulation of resonant and conformal metasurfaces.

title: Design and Control of Centimeter-Scale Reconfigurable Aquatic Modular Robots
authors: Kevin Macauley, Michael Zhao, Zhiheng Chen, Wei Wang
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This paper presents the Centimeter-scale Autonomous Reconfigurable Platform (CARP), a low-cost, self-assembling Autonomous Surface Vehicle (ASV) designed for modular and cooperative maritime operations. CARP features a 90 mm square top section with four independent active latching mechanisms, enabling dynamic connection and disconnection between units. The vehicle's motion is controlled using a Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR)-based optimal controller with weak formulation-based dynamic parameter estimation for improved performance and adaptability. We demonstrate CARP's capabilities through trajectory tracking experiments and multi-robot reconfiguration into collective structures. CARP's compact, low-cost, and modular design enables rapid fabrication and deployment of multiple vehicles, making it an effective platform for developing and testing control, reconfiguration, and swarm robotics algorithms.

title: WaveInst: A Frequency-Domain Enhanced Network for Fine-Grained Thin Tree Trunk Extraction in Forest Scenes
authors: Chenyang Fan, Xujie Zhu, Taige Luo, Zhulin Chen, Sheng Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Analyzing tree morphology, particularly trunk and branch extraction, is valuable for genetic breeding and forestry management. Existing image-based deep learning methods tend to misidentify overlapping trunks as a single trunk when structural discontinuities occur due to front-back overlap, while low contrast between trunk textures and the background further complicates segmentation. Moreover, limited juvenile tree data, coupled with substantial variations in trunk diameter across growth stages, restricts model performance in extracting thin trunks and branches. Based on this, we propose an instance segmentation network leveraging frequency-domain features, WaveInst. Its core is a Frequency-domain Feature Compensation branch, consisting of Discrete Wavelet Transform block and High-Frequency Enhancement block. The former performs high- and low-frequency decomposition and aggregates high-frequency responses along multiple directions, while the latter further refines the high-frequency features through multi-path processing. An Adaptive Gated Fusion Module is then applied to effectively integrate spatial-domain convolutional features with frequency-domain representations, allowing the decoder to utilize embedded frequency-domain features to enhance its fine-grained detail representation. We conduct experiments on public datasets including SynthTree43k, CaneTree100, and UrbanStreet, as well as PoplarDataset, which contains both mature and juvenile poplar trees. On the public datasets, WaveInst demonstrates strong performance and stable robustness across diverse scenarios. On PoplarDataset, it achieves a mean average precision of 53.1 for mature and 29.1 for juvenile, outperforming existing state-of-the-art methods on juvenile by 6.6 points, demonstrating its effectiveness in extracting thin tree trunk.

title: Big Data-Driven Multi-stress Coupling Creep Life Prediction of Rigid Polyurethane Foam Material
authors: Jianheng Li, Ruohai Di, Peng Wang, Liangliang Li, Xiaoyan Li, Zhigang Lv
publish: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing, Robotics and Automation (IMRA)
abstract: Rigid Polyurethane Foam performs structural support and thermal protection functions in high-end equipment such as aerospace, and its long-term creep behavior under multi-field coupling environments directly affects the safety and service life of the equipment.
