Aiming at the problems that traditional models lack physical mechanisms and are difficult to adapt to complex service scenarios, with a big data-driven approach as the core support, a physical model for multi-stress coupling creep life prediction based on the molecular relaxation theory and time-temperature superposition principle is proposed. Absolute humidity is innovatively incorporated into the model as an independent variable. Multi-source big data enables two key outcomes: normalization of creep data and extrapolation of long-term performance under multi-stress coupling. A multi-stress coupling creep experimental system is constructed, a large amount of aging data is obtained through accelerated experiments, and the model is verified with real data from nearly one year of natural storage. The results show that all indicators of the proposed model in this paper are superior to those of traditional models, providing scientific support for the environmental adaptability evaluation and full-life cycle management of Rigid Polyurethane Foam in high-end equipment.

title: Multi-Objective Optimal Reactive Power Compensation in Harmonic-Polluted Distribution Feeders Using an Enhanced MOPSO Algorithm
authors: Hassan Shokouhandeh, Mohammad Asadi, Mina Khalaj, Mohammad Ghaemmaghami, Kamran Yousefpour, Mahmood Hosseini Imani
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: This study investigates the optimal sizing and placement of capacitor banks in a standard 15-bus distribution network while considering the impact of harmonics. The main objective is to determine the best configuration of capacitor banks to improve voltage stability and minimize power losses. To achieve this, a novel objective function incorporating system harmonics is proposed. During the optimization process, several technical constraints are considered, including the maximum size of capacitor, voltage level limits, and the upper allowable total harmonic distortion (THD). Due to the nonlinearity introduced by harmonic effects, nonlinear optimization techniques are required. Therefore, the Multi Objective Particle Swarm Optimization (MOPSO) algorithm is employed to solve the problem. In the simulation process, the initial placement of capacitor banks is determined using the Power Loss Index (PLI) for each bus. Subsequently, the optimal capacities are obtained through the MOPSO algorithm to decrease cost and also enhance voltage stability index. The study is conducted under two different scenarios: the first assumes a harmonic-free network, and the second includes harmonic loads. The simulation results show that accounting for harmonics in the capacitor placement problem significantly influences the network's performance and optimization outcomes.

title: Building a Parallel ERA5 Download and Cleaning Pipeline for Large-Scale Weather Analysis and AI Training
authors: Harsh Patel, Eric Keller, Jonathan M. Snodgrass, Thomas J. Overbye
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: Large-scale weather data remains essential for power grid and renewable energy research, yet accessing decades of ERA5 reanalysis data presents significant practical challenges due to inconsistent file structures and asynchronous API constraints. This work develops a parallel, restart-safe pipeline that automates the end-to-end process of downloading, cleaning, and storing 84 years of ERA5 single-level data for 27 variables on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 2 5}^{\circ} \times \mathbf{0. 2 5}^{\circ}$</tex> global grid. The pipeline processes each calendar day as an independent task, retrieving one GRIB file per day from the Copernicus Climate Data Store API and consolidating five internal datasets into a unified <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4}$</tex>-hour structure. A centralized orchestrator coordinates two worker pools-thread-based for downloads and process-based for cleaning-while enforcing CDS rate limits and enabling fully idempotent restart behavior. Benchmarked on a 64 -core workstation with network-mounted storage, the parallel configuration achieves a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.7 \times$</tex> speedup over a minimally parallel baseline, reducing the projected 19402024 rebuild time from approximately 148 days to 40 days. These results demonstrate that maintaining a complete historical ERA5 archive is feasible on a single machine, enabling practical applications in large-scale analysis, database ingestion, and AI training workflows. The resulting database will support retrievalaugmented generation (RAG) systems for large language models (LLMs), providing contextual weather data for grid resilience analysis and decision support.

title: Undergraduate Research Toward a Linear Programming-Based Method for Estimating Feeder-Level Ev Charging Hosting Capacity Using Simulated Demand Data
authors: Siena Healy, Brennah Kennedy, Jonathan Snodgrass, Thomas J. Overbye
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: Electric vehicle (EV) charging can impose significant stress on distribution feeders, whose thermal and operational limits vary across space and time. This paper presents a linear programming (LP) method for estimating feeder-level EV hosting capacity using simulated demand data and publicly accessible feeder geometry and loading profiles. EV charging sites are geospatially mapped to feeders, baseline loading and hourly headroom are constructed, and a constrained <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4}$</tex>-hour charging schedule is solved for each node subject to feeder, substation, hardware, and availability limits. LP is selected for its convexity, guaranteed optimality, and scalability to thousands of independent node-level problems. In contrast to approaches requiring detailed proprietary distribution system models, the proposed workflow uses only publicly accessible data sources, making it suitable for undergraduateled research and reproducible by the broader community. Case studies on California feeders demonstrate how headroom patterns influence achievable charging, reveal binding constraints, and highlight sensitivity to charging efficiency and maximum power.

title: Design and Simulation of a Hybrid-Solid State Transformer with HEMP/GMD Mitigation Capabilities
authors: Timothy Donnelly, Lee Rashkin
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: A high-altitude electromagnetic pulse (HEMP) or naturally occurring geomagnetic disturbance (GMD) has the potential to impact electric power grids by inducing low-frequency common-mode (CM) currents. When these currents flow through grounded power transformers, they can drive the transformer core into magnetic saturation, degrading performance and potentially causing system failures. This work introduces a hybrid solid-state transformer (H-SST) that integrates a conventional magnetic transformer with a power-electronic-based solid state transformer. The proposed H-SST mitigates the harmful effects of HEMP/GMD-induced currents, thus enhancing grid resilience. Additionally, the H-SST also provides operational capabilities to the grid, including power factor correction, voltage regulation, and a fail-normal mode that ensures continued load service even if the power electronics malfunction. An overview of the H-SST architecture, power electronics, and control strategy is presented, and its performance is evaluated across normal and abnormal HEMP/GMD grid scenarios.

title: Lightweight 3D Gaussian Splatting Compression via Video Codec
authors: Qi Yang, Geert Van Der Auwera, Zhu Li
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: Current video-based GS compression methods rely on using Parallel Linear Assignment Sorting (PLAS) to convert 3D GS into smooth 2D maps, which are computationally expensive and time-consuming, limiting the application of GS on lightweight devices. In this paper, we propose a Lightweight 3D Gaussian Splatting (GS) Compression method based on Video codec (LGSCV). First, a two-stage Morton scan is proposed to generate blockwise 2D maps that are friendly for canonical video codecs in which the coding units (CU) are square blocks. A 3D Morton scan is used to permute GS primitives, followed by a 2D Morton scan to map the ordered GS primitives to 2D maps in a blockwise style. However, although the blockwise 2D maps report close performance to the PLAS map in high-bitrate regions, they show a quality collapse at medium-to-low bitrates. Therefore, a principal component analysis (PCA) is used to reduce the dimensionality of spherical harmonics (SH), and a MiniPLAS, which is flexible and fast, is designed to permute the primitives within certain block sizes. Incorporating SH PCA and MiniPLAS leads to a significant gain in rate-distortion (RD) performance, especially at medium and low bitrates. MiniPLAS can also guide the setting of the codec CU size configuration and significantly reduce encoding time. Experimental results on the MPEG dataset demonstrate that the proposed LGSCV achieves over 20% RD gain compared with state-of-the-art methods, while reducing 2D map generation time to approximately 1 second and cutting encoding time by 50%. The code is available at https://github.com/Qi-Yangsjtu/LGSCV.

title: Lightweight Multiscale Reference Frame Generation for VVC Inter Coding
authors: Pengyu Li, Cheolkon Jung
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a lightweight multiscale reference frame generation network for VVC inter coding, named LMRFG. Unlike the previous work [1], LMRFG does not employ the high-performance operation point (HOP) network as preprocessing for frame enhancement. LMRFG replaces the bidirectional motion estimation network with a dualbranch coordinated attention motion estimator (DBCA-ME), which integrates X-axis and Y-axis optical flows for accurate optical flow estimation. Moreover, LMRFG uses depthwise over-parameterized convolutional layer (DO-Conv) to reduce model complexity and minimize bitstreams while maintaining video quality. LMRFG adopts a quantization parameter (QP) distance-based training strategy that takes compressed data at higher QP as input and compressed data at lower QP as label for training, thus addressing the imbalanced QP gap between the compressed input and its uncompressed label. LMRFG is embedded between DPB and RPL to generate a new reference frame and replace the original reference frame in RPL. As shown in Table 1, LMRFG achieves average BD-rate gains of {RA: 4.31% (Y), 6.54% (U), 7.05% (V)} and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\{\text{LDB}: 3.81 \%(\mathrm{Y}), 8.90 \%(\mathrm{U}), 8.77 \%(\mathrm{V})\}$</tex> over the VTM_11.0-NNVC_10.0 (NN-tools ON) anchor, achieving state-of-the-art performance.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning Algorithm Based on Improved Heterogeneous Graph Attention Network for Solving Job Shop Scheduling Problem
authors: Ruihao Chen, Hongtao Tang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This study proposes an end-to-end deep reinforcement learning framework to solve workshop scheduling problems. First, an improved heterogeneous graph attention network is introduced to explicitly model topological relationships between heterogeneous processes and machines. Then, a type-aware projection matrix is employed to handle diverse edge semantics and fuse global-local features, aligning local operational constraints with global optimization objectives. Finally, the agent is trained using the Proximal Policy Optimization algorithm. Comparative evaluations against priority scheduling rules and other deep reinforcement learning algorithms on common benchmark datasets demonstrate superior performance in minimizing completion time and achieving zero-shot scalability.

title: Deformable Medical Image Registration Network Based on Context Fusion and Frequency-Separated Enhancement
authors: Xiao Hu, Ping Wang, Xiuyang Zhao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Unsupervised medical image registration aims to predict deformation fields via deep learning models to achieve precise alignment of anatomical structures, which is crucial for disease screening and treatment evaluation. However, existing deep learning-based registration methods often struggle to capture fine structural edges and large-scale deformations due to the neglect of cross-scale correlations, and they are prone to generating non-physical distortions. To address these challenges, we propose an unsupervised deformable medical image registration network based on context fusion and frequency-separated enhancement. The core of the network consists of a Context Fusion Feature Extraction (CFE) Module and a Frequency-Separated Global-Local Feature Enhancement (GLE) Module. The former integrates multi-layer encoder features via weighted fusion, leveraging inter-channel similarities to capture rich spatial details. The latter employs frequency separation techniques combined with Global Contextual Convolution (GCN) and dual attention mechanisms (spatial and channel attention) to simultaneously reinforce the model's perception of high-frequency local details and low-frequency global contours. Experimental results on cardiac MRI datasets demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms several state-of-the-art algorithms in terms of Dice and HD95 metrics. The results validate the superiority of the proposed architecture in capturing complex anatomical deformations, providing an effective high-performance registration solution for precision clinical diagnosis.

title: Bearing Fault Diagnosis Based on Multi-Level Feature Aggregation
authors: Wanzhen Wang, Beibei Kong, Xiaomei Ni, Miaomiao Xin, Min Song, Ziyuan Tan, Lipan Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This study aims to achieve accurate bearing fault diagnosis through a feature fusion strategy. Initially, vibration signals are segmented into prediction samples using a sliding window method. Subsequently, features from different scales are extracted and fused through a designed multi-level, multi-scale feature extraction and fusion model. The fused features are then input into an attention mechanism to learn attention scores, thereby enhancing the weights of specific features. Finally, the fault diagnosis results are obtained through a fully connected layer. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves accuracy, precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99.32 \%, 0.994$</tex>, 0.993, and 0.993, respectively, outperforming other comparison models. Additionally, the model maintains high accuracy even when the training sample size is as low as 0.1, confirming its effectiveness in small-sample settings. The proposed model can potentially play a significant role in industrial bearing fault diagnosis.

title: A Semantic-Guided Adaptive Exploration Strategy for Multi-Robot Dynamic Target Search in Unknown Environments
authors: Kang Wen, Shaowu Yang, Yu Tian
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Autonomous dynamic target search in unknown environments remains a key challenge for multi-robot systems. The core difficulty lies in the invalidation of historical information caused by target motion and the semantic blindness of pure geometric exploration. While existing prediction-based models track target dynamics, they often ignore environmental semantic priors, leading to inefficient and redundant search behaviors. Meanwhile, current zero-shot semantic navigation frameworks utilize Vision-Language Models (VLMs) for static navigation but lack mechanisms to handle the temporal relocation of targets, resulting in catastrophic failures when targets move between previously searched regions. To address these limitations, we propose a semantic-guided adaptive exploration strategy for multi-robot dynamic target search. In this framework, the multi-robot swarm dynamically adapts its search behavior based on environmental semantic distribution: a semantic searcher that utilizes VLM priors to intelligently revisit highly-correlated regions, and a geometric explorer dedicated to rapid area coverage. To achieve such cognitive adaptability, we developed a tri-factor Bayesian prediction module and a hierarchical topological scene graph that work seamlessly with semantic value mapping and temporal fusion layers, building a cohesive system for multi-robot semanticaware search. Comprehensive evaluations conducted in HabitatSim reveal that our proposed framework consistently achieves superior success rates and reduced search latency compared to baselines, particularly for targets exhibiting semantic behavioral patterns.

title: Fault Diagnosis of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors Based on FedProx Federated Learning
authors: Fanfan Zheng, Xiaoliang Feng
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: To address the generalization degradation of permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) inter-turn short-circuit fault diagnosis under multiple operating conditions, as well as the performance drop in federated training caused by decentralized data and non-IID distributions, this paper proposes a multimodal federated fault diagnosis method. Using current and vibration signals as inputs, a dual-branch 1 D residual encoder is employed to extract modality features, and a gated mechanism is used for adaptive fusion. In addition, FedProx is introduced to mitigate client drift, and a validation-performance-based aggregation weighting scheme is adopted to improve robustness under non-IID settings. Experiments are conducted on data with multiple speeds, grounding resistance levels, and no-load/load conditions for three-task recognition. Results show that, compared with FedAvg with equal-weight fusion, the proposed method improves grounding-resistance level and load-state recognition by 12.41 and 6.94 percentage points, respectively, while reducing the overall loss; compared with FedProx with equal-weight fusion, it achieves better overall performance, validating the effectiveness of gated fusion and indicating that aggregation-weight design has a significant impact on performance.

title: Carbon-Grid Loss Collaborative Optimization Strategy for Real-Time Accommodation of Renewable Forecast Error Power: Tiered Pumped Storage-Thermal Power Dispatch
authors: Yonggang Jiao, Yangyang Qi, Xuan Wen
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: Large-scale integration of wind and solar power introduces significant forecast error power (FEP) that challenges system power balance. To address intraday FEP accommodation, this study develops a real-time strategy leveraging joint dispatch of pumped storage hydropower (PSH) and thermal power. First, a tiered accommodation framework combining PSH and thermal units is established. Second, a carbon-grid loss collaborative optimization model is formulated for PSH-thermal coordinated dispatch. Finally, a dual-layer hybrid intelligent optimization framework is implemented: an improved MOEA/D algorithm solves the carbon-grid loss Pareto front in the upper layer, while deep reinforcement learning enables minute-level autonomous response of PSH-thermal units in the lower layer. Results demonstrate that the proposed strategy effectively utilizes existing hydropower resources, reduces thermal power dependence, and maximizes renewable FEP accommodation.

title: Electro-Thermal Co-Simulation of SiC MOSFET Double-Pulse Tests Using a Peak-Power-Threshold (PPT) Method for Junction-Temperature Estimation
authors: Filip Simjanoski, Nils Lurie, Mladen Gagic, Aditya Shekhar, GuoQi Zhang, Willem Van Driel
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: Higher switching speeds and power densities enabled by SiC MOSFETs make accurate, time-resolved junction-temperature (T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">j</inf>) estimation under realistic switching conditions increasingly important for performance validation, thermal design, and reliability assessment. This work presents an electro-thermal co-simulation framework for SiC MOSFET double-pulse testing, combining measured switching waveforms with a compact thermal network (Cauer model) to translate transient switching-loss energy into (T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">j</inf>) evolution. A Peak-Power-Threshold (PPT) method is introduced to robustly identify the switching interval and compute instantaneous power and switching energies (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf>, E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</inf>) from experimental data, enabling consistent comparison between measurement and simulation across few operating points. The proposed approach links dynamic loss extraction to junction-temperature estimation in a unified workflow, supporting more reliable interpretation of double-pulse tests and improving confidence in electro-thermal estimation for high-performance SiC power converters.

title: Distributed Photovoltaic Power Forecasting Based on Spatiotemporal Attention Mechanism
authors: Shunxin Xu, Cheng Wu, Binbin Zhu, Xiangou Zhu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: Accurate photovoltaic (PV) power forecasting plays a crucial role in ensuring the safe and stable operation of power systems. However, due to meteorological factors like solar irradiance and ambient temperature, PV power output exhibits strong volatility and randomness, posing significant challenges for precise prediction. To address this issue, this paper proposes a distributed PV power forecasting model based on a spatiotemporal attention mechanism. First, a patch-wise embedding strategy is employed, and a temporal attention module captures the dependencies between embedded patch tokens for each PV site. Then, a global aggregation module integrates local patch-level information to obtain a global sequential representation per site. These representations are subsequently fed into a spatial attention mechanism to model correlations among different PV sites. Finally, the learned spatiotemporal features are projected linearly to generate future power generation forecasts for each site. Experimental results demonstrate that compared with several state-of-the-art forecasting models based on Transformer and multi-layer perceptron architectures, the proposed model achieves superior predictive performance.

title: Thermo-Elastic-Viscoplastic Damage Model for Sn-Ag-Cu Solder
authors: Torsten Hauck, Aleksandr Morozov, Wolfgang H. Müller, Golam Rakib Mazumder, Jeffrey C. Suhling
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: A continuum damage model is proposed aiming to assess the failure mechanism of Sn-Ag-Cu (SAC) solder joints subjected to thermal and mechanical cycling conditions. Constitutive relations take into account elasticity, rate independent plasticity and rate dependent creep. Damage is introduced by the formulation of KACHANOV-RABOTNOV, where the material deterioration is represented by a scalar damage parameter that accounts for microcracks and/or microscopic voids. All material parameters are identified. Material characterization is conducted by means of a micromechanical tester and using miniaturized material specimens. The modeling approach is demonstrated for a 3-D finite element simulation of a solder ball. The evolution of damage zones is predicted for various combinations of cycling conditions and solder joint geometries.

title: BO-FiLM-MLP-Based Nugget Diameter Prediction for Multi-Condition Body-in-White Resistance Spot Welding
authors: Cheng Chen, Qibing Lu, Junpeng Yu, Gaocai Fu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Accurate quality prediction for resistance spot welding (RSW) in multi-material body-in-white production is challenging because of strong nonlinearity, condition-dependent distribution shifts, and limited destructive testing samples. To address this issue, this paper proposes a Bayesian-optimized feature-wise linear modulation multilayer perceptron (BO-FiLM-MLP) for weld nugget diameter prediction. The proposed method encodes material thickness and strength intervals into a continuous condition vector and uses the FiLM mechanism to adaptively modulate process-parameter features, including current, time, and force, thereby enhancing the model's ability to handle variations across different welding conditions. Furthermore, Gaussian process-based Bayesian optimization is employed to automatically tune the network architecture and training hyperparameters, improving robustness and predictive accuracy under small-sample conditions. Experimental results on 400 real welding samples from four typical sheet-material combinations show that BO-FiLM-MLP achieves an MAE of 0.0667, an RMSE of 0.0779, and a MAPE of 1.1172 % on the test set. The results indicate that the proposed method provides an effective solution for multi-condition weld nugget diameter prediction and offers reliable data-driven support for intelligent quality assessment in multi-material welding scenarios.

title: An Improved Convex Optimization Method for Power-Augmented Reentry Trajectory Optimization
authors: Zheyu Tong, Changjv Wu, Weifang Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This paper addresses the complex trajectory optimization problem for power-augmented gliding hypersonic vehicles (HGVs) equipped with solid rocket motors. Unlike traditional unpowered reentry, the constant-thrust nature of solid motors transforms the optimization into a challenging “bangbang” optimal control problem characterized by multi-stage discontinuous dynamics and sensitive ignition timing. To tackle these difficulties, an improved sequential convex optimization (SCP) framework is proposed. The methodology introduces an efficient initial trajectory generation technique and utilizes a multi-stage adaptive grid update strategy. By employing time parameterization, the uncertain engine ignition and burnout moments are transformed into stage-wise time-domain optimization variables, significantly reducing computational complexity while ensuring continuity between phases. Furthermore, an augmented state vector incorporating control rates is adopted to avoid high-frequency oscillations during linearization. Simulation results indicate that the proposed method effectively handles multiple path constraints, including stagnation point heat flux, load factor, and dynamic pressure. Compared to the unpowered gliding baseline, the single-boost strategy achieves an 18.27% increase in total flight range and an 8.9% extension in duration, demonstrating superior convergence efficiency and maneuverability enhancement.

title: Transformer-based SHM of Semiconductor Probe Cards: Unifying Optimal Sensor Placement and Missing-Data Imputation for Reliable Online Monitoring
authors: Mehdi Bejani, Marco Mauri, Stefano Mariani
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: Structural health monitoring of semiconductor probe cards is essential to prevent downtime and yield loss during wafer-level testing. However, the reliability of monitoring systems is frequently compromised by sensor data loss during operation and the challenge of identifying the most informative sensor locations. This work proposes a unified deep learning framework that merges optimal sensor placement with advanced missing data imputation techniques. We employ a hybrid convolutional neural network-Transformer architecture to classify failure modes, including cracks and loose screws, based on frequency response functions, and utilize attention weights to identify optimal sensor configurations. To address data completeness, we integrate the self-attention-based imputation for time series model. We evaluate the robustness of our framework by comparing this imputation method against k- nearest neighbors imputation and complete data as a baseline. Results demonstrate that while standard k-nearest neighbors imputation causes a drastic drop in diagnostic balanced accuracy (from 96.2% to 64.7%) and renders rare failure modes nearly undetectable (19.5% recall), the proposed self-attention-based approach significantly recovers performance (74.3% balanced accuracy, 40.0% recall). Furthermore, the self-attention-based method preserves the physical hierarchy of sensor importance, ensuring reliable optimal sensor placement even in the presence of data loss.

title: Intelligent Reverse Vending Machine for Waste Plastic Bottles Integrated with Compression System
authors: Dartwin, Vieri Kristianto Wijaya, Nicholas Biantoro, Endra Joelianto, Maria Ekawati Prasetyo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)
abstract: One of the existing preventive and persuasive solutions to promote the collection of plastic bottles are the reverse vending machine. However, the existing reverse vending machines have some weakness, such as their large size, high production costs, and the lack of a solution to reduce the size of the collected plastic bottles. The purpose of this study was to create an innovation in the form of reverse vending machine that, in addition to having a smaller size and lower production costs, also has an integrated compression system to reduce the volume of the plastic bottle waste. The methods used in this study include the stages of equipment procurement and design creation, programming, assembly, and testing of the system. The development resulted in a reverse vending machine with a size of 60 x 60 x 150 cm, lower production costs, and an integrated compression system that can reduce the volume of the plastic bottle waste by 65.17% for 1500 mL plastic bottles and 46.15% for 600 mL plastic bottles.

title: Deep Learning Model for Deforestation Detection Using Satellite Images
authors: Aruna M, Karthik Mani, Prabhu Krishnaswamy, Kalyan Kondisetty, Sundaravadivel Thilagaraj, Vijayakumar P
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Using satellite photography to identify deforestation is essential for monitoring environmental variations as well as protecting biodiversity. It precisely identifies patterns of forest decline by analyzing multispectral as well as temporal information. However, challenges such as cloud cover, seasonal variations, information imbalances, as well as complex terrain characteristics often hinder precise identification and method generalization over a range of domains. Hence, this paper performs the Deforestation Detection using Satellite Images (DDSI) model with the help of deep learning framework. The dataset is initially collected from the Mendeley data repository. The pre-processing is then performed using the cloud and seasonal variation mitigation technique. The extraction of the features is done with the help of Vision Transformer (ViT) embedding approach. Finally, the detection phase is accomplished by the Deep Neural Network (DNN) framework that in turn returns the final detected output of the proposed DDSI model. The proposed ViT-DNN is 17.29 % and 16.89 % superior to the other considered existing methods in terms of accuracy and recall for the developed DDSI model respectively.

title: FTA-SGNN: Encrypted Malicious Traffic Detection via Timestamp-Aware Encoding and Graph Learning Resistant to Frame-Level Perturbation
authors: Chengxian Wang, Shixuan Li, Ying Cui, Ding Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: With widespread adoption of encryption protocols such as TLS, malicious traffic increasingly exploits encrypted channels to evade detection, rendering payload-based inspection ineffective. We propose FTA-SGNN, an encrypted traffic detection framework that jointly models intra-session temporal semantics and inter-session graph topology without payload decryption. A KNN-driven graph reconstruction captures latent structural relationships across flows, while a timestamp-aware encoder and masked inductive graph aggregator learn discriminative representations resistant to protocol-compliant adversarial manipulation. Evaluations on USTC-TFC2016 demonstrate 94.72% accuracy on malware detection and 99.70% on benign traffic classification. Under protocol-aware pre-padding attacks, FTA-SGNN maintains over 93.5% and an FNR of 0.0011, whereas CNN and graph-only baselines degrade catastrophically to 58.44%. Under timestamp jitter up to 500 ms, accuracy degrades by less than 0.1%.

title: Lightweight Pose Estimation Optimization and Realtime Feedback System Design for Volleyballspecific Training
authors: Jinling Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Volleyball training increasingly relies on videobased analytics, yet most pose estimators remain either too computationally heavy for edge deployment or too fragile under the fast arm swings, frequent self-occlusions, and multi-player interactions typical of volleyball drills. This paper presents a computer-vision-driven decision support and coaching feedback system that runs in real time on resource-constrained devices. We propose VolleyPose-Lite, an edge-oriented pose estimation pipeline that combines (i) a multi-resolution shuffle backbone with conditional channel reweighting to reduce pointwise-convolution bottlenecks, (ii) a coordinate-classification head (SimCC-style) for sub-pixel localization without expensive upsampling, and (iii) a lightweight temporal consistency module that suppresses jitter using confidence-gated 1D convolutions and a compact GRU. On top of keypoints, we design a volleyball-specific feedback engine that translates kinematic features (joint angles, approach rhythm, shoulder-hip separation, take-off symmetry, and landing stability) into interpretable coaching cues with millisecond-scale latency. A complete end-to-end implementation is provided, including model compression (structured pruning and quantization-aware training), ONNX/TensorRT deployment, and a rule-and-template based feedback layer that remains explainable to coaches. Experiments on COCO as well as a pilot volleyball dataset demonstrate that VolleyPose-Lite achieves a favorable accuracylatency trade-off, sustaining 60+ FPS on an embedded GPU while improving keypoint stability and feedback consistency during high-speed spike and block sequences.

title: An Efficient Approximate Toffoli-Based Quantum Adder for Low-Power and High-Performance Applications
authors: B N Lavanya, K. Rasadurai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The basic components of both quantum and quantum-inspired arithmetic systems are quantum adders. Traditional direct quantum adders like the Vedral and Cuccaro ripple-carry adders provide accurate computation but have higher circuit depth, longer propagation delay and complexity of hardware implementation based upon extensive usage of Toffoli gates. This article brings about a comparison analysis of the current quantum adder designs, and it suggests an Approximate Toffoli Adder in order to enhance performance and hardware efficiency. The suggested design is designed with the help of Verilog HDL and run with Xilinx Vivado 2025.2. As can be seen from experimental results, the Vedral and Cuccaro adders have a critical path delay of 7.999 ns and 7.979 ns, respectively, and the total on-chip power consumption of 2.945 W. Conversely, the Approximate Toffoli Adder proposed has a low critical path delay of 7.261 ns, lower power consumption of 2.442 W and less hardware consumption with 18 logic cells than the current designs with 20 logic cells. Analysis of errors ensures that there is a minimal difference in the accuracy of the output. It is proven that approximate arithmetic offers a successful trade-off between precision, speed, and power efficiency to use in quantum-inspired computing.

title: A Random Augment and Class-Specific Focal Loss Enhanced Semi-Supervised Learning Method for Embryo Multi-Stage Classification
authors: Chuanlong Ma, Wenbin Du, Haicui Wu, Yuang Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: In vitro fertilization (IVF) is one of the most effective treatments to solve fertility challenges. It is essential to select the most promising embryo for transfer during IVF. Nowadays, several studies have employed deep learning methods for embryo classification, which can help to screen high-quality embryos. However, most embryo classification methods rely on large-scale labeled datasets, the annotation of which is both time-consuming and costly. To alleviate the burden of annotation and leverage the vast amount of unlabeled embryo data, we propose a Random augment and Class-specific focal loss enhanced Semi-supervised learning (SSL) method for Embryo multi-stage classification, which is named Embryo-RCS. First, we present a data augmentation method tailored for embryo image, named Embryo Random Augment (ERA). Second, we propose a Class-Specific Focal (CSF) loss function, which can effectively mitigate the issue of class imbalance in embryo classification. Finally, to enhance the model's prediction certainty for unlabeled embryo data, we incorporate an entropy minimization regularization term into the loss function. The extensive experimental results show that Embryo-RCS achieves an accuracy of 49.28%, F1 score of 44.08%, and AUC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 8. 2 2 {\%}}$</tex>, respectively, demonstrating Embryo-RCS outperforms the related state-of-the-art SSL methods for embryo multi-stage classification.

title:
A Feedback-Free Slope-to-Voltage Ratio MPPT Decoupled from T
                    <sub>ON</sub>
                    Generator for Thermoelectric Energy Harvesting with 2.5mV–750mV Input Range
authors: Xiudeng Wang, Jiakai Chen, Zhiwen Hou, Libo Qian, Qiujin Chen, Yang Liu, Yongyuan Li, Zhangming Zhu
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This work presents a feedback-free Slope-to-Voltage Ratio Locked (SVRL) maximum power point tracking (MPPT) for thermoelectric energy harvesting. The proposed SVRL-MPPT scheme operates by locking the ratio of the input capacitor’s voltage to its slope to track the maximum power point (MPP), thereby achieving three key advantages: automatic tracking, clock-free operation, and the elimination of feedback circuitry. Additionally, the MPPT controller operates independently of the TON generator, thereby contributing to the simultaneous achievement of ultra-low input voltage and a wide input range. The proposed harvester is fabricated in a 0.18-µm CMOS process with a low quiescent current of 25.5 nA. Measurements indicate a minimum cold-start voltage of 89 mV, with an input voltage range spanning 2.5 mV to 750 mV.

title: STELLA: A 16nm Spatio-Temporal Elastic Low-Latency CGRA for Multi-Stage Pipelined Applications
authors: Jun Yin, Chao Fang, Ryan Antonio, Xiaoling Yi, Yunhao Deng, Fanchen Kong, Marian Verhelst
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: Emerging non-matrix ML kernels, such as LayerNorm, GeLu, FFT or circular convolutions, demand low-latency, energy-efficient spatial accelerators beyond MatMul-centric arrays. STELLA presents a spatio-temporal elastic 16 nm coarse-grained reconfigurable array (CGRA) with a rapid configuration path, per-PE hardware loop control, and a low-latency, deeply pipelined elastic fabric with spatio-temporal data reuse. STELLA reaches up to 110 GOPS/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> at 850 MHz, and improves effective kernel throughput by 4.84-7.14x over baseline CGRAs.

title: Study of the Installation Sequence Planning of a Pipeline in an Aircraft Fuel Tank Fuel System
authors: Chun Liu, Liuwei Xiao, Jinhui Song, Changle Tian, Xuezhen Chen, Qiuyue Ran, Chenhao Ye, Huan Xiao, Shuxian Du, Yuangui Wu
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: In traditional papers on planning the installation sequence of pipelines in aircraft fuel tank fuel systems, high human involvement and limited efficiency are common issues. Therefore, this paper proposes an assembly sequence planning method using an interference matrix. A fuel tank test piece model with a complex pipeline system was constructed. By building an interference matrix to quantify the constraint relationships between assembly units, the precondition sets and postcondition sets were extracted, and the initial installation sequence was planned using the number of preconditions as an indicator. Meanwhile, based on limited human resources and with path balance as the goal, path integration and balanced allocation are achieved through multi-criteria optimization. Finally, the assembly efficiency was improved by 38.46% in the scenario of 3-person assembly, and in the verification of multiple cabins of the product, the overall assembly efficiency increased by 25% to 35%. The method digitally expresses the assembly sequence through an interference matrix and combines it with a human resource optimization model, providing a systematic solution for the efficient assembly of aircraft fuel tank pipelines and holding significant reference value for the intelligentization of aviation assembly processes.

title: Assisted Framework for Advanced IoT Intrusion Detection
authors: Zina Houhamdi, Mohamed Raid Athamena, Belkacem Athamena
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: In this paper, we propose an Assisted Framework for Intrusion Detection (AF-ID) as a structured approach to enhancing security in the IoT environment. The design model effectively detects and classifies advanced cyber-attacks through machine learning. AF-ID is interested in the attributes of data and domain knowledge involved during the feature selection process. AF-ID enhances the detection accuracy and efficiency of attacks, either by identifying whether they are malicious or non-malicious or ascertaining the precise types of attacks with high accuracy. Such attacks include HTTP-based intrusions, ICMP/UDP floods, Man–in–the–Middle (MITM), TCP SYN floods, brute-force password attacks, SQL injections, and XSS attacks. AF-ID can be employed to protect dynamic IoT systems, as its inherent scalability provides protection. That it does, in fact, meet the right real-world promise is verified via theoretical analysis.

title: A Semantic Matching Framework with Enhanced Spatiotemporal Consistency for Text-to-Image Personalization
authors: Hongyuan Gao, Yang Zou
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The objective of text-to-image (T2I) personalization is to incorporate user-provided reference concepts into pretrained diffusion models, enabling the generation of diverse images that faithfully reflect target text prompts. While existing methods, such as DreamMatcher, transfer reference appearance via semantic matching, they often struggle with complex scenarios, including discontinuous flow fields, temporal instability, and local artifacts. To address these challenges, we propose an enhanced semantic matching framework that substantially improves spatiotemporal consistency. Our approach features a multi-scale flow field fusion mechanism that estimates flow fields across resolutions using a confidence-weighted strategy to handle both large-range displacements and fine details. An edge-aware refinement module preserves boundary sharpness by optimizing flow fields around object boundaries, while a temporal consistency smoother leverages historical flow field information to alleviate flickering across frames. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms existing approaches in both subject fidelity and prompt alignment, substantially enhancing the visual quality and temporal stability of generated results.

title: Robot System for End-Beam Spraying and Beveling Based on Visual Perception and Cooperative Motion Planning
authors: Jingxiang Hu, Nan Hu, Yisheng Zhang, Kou Qiu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: In the intelligent production of prefabricated concrete bridge components, beam end roughening and interface agent spraying are critical processes ensuring effective bonding between new and old concrete. Traditional manual operations suffer from low efficiency, inconsistent quality, and high occupational health risks, while existing fixed automated equipment struggles to adapt to beam placement deviations and multi-beam type switching requirements, lacking environmental perception and adaptive capabilities. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a mobile beam end spraying and roughening robotic system based on visual perception and chassis-mechanical arm collaborative motion planning. The system integrates four core technologies: lightweight convolutional neural networks (CNN), multi-source coordinate system dynamic fusion, operability-driven collaborative path planning, and visual closed-loop feedback control. First, RGB-D cameras capture real-time beam end images, utilizing end-to-end trained lightweight CNNs to achieve unlabeled, high-robustness 6-degree-of-freedom (6D) pose estimation. Second, a complete pose transformation chain from camera coordinates to mechanical arm base coordinates is established to ensure precise mapping of perception results to the execution space. Third, an optimized chassis docking pose strategy maximizes mechanical arm operability by jointly planning mobile chassis paths and mechanical arm trajectories, effectively avoiding singular configurations and joint limits. Finally, a visual closed-loop feedback mechanism continuously monitors beam end pose changes during operation and dynamically corrects end-effector trajectories, enhancing the system's robustness to environmental disturbances. Conducted in a real prefabricated beam plant environment, the experiment covered various cross-sectional types including T-beams, box girders, and I-beams. Results demonstrated that the system operates stably with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 10 \text{cm}$</tex> beam positioning deviation, maintains a burr depth error within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 1 \text{mm}$</tex>, achieves a spray coverage rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{} \mathbf{9 8 \%}$</tex>, and reduces end processing time per beam by over 35% compared to manual methods. This study provides a viable technical approach for high-precision and flexible surface treatment in intelligent construction scenarios, demonstrating significant engineering application value and broad promotion potential.

title: Intelligent Routing Strategies for Quality of Service in Autonomous Vehicular Networks
authors: Kanagamalliga S, Vinayagam P, Reshma S, Sowmithra R
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Reliable communication is considered a critical requirement in autonomous vehicular networks due to their highly dynamic topology and strict Quality of Service demands. Rapid variations in vehicle speed, node density, and wireless channel conditions are known to cause frequent route failures and inconsistent data delivery. Conventional routing techniques are often found to be insufficient in maintaining low latency, high throughput, and reliable packet delivery under such volatile environments. Therefore, intelligent approaches have been increasingly explored to support adaptive decision-making in vehicular communications. The incorporation of data-driven learning methods has been recognized as a promising direction for enhancing routing adaptability and responsiveness. Network performance has been significantly influenced by factors such as link instability, mobility-induced disconnections, and unpredictable traffic patterns. At the same time, scalability and real-time responsiveness have remained challenging due to computational and communication overheads. Security vulnerabilities and privacy concerns have also been intensified by the open nature of vehicular communication channels. As a result, ensuring consistent Quality of Service while handling dynamic network conditions has been identified as a major research challenge. This study emphasizes the importance of intelligent routing strategies to address these issues and improve communication reliability in autonomous vehicular networking environments.

title: Cloud-Edge Collaborative 60-GHz FMCW Radar for Industrial Level Sensing
authors: Yue Yuan, Jinshuo Tian, Xijun Zhang, Xingyu Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: To address the issues of significant data drift and high maintenance costs of traditional liquid level sensors in harsh environments such as high pressure, corrosion, and high dust in modern process industries, this paper proposes a non-contact level perception solution based on 60 GHz Frequency Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW) and a cloud-edge collaborative architecture. The frontend utilizes 60 GHz wideband FMCW technology to achieve high-penetration millimeter-wave detection, effectively overcoming the multipath fading of traditional ultrasonic and infrared technologies in steam-heavy environments. For efficient data analysis, the system employs a low-power processor based on the Cortex-M3 core to perform Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) and frequency-domain peak feature extraction on serial data streams. Combined with a Direct Memory Access (DMA) mechanism, the real-time conversion of raw echo data into high-precision distance information is achieved. Meanwhile, the hardware baseband introduces an isolation barrier based on magnetic coupling technology and a high-frequency synchronous buck topology, significantly enhancing common-mode interference rejection. Furthermore, the system builds a lowlatency lightweight telemetry link using the MQTT protocol and innovatively proposes an Over-the-Air (OTA) firmware hotupdate strategy combining Vector Table Offset Register (VTOR) and Flash partitioning, solving the pain point of difficult on-site maintenance in harsh environments. Experimental and on-site simulation results show that the architecture achieves an absolute measurement accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 5 \text{mm}$</tex> under complex interference, with an error rate strictly controlled within 1%, demonstrating excellent robustness and industrial deployment value.

title: Leveraging Business Intelligence and Machine Learning for Robust Stock Price Prediction
authors: Zareen Alamgir, Faiqa Ashraf, Haroon Mahmood, Saira Karim
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Forecasting stock market closing prices is a critical task for business intelligence, as it provides insights for financial planning and decision-making. The complexity arises due to diverse market patterns and various external factors that influence stock prices. This study presents a robust framework that incorporates market styles, economic indicators and news sentiments to enhance prediction power. The machine learning models are trained on aggregated datasets from companies exhibiting similar market styles. Experiments using Yahoo Finance data from five major companies (Apple, Amazon, Google, Meta, and Nike) show that accurate identification of market styles by the proposed model significantly improves pattern detection, while sentiment and economic indicators further enhance prediction quality. The findings demonstrate how multi-factor modeling can strengthen business intelligence systems by providing more reliable forecasts and supporting data-driven financial strategies.

title: Analysis and Evaluation of Four-Wave Mixing Effects in Ultra-Dense WDM Optical Fiber Networks
authors: Sabrin Henish, Farhat M. Shaltami
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: According to the data center requirements for high-capacity information transmission, such as 5 G and 6 G networks, it becomes necessary to advance dense wavelength division multiplexing (DWDM) to new technology, such as ultra-dense wavelength division multiplexing (UDWDM) by utilizing the full available optical spectrum. This work analyzes the impact of the four-wave mixing (FWM) on the performance of optical (UDWDM) fiber communication systems over longhaul transmission. The study is conducted under varying conditions, including input (launch) power, different channel spacings $(12.5,6.25$, and 3.125 GHz). Eight-channel frequencies are selected using the Inverse Littlewood-Offord theory [7] to reduce FWM interactions. A detailed analysis of the FWM equations was performed to determine the channels responsible for generating FWM products and causing crosstalk. The analysis will focus on key performance parameters such as the eye diagram, bit error rate (BER), Q-factor(Q), and optical signal-to-noise ratio (OSNR) through experimental simulations using OptiSystem 7 and MATLAB 2022. The results demonstrate a noticeable improvement in BER, Q-factor, and OSNR.

title: Identification of URL based Attack from IP Data
authors: Cinthuja K, Kavitha Subramani, Naveen Raju D, AaliyaFathima A, Akshya U, Chubaranjani S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Cybersecurity has become a growing concern for both individuals and organizations due to the continuous discovery of new vulnerabilities in software applications and network communication protocols. Among these, the Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP), which forms the backbone of web communication, is a common target for cyber attacks, especially through the exploitation of URL fields caused by inadequate input validation, application-level weaknesses, and improper server configurations. This project proposes a system for the detection and analysis of URL-based cyber attacks by leveraging Internet Protocol Detail Records (IPDR) and distinctive features extracted from HTTP requests, enabling the identification of both attempted and successful intrusions. The model is trained and evaluated using simulated datasets generated through penetration testing and security assessment tools such as Wireshark, SQLmap, Burp Suite, XSStrike, Commix, and related frameworks. The proposed system is capable of identifying a wide range of attack categories, including typosquatting and fraudulent URLs, SQL injection, cross-site scripting (XSS), directory traversal, command injection, server-side request forgery (SSRF), local and remote file inclusion, credential stuffing and brute-force attempts, HTTP parameter pollution, XML external entity (XXE) injection, and malicious web shell uploads. Furthermore, an interactive graphical user interface (GUI) is provided to support visualization, searching, and filtering of detected attacks based on type, source IP address or IP range, and attack outcome, along with integrated PCAP analysis and result export in CSV and JSON formats for forensic and security investigations. In addition, the system incorporates an immutable forensic ledger to guarantee tamper-resistant evidence storage and a cryptographically verifiable chain of custody for all detected incidents.

title: An Innovative Methodology to Measure the Electric Charge on Honeybees
authors: Francesco Rigo, Alessandro Pozzebon, Susanna Spinsante, Stefania Cecchi, Ada Fort
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: This study presents a novel method for monitoring honeybee colonies by measuring the static electric charges carried by individual bees. Unlike conventional techniques, this approach captures bees’ activity through the detection of induced charges as bees enter or exit the hive. A dedicated chamber was designed to simulate hive conditions and house the charge sensor, which was connected to a charge amplifier and a data acquisition system. The results demonstrate the system ability to measure charges of the order of tens of pC, and so to detect individual bees at the entrance of the hive. As the literature proved the possibility to use the bees’ charge variations as a potential indicator of colony health, swarming behavior, or the presence of a queen, when integrated with traditional environmental sensors, the charge-based method herein proposed can contribute to a more comprehensive understanding of colony dynamics, and their interaction with the surrounding environment.

title: Research on Generative Design Method for Modular Intelligent Manufacturing Cell Layout based on Diffusion Models
authors: Zhihui Pang, Cheng Li, Wenkai Zhao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Manufacturing cell layout optimization is a critical combinatorial problem that significantly impacts production efficiency and operational costs. Traditional optimization methods and recent deep learning approaches struggle to balance multiple competing objectives while satisfying complex spatial constraints. This paper proposes a novel conditional diffusion model framework for intelligent manufacturing layout generation that integrates generative modeling with explicit multi-objective optimization. The approach develops a multi-channel grid representation encoding geometric, relational, and constraint information, enabling end-to-end learning of layout patterns. A hierarchical conditioning strategy combines global parameters, graph neural network-encoded material flow relationships, and spatial constraints through cross-attention mechanisms for fine-grained generation control. Most importantly, we introduce a gradient-guided sampling mechanism with annealing schedule that explicitly optimizes material handling cost and space utilization during the reverse diffusion process, rather than relying solely on conditioning. Experiments on 800 synthetic layouts and 3 real-world industrial cases demonstrate that the proposed method achieves material handling cost of 2847 units and constraint satisfaction rate of 94.3 %, representing 36.8 % improvement over traditional optimization algorithms and 9.8% over the best deep learning baseline. Ablation studies confirm the gradient guidance mechanism contributes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8. 3 \%}$</tex> performance gain. The framework provides a scalable solution for intelligent manufacturing design automation with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 - 1 0 0} {\times}$</tex> speedup over traditional methods.

title: Comparative Study of Global Safety Standards for Electric Battery Testing Laboratory
authors: Mahyati, Muhira Dzar Faraby, Ahmad Hamim Su’udy, Khairudin, Fitriani, Bagus Putu Yudhia, Ridhawati, Sirmayanti, Yoan Elviralita
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)
abstract: The rapid expansion of the electric vehicle (EV) and energy storage system (ESS) sectors has intensified the need for reliable and safe battery testing laboratories worldwide. Nevertheless, substantial disparities persist among existing international safety standards, resulting in inconsistencies in laboratory operations, certification procedures, and risk management frameworks. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative review of global safety standards applicable to electric battery testing laboratories, emphasizing key regulatory frameworks such as IEC 62619, IEC 62133, ISO/IEC 17025, UL 9540A, and major regional standards from the United States, European Union, China, and Japan. The analysis identifies fundamental similarities, critical divergences, and systemic challenges that hinder global harmonization of safety practices. Furthermore, the study proposes a roadmap for developing an integrated international safety standard aimed at enhancing laboratory safety performance, interoperability, and regulatory alignment within the global battery testing ecosystem.

title: TIME-E 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)

title: Large Language Models for Synchronized Power O…M Planning: Low-Cost Fine-Tuning and Alignment Via Power-Industry Requirements and Expert Feedback
authors: Rongfu Zhong, Jiahao Lai, Jiaju Lin
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: In the power industry, synchronized operation and maintenance (O&M) play a crucial role in ensuring grid stability and reliable electricity supply. Traditional O&M decision-making often faces challenges such as fragmented information, manual planning, and difficulties in adapting to dynamic conditions. Large Language Models (LLMs) offer a promising solution, capable of synthesizing heterogeneous data and generating structured, executable O&M strategies. This paper proposes a low-cost finetuning framework that adapts LLMs to power-industry requirements, using expert feedback to enhance model alignment. We focus on generating execution-oriented, auditable plans that can integrate seamlessly into operational systems while adhering to strict confidentiality and safety standards. The proposed method utilizes a two-stage process: first, fine-tuning the model with expert demonstrations, and then aligning the output to deployment protocols for verifiable, risk-aware decision-making. Our results demonstrate that incorporating deployment-oriented protocols significantly improves model performance in terms of truthfulness, informativeness, and confidentiality compliance. The framework enhances O&M decision support, offering a robust tool for the digital transformation of grid operations.

title: ContextPilot: Adaptive Open Source Software Onboarding
authors: Mazen Al-Asali, Mohammad El-Ramly
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Open source software relies on volunteer contributors, yet onboarding newcomers remains difficult and practices for ensuring quality are fragmented. Existing continuous integration pipelines, linters, templates, and documentation rarely adapt to repository-specific norms or explain failures in actionable terms, producing low-quality pull requests, prolonged reviews, and contributor attrition. This paper presents ContextPilot, a modular, multi-agent framework that provides guidance grounded in project artifacts. ContextPilot ingests repository metadata-such as readme files, contribution guidelines, workflow definitions, and linter and test configurations-into a retrieval index. Specialized agents for repositories, feedback, editing environments, and conversation coordinate with a large language model to transform logs, code differences, and policies into human-readable recommendations. We detail the architecture, data schema, prompting strategies, and safeguards. A prototype integrates with GitHub webhooks and a Visual Studio Code extension. Illustrative scenarios show how ContextPilot explains pipeline failures, offers proactive guidance during local development, and reuses organizational knowledge from prior contributions to reduce repeated maintainer effort. We outline an evaluation plan comparing standard workflows with ContextPilot-augmented workflows across efficiency, contribution quality, maintainer workload, contributor retention, and scalability, and we discuss threats to validity. ContextPilot turns quality enforcement from a gatekeeping step into an instructive experience.

title: Research on Hidden Danger Feature Extraction and Health Assessment Technology of Wind Turbine Based on Multi-Sensor Data Fusion
authors: Xu Wang, Fushou Sun, Wenfeng Xu, Rundong He, Xiaolong Yin, Zheng Cao
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional singlesensor monitoring in wind turbines — such as limited data dimensions, high hidden hazard detection rates, and insufficient health assessment accuracy—the study proposes a multi-sensor data fusion-based method for hidden hazard feature extraction and health evaluation. By constructing a LSTM-Transformerbased health assessment model, the method achieves quantitative evaluation of key component degradation and prediction of remaining service life. Validation results demonstrate that compared to conventional single-sensor diagnostics, this approach improves hidden hazard recognition accuracy to 89.7%, extends gearbox fault warning time windows to 142 hours, and maintains root mean square error (RMSE) in health status assessments below 3.2%. The findings indicate that multi-sensor data fusion technology significantly enhances the reliability of hidden hazard identification and health assessment for wind turbines, providing technical support for predictive maintenance in wind farms.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Cybersecurity Learning Platform Based on Article Classification and Knowledge Sharing
authors: Yi-Wen Liao, Siang-Yang Chen, Hsiao-Chung Lin, Chuan-Sheng Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: This paper presents a cybersecurity learning platform designed to bridge the gap between technical complexity and public accessibility. The system integrates web crawling, Chinese natural language processing, TF-IDF feature extraction, and Naive Bayes classification to automatically organize cybersecurity articles into structured learning modules. Unlike conventional platforms focused on specialist training, this modular interface targets non-expert users, emphasizing intuitive navigation, keyword visualization, and topic-based exploration. The results validate a scalable and AI-assisted model for enhancing cybersecurity literacy through public-oriented digital education.

title: Marionette: Data Structure Description and Management for Heterogeneous Computing
authors: Nuno Dos Santos Fernandes, Pedro Tomás, Nuno Roma, Frank Winklmeier, Patricia Conde Muíño
publish: 2025 24th International Symposium on Parallel and Distributed Computing (ISPDC)
abstract: Adapting large, object-oriented C++ codebases for hardware acceleration might be extremely challenging, particularly when targeting heterogeneous platforms such as GPUs. Marionette is a C++17 library designed to address this by enabling flexible, efficient, and portable data structure definitions. It decouples data layout from the description of the interface, supports multiple memory management strategies, and provides efficient data transfers and conversions across devices, all of this with minimal runtime overhead due to the compile-time nature of its abstractions. By allowing interfaces to be augmented with arbitrary functions, Marionette maintains compatibility with existing code and offers a streamlined interface that supports both straightforward and advanced use cases. This paper outlines its design, usage, and performance, including a CUDA-based case study demonstrating its efficiency and flexibility.

title: Experimental Prototype of an Open-Source Gas Mixer for Mechanical Ventilation Based on PID Controllers in Multiloop and Parallel Configuration
authors: Luys R. Santana, Manuel Batista, Sorhay Vásquez, Alan Alvarado, Pedro Alvarez, Edward Arevalo
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of an open-source gas mixer for mechanical ventilators using multiloop Proportional–Integral–Derivative (PID) controllers in parallel configuration. The system was developed from gas mixing principles by partial volumes and implemented through an electro-pneumatic architecture with proportional valves, sensors, and a dsPIC microcontroller. Hardware and firmware were fully documented, including electronic schematics, 3D components, and source code. Experimental tests demonstrated accurate flow control and reliable oxygen concentration regulation across different operating conditions. Results showed low percentage errors, minimal variability, and consistent performance, validating the system’s precision and stability. The proposed design meets ventilator requirements for FiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and flow control, providing a cost-effective, replicable platform that contributes to advancing medical ventilation technology.

title: CONCAPAN 2025 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)

title: Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) Based Interoperability implementation Using Sensors for an Hybrid Electric Vehicles
authors: Gondu Vykunta Rao, Sudha Radhika
publish: 2025 18th International Conference on Sensing Technology (ICST)
abstract: A safe & secure hardware-in-loop based hybrid electric vehicle testing is prerequisite for an increasing share of electric mobility development sector. The monitoring, protection and control (MPC) of HEV can be done by sensors, which can improve the reliability & safety. With respective their standards, there were two major challenges in the sensor data interoperability of the HEV such are optional functions and implementation. The assurance and achievement of the measurement and assessment methodologies can be done by interoperability modelling and testing certification. This work proposes a HIL simulation-based interoperability testing method for HEV in the closed-loop MPC use cases of testing. In this testing method, a sensor connected cord as hardware or device user test (DUT) is connected into the closed-loop MPC application simulation of HEV to test its interoperability with the test of simulation system. The working illustration of IEC 61850-9-2 test case interoperability done by HIL-simulation of 4-pin terminal merging unit (TMU) with Ti-F28379D controller. The test method focuses on major aspects rather than just exchanging the data among 4-pin TMU & protective relay (PR), its tests how the information can be used by PR from the sensors for MPC application. The The time factor considered for both test cases executed at same interval of time in operating the closing and opening of CB at 1.1 is synchronized in both the cases. The testing data further under use for the research application and for BITS WILP students learning from various manufacturing companies and OEM’s to develop testing system which helps to achieve interoperability and ultimately, plugand play of HEV with various sub-systems and sensors integrated for various resources for a hybrid vehicle HEV.

title: Underwater Acoustic Signal Preprocessing Method Based on Improved Non-Local Means Filtering and Adaptive Residual Fusion
authors: Shuaipeng Fu, Ranlu Xi, Huapeng Yu, Hanmin Sheng
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Ships and Electromechanical System (ICISES)
abstract: Addressing the challenges of line spectrum extraction from underwater acoustic signals under low signal-to-noise ratio conditions, this paper proposes an underwater acoustic signal preprocessing method based on improved non-local means filtering and adaptive segmented fusion. Built upon the Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) framework, the method employs improved non-local means filtering to process signal-dominant modes and utilizes segmented adaptive fusion algorithms to fully exploit residual information. Experimental results on the SWellEx-96 dataset demonstrate that compared to the traditional VMDWTD method, the proposed method achieves an average SNR improvement of 13.86 dB and an RMSE reduction of 81.25%, showing good robustness.

title: AI-Assisted Battery Monitoring and Energy Management for Electric Vehicles Using a Bidirectional DC–DC Converter
authors: Shanthini E, Dharshini R, Akshaya G S, Boomikaasri K
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) depend on effective battery management systems to maintain safe operation, make efficient use of stored energy, and extend battery life. This work presents the design and hardware implementation of a charging and discharging system for an EV battery using a bidirectional DC–DC converter. The battery charges when energy is available and supplies power during periods of demand, allowing stored energy to be utilized efficiently. Along with the hardware system, an offline artificial intelligence–based monitoring approach is developed to estimate the State of Charge (SOC), State of Health (SOH), and power flow direction from measured battery voltage and current data. Several data-driven algorithms like Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, KNN are analyzed, and the Random Forest model is selected based on performance evaluation like RMSE and MAE, Voltage and Current Monitoring, Power flow direction, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\boldsymbol{\wedge}} \mathbf{2}$</tex>. The chosen software model is then interfaced with the hardware system to provide clear and practical monitoring results. The estimated battery parameters obtained from the hardware system are displayed and monitored using a Python-based interface for result visualization. Software-based estimations are compared with actual hardware behavior to verify accuracy and consistency. By combining intelligent data analysis with conventional hardware control, the proposed system improves battery monitoring and supports practical EV energy management applications.

title: AICPS 2025 Committees
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Cyber Physical Systems (AICPS)

title: Machine Learning-Based Prediction of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)
authors: Sanchita Sabat, Tarun Jain, Shailja Singh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Polycystic ovarian syndrome (PCOS) is an endocrine disorder that affects a notable share of women in their reproductive years, yet misdiagnosis and delayed detection remain genuinely common problems. Because symptoms differ so widely between patients, no single marker can reliably confirm or rule out the condition. This paper presents a machine learning pipeline designed with earlier and more consistent detection in mind. Data from both infertile and non-infertile patients were used, and the cleaning phase was extensive - two source files were merged, entry inconsistencies fixed, categorical variables encoded, and missing values handled through imputation. SMOTEENN was used to correct the class imbalance. All 41 clinical variables were retained throughout, rather than pruning the feature set, so that models had access to the more subtle cross-system correlations that a reduced input would obscure. A total of twelve classifiers were tested: logistic regression, decision trees, Naive Bayes, SVM, XGBoost, AdaBoost, KNN, two ensemble configurations, and a Functional API deep learning model. Evaluated over 50 trials with different random seeds, the deep learning model hit a top accuracy of 89.91 % and an F1score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 5. 7 1 \%}$</tex>, obtained at a classification threshold of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 3}$</tex>.

title: Intelligent Voting-Based Ensemble Model for Early Detection of Alzheimer's Disease
authors: Komal Parashar, Malavika Tirunagari, Mani Banu Sai Teja, Sohail Shaik
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Alzheimer disease (AD) is a neurodegenerative disease that disrupts the lives of millions of people all over the world causing them to lose memory, decline in cognitive capacities and eventually, become unable to perform at their own will. The most important thing is that early detection is the key to successful intervention and treatment since it may aid in the slowing of the disease progression and increase the living standards of the patients. The given paper presents an intelligent voting-based ensemble paradigm to identify the presence of the Alzheimer disease early on, employing a mixture of ML classifiers, such as Random Forest, XGBoost, SVM, and MLP. These single models are combined into a predictive adaptive weighted voting ensemble that is greatly beneficial in predictive performance. The offered ensemble strategy has an accuracy of 97% which suggests a solid and strong solution to the early detection of the AD. This approach shows the possibilities of ensemble learning in medical care, especially in enhancing the accuracy and reliability of the diagnostic technology of such a complicated disease as Alzheimer. The findings indicate that the ensemble model may be a useful instrument to clinicians, and it offers pertinent information needed to make an early diagnosis and adequate interventions in the management.

title: HDR-DTCP: High-Dynamic-Range Injectable Differential Tissue-Coupled Powering for Thread-Like Battery-Free Wireless Stimulators
