authors: Asif I. Omi, Anyu Jiang, Han Wu, Sultan Mahmud, Mali Halac, Shreyas Sen, Shriya Srinivasan, Adam Khalifa, Baibhab Chatterjee
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: Implantable stimulators increasingly demand wireless power, yet prevailing inductive, ultrasonic, magnetoelectric, optical, and mid-field RF links cannot simultaneously achieve high efficiency, alignment robustness, and deep nerve target in a truly needle-deliverable geometry. Strongly frequency and site-dependent tissue impedances, posture-driven coupling shifts, and changing stimulation loads further prevent conventional energy harvesters from maintaining a stable supply. We present High-Dynamic-Range Injectable Differential Tissue-Coupled Powering (HDR-DTCP), a high-frequency body-coupled technique where skin-surface differential electrodes drive AC through tissue and a threadlike implant differentially picks it up for on-chip HDR rectification. Because coupling is distributed over the implant length rather than its cross-section, HDR-DTCP yields length-scalable power transfer efficiency, enabling a thread-like form factor conducive to minimally invasive injection methods that are currently unattainable with coils or laminate or transducer-based approaches. The proposed 65 nm prototype integrates a reconfigurable 4-stage harvester that achieves 63.5% peak power conversion efficiency (PCE) at –16.3 dBm and sustains > 50% PCE over a 9 dB input range, together with a digitally tunable biphasic stimulator capable of driving loads up to R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</inf> > 1 kΩ and C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</inf> < 20 pF, pointing toward fully injectable neurostimulators.

title: A 28.71ppm/°C Voltage Reference with 0.00002% Line Sensitivity and 35μs Startup Time for High Voltage Applications
authors: Yining Wang, Xing Li, Yan Lu
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper proposes a small area, high PSRR, low quiescent current and fast startup voltage reference for high supply voltage. A current-source-based voltage-limiting loop reduces chip area and quiescent current significantly. A PSRR enhancement circuit and a startup acceleration circuit improve full-band PSRR and startup speed. The chip supports up to 100V and achieves 28.71 ppm/°C TC from -20°C to 80°C, 0.00002% LS, 185 nA quiescent current, -61dB full-band PSRR and 35 μs start-up time.

title: EinChip: A 4.6/1.5 TOPS/W 6/24b Log-compute Einsum-based Accelerator for Neuro Symbolic AI
authors: Lingyun Yao, Shirui Zhao, Marian Verhelst, Martin Andraud
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: Neuro Symbolic (NeSy) AI combines deep neural networks (DNNs) with symbolic reasoning, but existing DNN accelerators are ill-suited for NeSy workloads. We present EinChip, a unified NeSy accelerator that expresses both neuro and symbolic models in Einstein Summation Notation (Einsum) and executes them on an approximate 6/24-bit logarithmic processing unit. A 4mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> 16nm prototype achieves 4.6 TOPS/W on neuro tasks and 1.5 TOPS/W on symbolic tasks with only 0.1% accuracy loss.

title: Audio-Visual Cross-Modal Compression for Generative Face Video Coding
authors: Youmin Xu, Mengxi Guo, Shijie Zhao, Weiqi Li, Junlin Li, Li Zhang, Jian Zhang
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: Generative face video coding (GFVC) is vital for modern applications like video conferencing, yet existing methods primarily focus on video motion while neglecting the significant bitrate contribution of audio. Despite the well-established correlation between audio and lip movements, this cross-modal coherence has not been systematically exploited for compression. To address this, we propose an Audio-Visual Cross-Modal Compression (AVCC) framework that jointly compresses audio and video streams. Our framework extracts motion information from video and tokenizes audio features, then aligns them through a unified audio-video diffusion process. This allows synchronized reconstruction of both modalities from a shared representation. In extremely low-rate scenarios, AVCC can even reconstruct one modality from the other. Experiments show that AVCC significantly outperforms the Versatile Video Coding (VVC) standard and state-of-the-art GFVC schemes in rate-distortion performance, paving the way for more efficient multimodal communication systems.

title: Comparative Study of Power Control Strategies for DFIG Wind Turbines: PI vs Sliding Mode
authors: Mohamed Ilyas Rahal, Mourad Naidji, Adel Makhbouche, Zine Eddine Meguetta, Gerardo Ayala-Jaimes
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: The electrical subsystem of a wind energy conversion system is the main subject of this research. A thorough mathematical model of the Doubly-Fed Induction Generator (DFIG) is created in the first section, accounting for its dynamic behaviour in different wind situations. In order to achieve independent regulation of active and reactive power—a crucial component of grid stability and effective energy transfer—a control strategy is suggested based on this model. A Sliding Mode Controller (SMC) and a Proportional-Integral (PI) controller are the two controller kinds that are designed and put into use. The effectiveness of both control schemes is assessed through MATLAB/Simulink simulation. The outcomes show how well each technique works to guarantee dynamic stability and precise power control.

title: Optimal PI Controller Tuning for PMSM Speed Control Using PSO with an Asymmetric Cost Function
authors: Tariq Alkaar, Mohammed M. B. Makarussu, Ali Almaktoof, Salah Mousa, Abdulhamed Essed
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: This research examines a method to control the velocity of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSMs) more effectively, cases in relation to Field-Oriented Control (FOC) using Particle Swarm Optimising techniques to refine the parameters of Proportional-Integral Controllers (PID Controllers). The existing ways of tuning the above controllers using the trial-and-error method tend to yield substantial overshoots (23.63%) and long settling times (160 ms). Besides, tuning the PI Controllers using PSO with the classic methods of determining the cost functions (Integral Absolute Error (IAE) and Integral Square Error (ISE) will improve performance levels; however, an increase of at least 6.1% on overshooting is monitored still. Due to this situation, a new Asymmetrical Cost Function has been developed to reduce this percentage significantly by rewarding the optimum tracking error metric more as compared to the other two standard cost functions. The results of the simulation indicated that the proposed technique has successfully removed the overshoot (0%) and enabled the achieving the fasted settling time (24 ms) with the least steadystate error. Therefore, the Asymmetrical PSO method has produced a $\mathbf{5 1 \%}$ reduction of the settling time when compared to the original PSO method and an 85% reduction when compared to manual tuning of the controller error metrics. This work has been able to provide a framework for enhancing PMSM Controller Performance in applications requiring a high level of precision and minimal if not no error percent overshoot.

title: Sentiment Analysis Method of Chinese Fraud Short Messages Based on Transformer Model
authors: Yang Chen, Nitthanet Natthakunlanan, Parkpoom Chaisiriprasert
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: Telecom fraud poses a significant threat to public safety and financial security, particularly in regions with high mobile phone usage. This study proposes a Chinese text classification framework for detecting telecom fraud using advanced natural language processing techniques. The methodology begins with a robust data pre-processing pipeline, including data cleaning, handling missing values, and Chinesespecific tokenization using HanLP, followed by auto tokenization and the removal of stopwords and punctuation. These steps ensure the linguistic accuracy of the processed Chinese text. This study acquired two public datasets from CSDN and GitHub were used, comprising 40,212 Chinese telecom fraud and legitimate messages. After preprocessing, text is converted into numerical features using TF-IDF and word embeddings.The extracted features are then used to train and evaluate three pre-trained transformer-based language models: MacBERT, RoBERTa, and ALBERT each fine-tuned for Chinese language processing. The classification performance of these models is compared to identify the most effective approach for telecom fraud detection. These models have demonstrated performance of above 99% in terms of accuracy, F1 score, mAP, and AUC, according to the experimental findings. The display results after using HanLP word segmentation preprocessing revealed that the accuracy was 99.86%, the F1 score was 99.855%, the mAP was 100%, and the AUC was 100%. The results of this study demonstrate the feasibility of using deep learning-based NLP methods for classifying Chinese text in fraud-related contexts. This research contributes to the development of intelligent systems capable of enhancing telecom security by identifying fraudulent content with high accuracy.

title: A Sub-microwatt Blocker-Resilient Crystal-free Receiver for Ambient IoT Applications
authors: Ahmed Gharib Gadelkarim, Patrick Mercier
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper presents a receiver designed for Ambient IoT-style needs: sub-μW power, SIR<-40dB, and no crystal or off-chip matching networks. It does so by exploiting the power tone used for RF harvesting as an intermodulator processed by an input rectifier to mix the signal to IF with zero power. There, an N-path HRM is used to provide 51dB of rejection while down-converting to an uncertain 2nd IF, where I/Q signals are then demodulated. Implemented in 22nm, the chip consumes 713nW while achieving an SIR of -45.9dB.

title: A Supervised Artificial Intelligence Framework for Schizophrenia Detection Using Twitter Data
authors: Hossein Sadr, Zeynab Barzgar, Mojdeh Nazari, Shayan Rotami
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Web Research (ICWR)
abstract: Detecting schizophrenia in individuals who do not actively seek clinical care remains a significant psychiatric challenge. While traditional diagnoses rely heavily on clinical interviews, the widespread use of social media offers a novel, data-rich avenue for observing behavioral and linguistic markers. However, extracting diagnostic insights for complex mental health conditions from unstructured textual data presents unique analytical difficulties. This study proposes a supervised machine learning approach to diagnose schizophrenia using natural language processing (NLP) on Twitter data. We constructed a dataset of 320,856 tweets from 328 users, consisting of 164 individuals with self-disclosed schizophrenia and 164 matched control subjects. By extracting textual and user-centric features via specialized psychological dictionaries, we evaluated the efficacy of logistic regression and random forest classifiers. The results demonstrated that the random forest model achieved a highly promising classification accuracy of 90.12%. Furthermore, profound linguistic differences were identified: users with schizophrenia frequently utilized first-person pronouns and vocabulary associated with health, religion, death, anxiety, auditory experiences, and biological processes. Conversely, the control group predominantly favored terms related to friendships, leisure, success, and time. Psychological evaluations further indicated that individuals with schizophrenia exhibited higher arousal levels but lower scores in self-control and values compared to the control group. These findings underscore the potential of NLP and machine learning techniques as supportive, non-invasive tools for early detection and monitoring of schizophrenia through digital footprints.

title: Semimage: HSV-Based Semantic Image Encoding for Disentangled Text Representation
authors: Mohammad Zare, Pirooz Shamsinejadbabaki
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Web Research (ICWR)
abstract: We propose SemImage, a novel document representation that transforms unstructured text into structured multichannel 2D images, enabling Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to leverage spatial inductive biases for textual analysis. Unlike traditional character-level or word-level grids, SemImage maps linguistic features into a disentangled HSV (Hue, Saturation, Value) color space. In this framework, the Hue channel encodes topical information, Saturation captures emotional intensity (sentiment), and Value represents semantic emphasis. To incorporate discourse structure, we introduce dynamic boundary rows between sentence representations, where pixel intensity is modulated by semantic dissimilarity (cosine distance) between adjacent sentences. We utilize a learned ColorMapper network and a multi-task training objective with auxiliary losses to ensure the physical separation of topic and sentiment into their respective visual channels. Empirical evaluations on the Multi-Label Reviews (MLR), 20 Newsgroups, and IMDB datasets demonstrate that SemImage is highly competitive with transformer-based models like BERT, particularly in multi-task scenarios where it reduces feature interference. Beyond performance, SemImage provides an inherently interpretable “glass-box” representation, allowing for the visual inspection of a document's topical flow and emotional density through a 2D semantic tapestry.

title: Instruction-Tuned vs Fine-Tuned Large Language Models: An Empirical Evaluation Across NLP Tasks
authors: Alex David S, Shenbagalaya A, Krishna Kumar S, Cyril Britto T, Arunbarathy D, Devi P P
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Modern Natural Language Processing (NLP) systems are now based on Large Language Models (LLMs), and two prominent types of adaptation are instruction tuning and task-specific fine-tuning. Whereas instruction-tuned models focus on flexibility and generalization across a wide range of tasks, fine-tuned models aim at achieving maximum performance for a specific goal. This paper presents a comprehensive empirical comparison of instruction-tuned and fine-tuned LLMs on multiple NLP tasks, including sentiment analysis, paraphrase detection, and abstractive text summarization. Standard benchmark datasets such as SST-2, MRPC, and CNN/DailyMail are evaluated in a unified and reproducible experimental setup using a Google Colab environment. Model performance is measured using Accuracy, Macro-F1, ROUGE-1, ROUGEL, and inference latency to reflect both effectiveness and computational efficiency. Results demonstrate that instruction-tuned models can perform competitively on classification and semantic reasoning tasks without task-specific retraining, while fine-tuned models achieve superior summarization quality and significantly lower inference latency for discriminative tasks. The findings highlight trade-offs between adaptability, accuracy, and efficiency, indicating that instruction-tuned and fine-tuned models play complementary roles in real-world NLP systems. The code is publicly available at following git-hub repository: https://github.com/mldlcode2026/Instruction-Tuned-vs-Fine-Tuned-Large-Language-Models-An-Empirical-Evaluation-Across-NLP-Tasks.

title: A Spatio-Temporal Attention-Based Deep Neural Network for Real-Time Violence Detection in CCTV Surveillance
authors: M. Prasannakumar, R. Venkatesan, S. Andal, B. Rajalingam, L. Vanitha, M. Revathi
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Intelligent surveillance systems must be able to comprehend real-time videos, especially when it comes to closed-circuit television (CCTV) networks' ability to recognize violence. However, traditional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have a limited recognition accuracy and a large processing overhead because they mainly concentrate on spatial data and are unable to effectively describe temporal dynamics across video frames. In order to overcome these obstacles, this research suggests a spatial-temporal Attention-Based Deep Neural Network (STADNN) for real-time violence detection that combines a transformer-based temporal attention mechanism with a lightweight convolutional backbone. The proposed approach is based on an adaptive frame skipping scheme that is controlled using temporal attention scores. This allows the network to selectively attend to important frames and skip the remaining ones, thereby reducing the inference delay without affecting the performance of the object detection task. The hybrid architecture leverages CNNs for the capture of fine-grained spatial cues and captures long-range temporal dependencies by means of a compact transformer encoder optimized for deployment on edge devices. Extensive experiments conducted on benchmark violence detection datasets have shown superior accuracy and F1-score performance exhibited by the proposed model in real time while considering standard CNN- and RNN-based approaches. In addition, the proposed framework has lower computational complexity and is more robust for challenging surveillance conditions such as occlusion and low lighting conditions. The experimental results confirm that the proposed spatio-temporal attention mechanism offers an operative and accessible solution for real-time video understanding in intelligent CCTV surveillance systems.

title: DevOps Excellence for Enterprise AI Systems: Continuous Deployment of Resilient Platforms With Angular Frontend Integration
authors: Sumith Sunny Dayal Thalary, Narendra Kumar Kuntamukkala, Raj Kiran Chennareddy, M. Sowmiya Manoj
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: The accelerated use of machine learning in data-driven applications has driven the demand of powerful and sound predictive models that have the ability to process intricate relationships in organized data. This paper suggests a hybrid ensemble machine learning structure, which will enhance the accuracy of classification and predictive stability by integrating the strengths of two or more machine learning algorithms. The proposed model will combine the three types of classifiers (Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and the Extreme Gradient Boosting) with the strategy of soft voting ensemble to increase the robustness of the decisions. In an effort to measure the performance of the proposed framework, the dataset of the survivability of the Titanic passengers was used to conduct experiments using the dataset of 891 instances. Before the training of the model, the dataset was preprocessed by encoding features, handling missing values, and normalization. A number of base line classifiers were used as a comparative analysis with Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and XGBoost. The experimental outcomes prove that the developed hybrid ensemble model has high classification performance, with the accuracy of 81.56, and the F1score of 0.766. In addition, Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) analysis reveals that the proposed model has the highest Area Under the Curve (AUC) value of 0.903, which implies that it is a highly discriminative model. These findings agree that a hybrid ensemble framework utilizing multiple classifiers can be more predictively reliable and stable than single algorithms. This methodology has proven the power of ensemble-based machine learning methods used to enhance predictive analytics and decisionsupport systems within structured data settings.

title: A Customized Web Engineering Framework for Semantic Web Application Development
authors: Navneet Kaur, Satyaveer Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: The Semantic Web represents a major realization of the World Wide Web, emphasizing the meaningful exchange and linking of data, along with automated reasoning capabilities. Traditional web engineering methodologies, originally developed for conventional applications, are insufficient to address the specific requirements of semantic web applications that rely heavily on ontologies, linked data, and reasoning. Building upon our earlier systematic literature review and comparative analysis of web engineering process models [Paper 1], this paper introduces a customized web engineering framework specialized for semantic web application development. The framework extends traditional models by incorporating ontology-driven design, semantic modeling schemas, reasoning integration, and dedicated tooling infrastructure to tackle challenges such as semantic data complexity, ontology evolution, interoperability, and developer usability. Structured into coherent phases spanning requirement analysis to deployment and evaluation, the framework aims to bridge the gap between classical methodologies and semantic web needs. This contribution provides both a theoretical basis for future validation and practical guidance for developing scalable, interoperable, and semantically enhanced web applications.

title: Hybrid GA-CS Optimized Deep Learning Framework for Fraud Detection in Ethereum Blockchain Transactions
authors: Yogesh Kumar Gupta, Laxman Solankee, Rahul Singh, Akanksha Parihar, Akanksha Soni, Mohit Choubey
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: The rapid growth of Ethereum smart contracts has attracted significant attention from both academia and industry, leading to the emergence of diverse commercial applications. However, the increasing prevalence of fraudulent activities such as phishing, bribery, and money laundering poses serious threats to the security and integrity of online transactions. To address these challenges, this study proposes a deep learning-based fraud detection framework enhanced with a novel metaheuristic optimization technique. Specifically, an Optimized Genetic Algorithm-Cuckoo Search (GA-CS) hybrid approach is integrated with a deep learning model to improve fraud classification accuracy. The proposed GA-CS algorithm leverages the global search capability of Cuckoo Search while employing Genetic Algorithm operations to overcome its inherent limitations and enhance convergence performance. Extensive experiments are conducted to evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed method against several widely used machine learning and deep learning classifiers, including Logistic Regression (LR), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), MultiLayer Perceptron (MLP), XGBoost, Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LGBM), Random Forest (RF), and Support Vector Classification (SVC), using a limited feature set. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed GA-CS-optimized deep learning model outperforms most baseline methods and achieves superior detection accuracy. Although its performance is marginally higher than that of the Random Forest model, the proposed approach and the SVC model achieve the highest overall accuracy, confirming the robustness and effectiveness of the proposed framework for fraudulent transaction detection on the Ethereum platform.

title: Multi-Layer Threat Assessment Model for Safeguarding Cloud Platforms Against Advanced Cyber Risks
authors: Miruthula T, Anandaselvakarthik T
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: To protect dynamic cloud environments from advanced cyber-attacks this study proposes a multi layered security architecture. By leveraging the control, infrastructure and storage layers the system provide a robust defence against advanced threats. The infrastructure layer manages virtualized environments supported by hypervisors, while the control layer manages database management, resource management and authentication. The storage layer secures users data across multiple hosts. The framework detect anomalies, unauthorized access and resource usage and through monitoring network connectivity and data flow. Real time risk mitigation, adaptive security policies, and automated response solutions enhance the secure operations of cloud environments by reducing attack surfaces and improving data integrity to increase resiliency against threats.

title: Explainable CNN Framework for DFU Severity Classification with Grad-CAM
authors: Rajasekaran Thangaraj, P. Sindhu, S. Karuppusamy
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: Diabetic Foot Ulcers (DFUs) are a frequent complication of diabetes, which can quickly escalate to infection, tissue damage, and even amputation if not evaluated in time. Proper severity evaluation is a must to help in medical treatment and to enhance patient outcomes. The severity evaluation of Diabetic Foot Ulcers (DFU) is very important for proper treatment management and to avoid complications in the limbs. The current state of deep learning methods is mostly centered on detection. Provide limited interpretability, making them less useful in practical settings. In this paper, a CNN strengthened by two layers of feature extraction, as well as four transfer learning models: ResNet101, DenseNet121, EfficientNet-B0, and MobileNetV2, intended for the classification of DFU into five grades (Grades 0-4), is proposed. The proposed Customized Baseline CNN achieves an accuracy of 94.17% and outperforms all other pre-trained models. The obtained results are validated by the use of confusion matrices, quantitative analysis, and a combined bar chart. The interpretability of the proposed Customized CNN is further validated by the use of Grad-CAM analysis, which verifies that the Customized CNN focuses on the appropriate areas of the ulcer, thus making it more suitable for real-world applications.

title: The Impact of Self-Service Technology, Mobile Food Ordering Applications, and Digital Payments on Customer Experience in Fast Food Restaurants
authors: Elrika Indra, Monica Michelle Aura Wibowo, Risia Sumaredi, Leo Andri Yulius Caesar
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: This journal explores how Self-Servicee Technology (SST), Mobile Food Ordering Application (MFOA), and digital payment impact the customer experience in fast food restaurants. This study also evaluates how these technologies can contribute to service innovation to enhance customer experience in the fast-food industry. This study adopts a quantitative method with purposive sampling, and the data was analyzed using Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) through SmartPLS. The results show that all three factors have a positive and significant effect on customer experience, with an R square of 0.696. Among these, MFOA has the biggest impact, followed by digital payment and then SST.

title: Depth Estimation for Oyster Mushrooms Using a CBAM-Enhanced U-Net to Support Growth Monitoring
authors: Meiqing Wang, Sari Nuwayhid, Dimitrios Argyropoulos
publish: 2025 IEEE International Workshop on Metrology for Agriculture and Forestry (MetroAgriFor)
abstract: Oyster mushrooms (Pleurotus ostreatus) support sustainable agriculture through smart and digital farming innovations, where monitoring their growth is key to optimizing harvest timing and enhancing production quality. While RGB imaging is commonly used due to its simplicity and low cost, it does not provide depth information, which is useful for assessing growth stages and estimating volume. To overcome this limitation, recent advances in computer vision have explored monocular depth estimation from RGB images. Following this direction, a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM)-based U-Net model was proposed for depth estimation in farm environments. The U-Net architecture captured multi-scale features, while the CBAM block enhanced the model’s focus on relevant foreground regions, such as mushroom caps and stems. The model was trained from scratch using a dataset collected under farm conditions, enabling it to learn domain-specific visual patterns effectively. Experimental results showed an average mean absolute error (MAE) of 47.79 mm on the test set, indicating that the model can provide reliable depth estimates for practical mushroom growth analysis.

title: Assessment of the Self-Sufficiency of an Electric Vehicle in Urban Environments and Different Terrains
authors: Mario Jafet Triminio, Vielka Sofia Barahona, Maria Elena Perdomo
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: In Honduras, the rapid growth of the vehicle fleet has made the transport sector one of the main emitters of greenhouse gases in the country. In light of this problem, electric mobility is seen as a strategic opportunity to reduce these emissions and decrease dependence on fossil fuels. In this context, this research proposes the evaluation of an electric vehicle, focusing on analyzing its range and performance both in an urban environment represented by the city of San Pedro Sula and on different types of terrain within the UNITEC San Pedro Sula campus. This evaluation will be conducted through experimental tests that will measure its energy consumption, battery capacity, and overall vehicle performance under real conditions.

title: Towards Adaptive Rehabilitation: Smart 3D-Printed Orthosis with Real-Time Compliance Feedback
authors: Morten Ødegård, Anne-Marthe Sanders, Olga Korostynskas
publish: 2025 18th International Conference on Sensing Technology (ICST)
abstract: Effective rehabilitation is paramount for individuals recovering from life-changing trauma, as it directly impacts functional recovery, pain management, psychological well-being, and ultimately, their ability to regain independence and quality of life. Rehabilitation compliance to follow physical therapist’s instructions and completing prescribed home exercise program is essential for medical and legal purposes. However, the process of monitoring this compliance is in many cases still mainly subjective: it is a patient’s word that is the basis of assessment, without objective measurable indicators. There are emerging monitoring technologies that can be used for this purpose, with accelerometers and motion sensors being the more common to implement. This paper reports on design, manufacture and testing of a novel 3D printed smart orthosis with integrated sensors to provide the physicians and welfare authorities with an option for objective assessment of rehabilitation regime compliance. The smart orthosis prototype was developed in collaboration with rehabilitation expert to ensure the device’s clinical relevance. It measures the level of motion, which was accurately linked to an activity type and correlated with prescribed rehabilitation plan.

title: A 56 Gb/s/wire, 0.75 pJ/b Transceiver for Die-to-Die Interface Using Simultaneous Bi-Directional Signaling
authors: Sujay Patel, Mingyuan Han, Andrew Ng, Ruhao Xia, Mahmoud Khalil, Mohamed Badr Younis, Jack Kenney, Michael StGermain, Wei-Hung Chen, Pavan Kumar Hanumolu
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper presents a 56-Gb/s/wire, 0.75-pJ/b single-ended die-to-die transceiver employing simultaneous bi-directional signaling. A matched passive hybrid replicates interposer loading and achieves more than 30 dB of near-end echo suppression. Meanwhile, a common-mode-reject slicer enables high-speed differential detection despite rail-to-rail common-mode input variation. Fabricated in 16-nm CMOS, the prototype demonstrates stable eye openings in both synchronous and asynchronous modes. The results highlight the effectiveness of matched-load passive hybrids in enabling high-bandwidth, energy-efficient die-to-die interconnects.

title: The Influence of Technology-Enhanced Visual Merchandising and Store Atmosphere on Impulsive Buying Behavior in Retail
authors: Yesha Gustira, Ziphora Jessica Addu, Ronny Samsul Bahri
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)
abstract: This research was conducted with the aim of finding out how the use of technology in Visual Merchandising and Store Atmosphere can influence and encourage consumers to carry out Impulsive Buying behavior in the retail industry, especially in Oh! Some Indonesia. This research was conducted through a quantitative approach, with a purposive sampling method through the distribution of questionnaire surveys with a total of 300 respondents offline and online. The Likert scale is used as a measuring tool for each variable (the use of technology in Visual Merchandising, Store Atmosphere, and Impulsive Buying), and SmartPLS software is used as a data management application with SEM-PLS testing and bootstrapping. The results show that the use of technology in Visual Merchandising cannot directly affect Impulsive Buying but can affect Impulsive Buying through the Store Atmosphere as mediation. This shows that Visual Merchandising technology is only effective in encouraging impulsive shopping behavior if it succeeds in creating or reinforcing a positive and attractive store atmosphere. Therefore, it is important for retailers to know that the use of technology is not just modernization but also needs to create an immersive and emotionally engaging store atmosphere.

title: Evaluating and Reporting the Carbon Footprint of Shared Computing Platforms: Choices and Limits
authors: Gaël Guennebaud, Anne-Laure Ligozat, Anne-Cécile Orgerie, Matthieu Simonin
publish: 2025 24th International Symposium on Parallel and Distributed Computing (ISPDC)
abstract: Attributing the carbon costs of shared ICT infrastructures to its end-users is frequently promoted as a way to encourage awareness of environmental impacts and advocate for more sustainable practices. This paper explores the intricacies of this approach by focusing on shared ICT infrastructures specifically dedicated to academic research, several of which having recently introduced carbon intensity values for their users. This scenario serves as a practical case study for examining the methodologies and challenges associated with evaluating the carbon intensity of shared ICT infrastructures. We explore the choices with their limitations, discuss the objectives behind their implementation of this type of environmental indicator and offer actionable insights. This analysis aims to contribute to the broader discussion on sustainable computing practices and the role of environmental indicators in driving meaningful change.

title: Optimized Pipeline Deployment for Efficient Edge AI Applications
authors: Alberto Marroquín, Federico Machado
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: The growing adoption of artificial intelligence has been fueled by advances in embedded hardware, enabling microcomputers with limited resources to perform inference with Artificial Intelligence (AI) models without relying on external servers or the cloud. This paradigm, known as Edge Computing, allows real time data processing directly on local devices, providing low latency, enhanced privacy, and reduced bandwidth consumption in digital solutions. This work presents a methodology to optimize object detection models for Edge AI deployment, focusing on integrating preprocessing, inference, and rendering tasks in a multithreaded pipeline. We evaluate configurations using YOLO models on the NVIDIA Jetson AGX Xavier platform, exploring batch processing strategies and model optimization with TensorRT through FP16 and INT8 quantization. Experimental results show that combining thread-based parallelization with precision reduction significantly improves inference speed (up to $232 \%$), decreases GPU load, and reduces energy consumption by more than $70 \%$, while maintaining reliable detection accuracy.

title: Multivariate statistical process control for condition monitoring in spur gearboxes
authors: Antonio Pérez-Torres, Jean Navarrete-Campos, Reinier Fernández-López, Jorge Figueroa-Zuñiga
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: Gearboxes are critical components in industrial production lines, where unplanned stops can result in significant economic losses. This paper presents a Multivariate Statistical Process Control (MSPC) methodology based on Dynamic Principal Component Analysis (DPCA) for early failure detection in spur gears using vibration signals. Unlike conventional PCA, DPCA incorporates time lags to capture the system dynamics, improving sensitivity to progressive degradation under variable load and speed conditions. Features were extracted from vibration signals using condition indicators (CIs) in both the time and frequency domains, and these were utilised as process variables. The Hotelling’s $T^{2}$ and SPE statistics defined control limits for fault detection. The method was validated on an experimental gearbox test bench with different failure severities, achieving consistent alarms that correlate with the degradation level and detect incipient failures. The approach is computationally efficient, fully reproducible in software $\mathbf{R}$ using open source libraries, and can be integrated into predictive maintenance schemes for online gearbox monitoring, contributing to reduced downtime and operational costs.

title: Towards Transition from Natural Gas to Green Energy Sources: A Roadmap for Converting Algeria's Gas-Fired Power Plants
authors: Madjid Amani, Arezki Smaili
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: The urgency of the energy transition is greater than ever as we need to shift away from fossil fuels and reduce greenhouse gas emissions. Algeria has recognized this need and has set an ambitious target to produce 15,000 MW of electricity from solar energy by 2035. Despite having abundant solar resources, 98% of the country's electricity production comes from gas-fired power plants that rely on fossil fuels, primarily natural gas. This paper reviews the energy status of Algeria and considers the challenges that the country will face once the supply of natural gas is depleted. One of the challenges is the shutdown of a significant number of gas-fired power plants. Therefore, the feasibility of converting these plants based on natural gas to run with renewable energy sources is carried out. Various techniques are proposed, taking into consideration their technical feasibility, to enable the successful conversion of these power plants to operate on renewable energy sources.

title: MobileNetV3-Large-Based Automated Classification of Mulberry Leaf Diseases for Sericulture Applications
authors: S. Muthulingam, Mahip Aggarwal, Md Ankushavali
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: This research suggests a deep learning-based technique for the automated classification of mulberry leaf disease using the MobileNetV3-Large architecture. A data set consisting of 1091 annotated images of mulberry leaves under three categories, namely Healthy Leaves, Leaf Rust, and Leaf Spot, was used to develop and test the model. Images were preprocessed by applying resizing and a MobileNet-specific normalization, and transfer learning was used to improve the efficiency of feature extraction. The dataset was split into three sets, training, validation, and testing, using a 70:20:10 stratified split. Experimental results show that the accuracy of the overall classification was 92.66% in the test set for the proposed model. High precision, recall, and F1-scores across all classes imply reliable discrimination of the disease, with only weak misclassification between visually similar categories. The lightweight design and powerful performance of MobileNetV3-Large make it suitable for deployment in real-time and resource-constrained agricultural environments. The proposed framework brings into focus the possibility of efficient deep learning based models in the diagnosis of mulberry leaf disease and in the intelligent monitoring of crop health.

title: Edge AI-Powered Real-Time Speed Estimation and License Plate Recognition for Urban Road Safety
authors: Prach Asavanarakul, Aran Asavanarakul, Phongsuk Ampha, Wissaroot Ounthong, Dutsadi Namracha, Noppharat Khongsuwan
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: This paper presents a real-time vehicle speed estimation and license plate recognition (LPR) system designed for urban and community deployment. The proposed system integrates a Thai-optimized dual-AI LPR framework with high-precision FMCW millimeter-wave radar, replacing earlier microwave radar and traditional image processing. A 30 km/h threshold is applied, with radar-triggered detection within 10-20 meters operational range, automatically capturing images when vehicles exceed limits. License plate detection utilizes YOLOv8s, followed by YOLOv8m for character recognition with coordinate-based sequencing, achieving markedly superior performance compared with OpenCV-based approaches. The architecture is implemented on the NVIDIA Jetson Xavier platform, enabling sub-100ms edge processing without a centralized server dependency. Experimental evaluation on 2000 vehicle samples demonstrates 94.95% accuracy for combined speed estimation and Thai license plate recognition within a 30–60 km/h range. Real-time outputs, including timestamps, plate numbers, and speeds, are logged and displayed on roadside monitors for driver feedback. Thailand faces critical traffic enforcement gaps, with 72.17% of accidents occurring in local areas and 25.4 fatalities per 100,000 population. The proposed system addresses this challenge through cost-effective edge-AI deployment for community traffic management. Limitations include untested performance under low-light, nighttime, and adverse weather conditions. These areas will be investigated in future work.

title: Towards Quieter and More Efficient Wind Farms: Multi-Objective Layout Optimization
authors: Abdessabour Amoura, Mohammed Nadjib Hamlaoui, Arezki Smaili, Sofiane Khelladi, Abdelhamid Bouhelal
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: This study proposes an aeroacoustics optimization framework for wind farm layout design, explicitly accounting for wake induced effects on turbine noise emissions. The methodology consists of two main phases. In the assessment phase, aerodynamic simulations are conducted using the Actuator Disk Model within a compressible Large Eddy Simulation framework, coupled with a dynamic Smagorinsky subgrid-scale model, to characterize near-field turbulent flows. Simultaneously, the Farassat 1A formulation of the Ffowcs Williams–Hawkings analogy estimates sound power levels under various operational conditions, generating a predefined Sound Power Level dataset as a function of operating conditions. In the optimization phase, the Jensen wake model is employed to predict velocity deficits resulting from wake interactions, thereby determining the appropriate SWL for each turbine. Sound propagation is evaluated using ISO 9613-2 to account for attenuation to receptor locations. A Particle Swarm Optimization algorithm iteratively refines turbine positions, achieving a trade-off between aerodynamic efficiency and noise constraints. By linking each turbine’s noise emission to its local flow conditions and position, the framework offers a simplified yet accurate optimization approach, enhancing the reliability of noise modeling in wind farm design.

title: Enhanced Kidney Disease Classification using a Fine-tuned VGG16 Model for CT Image Analysis
authors: NiharikaVemula, Durgesh Nandan
publish: 2025 IEEE Technology &amp; Engineering Management Conference - Asia Pacific (TEMSCON-ASPAC)
abstract: Renal dysfunction is a significant global health is concern that affects individuals across all age groups. The growing prevalence of chronic kidney disease (CKD), combined with a shortage of nephrologists, underscores the need for automated diagnostic solutions. CKD often progresses silently, delaying timely diagnosis and intervention. Common kidney abnormalities-such as cysts, stones, and tumors - are frequently associated with impaired renal function and must be accurately classified for appropriate treatment planning. In this study, we propose a deep learning-based diagnostic framework utilizing a fine-tuned VGG16 model for the multiclass classification of CT kidney images. To mitigate class imbalance, random undersampling was applied to ensure uniform class distribution. The model achieved a classification accuracy of 99.89% and was evaluated using metrics including precision, recall, F1-score, and specificity. The proposed approach demonstrates strong potential for improving the early detection and diagnosis of kidney disorders in clinical settings.

title: MPPT-Assisted Multilevel Space Vector Modulation for a Three-Phase Switched Capacitor Boost Inverter in Photovoltaic Pumping Systems
authors: Rania Omar Amrani, Ali Benachour, Bouchra Belili, Selma Chelal, El Madjid Berkouk
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)
abstract: This paper presents a Multilevel Space Vector Modulation (SVM) control strategy applied to a three-phase switched-capacitor boost inverter designed for driving an induction motor in photovoltaic water pumping systems. The proposed topology integrates three cascaded switched capacitor units within the inverter, eliminating the need for bulky DC/DC converters and achieving a compact structure with a voltage boosting factor of four. To enhance power extraction from the photovoltaic array, a maximum power point tracking (MPPT) algorithm adjusts the voltage ratio r within the multilevel SVM, optimizing inverter operation across varying meteorological conditions. The combined MPPT and adaptive SVM approach extends the inverter’s voltage range and improves the overall pumping system efficiency. Simulation results are provided, validated experimentally under both steady-state and dynamic conditions, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Event-Driven Reinforcement Learning Frameworks for Proactive Customer Friction Detection in Large-Scale Digital Platforms
authors: Paramesh Sethuraman, Anvesh Katipelly, M. Sowmiya Manoj
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: In the current landscape of hyperscale digital commerce and FinTech, the latency between the occurrence of customer friction and its subsequent resolution represents a multi-billion dollar challenge in user churn and operational overhead. Legacy CX management architectures continue to rely on post-hoc analytics and static, rule-based triggers, which inherently encounter a “reactive gap.” This paper proposes a new architectural turn toward proactive intelligence, integrating high-throughput event-driven architectures (EDA) with deep reinforcement learning (DRL) models. The framework models the complex customer interaction stream as a non-stationary MDP, where subtle behavioral aberrationssuch as erratic navigation, API latency spikes, and consecutive input retries-are ingested as live state vectors. Rather than using the exhaustively labelled datasets employed by supervised learning models, our reinforcement learning agent uses a custom-weighted reward function designed to maximise longterm transaction completion and penalise friction magnitude. It's this closed-loop feedback mechanism which enables autonomous, sub-second interventions from predictive data pre-fetching to real-time deployment of custom microservices. We test this architecture's scalability in a distributed setting at over 100,000 events per second. Our experiments demonstrate how this stance translates to 35 % more preemptive resolution and significantly reduced cognitive burdens on human support, establishing a new benchmark for autonomous decision platforms in high-volume digital ecologies.

title: A 0.6V 976nW 93.5dB-SNDR Charge-Pump ADC with 183.6dB-FoMs Using Passive-Reference Boost and NS-Assisted Charge-Balance-Calibration
authors: Shan Zhang, Lingxin Meng, Wanyuan Qu, Menglian Zhao, Zhichao Tan
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel charge-pump (CP) analog-to-digital converter (ADC) architecture that supports a 2×V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DD</inf> input range under a single supply voltage V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DD</inf>, targeting next-generation low-voltage, low-power, and high-precision signal-chain applications. At the circuit level, this paper proposes a local passive-reference boost (PRB) technique based on the charge-pump circuit to internally double the reference voltage to 2×V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DD</inf>, thereby increasing the input range. Furthermore, charge-pump floating inverter amplifiers (CP-FIAs) in the noise-shaping (NS) filter also boost their analog voltages to 2×V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DD</inf>, tackling the design challenge in analog circuits. However, when the 2×V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DD</inf> PRB units are inserted into the CDAC during bit trials, charge sharing between the PRB capacitors and CDAC bit capacitors leads to a reference voltage droop, resulting in non-binary bit weights. To address this, an NS-assisted charge-balance-calibration (NS-CBC) technique is proposed, calibrating the bit weights and compensating for mismatch errors. Fabricated in a 65-nm CMOS process, the prototype of a 0.6-V CP NS-SAR ADC supports a 1.2-V input range and delivers 93.5-dB SNDR. With a power consumption of 975.6 nW, it achieves the Schreier FoM (FoMs) of 183.6 dB. It also maintains stable SNDR and FoMs across voltage/temperature variations and the sampling-frequency scaling.

title: Portable OpenMP Offloading Across Clusters
authors: Ilias K. Kasmeridis, Spyros Mantelos, Vassilios V. Dimakopoulos
publish: 2025 24th International Symposium on Parallel and Distributed Computing (ISPDC)
abstract: Contemporary clusters are characterized by various degrees of heterogeneity with nodes containing different computational resources. Even if nodes are identically configured, within each one of them there exist compute units of different architectures such as CPUS and GPUS. Programming such systems usually requires complex techniques to be applied and various apis to be employed. Furthermore, because nodes are connected through a network, effective communication management is essential. This complexity necessitates a unified, yet simple and versatile model to fully utilize the existing capabilities. In this paper we present an enhanced implementation of the openmp offloading model, which can be used to harness all the computational resources in a cluster. It exposes remote nodes and accelerators by virtualizing them as local openmp devices, attached to the node executing the main part of the application. For performance and portability, the communication layer is based on MPI. Our approach allows offloading code to both remote CPUS and GPUS in a transparent and seamless way, and has been designed for simple and flexible deployment in common cluster environments.

title: Performance Comparison of a Dynamic Voltage Restorer Using DQ and PQ Control Methods
authors: Saad F. Algahtani, Elbarbary Z. M. Salem, Shaik M. Irshad, Haitham Z. Azazi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In the dominion of power systems, voltage sags and swells are transient disturbances that can significantly impact the performance and stability of the grid. This issue must be addressed with robust control techniques to have good performance, with low harmonic and stable operation of the power system. Many techniques for instance, dynamic voltage restorers (DVRs) and series active power filters were proposed based on conventional theories having merits and demerits. This research aims to provide a comprehensive comparison between two DVR control techniques. The First technique is the modified instantaneous reactive power theory (PQ), whereas the second is modified synchronous stationary frame (DQ) method under various sag and swell voltage conditions. The extracted components of the grid voltage positive sequence and load current negative sequence form the basis of the improved approaches. to detect the disturbance and generate the control signal of the DVR. The study encompasses Unbalanced load voltage with 80% sag, unbalanced load voltage with 70% swell, balanced 80% voltage sag, and a 50% balanced load change are the four different operating conditions. Both control approaches, with their modified versions, are mathematically represented and evaluated under different specified severe power quality conditions. The methods are performed in MATLAB/Simulink environment and implemented experimentally using DSPACE DS1104 controller-based system to provide a comparison analysis and validate the system's voltage profile and harmonic distortion performance study. The obtained results and analysis illustrate the effectiveness of the proposed two control methods. Each technique demonstrates their effectiveness for addressing the power quality issue.

title: Evolution of Deep Learning Techniques and Challenges for Vehicle Trajectory Prediction
authors: Sidra Rashid, Muazzam A. Khan, Usman Akram, Raheel Mumtaz, Toqeer Ali Syed, It Ee Lee, Qamar Wali
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Accurate vehicle trajectory prediction (VTP) plays a fundamental role in the advancement of intelligent transportation systems and autonomous driving. It improves situational awareness, optimizes decision-making and safety in complex traffic scenarios. Although several surveys have reviewed vehicle trajectory prediction approaches, a comprehensive review of recent deep learning-based approaches from a system and modeling perspective is still lacking. To bridge this gap, we conducted a systematic review of the literature on deep learning-based methods by critically analyzing emerging trends in model design. We introduce a system-oriented taxonomy of vehicle trajectory prediction methods which includes classical, reinforcement learning-based, collaborative, and deep learning-based methods. We evaluate their capacity to model uncertainty, inter-agent interactions, and dynamic traffic behavior. This survey emphasizes three key perspectives that drive performance in deep learning-based models including data preprocessing and feature extraction, model creation and attention mechanisms. Deep-learning-based methods are critically analyzed in design dimensions, e.g. temporal modeling strategy, interaction encoding, modeling capability, suitable deployment scenarios and their key strengths for trajectory prediction. Finally, we discuss open research challenges and propose practical recommendations for next-generation VTP in autonomous driving.

title: Self-Referenced Terahertz Sheet Conductance Determination of Thin Semiconductor Layers in Reflection and Transmission Geometry
authors: Konstantin Wenzel, Steffen Breuer, Alexander Kleinkamp, Alexander Dohms, Tina-Celine Heßelmann, Shahram Keyvaninia, Martin Schell, Robert B. Kohlhaas, Lars Liebermeister
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology
abstract: Accurate characterization of thin semiconductor layers is crucial for quality control in the production of semiconductor devices. Traditional methods, like the four-point probe technique, require direct contact and are thus destructive. This study explores terahertz time-domain spectroscopy (THz TDS) as a promising non-contact alternative for measuring the electrical properties of semiconductor layers. We investigated 1 μm to 2 μm thick highly doped indium gallium arsenide (InGaAs) layers on indium phosphide (InP) substrates, with electrical sheet conductance between 10-7 and 10-1 S/sq. Utilizing a fiber-coupled THz-TDS system, we performed measurements in both reflection and transmission geometry. For the first time, we apply the self-referenced method to thin semiconductor layers as well as systematically compare self-referenced and referenced based methods to extract the electrical sheet conductance of thin semiconductor layers. In reflection geometry, self-referencing improves accuracy by minimizing operator error and eliminating frequent re-referencing. Agreement with contact-based four-point probes validates THz-TDS for nondestructive evaluation of thin semiconductor layers and extends its measurable conductance range, enabling reference-free deployments.

title: Anticipating Human Hand Grasps for Enhanced Interaction: A Bayesian Approach to Negative Latency in Human-Machine Interfaces
authors: Jonas Schulz, Frederic Benken, Evelyn Muschter, Patrick Seeling, Stefanie Speidel, Frank H. P. Fitzek, Dario Cuevas Rivera
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Human–machine interfaces increasingly rely on hand gesture recognition to enable intuitive interaction in immersive technologies and robotic systems. However, the inherent latency of gesture recognition pipelines, exacerbated by computational and network delays, remains a major obstacle to seamless real-time interaction. In this work, we introduce a lightweight, Bayesian model that leverages hand kinematics to predict target gestures before their completion, thereby recognizing hand gestures before they are completed (i.e. negative latency). Our approach is based on the continuous updating of posterior beliefs over a set of internal models, each with its gesture-specific dynamical system. These posteriors represent the probability that a gesture will be performed. We validate the model using a novel dataset of reach-to-grasp tasks recorded with a sensor glove. Our results show that our model consistently predicts gestures several hundred milliseconds prior to completion. Furthermore, we show that reliable gesture prediction is achievable with reduced sensor dimensionality. These findings illustrate how anticipatory computing can substantially improve responsiveness and naturalness in a range of latency-sensitive applications, including immersive environments and robotic systems.

title: BurnDC: A Progressive Propagation Framework for Low Coverage Depth Completion
authors: Zhengyu Zhu, Cong Zhang, Hongmin Liu, Bin Fan
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: The growing adoption of compact and cost-effective solid-state LiDARs has greatly advanced robotics. However, their inherently limited Field-of-View (FOV) hinders their application in tasks requiring wide-range depth perception. To overcome this limitation, we introduce the Low Coverage Depth Completion (LCDC) task, which aims to generate a full-scene depth map from a low coverage sparse depth map and a corresponding RGB image, and propose a tailored framework named BurnDC. BurnDC progressively expands the propagation frontier around reliable depth anchors via Progressive Depth Burn (PDB) and utilizes Weighted Ring Attention (WRA) to inject stable geometric context into boundary regions, achieving a controlled refinement and completion of the entire depth map. To evaluate LCDC, we construct a real-world solid-state LiDAR based benchmark, LC-TIERS, and simulate low coverage settings in NYUv2 and KITTI. Experimental results demonstrate that BurnDC significantly outperforms existing methods in low coverage scenarios, with an RMSE reduction of 10-20% over top competitors. Our work provides a promising solution for unlocking the full potential of solid-state LiDARs in various applications. Code available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/yudmoe/burnDC/</uri>.

title: An Automated CNN–BiLSTM Pipeline for Online Detection of Partial Discharge Pulses from HFCT Measurements with PRPD-Based Experimental Validation
authors: Eleanor Dias De Sousa, Marcel Antonionni De Andrade Romano, André Melo De Morais, Marcus Vinnicius Alves Nunes, Ubiratan Holanda Bezerra
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Reliable detection of partial discharges (PD) is essential for condition monitoring of high-voltage electrical equipment. In applications based on high-frequency current transformer (HFCT) sensors, the construction of phase-resolved partial discharge (PRPD) diagrams depends on the accurate discrimination of pulses in signals often contaminated by noise and interference. Traditional signal processing methods present limitations regarding parameterization, scalability, and automation, particularly in continuous monitoring scenarios. This work proposes a new automated pipeline for detecting time windows containing PD pulses from time-domain raw signals acquired with an off-the-shelf 8-bit data acquisition system coupled with an HFCT sensor, integrating deep-learning-based detection with the construction of PRPD diagrams. An aspect that characterizes the originality of this work lies in the fact that a hybrid convolutional neural network (CNN) and a bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) model are employed to classify temporal segments without the use of fixed statistical thresholds or heuristic rules. The identified windows are used to construct PRPD diagrams, explicitly preserving the phase and amplitude information associated with each pulse. In the test set, composed of acquisitions in a high-voltage (HV) laboratory involving equipment taken out of operation, the model achieved an accuracy of 97.49%, with a recall greater than 97% for the positive class and an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.993. The amplitude estimate derived from the maximum absolute value of the pulse is consistent with baseline reference results and conventional measurements in accordance with the IEC 60270 standard, providing quantitative and qualitative validation of the constructed PRPD patterns. The results indicate that the proposed original method increases the level of automation, preserves physical interpretability, and shows strong potential for application in intelligent continuous monitoring systems for HV equipment.

title: Matching Magneto-Inductive Waves at Discontinuities in One- and Two-Dimensional Structures
authors: Alessandro Dellabate, Danilo Brizi
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: Magneto-inductive (MI) waves, propagating through near-field magnetic coupling in resonant loop arrays, are crucial for energy transfer, sensing and communication within low-frequency magnetic metamaterials and metasurfaces. Reflection at discontinuities in MI arrays affects device performance, yet matching conditions, especially in two-dimensional structures, are inadequately characterized. Here we present an analytical approach to derive matching impedances at the junction of two dissimilar one-dimensional MI waveguide and at two-dimensional array edges, incorporating frequency and propagation direction dependencies. Validated by circuit-based and full-wave simulations, the proposed conditions significantly suppress reflected waves and enable effective energy absorption at array boundaries. These results offer valuable insights and practical guidelines for designing matched MI structures, potentially improving performance in wireless power transfer systems, sensing devices, and magnetic metasurface technologies.

title: A D -Band Planar 2-D Multibeam Modulated Metasurface Antenna Based on Gaussian Non-uniform Modulation
authors: Jia-Hui Zhao, Chen-Yu Ding, Zhuo-Wei Miao, Wei Hong, Stefano Maci, Zhang-Cheng Hao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a planar two-dimensional (2-D) multibeam modulated metasurface antenna (MMA) operating in the D-band. Conventional multibeam MMAs often suffer from substantial gain and 3-dB gain bandwidth degradation when beams steer to large scanning angles, limiting their practical utility in emerging terahertz systems. To address this challenge, a Gaussian non-uniform modulation technique is introduced, which optimizes the aperture field distribution and energy concentration across the scanning range. For experimental verification, a 2-D nine-beam MMA operating at 126 GHz is designed, incorporating a compact rectangular waveguide-to-monopole transition structure for efficient surface wave excitation. In addition, the implementation of gap waveguide at the feed interface ensures reliable multi-port feeding with a simple mounting structure. The proposed multibeam MMA is fabricated by quartz photolithography. Measurement results demonstrate a wide scanning coverage of ±60° with a low scanning loss of only 0.5 dB and a minimal variation in the gain–bandwidth (GB) product. To the best of our knowledge, this work represents the first multibeam MMA realized at terahertz frequencies, offering a compelling solution for next-generation radar systems requiring agile, multi-user, and wide-angle beam steering.
