title: Secondary-Level State-of-Charge Balancing Control for Accurate Power Sharing Among Battery Energy Storage Systems in Microgrids
authors: Ahmed M. Abouassy, Mahdi Mehrtash, Hanif Livani
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: Battery energy storage systems (BESSs) are essential in renewable-energy-based microgrids for mitigating power fluctuations and enhancing stability. However, when multiple BESS units operate in parallel, discrepancies in their charging and discharging behaviors result in unequal state-of-charge (SoC) levels, accelerating degradation and reducing system reliability. Existing adaptive droop control strategies address this issue by dynamically adjusting the droop coefficient to align BESS SoC levels. However, many approaches rely on average SoC tracking, introduce high nonlinearity, fail to constrain the SoC within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S o C_{\text{min }}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S o C_{\text{max }}$</tex>, or require external limit checks. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a secondary-level controller that distributes charging currents based on the required capacity and discharging currents based on the available capacity of each BESS. The controller integrates SoC boundaries within its control law, ensuring safe operation without additional supervisory layers. The proposed algorithm achieves effective SoC balancing without the need for high exponents. Moreover, when higher-order convergence is applied, it outperforms existing adaptive droop-based methods in terms of response speed and balancing accuracy. Simulation results validate the effectiveness, robustness, and superiority of the proposed controller over state-of-the-art techniques.

title: Experimental Evaluation and Analysis of the Impacts of Soiling on Polar-Mounted Photovoltaic (PV) Systems in Denton, Texas
authors: Godslove Ebiega, Ovis Irefu, Breana Smithers, Miguel F. Acevedo
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: Solar power is at the core of transitioning to clean, affordable, and dispatchable electric power due to its scalability and abundance of the sun. Today, the contributions of solar photovoltaic (PV) panels exceed 1-terawatt in installed capacity globally. However, only a small part of incident solar energy on PV panels gets converted to electrical power due to physical and environmental factors like PV material composition, soiling, PV orientation, excessive temperature, and degradation. Loss due to soiling is non-negligible in PV systems, thus, any small improvements that curtail soiling is a significant gain in terms of reliability and revenue recovery. The first step to achieving this is to quantify the impacts of local dust on PV efficiency at proposed PV installation site, because soiling is a site-specific problem. In this study, the impact of fine dust on the efficiency of PV systems is evaluated utilizing specialized environmental monitoring station installed at the test site. The study involves the physical implementation of soiling tests, and statistical analysis using analysis of variance (ANOVA) to validate the experimental results. This comprehensive approach shows that four soiling levels of dust examined results in array power loss of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$18.18 \%-55 \%$</tex>, depending on the density of the applied dust. Applying one-way ANOVA test results in a statistically significant difference in the array power loss between the four soiling levels

title: Where AI Load is Located Matters- Economic Impacts and Planning Challenges of Large Loads on Deregulated Wholesale Markets
authors: Sumedh Milind Halbe
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) data centers represent a new class of large loads distinguished by their unprecedented power requirements and the speed with which they can be constructed. While much of the existing research focuses on estimating the magnitude and timing of this new demand, this paper addresses the equally critical question of where such loads should be located and why. The deregulated wholesale market impacts of AI data centers are highly locational, with transmission emerging as one of the most significant bottlenecks in accommodating this demand. The economic effects depend strongly on whether data centers are sited in transmissionconstrained, capacity and renewable-scarce regions, or in unconstrained, renewable-rich areas. The pace at which the loads are being developed also presents challenges to the operators and stakeholders. This paper quantifies the wholesale market impacts of AI data center siting decisions and highlights the planning challenges they pose for both transmission and generation development.

title: Interventional Security: A Novel Cyber-Physical Threat-Mitigation Framework for Power-Control Networks
authors: Adam King, Santiago Grijalva
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: Even with accurate information about the electrical grid state, security information available to bulk powersystem operators is limited by the computational burden of full contingency analysis. Sub-second power-system security evaluation not only would improve operators' situational awareness but also drastically would reduce time spent during operator study mode. Further, such near-instantaneous analysis could aid in insider- and advanced-persistent-threat mitigation. Previous work demonstrated how a new method for direct power-security estimation using deep neural networks can achieve higher Rsquared score than DC-based contingency analysis while cutting computation time by two orders of magnitude. This prior research is leveraged herein to construct a faster-than-real-time pipeline for augmenting situational awareness and predictive decision support (operator study mode) and for enabling threat mitigation via proactive command screening that predicts a control action's impact and flags any security-degrading command for secondary human approval. Experimental results validate this end-to-end pipeline in continually estimating power-system security and in forecasting post-action security under a conditional human-in-theloop workflow. In this way, a proactive cyber-physical security paradigm that combines the ability to intercept communications with centralized decision-making that determines when, where, and to what extent to intervene, thereby mitigating unsafe states and trajectories before they can cause harm, is instantiated herein as “interventional security.”

title: Techno-Economic Comparison of Feeder Capacity Upgrades and Battery Energy Storage System Integration in EV Fast Charging Stations
authors: Seyed Mohammad Javad Hosseini, Chance Rowland, Stephen Kearny, Thomas Field, Mehdi Farasat
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: The rapid growth of Electric Vehicle (EV) adoption has created substantial stress on distribution networks, particularly at Fast-Charging Stations (FCSs). Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) are widely proposed to mitigate peak demand and improve resilience. However, few studies compare the economic performance of BESS solutions with traditional feeder capacity upgrades. This paper compares the cost of installing, operating, and maintaining an optimally sized BESS with the cost of increasing feeder capacity. Mixed-Integer Linear Programming (MILP) is utilized to minimize BESS investment, grid costs, peak penalties, and resilience violations. Peak intervals are identified using feeder data from the utility partner. Resilience is modeled through the probability that the required State-of-Charge (SOC) is available to reach healthy neighboring stations during outages. Stochastic models capture uncertainty in EVs' initial SOC and arrival times needed for modeling the FCS demand. FCS data from the utility partner's network provide the mean and standard deviation (SD) of arrival times. Feeder upgrade costs are based on reconductoring and labor estimates provided by the utility partner.

title: LP-Based Optimal Scheduling and Closed-Loop Control of Battery Energy Storage Systems
authors: Javad Zare, Ali Nasr Esfahani, Abolfazl Babaei
publish: 2026 IEEE Texas Power and Energy Conference (TPEC)
abstract: This paper presents a linear programming framework for the real-time operational scheduling of a grid-connected battery energy storage system (BESS), supported by a streaming data exchange architecture. The optimization model generates charge and discharge trajectories that minimize peak demand and smooth feeder loading while satisfying state-of-charge, power, and efficiency constraints. The resulting schedules are transmitted to a detailed battery simulation through an Apache Kafka interface, enabling closed-loop validation of both optimization and control performance. The results show that the storage system closely follows the optimal dispatch with minimal tracking error, confirming that the proposed method can be solved and communicated fast enough for continuous operation. The results highlight that LP-based scheduling can be executed fast enough for continuous operation and can be followed accurately by standard inverter-level controls, making it a viable strategy for advanced feeder-level optimization and real-time BESS control.

title: CRB-Based Resource Allocation in Multi-User Uplink Transmissions
authors: Xue Zhang, Abla Kammoun, Mohamed-Slim Alouini
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: In this work, we study the design of receivers for uplink multi-user systems, aiming to estimate both the channel and the transmitted symbols. We consider two estimation strategies: 1) a joint estimation approach, where the channel and symbols are estimated simultaneously; and 2) a sequential estimation approach, where the channel is first estimated and then used for symbol detection. For both strategies, we derive the Cramér-Rao Bound (CRB) for symbol estimation to characterize fundamental performance limits. When efficient receivers achieving the CRB exist, these bounds provide accurate lower bounds on the mutual information. In general, however, such receivers may not be available, and we instead use these same CRB-based metrics as practical proxies for achievable throughput. Leveraging tools from random matrix theory (RMT), we analyze the asymptotic behavior of these lower bounds under various asymptotic regimes for both estimation strategies. This analysis enables the derivation of generic power allocation guidelines that asymptotically maximize the proxy metrics. Simulation results confirm the accuracy of the asymptotic expressions and their effectiveness in guiding resource allocation decisions.

title: Key Node Monitoring Strategy for Group Status of Metal Oxide Arresters in Power Distribution Lines
authors: Yuqing Wang, Yang Shen, Siyuan Wang, Zhiyuan Zhang, Jingtong Feng, Hongshun Liu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: To address the monitoring costs and coverage gaps of Metal Oxide Arresters in 10 kV distribution networks, this paper proposes a collective state inversion method based on key node monitoring. First, electromagnetic transient simulations analyze the spatiotemporal evolution of lightning surges to identify key nodes with extreme stress. Subsequently, an “electrical-thermal-mechanical isomorphous field” model is constructed. By utilizing spatial interpolation on data from these key nodes, the insulation, thermal, and fatigue states of all arresters are reconstructed. Finally, a multidimensional evaluation system is established for precise health grading. Results demonstrate that this approach significantly reduces sensor costs while enabling real-time, collective health diagnosis, providing technical support for differentiated maintenance.

title: A Fast Dynamic Modeling Method for Converter Transformer Transport Shocks Based on Response Surface Methodology
authors: Zhixin Li, Feng Xie, Junfeng Fang, Yingjie Qiu, Rundong Ge
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: This paper proposes a rapid modeling method for assessing transport shock damage in converter transformers using Response Surface Methodology (RSM). Traditional approaches—empirical thresholds or finite element (FE) simulations—are either oversimplified or computationally intensive. The method constructs a 6th-order polynomial RSM based on FE-derived stress data under varying acceleration/frequency conditions, enhanced by Box–Cox transformation for residual optimization. The optimized model achieves high accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}>0.998$</tex>, error <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.5 \%$</tex>) and speeds up calculations by hundreds of times versus direct FE analysis. It enables efficient stress prediction for transport safety evaluation and structural optimization, with potential extensions to multi-directional shocks and field-data fusion for smart applications.

title: The Perceptions of Upper Undergraduate Students of the Implementation of the National Blended Learning Programs in Jordanian Universities
authors: Thafer Assaraira, Saad Bani-Mohammad, Ismail Ababneh
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: As a result of the experience with the online and blended university teaching under the COVID-19 pandemic, the adoption of blended learning programs has been mandated in all Jordanian higher-education institutions since late 2021. A framework for these programs was defined. This study investigates the perceptions of upper undergraduate students regarding the implementation of these programs, the technical resources and skills available, the benefits of blended learning, and the suitability of the specific teaching model used for the various courses. The findings reveal a broad satisfaction across students. However, there exists some technical obstacles to remote online learning, and a significant percentage of students had neutral opinions on various aspects of the programs. These findings point to the need for better technical support to be provided to students by the universities, and to including the suitability of the teaching model and the needed resources in course assessment questionnaires.

title: A Survey on Crowd Scene Anomaly Detection: Trends, Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: Mowafaq Salem Alzboon, Marwan Tayseer Al Zawahra, Mohammad Subhi Al-Batah, Muhyeeddin Alqaraleh
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: For public safety reasons, technology for public surveillance has expanded. With this, it has become a very difficult task in computer vision to detect abnormal phenomena in populated areas. The use of fixed cameras demands greater attention on automated surveillance of large public areas. Existing anomaly detection systems do not perform well under dense occlusions, continuous high frame rate video, and behaviors that involve contextually difficult inference. The fulfillment of these requirements indicates a demand for real-time anomaly detection, enabling the use of thousands of cameras, and accommodating highly unpredictable programmed missions and ambient conditions. This systematic review aims to describe and evaluate strategies for detecting crowd-scene anomalies that have appeared in peer-reviewed documents in the IEEE, ACM, Springer, and similar repositories. The rest of the paper is separated into categories of classical statistical techniques, modern machine learning, and new deep learning. Within these categories, the frameworks are classified on the basis of supervised, unsupervised, and self-supervised learning. Special attention is paid to the benchmark datasets utilized, the parameters for assessing performance, and the controlling conditions for each operational framework. The conclusion includes a qualitative comparison concerning the evidential and theoretical value of the methodologies surveyed. In practical effectiveness and diagnostic accuracy, the performance of deep learning representations, particularly Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), autoencoders, and graph-based Neural Networks (GN), greatly exceeds that of the traditional statistical methodologies. However, the speed of execution, causal reasoning, and the practical application of derived models remain significant barriers to adoption. Recent studies have shown promising approaches to crowd anomaly detection through the orchestration of edge computing, self-supervised learning, and the integration of disparate sensor networks. This paper is a comprehensive survey of crowd anomaly detection techniques and provides a cross-cutting taxonomy that integrates strengths and weaknesses, empirical performance, and future directions of the field. The comprehensive synthesis presented here aims to support both theorists and practitioners in developing reliable and efficient surveillance systems. However, there is still a need for the ongoing research to focus on higher degradation interpretability, lower computational overhead, and detectors tuned to the diverse urban environments and activity patterns

title: Reactive Power Fluctuation Optimization Control Method for Multi-Source Distribution Network Random Load Converter Based on Zebra Optimization Algorithm
authors: Jie Zhang, Shengchun Liu, Yuanhong Hou
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: Random loads cause dynamic distribution of reactive power cycle current vectors on the converter side, making it difficult to converge reactive power fluctuations in the distribution network. To this end, a study is conducted on the optimization control method for reactive current fluctuations in multi-source distribution network random load converters based on zebra optimization algorithm: the distribution of reactive current circulation is analyzed in vector form, and the dynamic relationship between zero reactive current conditions and related vectors is clarified; When using the zebra algorithm to solve inverter parameters, a nonlinear mechanism is set for the convergence factor to balance the search and output the optimal solution that adapts to load fluctuations. Simulation shows that this method can effectively reduce the reactive cycle current before optimization. After coordinate transformation, the active and reactive currents of the three-phase current on the AC side of the inverter converge, and the reactive power fluctuation remains stable within 3.2 kW.

title: A 63.9-aF 17.6-bit Adaptive-Load and Configurable Zoom Capacitance-to-Digital Converter with 43.3-pF Range
authors: Qingjiang Xia, Fei Zhou, Yuze Niu, Zhaofeng Huang, Mingzhong He, Xuefei Du, Wengao Lu, Yacong Zhang, Zhongjian Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper presents a Zoom capacitance-to-digital converter (CDC) for single-ended capacitive sensors. An adaptive-load scheme proposed reduces gain loss and signal attenuation in pseudo-differential Δ∑M. A configurable Zoom CDC introduced enhances performance at moderate resolution. The prototype is validated using two sensors with a 43.3-pF capacitance range, achieving 1) 63.9-aF resolution (17.6-bit ENOB) and 176.4 dB FoMs in Zoom mode, and 2) 4-μs response time, 173.8-fJ/conversion-step, and power-bandwidth scalability in SAR mode.

title: Design and Simulation of Thermal Management Systems for Charging Piles of New Energy Vessels under Inland Waterway Conditions
authors: Xuegang Zhao, Siqian Zhang, Jianlei Yang, Zhe Gao, Tianxiang Niu, Weiqiang Kong, Baocheng Lu, Wei Zhai
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Ships and Electromechanical System (ICISES)
abstract: With the escalating global energy shortage and environmental pollution, "carbon peaking and carbon neutrality" has become a core strategy to address climate change and achieve sustainable development. As one of the major sources of greenhouse gas emissions, the transportation sector faces prominent issues: traditional fuel-powered inland vessels not only consume substantial amounts of fossil energy but also exacerbate regional air pollution and carbon emissions. Currently, the transition of inland waterway shipping to new energy has become an inevitable trend; however, the performance and safety of new energy vessels heavily depend on thermal management systems. Batteries tend to generate significant amounts of heat during charging and discharging processes, and ineffective heat dissipation will lead to battery capacity degradation, shortened cycle life, and even trigger thermal runaway. This issue is particularly prominent in the application of lithium-ion batteries. While its core requirements align with those of battery thermal management for new energy vehicles, the marine scenario presents unique challenges.

title: Research on the Application of Speech Recognition in Marine Engine Room Simulator
authors: Tao Feng
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Intelligent Ships and Electromechanical System (ICISES)
abstract: To address the issue of the operator of the traditional marine engine room simulator having multiple responsibilities and complex human-machine interaction, speech recognition technology is applied to the marine engine room simulator. Collect and construct a corpus in the marine engineering field. Extract the MFCC features of speech signals. Establish an acoustic model based on the Kaldi platform. Use the SRILM tool to build a language model and compare the performance differences among various n-gram language models. To address the problem of sparse corpus, the Kneser-Ney smoothing algorithm is employed. The test results demonstrate that it is feasible to operate the marine engine room simulator through speech commands.

title: Automated Metadata Retrieval from Arabic Book Covers Using Deep Learning-Based Text Recognition Methods
authors: Nawal Aljuhaymi, Hasan Alkhadafe, Ibrahim Nasir
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Automatic extraction of bibliographic metadata from Arabic book covers remains a challenging task due to the artistic nature of cover designs, the diversity of Arabic typography, and the intrinsic complexity of Arabic script. In this paper, we propose a comprehensive deep learning-based framework for automatic metadata extraction from Arabic book cover images. The proposed system integrates robust image preprocessing, deep scene text detection, Arabic-optimized text recognition, and multimodal metadata classification. Text regions are first localized using YOLOv9, a state-of-the-art single-stage detector with Programmable Gradient Information (PGI) and Generalized Efficient Layer Aggregation Network (GELAN), capable of handling multi-scale and arbitrarily oriented text with superior accuracy and speed. Recognized text is then processed in parallel through spatial layout analysis, typographic feature extraction, and semantic embedding modules. These complementary features are fused through an attention-based mechanism to accurately classify text elements into bibliographic metadata categories such as title, author, and publisher. To support this work, we introduce a large, richly annotated dataset of Arabic book covers capturing wide variability in genre, design style, and image quality. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed system significantly outperforms state-of-the-art Arabic OCR and scene text recognition baselines, achieving an overall metadata extraction accuracy of 87.6%, with improvements exceeding 10 percentage points over the best competing method. The results confirm the effectiveness of multimodal feature fusion and specialized preprocessing for Arabic book cover analysis, offering a practical and scalable solution for digital library automation and cultural heritage preservation.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Hybrid Predictive-Inversion Self-Tuning Regulator for Automotive Speed Control in Comparison With IMC-Based PID
authors: Abdunaser Abdusamad, Farag I. k. Mousa
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: In many real-world driving scenarios, maintaining a desired vehicle speed becomes challenging when road conditions vary, particularly on uphill and downhill segments, which can degrade driving comfort and safety in automated driving modes. These terrain-induced disturbances motivate the need for adaptive and robust speed control strategies. This paper proposes a hybrid predictive inversion self-tuning regulator (STR) for automotive longitudinal speed control under nonlinear dynamics and external disturbances. The controller combines recursive least-squares (RLS) parameter estimation, online model inversion, and proportional integral (PI) feedback to enable real-time adaptation and robust tracking performance. A discrete-time state-space vehicle model is employed for online identification and predictive control updating. The proposed approach is evaluated through MATLAB simulations under multiple uphill and downhill driving scenarios and compared with a well-tuned IMCbased PID controller. Results demonstrate that the hybrid STR provides improved disturbance rejection, faster speed recovery, and reduced control effort, confirming its effectiveness for robust vehicle speed regulation in realistic driving conditions.

title: Adaptive Federated Learning Based Resource Management in Mobile Edge Computing for Machine Type Communication in 5G Network
authors: Asia E. Ahmed, Esraa Y. Hassan, Rashid A. Saeed, Ruaa S. Mohamed, Yusra Y. Hassan, Mamoon M. Saeed, Rania A Mokhtar, Omnia A. Ali, Mohammed S. Elbasheir
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) is an attractive method that allows the devices to train local models on the basis of private data and share only the model parameters with a central server, which guarantees privacy of data in terms of collaborative model training. Nevertheless, synchronous model updates and aggregation of all involved devices are impractical in mobile devices because of the limited wireless bandwidth and energy capabilities. In such a way, the choice of devices to fit in each training round is a serious issue, which influences the use of resources, energy efficiency, and communication delays. The issues are compounded by the device disparities and evolving network environments in 5G-enabled Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) environments that are capable of supporting Machine Type Communication (MTC). To sustain the performance and scalability, a well-coordinated resource management is required. To solve this, an adaptive resource management framework of MEC-assisted FL has been suggested and integrates smart device choice and dynamic resource allocation based on a Double Deep Q-Network (DDQN). The model used to model the device selection process as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) is that the DDQN can make adaptive decisions based on experience, considering energy consumption, computational load, and bandwidth consumption under different network and device conditions.

title: MSR-DCNet: Multi-Scale Spatial Re-Parameterization and Dual-Cross Fusion Attention for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Ruolin Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Retinal vessel segmentation plays a critical role in diagnosing neuro-ophthalmologic diseases and assessing cardiovascular health. However, the traditional manual segmentation is limited by low efficiency and high subjectivity. The current models for processing fundus images still face significant challenges such as poor background noise suppression, difficulty in balancing multi-scale morphological variations, and limited directional sensitivity of blood vessel structure. In order to address these limitations, we propose MSR-DCNet, which is a novel framework for accurate retinal vessel segmentation. The framework integrates a multi-scale spatial re-parameterization block (SRB), which can effectively expand the receptive field and capture multi-scale spatial features while maintaining the inference speed. It further uses a mask guided multi-scale feature enhancer (MSFE) to filter the background noise in the skip connection and bridge the semantic gap between the encoder and decoder. In addition, the dual crossover fusion attention (DCFA) mechanism is deployed to model long-range dependence, ensuring vascular connectivity and the preservation of fine branching details. Extensive verification on the public benchmark has confirmed its accuracy and robustness superior to the state-of-the- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\operatorname{art}($</tex> SOTA) methods, making it a reliable tool for clinical decision support.

title: HMU-Net: Hybrid Mamba U-Net for Breast Lesion Segmentation in Ultrasound
authors: Jun Geng, Yuxin Xie
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: In recent years, numerous deep learning methods have been proposed for tumor lesion segmentation in breast ultrasound images. However, accurate segmentation remains challenging due to factors such as similar intensity distributions between lesions and surrounding tissues, blurred lesion boundaries, and large variations in lesion size. In this paper, we propose a hybrid architecture, termed HMU-Net, for breast ultrasound image segmentation. HMU-Net takes VMU-net as the baseline and introduces ResNet34 as an auxiliary branch. With a dual-branch hybrid encoding strategy, the model enhances the extraction of local texture and boundary cues. In addition, we design a visual state-space large-kernel fusion module at the deep bottleneck to strengthen feature representation, and further improve the multipath SS2D scanning mechanism to capture richer contextual information. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed HMU-Net achieves superior segmentation performance compared with several state-of-the-art methods, confirming the effectiveness and advantage of the proposed hybrid architecture for breast tumor segmentation.

title: An 8-GHz Stacked-Core VCO With Reliability Under Standard Supply and Explicit Second-Harmonic Tuning Achieving 193.4dBc/Hz FoM in 65-nm CMOS
authors: Zhirui Zong, Renxu Yang
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: System-level overhead of reduced supply voltage and device reliability is often compromised for the performance of LC oscillators. This paper proposes a stacked-core voltage-controlled oscillator (VCO) with long-term reliability using standard supply voltage. Two symmetric oscillator units are stacked in series on the DC current path, so that each unit assumes half of the standard supply voltage. It creates margin for the oscillation swing to exceed the local supply voltage for higher power efficiency without reliability concerns. An auxiliary LC tank is introduced in the common-mode (CM) tail path between the two oscillator units to realize second-harmonic resonance and suppress the 1/f<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> phase noise (PN). It is tuned with differential capacitance for better robustness against parasitics. All the passives are implemented within a single-inductor footprint. Prototyped in 65-nm CMOS, the proposed VCO demonstrates a figure-of-merit (FoM) of 193.4dBc/Hz at 8GHz with 1-V supply and 310-410kHz 1/f<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> PN corner.

title: MedSRGAN: Hybrid CNN-Transformer GAN for High-Quality MRI Image Reconstruction
authors: Devi Priya R, Kaviya Sree S, Rajagopal B, Lakshitha Shree R M, Pavithra P, Sarmili M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Medical diagnostic systems play an important role in identifying and localizing affected regions in patients using Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI), where high-resolution MRI images provide clear structural information that supports early and accurate clinical decision-making. However, in general clinical practice, there are limitations in MRI scan image resolution due to hardware constraints, scanning time, and patient safety, which result in low-resolution images and reduce the visibility of fine anatomical details and small pathological changes. Because of this limitation, deep learning models are widely applied to enhance low-resolution MRI images into super-resolution images and improve the diagnostic performance. In this work, we propose a novel deep learning methodology called MedSRGAN, which is a hybrid generative adversarial network designed for medical image super-resolution. The proposed framework inte- grates an attention-based mechanism with a Vision Transformer (ViT) architecture to improve feature representation, where the transformer module is mainly used in the encoder to capture longrange contextual information and global dependencies, while the GAN structure helps in reconstructing realistic textures and preserving important structural details. This hybrid design enables the model to enhance spatial resolution while maintaining the clinical integrity of MRI images. The experimental results show that the proposed model achieved a 32.5 dB Peak Signal to Noise Ratio (PSNR) and a 0.93 Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM) for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\times 2$</tex> upsampling, which indicates better performance and structural consistency. Therefore, the proposed MedSRGAN framework can support super resolution of medical image for the critical diagnosis.

title: Neural Combinatorial Optimization for Real-World Vehicle Routing Problems with Limited Vehicles
authors: Yuanxu Yang, Zikang Yu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Vehicle routing problems (VRPs) play an important role in logistics and transportation planning. Although neural combinatorial optimization has recently achieved promising results for routing tasks, most existing approaches are developed and evaluated on simplified benchmark datasets. They rarely consider strict fleet-size limitations that commonly arise in real-world operations. To address this gap, we propose the Limited-vehicle Constrained Neural Combinatorial Optimization (LCNCO) framework. Specifically, we construct routing instances from real-world map data and study two problem variants involving asymmetric travel costs and limited vehicles. LCNCO employs an encoder-decoder neural architecture and a penalty-based reinforcement learning objective to guide the model toward feasible solutions. Experimental results on real-world datasets show that the proposed method produces solutions with high feasibility and competitive routing costs compared with existing methods.

title: Using Classification Algorithms to Predict and Evaluate the Performance of Kidney Disease
authors: Zakaria Suliman Zubi, Bassam Mohamed Alshehomiue
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: CKD) is a progressive condition that gradually impairs kidney function and may lead to End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) if not detected early. Recently, machine learning (ML) techniques have shown great potential in supporting medical diagnosis through the analysis of clinical data.This paper proposes a predictive framework for CKD diagnosis using four classification algorithms: K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Logistic Regression (LR), Decision Tree (DT), and Naïve Bayes. The models were trained on a dataset of 400 medical records (250 CKD and 150 non-CKD) and evaluated using 150 real patient records obtained from Misurata Kidney Center.The results show that the KNN model achieved the highest accuracy of 99%, while the other models produced accuracies between 97% and 99%. Ensemble methods, including Voting and Stacking classifiers, also achieved 99% accuracy. The proposed system demonstrates strong potential for assisting early CKD diagnosis in real clinical environments.

title: Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)

title: AlGaAs / GaAs Single Junction Solar Cell
authors: Archita Barman, Ram Krishan Kumar, Munna Kumar, Jayanti Choudhary
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: This thesis explores analytical models to enhance our understanding of the internal mechanisms limiting the efficiency of single-junction solar cells. The three primary subjects addressed are the fundamentals of solar cell functioning, variations in efficiency associated with AlGaAs/GaAs single-junction solar cell thickness, and variations in efficiency associated with AlGaAs/GaAs material doping. The thesis is separated into two parts for greater depth & clarity. The first part offers a comprehensive analysis of both basic and advanced solar cell physics ideas. It addresses subjects like the dynamics of charge carriers, the photovoltaic effect, and how material characteristics affect solar cell performance. Review topics include the photovoltaic effect, which turns light into electrical energy, and the flow and recombination of electrons and holes inside the cell. It also discusses how material characteristics like bandgap, absorption coefficient, and recombination rates affect the efficiency of a solar cell. A number of in-depth studies examining different aspects of solar cell efficiency make up the second part. This research specifically looks into how changing the thickness and doping levels of AlGaAs/GaAs material affects the overall efficiency of single-junction solar cells. The goal of the research is to determine the ideal thickness of the AlGaAs/GaAs layers in order to maximize light absorption and minimize recombination losses. It also looks at how thickness variations affect important variables, including fill factor, open-circuit voltage, and short-circuit current.

title: CPG-DIL-Based Locomotion Control for Quadruped Robots on Unstructured Terrain
authors: Jiayao Song, Zhiming Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This paper proposes a Hopf-CPG gait controller with DIL-based online frequency adaptation for quadruped locomotion on mildly undulating unstructured terrain. A fouroscillator coupled CPG network generates a trot gait via prescribed phase biases, and a joint mapping function produces hip and knee reference trajectories. A DIL module driven by joint amplitude errors tunes the intrinsic CPG frequency online under saturation and smoothness constraints to improve robustness to terrain disturbances and dynamics mismatch. Simulations in Isaac Gym show that, compared with a fixed-frequency CPG baseline, the proposed method rapidly adapts its frequency, reduces joint amplitude errors and body angular-velocity fluctuations, and improves locomotion stability on lightly uneven terrain.

title: Prompt-Optimization with Contextual Mining for Cross-Modal Image Compression
authors: Shenpeng Song, Zhimeng Huang, Junlong Gao, Chuanmin Jia, Siwei Ma
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: Recent advances in cross-modal compression(CMC) have opened new horizons for perceptual image coding at ultra-low bitrates (below 0.1 bpp) within a generative compression paradigm, but reconstruction fidelity is often compromised, yielding visually plausible yet semantically inconsistent reconstructions. While prompt engineering with contextual optimization has been extensively explored in generative models, its potential for controlling perception-fidelity trade-offs in image compression remains largely under-explored. To address these challenges, we propose PO-CMC, a novel diffusion-based cross-modal image compression approach that introduces contextual prompt optimization to achieve efficient and perceptually faithful reconstruction. The proposed method comprises three synergistic components: an optimized image codec that produces a compact structural prior, a contextual prompt module that adaptively encodes semantic cues into compact textual embeddings, and a diffusion-based decoder that fuses the structural and semantic priors to reconstruct high-fidelity images. Extensive experiments show that PO-CMC achieves superior perceptual quality while maintaining comparable reconstruction fidelity, yielding an average BD-rate saving of 72.5 % and 79.8 % over VVC at equivalent LPIPS and DISTS levels, respectively.

title: EEG Analysis with Power Spectral Density, Spectral Entropy, and Alpha/Beta Ratio for Brain State Classification
authors: Rodina Belkasem Taher, Salma Belal Ali
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Maghreb Meeting of the Conference on Sciences and Techniques of Automatic Control and Computer Engineering (MI-STA)
abstract: Electroencephalography (% EEG) analysis faces persistent challenges from low signal-to-noise ratio and artifact contamination. This study presents a hybrid analytical framework that integrates Power Spectral Density (PSD), Spectral Entropy (SE), and the alpha-to-beta ($\alpha / \beta$) power ratio for multi-feature EEG-based brain state classification. The analysis covers 30 subjects drawn from the PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Database across three distinct conditions: eyes closed, eyes open, and motor task. A fourstage preprocessing pipeline including ICA-based artifact removal is applied before feature extraction. Statistical validation through ANOVA and post-hoc t-tests reveals that the $\alpha / \beta$ ratio provides significant discrimination across all conditions (p<0.0001), while SE shows no statistically significant differences between states (p>0.05). An SVM classifier trained on the combined feature set achieves 89.3% overall accuracy, outperforming each single-metric approach: PSD alone (77.8%), SE alone (62.5%), and $\alpha/\beta$ ratio alone (84.1%). These results confirm that multi-feature integration improves classification reliability over individual metrics, even when not all features independently discriminate between conditions.

title: Optimizing Thermal Comfort and Energy Efficiency in Tropical Houses: A Case Study on Low-Enthalpy Geothermal Pre-cooling Systems
authors: Carlos Bran, Gilberto Carrillo
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: This paper presents a technical and economic feasibility study of low-enthalpy geothermal systems for air preconditioning in tropical housing. A real-world classroom in El Salvador served as the reference model for simulations and empirical measurements. Using CFD analysis in ANSYS Discovery and temperature data collected at $\mathbf{3 m}$ depth, the system’s behavior under varying soil and air conditions was examined. The results show that the geothermal system can reduce indoor air temperature by up to $5.6^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ without requiring electricity for compression-based cooling. Over a 20-year life cycle, the system offers savings of over $41,000 \mathrm{kWh}$ and avoids approximately 28.6 tons of CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emissions. Compared to conventional inverter-type air conditioning, the proposed solution reduces operating costs by up to $58.9 \%$ in new buildings. These findings support the integration of geothermal preconditioning systems as a sustainable, resilient, and economically viable strategy for climate adaptation in tropical regions. This work outlines the energy challenges of air conditioning in tropical regions, presents the design methodology for a low-enthalpy geothermal heat exchanger, and evaluates its performance through simulations and economic analysis.

title: MEEGET 2025 Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 First International Conference on Mechanical and Electrical Engineering for Green Energy Technologies (MEEGET)

title: Real-Time Monitoring of Supraharmonic Emissions in Low-Voltage Electric Equipment up to 150 kHz Frequency Range
authors: Julio Cesar Godinez-Delgado, Rafael Cisneros-Magaña, Aurelio Medina-Rios, Nandinii Barbosa-Cendejas
publish: 2025 IEEE 43rd Central America and Panama Convention (CONCAPAN XLIII)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid time-frequency approach for assessment supraharmonic emissions (up to 150 kHz) in low-voltage electric equipment in real-time. It combines discrete time-domain models with an extension of the discrete Fourier transform to improve the detection and analysis of harmonics and supraharmonics emissions in real-time. The simulation is performed using MatLab/Simulink® interacting with the RT-LAB ${ }^{\circledR}$ setup, allowing for continuous study of supraharmonic emissions over a wide frequency range up to 150 kHz. Future work is detailed and analyzed, as well as the advantages of this type of analysis for real-time monitoring.

title: Analysis of Convolutional Neural Networks on Real-Time Multi-Core Embedded Platforms
authors: Sarah Lounnaci, Djamila Bendouda, Pierre-Emmanuel Hladik, Claire Maiza
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Cyber Physical Systems (AICPS)
abstract: This article provides a bibliographical study of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) architecture analysis and methods of deploying them in embedded real-time multi-core platforms. We first review the main structures of CNNs and their representation as Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs). We then analyse the real-time execution challenges on multi-core architectures, focusing on shared-resource interference, common arbitration mechanisms, and discuss how Worst-Case Execution Time (WCET) and Worst-Case Response Time (WCRT) can be estimated using tools like OTAWA and MIA. Next, we survey existing analytical methods for timing analysis of CNN inference, including model representation formats and toolchains. Finally, we outline our ongoing research effort developing the MIND (Model Inference Network Designer) tool to automatically extract CNN structure into a DAG compatible with the MIA timing-analysis tool, and we end this article by giving some perspectives.

title: Explaining Arabic Sign Language Recognition: A YOLOv11 and Transformer Hybrid Model with Grad-CAM Visualizations
authors: Nabil Hezil, Ahmed Bouridane, Ghania Droua Hamdani, Oleg Rogov
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Cyber Physical Systems (AICPS)
abstract: Arabic Sign Language (ArSL) recognition plays a vital role in facilitating communication between deaf communities and the hearing population in Arab countries. This paper presents a novel approach to ArSL recognition from video using a combination of YOLOv11 and Transformer models. YOLOv11 is employed to detect and track hands in each video frame, enabling efficient extraction of spatial features, while a Transformer is used to model the temporal dependencies between frames for accurate gesture recognition. This hybrid model allows for the recognition of both isolated and continuous gestures, making it highly adaptable to real-world scenarios. To further enhance the explainability of the model, we integrated Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM), which provides visual insights into the areas of the input frames that the model focuses on during gesture recognition. By generating heatmaps, Grad-CAM helps identify whether the model accurately captures the relevant hand movements or if it mistakenly focuses on background elements, improving the interpretability of the model’s decision-making process. The proposed model was evaluated on the KArSL-100 dataset, achieving a training accuracy of 99.5% and a test accuracy of 99.5%, with a loss of 0.02. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of combining YOLOvll and Transformers for ArSL recognition, with strong performance in both training and testing phases, minimal overfitting, and robust explainability through Grad-CAM visualizations.

title: Contribution to the methodology of representing discontinuities: ideal and realistic approaches
authors: Mokhtar Bouhalouane, Larbi Sekhri
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Cyber Physical Systems (AICPS)
abstract: Combinatorial explosion is widely recognized as a challenging problem, especially in control theory. The complexity in hybrid dynamic systems lies in its heterogeneous dynamics where continuous dynamics and other discrete ones interact and influence each other. In this paper, we investigate through a model-oriented approach combining Bond graph and Petri nets, the space of reachable control states in a benchmarking process as a use case study. The benchmark interconnects two subsystems where the problem of tractability will be solved by involving fuzzy logic with distinguished notions of predictive and effective values. Some steps are taken to achieve this goal. The proposed method offers within the same model an induction of several cases which could only be treated case by case separately by previous works given in literature.

title: Decision-Oriented Carbon-Aware Scheduling for Green Cloud Infrastructure Management
authors: Rahul Bhatt, Ritika Mehra, Kamal Upreti
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: The rapid expansion of cloud computing infrastructures has led to significant growth in energy consumption and associated carbon emissions, creating an urgent need for sustainable scheduling strategies. While traditional task schedulers primarily optimize performance metrics such as latency and throughput, they largely ignore carbon efficiency and emission-aware decision making. This paper proposes a decision-oriented carbon-aware scheduling framework that integrates machine-learning-assisted queue prioritization with emission-sensitive placement policies in heterogeneous cloud clusters. Using large-scale production workload traces comprising over 405,000 jobs, the proposed framework is evaluated against conventional priority-based and ML-only schedulers. Experimental results show that carbonaware scheduling reduces per-job carbon emissions by up to 8.3% compared to baseline strategies while maintaining comparable throughput and zero SLA violations.

title: Path Planning Optimization for Mobile Robots Based on Improved Ant Colony Algorithm
authors: Li Li, Zhaorun Cao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: This paper presents an enhanced ant colony algorithm designed to overcome the inherent limitations of traditional approaches in mobile robot path planning, specifically their slow convergence, tendency to become trapped in local optima, and the generation of non-smooth paths.. First, nonuniform initialization of pheromone concentration and a hybrid transition strategy are introduced to enhance the algorithm's optimization capability in the early stages and accelerate convergence. Then, to improve global search ability and the quality of optimal solutions while avoiding blind search, a pheromone diffusion strategy and enhanced directional heuristics are incorporated into the pheromone update mechanism. By incorporating a multi-objective evaluation mechanism, the path planning problem is effectively converted into a multi-objective optimization task, leading to the comprehensive optimization of mobile robot path planning. To effectively reflect the performance of the proposed algorithm, experiments were conducted in two grid maps and compared with other improved ant colony algorithms.

title: ASIM 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)

title: Synesthesia-Integrated UAV Swarm Cooperative Perception and Communication Relay Optimization Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: Chen Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: The challenges inherent to the application of Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) drone swarms include the fact that accuracy of sensing and quality of communication is mutually constrained, that the complexity of deploying resources makes them incompatible and unable to adapt to dynamic environments when performing collaborative tasks. This article presents a multi-UAV collaborative optimization model described as a deep reinforcement learning that balances the sensing and communication performance through the simultaneous optimization of routes of UAVs, beamforming, and power distribution. To begin with, an ISAC system model is created, and the optimization problem is categorized as a Markov Decision Process (MDP). Second, a Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) algorithm is to be used to address the issue. The Actor network generates the control policy of the continuous action space and the Critic network appraises the state-action value function. Lastly, the priority experience replay is presented to expedite convergence. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithm achieves a positioning error of 1.87 m in time slot 300, with a communication interruption probability as low as 3.1 %, and maintains an energy efficiency of 4.29 Mbit/J in a high-density scenario with 16 users. This scheme effectively solves the problem of multi-objective coupled optimization and provides a new approach for intelligent decision-making in ISAC UAV swarms.

title: Improved CMOPSO for PID Tuning of a Hydraulic Synchronization System in Dynamic Environments
authors: Ying Hu, Shutao Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: In master-slave synchronous control of electro-hydraulic servo systems operating in dynamic environments, maintaining online optimal PID parameters is challenging. This paper proposes a prediction- and transfer-learning-driven dynamic multiobjective competitive mechanism particle swarm optimizer (BDA-DMOCMOPSO). Built upon the competitive mechanism based multi-objective particle swarm optimizer (CMOPSO), the proposed method introduces environment perception and migration learning. A Balanced Distribution Adaptation (BDA)-based prediction model forecasts promising solutions after environmental changes to rapidly initialize the population. In addition, a multi-source transfer strategy exploits elite populations from historical environments to improve tracking capability and convergence stability. Simulation results show that, compared with CMOPSO without transfer mechanisms, the proposed algorithm responds faster to parameter perturbations, significantly reduces synchronization error, and maintains good solution-set distribution and robustness. These results demonstrate the effectiveness and engineering potential of the proposed method for dynamic controller-parameter optimization.

title: Method for Lightning Fault Location in Distribution Networks Based on the Initiation Time of ZnO Surge Arrester Operating Current
authors: Yang Shen, Xi Chen, Siyuan Wang, Zhiyuan Zhang, Jiali Liu, Hongshun Liu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: After a power distribution network is struck by lightning, lightning overvoltage can cause insulation breakdown of equipment or failure of line functions, affecting the stable and efficient operation of the distribution network. Traditional lightning strike fault location methods are often interfered by the power frequency voltage and current of the power grid, resulting in difficulties in extracting lightning current characteristic parameters and insufficient location accuracy. Moreover, they generally adopt a linear model without considering the impact of the complex topological structure of the power grid. Based on the starting time of the operating current of zinc oxide surge arresters widely installed in the distribution network, this paper proposes a new lightning strike fault location method. This method uses the starting time of the operating current of surge arresters and combines distance inversion calculation to achieve accurate fault location, and analyzes the location reliability of this method in different topological networks. Simulation results show that this method can accurately locate fault points in complex distribution network topologies, is not affected by power grid power frequency current and load fluctuations, and has stronger anti-interference ability, providing an efficient solution for the rapid troubleshooting and location of lightning strike faults.

title: The Determinants of Adoption of Open Access Scholarly Communication Among Researchers
authors: Noor Abutayeh, Reda Al-Mawadieh, Malek Jdaitawi, Mohammad Alzoubi
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: This study aims at investigating the factors affecting the adoption of using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O A$</tex> scholarly communication in Jordan. The study employed a mixed approach using a stratified random sampling methodology to survey 84 faculty members from humanities and social science disciplines. Results indicate that the researchers have high awareness of OA platforms and strong recognition of their importance for scholarly communication. Additionally, the results revealed that perceived efficiency in knowledge dissemination, visibility and appeal of the researcher work as well as career-related motivations are the most crucial factors in adopting <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O A$</tex> decisions. Based on these findings, the study provides several recommendations including enhanced university and library support for OA infrastructure, advocate for comprehensive training initiatives, suggesting regular workshops to develop faculty publishing competencies in OA environments. Additionally, the study emphasizes the need for structured knowledge-sharing mechanisms to foster sustainable OA practices across academic departments. The findings offer valuable insights for academic administrators and library professionals working to enhance scholarly communication systems in comparable higher education contexts.

title: Smart Crop Disease Detection Using Federated Learning
authors: Nalam Venkata Naga Avinash, Mohammad Roshan, E. Sophia
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: The health monitoring of crops is an essential part of agricultural activities that is friendly to the environment. The early detection of diseases affecting the crops is useful for farmers since proper preventive and remedial measures are taken on time, hence saving money. The research work presented shows that deep learning along with artificial intelligence is used for the development of the Crop Health Management System (CHMS). The proposed system uses CNN for identifying plant diseases on the basis of images of leaves. The ordered body of knowledge is used for providing adequate intervention and treatment measures. The aim of the research is to enable the development of the web-based system that facilitates the farmer in capturing the image of the leaves. The proposed system is based on the model of artificial intelligence, which is presented with the help of a neural network that uses deep learning for creating a model of the CNN. The discrete knowledge of agriculture is used for providing adequate guidelines to a farmer. In addition, it has been clearly presented that the accuracy of the system is 95% and the inference is of low latency. This shows that the system is appropriate for implementation.

title: 3D Parametric Simulations of Mass Transport and Mechanical Properties of a Microfluidic Barrier-on-Chip Device
authors: S.D.M. De Jong, P.V. Tawade, M. Mastrangeli, W.D. Van Driel
publish: 2026 27th International Conference on Thermal, Mechanical and Multi-Physics Simulation and Experiments in Microelectronics and Microsystems (EuroSimE)
abstract: Organ-on-Chip (OoC) devices enable controlled replication of physiological microenvironments and are increasingly used in biomedical engineering and drug discovery. This study presents a comprehensive 3D simulation-based characterization of mass transport and mechanical properties of a silicon-based Barrier-on-Chip (BoC) device comprising two stacked microfluidic channels separated by a porous Si<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf> membrane. Steady-state laminar flow simulations are performed to obtain the velocity field, after which transient convection–diffusion simulations are conducted to evaluate species transport toward and across the membrane. The mechanical performance of the membrane is analyzed using coupled Fluid–Structure Interaction (FSI) simulations with effective material properties accounting for porosity. Three flow configurations namely coflow, counter-flow, and single-channel flow are investigated across four volumetric flow rates. The results show that both flow configuration and flow rate strongly influence concentration distributions along the membrane. Counter-flow produces the strongest axial concentration gradient, which becomes more spatially uniform at higher flow rates as convection-dominated transport increasingly governs the system. Higher flow rates also result in larger membrane deflections, with counter-flow producing deflections approximately two orders of magnitude greater than co-flow at low flow rates for both configurations. Wall shear stress increases linearly with flow rate, spanning the physiologically relevant ranges for intestinal epithelial and blood-brain barrier endothelial cells. These findings provide quantitative design guidelines for selecting flow configurations and rates based on target concentration gradients, shear stress levels, and mechanical constraints in BoC applications.

title: Topology of a Bidirectional Solid-State DC Circuit Breaker Based on Resonant Commutation and Reusable Charging-Discharging Energy
authors: Yanan Fei
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel bidirectional solid-state DC circuit breaker (SSCB) that integrates resonant commutation with component reuse technology. The core innovation is a reconfigurable auxiliary branch enabling cyclic reuse of capacitors and resistors across multiple operational stages, significantly reducing cost and complexity. Simulations under ± 10 kV/20 kA conditions verify a 300 µs interruption time and 250 ms voltage recovery, demonstrating a compact, high-performance solution for MVDC systems.

title: Improved Panoramic Image Stitching Using Relative Attributes of Detected Features
authors: Joseph Nabhan, Lama Affara
publish: 2025 26th International Arab Conference on Information Technology (ACIT)
abstract: Image stitching is an area of research at the intersection of the field of computer vision and image processing, involving the seamless merging of multiple overlapping images into a cohesive, panoramic representation. This work focuses on reducing the time complexity of the state-of-the-art image stitching process by calculating and detecting the outliers in the interest points' relative attributes. We apply different clustering and outlier detection techniques, compare them with the state of the art and analyze the performance of our approach both in terms of stitching accuracy and computation speed. We perform our experiments on UDIS-D and GES image stitching datasets including image sequences of varying indoor and outdoor scenes, sizes, lighting conditions, and perspective distortion measures. The results of our evaluation show that up to 5x average speedup compared to the state of the art approach can be achieved by making use of the attributes inherited within the feature detection step, and more interestingly, without sacrificing precision of the panoramic stitching result.

title: A Small Animal Detection Framework for Power Transmission and Distribution Scenarios Based on Collaboration Between Small and Large Models
authors: Minghao Liu, Yingyi Yang, Jiayang Lai, Xian Qu, Shiran Li, Lin Yi, Xiaoming Mai
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Advanced Sensing, Intelligent Manufacturing (ASIM)
abstract: Aiming at the core pain points of small animal detection in power transmission and distribution scenarios—difficulty in balancing real-time performance and accuracy, and complex scene interference—this paper proposes a collaborative framework of "YOLO series small models for initial detection + Qwen2.5-VL large model for precise judgment". First, the YOLO series model optimized for power transmission and distribution scenarios is used to quickly locate small animals around equipment and automatically crop target areas. Then, the cross-modal semantic understanding capability of Qwen2.5-VL is utilized to eliminate interferences such as metal reflections and cable occlusions, correct misjudgments of similar species, and determine animal categories. Experiments based on Power-Animal-2024, a dedicated dataset for power transmission and distribution, show that: the framework achieves a detection speed of 15 FPS; its mAP@0.5 is 6.3% higher than that of the standalone YOLO series; and the false detection rate is reduced by 8.9%. This framework can effectively prevent power accidents caused by small animal intrusion and meet the operation and maintenance needs of multiple scenarios, such as substations, distribution rooms, and line towers.

title: Implementation of Smart Agriculture using ESP32-based IoT and Machine Learning for Monitoring Crop
authors: Balagani Praveena, Kuntrapakam Punith, K Lalith Kumar, Shaik Mohammad Rabbani, P. Geetha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Trends in Material Science and Inventive Materials (ICTMIM)
abstract: Smart agriculture has become a sustainable solution to enhance the productivity of crops through the use of Internet of Things (IoT) technology and machine learning. In this project Smart Agriculture Implementation through the ESP-32 based IoT and machine learning to monitor crops and provide recommendations are presented, thus connecting real-time field monitoring to cloud connectivity and intelligent decision-making. The given system uses ESP32 microcontroller as the central processing unit, connected to various sensors including DHT11 (temperature and humidity), soil-related sensors (via LDR / light intensity), PIR sensor and flame sensor to constantly control the condition of the environment and field. A solar panel with charge controller is used to provide power to the system and is therefore energy efficient with its ability to be installed in remote agricultural fields. The ESP32 captures sensor data which are shown on a 16X2 LCD display and at the same time sent to the cloud via Blynk IoT to allow farmers to monitor their farms or farms in real time. The obtained IoT data is pre-processed, segmented and features extracted to discard noise and derive suitable parameters. A LSTM (Long Short- Term Memory) model is then used to examine the temporal trends in the realtime and historical data of agriculture. Such a model does not only foretell the future of the environment, but also proposes the most appropriate crop as per the prevailing temperatures, humidity, and light intensity among other factors. The new system will improve precision farming through data-driven crop advice, early warning, and constant surveillance, which will lead to resource saving and high-quality yields. The system is costeffective, scalable, and appropriate to be used in smart farming because it integrates renewable energy, IoT and machine learning to enhance this system.

title: LRCF-UNet: A Dual-Encoder Photovoltaic Panel Segmentation Network Based on Hybrid Edge Priors
authors: Lvrong Lin, Wenpei Han
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
abstract: Automatic segmentation of photovoltaic (PV) modules from Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) imagery is crucial for solar plant maintenance but remains challenging due to dense arrangements and complex backgrounds. Existing methods often struggle with boundary delineation, leading to module “adhesion.” To address this, we propose LRCF-UNet, a Dual-Branch Edge-Aware U-Net. Our method features: 1) a hybrid edge enhancement module combining the Laplacian operator and Richer Convolutional Features (RCF) to generate robust edge priors; 2) a dual-branch independent encoder that decouples semantic texture extraction from geometric boundary learning; and 3) a deep fusion decoder to integrate these features. Experimental results demonstrate that LRCF-UNet significantly outperforms the baseline U-Net and mainstream networks, achieving superior edge accuracy and effectively resolving module adhesion.

title: Epileptic Seizure Detection Based on Hybrid Spike Detection with Wavelet Transform and Temporal Gradient Analysis Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Brahim Cherouati, Youcef Fekir
publish: 2025 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Cyber Physical Systems (AICPS)
abstract: This work presents an approach for epileptic seizure detection based on hybrid spike detection combining wavelet transform and temporal gradient analysis, followed by classification using artificial intelligence. EEG signals are first processed using a discrete wavelet transform to extract frequency-based features, while temporal gradients highlight rapid signal changes indicative of epileptic spikes. A hybrid method merges both techniques to detect spike positions, around which fixed-size segments are extracted. These segments are labeled and used to train two classifiers, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and a Support Vector Machine (SVM). Experiments are conducted on labeled Bonn EEG data containing interictal and ictal segments. The proposed method achieves promising results, with the CNN reaching an average accuracy of 98.01% and the SVM achieving 93.16%. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of combining signal processing and Al techniques for robust seizure detection, supporting the potential of hybrid approaches for real-world EEG-based diagnostic systems.

title: A Melodic Authentication Mechanism: An Approach Integrating TRIZ, Combinatorics, and User Experience Modeling
authors: Tsei-Hsuan Chen, Chia-Nan Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Technology, Informatics, Management, Engineering and Environment (TIME-E)
abstract: The vulnerability of critical infrastructure to cyberattacks reveals fundamental weaknesses in traditional One-Time Passwords (OTPs), which are susceptible to interception and impose usability challenges. This study introduces a melodic authentication mechanism as a selective form of multi-factor authentication (MFA) that leverages long-term auditory memory and voice interaction to balance security and convenience. Rooted in the Theory of Inventive Problem Solving (TRIZ), the mechanism transforms password entry into perceptual recognition of familiar melodies, addressing the classical contradiction between reliability and usability. Experimental design analysis suggests that with a curated library of 3,000 melodies, randomly shuffled clips presented during each session can ensure high resistance to guessing and replay attacks while maintaining short interaction time and low cognitive effort. Preliminary comparisons with OTP indicate advantages in interception resistance, accessibility for visually impaired users, and memory efficiency. Rather than serving as a universal replacement, this mechanism is envisioned as a high-assurance layer for privileged access systems in healthcare, finance, and industrial control. The study provides a conceptual yet quantifiable foundation for integrating TRIZ-based reasoning into human-centered authentication design.

title: Human-AI Collaboration: Redefining HR Roles and Capabilities
authors: Gurwinder Singh, Anil Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE Madhya Pradesh Section Conference (MPCON)
abstract: The introduction of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Human Resources (HR) is changing its nature from a simple automation tool to a catalytic factor that is forming a new paradigm of human-AI interaction. This paper argues that AI will not replace HR; instead, a symbiotic connection will emerge that redefines the strategic and augmented values of HR with operational efficiency. The paper thoroughly examines the role of AI in optimizing the critical HR functions, such as talent acquisition, on-boarding, learning and development, performance management, and employee engagement, which enables professionals to work on higher-order functions that require strategic, empathetic, and creative capabilities. We also closely analyze the significant challenges associated with this transition, such as the ethical considerations, the data protection issues, the change management, and the necessity of new skills. Further, the paper presents a future model of building HR capabilities, with a special focus on the competencies of AI stewardship, data literacy, ethical governance, and strategic impact. Finally, the study formalizes the Human-AI-collaboration as a measurable sociotechnical framework and defines the quantitative evaluation indicators that can be used to assess the collaborative HR decision-making.

title: Voxel-GS: Quantized Scaffold Gaussian Splatting Compression with Run-Length Coding
authors: Chunyang Fu, Xiangrui Liu, Shiqi Wang, Zhu Li
publish: 2026 Data Compression Conference (DCC)
abstract: Substantial Gaussian splatting format point clouds require effective compression. In this paper, we propose Voxel-GS, a simple yet highly effective framework that departs from the complex neural entropy models of prior work, instead achieving competitive performance using only a lightweight rate proxy and run-length coding. Specifically, we employ a differentiable quantization to discretize the Gaussian attributes of Scaffold-GS. Subsequently, a Laplacian-based rate proxy is devised to impose an entropy constraint, guiding the generation of high-fidelity and compact reconstructions. Finally, this integer-type Gaussian point cloud is compressed losslessly using Octree and run-length coding. Experiments validate that the proposed rate proxy accurately estimates the bitrate of run-length coding, enabling Voxel-GS to eliminate redundancy and optimize for a more compact representation. Consequently, our method achieves a remarkable compression ratio with significantly faster coding speeds than prior art. The code is available at https://github.com/zb12138/VoxelGS.

title:
A 175-dB FoM
                    <sub>S</sub>
                    12-bit 400-MS/s Pipeline-SAR ADC With an Optimized Detect-and-Skip Technique and a High-Efficiency Gain-Calibration-Free Amplification Scheme
authors: Jiang Xu, Xuqiang Zheng, Zedong Wang, Hua Xu, Sai Li, Fangxu Lv, Mingche Lai, Xinyu Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE Custom Integrated Circuits Conference (CICC)
abstract: This paper presents a ring-amplifier-based pipeline-SAR ADC that incorporates both optimized detect-and-skip (DAS) and weighted-averaging correlated level shifting (WACLS) to improve its power efficiency and accuracy. In the first stage, a tiny level-shifting capacitor array with simple control logic is introduced to replace the conventional large gain-mismatch compensation capacitor. It compensates for the gain mismatch between the coarse and fine quantization DACs by properly shifting the DAC output without any area, power, or speed penalty. The WACLS and second-stage DAS are co-designed by rearranging the second-stage capacitor array into the coarse and fine DACs required for WACLS, while reusing the coarse DAC for the DAS operation. This combined approach simultaneously increases the ring amplifier's equivalent gain, enhances the second-stage operation speed, and reduces the second-stage DAC switching power. Fabricated in 28-nm CMOS, the ADC consumes 2.58 mW from a 1-V supply and occupies 0.01-mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> core area while achieving 63.16-dB signal-to-noise and distortion ratio and 80.1-dB spurious-free dynamic range at Nyquist, resulting in Schreier and Walden figure of merit of 175.0 dB and 5.49 fJ/conv-step, respectively.
